answer: Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
1,1,3,3-tetramethyl-1,3-disiloxanediol (HOSiMe2OSiMe2OH) reacts with Ta(OEt)5 to form siloxy complexes where it acts as a bridging ligand, replacing terminal ethoxide groups.
Synthesis, structural and spectroscopic characterization, catalytic properties, and thermal transformations of new cyclic di- and trisiloxanediolato tantalum complexes.
Monofluoro-cyclooctyne can be used to facilitate bioconjugation to azide-modified peptides via Cu-free click chemistry.
A DOTA-peptide conjugate by copper-free click chemistry.
Cyclopropeniminium scaffolds are sufficiently stable in biological media to be used in bioorthogonal ligation reactions.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

Oxindolyl moieties are formed by myeloperoxidase-mediated conversion of tryptophan residues at positions 8, 50, 72, and 108 of human apoA1, resulting in loss of the athero-protective properties of HDL.
An abundant dysfunctional apolipoprotein A1 in human atheroma.

Benzoylphosphonic acid methyl ester forms a covalent adduct with thiamin diphosphate (ThDP) in benzoylformate decarboxylase, adopting a similar configuration at C2alpha as observed in other thiamin enzymes.
Detection and time course of formation of major thiamin diphosphate-bound covalent intermediates derived from a chromophoric substrate analogue on benzoylformate decarboxylase.

Biimidazoline is a chiral ligand used in Ni/photoredox-catalyzed enantioselective cross-electrophile coupling of styrene oxides with aryl iodides, enabling access to enantioenriched 2,2-diarylalcohols.
Ni/Photoredox-Catalyzed Enantioselective Cross-Electrophile Coupling of Styrene Oxides with Aryl Iodides.
5-azidoneuraminic acid (Neu5N3) can be incorporated into α2-8-linked sialosides via a chemoenzymatic α2-8-sialylation system using Neu5Acα2-3GalβpNP as an acceptor.
Parallel chemoenzymatic synthesis of sialosides containing a C5-diversified sialic acid.
Copper(i) acetylides exhibit 'unstoppable' reactivity with organic azides, enabling high-yielding and regioselective formation of 1,4-disubstituted 1,2,3-triazole products.
Copper(I)-catalyzed cycloaddition of bismuth(III) acetylides with organic azides: synthesis of stable triazole anion equivalents.
The electronic character of biimidazoline ligands is the main contributor to enantioinduction in Ni/photoredox-catalyzed cross-coupling reactions, as shown by multivariate linear regression analysis.
Ni/Photoredox-Catalyzed Enantioselective Cross-Electrophile Coupling of Styrene Oxides with Aryl Iodides.

The decarboxylation of 1-(β-d-erythrofuranosyl)orotate is activated by interactions between its phosphodianion and OMPDC, which provide 12 of the 31 kcal/mol transition state stabilization for this reaction.
Enzyme Architecture: Erection of Active Orotidine 5'-Monophosphate Decarboxylase by Substrate-Induced Conformational Changes.

The enzyme-catalyzed deuterium exchange reaction of 1-(β-D-erythrofuranosyl) 5-fluorouracil by OMPDC is activated by binding of phosphite dianion (HPO32-), resulting in an 11.2 kcal/mol increase in the second-order rate constant (kcat/Km).
OMP decarboxylase: phosphodianion binding energy is used to stabilize a vinyl carbanion intermediate.
Binding of benzoylphosphonic acid methyl ester to benzoylformate decarboxylase involves a two-step mechanism, with carbonyl addition to ThDP being 200-fold slower than for benzoylformic acid.
Detection and time course of formation of major thiamin diphosphate-bound covalent intermediates derived from a chromophoric substrate analogue on benzoylformate decarboxylase.
2′-O-(o-nitrobenzyl)uridine can be used to synthesize uridine-containing oligoribonucleotides with photolabile 2′-O-(o-nitrobenzyl) groups, enabling ribozyme-catalyzed reactions after photolysis.
Synthesis and incorporation of the phosphoramidite derivative of 2'-O-photocaged 3'-s-thioguanosine into oligoribonucleotides: substrate for probing the mechanism of RNA catalysis.

4,5-dimethoxyhomophthalic anhydride is synthesized by established literature procedures and is used in the condensation with a Schiff base to form an isoquinolone ring system in hydroxyindenoisoquinoline synthesis.
Facile and convenient syntheses of 6,11-dihydro-5H-indeno[1,2-c]isoquinolin- 5-ones and 6,11-dihydro-5H-indolo[3,2-c]isoquinolin-5-one.
Norcarane-d 4 serves as a substrate in AlkB-catalyzed reactions, with deuterium incorporation resulting in a large isotope effect for desaturation and hydroxylation at C3.
Parallel and competitive pathways for substrate desaturation, hydroxylation, and radical rearrangement by the non-heme diiron hydroxylase AlkB.
Cyclopropeniminium ions (CpN+) function as bioorthogonal reagents capable of reacting with cellular nucleophiles such as thiols and amines.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
The Gly-Gly-Pro-Nle sequence is arranged in subsites S1–S4 of the cathepsin K active site, facilitating the stepwise release of PGE1 from HPMA copolymer conjugates.
Water-soluble HPMA copolymer--prostaglandin E1 conjugates containing a cathepsin K sensitive spacer.

2-ethyl-1-indanone undergoes asymmetric α-arylation with chloroarenes catalyzed by Ni(COD)2 and (ε)-BINAP, resulting in coupled products with high enantiomeric excess (90–99%) and good to excellent yield.
Nickel-catalyzed asymmetric α-arylation and heteroarylation of ketones with chloroarenes: effect of halide on selectivity, oxidation state, and room-temperature reactions.

3-methyl-2-vinylindole undergoes catalytic asymmetric formal [3+2] cycloaddition with 3-indolylmethanol to form spiro[cyclopenta[b]indole-1,3'-oxindole] derivatives with high stereoselectivity and enantioselectivity.
One-Pot Catalytic Asymmetric Synthesis of Tetrahydrocarbazoles.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
Concise means for accessing an advanced precursor to 1-deoxypaclitaxel.

Plagionicin A is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.
Manadosterol A is a dimeric sterol isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa that exhibits inhibitory activity against the formation of the Ubc13-Uev1A complex.
Manadosterols A and B, sulfonated sterol dimers inhibiting the Ubc13-Uev1A interaction, isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa.
Integramycin is a complex natural product with significant and novel activity against HIV-1 integrase, making it a promising candidate for new anti-HIV therapy.
Structure, stereochemistry, and biological activity of integramycin, a novel hexacyclic natural product produced by Actinoplanes sp. that inhibits HIV-1 integrase.

Plagioneurin B is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.

Plagioneurin D is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.
22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A is a newly identified 3-alkylpiperidine alkaloid isolated from the marine sponge Amphimedon sp. and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P388 murine leukemia cells.
Tetradehydrohalicyclamine A and 22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A, new cytotoxic bis-piperidine alkaloids from a marine sponge Amphimedon sp.
1-deoxypaclitaxel analogues can be synthesized from the naturally occurring compound 1-deoxybaccatin VI via selective deacylation at specific acetyl positions.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
Zooxanthellamide D (ZAD-D, C54H83NO19) is a long-chain polyhydroxy polyene amide isolated from a cultured marine dinoflagellate of the genus Symbiodinium.
Zooxanthellamide D, a polyhydroxy polyene amide from a marine dinoflagellate, and chemotaxonomic perspective of the Symbiodinium polyols.
28-epirapamycin is an epimerized form of the immunosuppressant rapamycin, produced under mild conditions via a two-step retroaldol/aldol macrocycle ring-opening/ring-closing sequence.
Selective epimerization of rapamycin via a retroaldol/aldol mechanism mediated by titanium tetraisopropoxide.

Riisein B is a steroidal glycoside isolated from the Brazilian telestacean octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei, with the structure 3beta-O-(4'-O-acetyl-beta-D-arabinopyranosyl)-25xi-choles tan e-3beta, 5alpha,6beta,26-tetrol-26-acetate.
New cytotoxic sterol glycosides from the octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei.
Nocathiacin IV can be synthesized from nocathiacin I by cleavage of the dehydroalanine unit using a mixture of iodomethane and hydriodic acid in tetrahydrofuran at 45°C.
Mild method for cleavage of dehydroalanine units: highly efficient conversion of nocathiacin I to nocathiacin IV.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the aqueous extract of the lithistid demosponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis and is responsible for the observed anti-tumor activity in HCT-116 human colon carcinoma cells.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.

Streptovaricin U is an ansa-like antibiotic that contains a C-3/C-13 fragment synthesized via stereoselective addition of sulfur ylides to aldehydes, enabling the construction of polypropionate frameworks.
Stereoselective synthesis of macrolide-type antibiotics from epoxy amides. Synthesis of the polypropionate chain of streptovaricin u.

Alstiphyllanine B is an alkaloid isolated from Alstonia macrophylla that shows moderate antiplasmodial activity against Plasmodium falciparum and vasorelaxant activity against phenylephrine-induced contraction of isolated rat aorta.
Alstiphyllanines A-D, indole alkaloids from Alstonia macrophylla.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis, characterized by a 35-membered macrolide lactam ring, a pentadiene conjugated system, a tetrasubstituted tetrahydropyran ring, and a linear polyketide moiety attached via an amide linkage.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis, characterized by a 35-membered macrolide lactam ring, a pentadiene conjugated system, a tetrasubstituted tetrahydropyran ring, and a linear polyketide moiety attached via an amide linkage.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.
Goniomedine A is a bisindole alkaloid with a quebrachamine-pleioarpamine-type skeleton featuring indole moieties fused via a dihydropyran unit, isolated from the stem bark of Gonioma malagasy.
Goniomedines A and B: unprecedented bisindole alkaloids formed through fusion of two indole moieties via a dihydropyran unit.

N-carbobenzoxyglycine vinyl ester inhibits pregnancy in mice when administered intravaginally and also inhibits acrosin enzymatic activity as measured by N-alpha-benzoyl-L-arginine ethyl ester and azocasein hydrolysis assays.
Antifertility activity of N-protected glycine activated esters.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
Synthesis of trimethylhydroquinone derivatives as anti-allergic agents with anti-oxidative actions.
Landomycin A inhibits the uptake of [3H]thymidine into DNA in murine smooth muscle cells, indicating decreased DNA synthesis.
Landomycin A inhibits DNA synthesis and G1/S cell cycle progression.
3-methyl-3-piperidinooxindole exhibits antiarrhythmic activity against chloroform-induced arrhythmias in mice, with efficacy equal to lidocaine but with only one-third the acute toxicity.
Antiarrhythmic activity of 3-amino-3-methyloxindoles.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene (trans-B[a]P-4,5-diol) is a K-region dihydrodiol metabolite of benzo[a]pyrene that can be metabolized by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to six major products: two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:7,8-tetrahydroxy-4,5:7,8-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:9,10-tetrahydroxy-4,5:9,10-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, and two diastereomers of trans,trans-1,2:4,5-tetrahydroxy-1,2:4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.
MPP+ (the active metabolite of MPTP) at 10⁻⁶ M and 10⁻⁵ M potently and rapidly inhibits DOPAC output and stimulates dopamine (DA) release in the rat corpus striatum in a dose-dependent manner.
The mechanism of action of MPTP and MPP+ on endogenous dopamine release from the rat corpus striatum superfused in vitro.
Arachidic acid ethyl ester (20:0) elevates intracellular Ca 2+ in a concentration-dependent manner, contributing to ethanol-induced pancreatic injury.
Ethanol toxicity in pancreatic acinar cells: mediation by nonoxidative fatty acid metabolites.
Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds are inhibitors of mitochondrial threonine dehydrogenase (TDH) that impede cell growth and induce autophagy in mouse embryonic stem (ES) cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.

Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds selectively inhibit TDH-mediated threonine catabolism in mouse ES cells, failing to affect the growth of HeLa cells at concentrations 300 times higher than those required to kill ES cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene can be metabolically activated by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to form stable polar DNA adducts, with the trans,trans-B[a]P-4,5:7,8-bis-diols serving as metabolic intermediates in this process.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-methoxyethanamine (PCMEA) is a phencyclidine-derived designer drug that undergoes metabolism in rats to common metabolites through N-dealkylation, O-dealkylation, oxidation, and hydroxylation of the cyclohexyl ring, with all metabolites partially excreted in conjugated form.

New designer drugs N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-ethoxyethanamine (PCEEA) and N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-methoxyethanamine (PCMEA): Studies on their metabolism and toxicological detection in rat urine using gas chromatographic/mass spectrometric techniques.

The R(+) isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate causes delayed neuropathy by covalently inhibiting NTE at the active site without requiring an 'aging' reaction, suggesting that molecular environment around the phosphorus atom is critical for DN induction.
The R-(+)isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate causes delayed neuropathy in hens after generation of a form of inhibited neuropathy target esterase (NTE) which can be reactivated ex vivo.
Kainite induces excitotoxic injury in rat hippocampal slices, which is associated with decreased GluR1 neuronal marker and increased cPLA(2) and 4-HNE immunoreactivities.
Differential effects of calcium-dependent and calcium-independent phospholipase A(2) inhibitors on kainate-induced neuronal injury in rat hippocampal slices.
Caffeine oleate exhibits high bioactivity against Hypothenemus hampei when formulated as an emulsion, but shows no bioactivity when formulated in aqueous solution.
Insecticidal activity of caffeine aqueous solutions and caffeine oleate emulsions against Drosophila melanogaster and Hypothenemus hampei.

[11C]-Busulfan rapidly crosses the blood-brain barrier in vivo, with brain radioactivity peaking in the cortex and white matter at a ratio of 1.25 at 3 min and declining to approximately 1 after 30 min in cynomolgus monkeys.
In vivo distribution of [11C]-busulfan in cynomolgus monkey and in the brain of a human patient.

3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-dibenz[a,h]anthracene is a distal bisdihydrodiol metabolite of dibenz[a,h]anthracene that forms highly polar DNA adducts contributing 38% ± 12 pmol/mg DNA to adducts in Aroclor 1254-treated rat liver microsome incubations.
Characterization of highly polar DNA adducts derived from dibenz[A,H]anthracene (DBA), 3,4-dihydroxy-3,4-dihydro-DBA, and 3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-DBA.

Repetitive intratesticular injection of 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline complex of oxovanadium(IV) at 7.5 mg/kg testis induces germ cell apoptosis, decreased sperm counts, and reduced testicular weights in vivo.
Bis(4,7-dimethyl and 5-dinitro-1,10-phenanthroline) sulfato-oxovanadium(IV)-mediated in vivo male germ cell apoptosis.
22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A is a newly identified 3-alkylpiperidine alkaloid isolated from the marine sponge Amphimedon sp. and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P388 murine leukemia cells.
Tetradehydrohalicyclamine A and 22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A, new cytotoxic bis-piperidine alkaloids from a marine sponge Amphimedon sp.
7-Hydroxymethyl-12-methylbenzanthracene 3,4-diol is more tumorigenic than 7,12-Dimethylbenzanthracene in causing lung adenomas when injected into newborn mice.
Tumorigenicity of 7,12-dimethylbenz[a]anthracene, its hydroxymethylated derivatives and selected dihydrodiols in the newborn mouse.

1,4-butanediol di-2,2,2-trifluoroethanesulfonate (BFS) is a fluorine-containing busulfan analog that, when administered as five daily intraperitoneal treatments, significantly increases survival and reduces toxicity in sarcoma 180-bearing mice compared to busulfan.
Antitumor activity and neutrophil-selective hematopoietic toxicity of busulfan analogs in mice.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.

Bis-chelated gold(I) diphosphine complexes, such as [Au(d2pypp)2]Cl, selectively induce apoptosis in breast cancer cells at submicromolar concentrations by targeting mitochondria and inhibiting the thioredoxin/thioredoxin reductase system.
A gold(I) phosphine complex selectively induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells: implications for anticancer therapeutics targeted to mitochondria.
1-methyl-1 H-pyrazole-5-carboxamide derivatives inhibit the development of Haemonchus contortus larvae, with the first identified compound showing an IC50 of 0.29 µM.
Optimization of Novel 1-Methyl-1 H-Pyrazole-5-carboxamides Leads to High Potency Larval Development Inhibitors of the Barber's Pole Worm.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.
6-hydroxy-tetrahydroquinoline serves as a scaffold for a new series of estrogen receptor ligands with high binding affinity and functional activity in MCF-7 cells.
Tetrahydroquinoline-based selective estrogen receptor modulators (SERMs).

Methyl 3-[(4-methoxyphenyl)thio]-5-chloro-1H-indole-2-carboxylate is a novel indole derivative that inhibits tubulin polymerization, colchicine binding, and the growth of MCF-7 human breast carcinoma cells.
Arylthioindoles, potent inhibitors of tubulin polymerization.

Ethyl 3-ferrocenyl-1H-pyrazole-5-carboxylate derivatives, specifically ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives, inhibit the growth of A549 lung cancer cells in a dose-dependent manner via cell cycle arrest and induce apoptosis.
Synthesis, single-crystal characterization and preliminary biological evaluation of novel ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives with alkylamino side chains act as highly selective ligands for binding to telomeric G-quadruplex DNA, effectively stabilizing the G-quadruplex structure and showing significant selectivity over duplex DNA.
Disubstituted 2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives as a new type of highly selective ligands for telomeric G-quadruplex DNA.

A specific thiazole-naphthalene derivative (compound 5b) inhibits tubulin polymerisation with an IC50 of 3.3 µM and shows antiproliferative activity with IC50 values of 0.48 ± 0.03 µM in MCF-7 and 0.97 ± 0.13 µM in A549 cancer cells.
Design, synthesis and biological evaluation of novel thiazole-naphthalene derivatives as potential anticancer agents and tubulin polymerisation inhibitors.

6-chloro-N-(3,4-dimethoxybenzyl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroacridin-9-amine is a dual cholinesterase and amyloid aggregation inhibitor, with acetylcholinesterase IC50 = 2.2 µM, butyrylcholinesterase IC50 = 20 nM, and 40% inhibition of amyloid aggregation at 25 µM.
Structure-activity relationship studies of benzyl-, phenethyl-, and pyridyl-substituted tetrahydroacridin-9-amines as multitargeting agents to treat Alzheimer's disease.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been designed and synthesized as inhibitors of Aurora A kinase.
Design, synthesis, biological evaluation of 6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives as novel anticancer agents with Aurora kinase inhibition.
Ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives can inhibit the growth of A549 lung cancer cells in a dose-dependent manner by inducing cell cycle arrest.
Synthesis, single-crystal characterization and preliminary biological evaluation of novel ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives.

1-ferrocenyl-2-(5-phenyl-1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-ylthio)ethanone derivatives induce G1-phase cell cycle arrest and senescence in A549 human lung cancer cells via the ROS/p38 MAP-kinase pathway, without causing apoptosis or necrosis.
Novel ferrocenyl derivatives exert anti-cancer effect in human lung cancer cells in vitro via inducing G1-phase arrest and senescence.
EED226 is a potent inhibitor of PRC2 activity that binds to the H3K27me3 pocket of EED and induces regression of tumor xenograft in vivo.
An allosteric PRC2 inhibitor targeting the H3K27me3 binding pocket of EED.
Imidazopyridine-dipropylacetamide functions as a TSPO ligand in the first reported rhenium complex containing this ligand, which shows high affinity for TSPO in C6 rat glioma cell membrane extracts.
A model radiopharmaceutical agent targeted to translocator protein 18 kDa (TSPO).

The induction of apoptosis by 4-[3-(1-heptyl-4,4-dimethyl-2-oxo-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroquinolin-6-yl)-3-oxo-E-propenyl] benzoic acid (AGN193198) is caspase-mediated and independent of retinoic acid receptors (RARs) and retinoid X receptors (RXRs).
A retinoid-related molecule that does not bind to classical retinoid receptors potently induces apoptosis in human prostate cancer cells through rapid caspase activation.

2,5-bis(methylsulfinyl)-1,4-diaminobenzene is a nitroreductase-responsive fluorescent probe (BBP) that selectively and sensitively detects nitroreductase activity with a sensitivity of 20 ng/mL and a response time as fast as 10 minutes.
A sensitive and fast responsive fluorescent probe for imaging hypoxic tumors.

5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) enables radiosensitization in both MMR-proficient and MMR-deficient human colon cancer xenografts, with a sensitizer enhancement ratio (SER) of 1.48 at 2 Gy per fraction and 1.41 at 4 Gy per fraction in MMR-deficient tumors, and 1.21 at 2 Gy and 1.20 at 4 Gy in MMR-proficient tumors.
Differential radiosensitization in DNA mismatch repair-proficient and -deficient human colon cancer xenografts with 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose.

2,5-bis(methylsulfinyl)-1,4-diaminobenzene (BBP) can be used to monitor nitroreductase activity in 2D cell monolayers, 3D tumor spheroids, and solid tumors in mice, enabling detection of early tumor formation and treatment response.
A sensitive and fast responsive fluorescent probe for imaging hypoxic tumors.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated as nonsteroidal progesterone receptor modulators with antiprogestant activity.
Development of progesterone receptor antagonists from 1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline agonist pharmacophore.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.

6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) enhances natural host resistance against Pseudomonas aeruginosa in cyclophosphamide-treated mice in a dose-dependent manner.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.

The (99m)TcN-DGDTC complex of deoxyglucose dithiocarbamate shows high tumor uptake and good retention in S180 tumor-bearing mice, with tumor/blood and tumor/muscle ratios increasing to 2.32 and 1.68 at 4 hours post-injection, respectively.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a novel (99m)Tc nitrido radiopharmaceutical with deoxyglucose dithiocarbamate, showing tumor uptake.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.
Deoxyglucose dithiocarbamate can be synthesized and radiolabelled with [(99m)TcN](2+) to form a (99m)TcN-DGDTC complex with radiochemical purity over 90% and a hydrophilic, neutral character.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a novel (99m)Tc nitrido radiopharmaceutical with deoxyglucose dithiocarbamate, showing tumor uptake.
Hexane-bisammonium-type compounds containing lateral phthalimide moieties have high affinity for the allosteric site of muscarinic M2 receptors.
Structure-activity relationships in a series of bisquaternary bisphthalimidine derivatives modulating the muscarinic M(2)-receptor allosterically.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

2-acetylpyrazine thiosemicarbazone forms complexes with gallium(III) and iron(III) that display different effects on cytotoxicity, with gallium(III) enhancing and iron(III) attenuating antiproliferative activity.
Gallium(III) and iron(III) complexes of alpha-N-heterocyclic thiosemicarbazones: synthesis, characterization, cytotoxicity, and interaction with ribonucleotide reductase.
(+)-3-[2-(benzo[b]thiophen-2-yl)-2oxoethyl]-1-azabicyclo[2.2.2]octane is a potent agonist for the a7 receptor.
(+)-3-[2-(Benzo[b]thiophen-2-yl)-2-oxoethyl]-1-azabicyclo[2.2.2]octane as potent agonists for the alpha7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor.
3,3-dimethyl-N-(tetrahydronaphthalen-1-yl)piperidine derivatives are potent and selective σ 1 ligands with poor affinity for D2, 5-HT 1A, and PCP receptors.
Novel potent sigma 1 ligands: N-[omega-(tetralin-1-yl)alkyl]piperidine derivatives.
6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) is substantially more protective against Pseudomonas aeruginosa than its alpha anomer.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.
5′-bromo-2′-hydroxy-6-methylflavone is a high-affinity flavone derivative designed based on a refined pharmacophore model for binding to the GABA A receptor.
Refinement and evaluation of a pharmacophore model for flavone derivatives binding to the benzodiazepine site of the GABA(A) receptor.
ethyl-N-[3-(1-methyl-4-piperidinyl)-1H-pyrrolo[3,2-b]pyridin-5-yl]amide is a potent and selective agonist of the 5-HT 1F receptor.
Novel potent 5-HT(1F) receptor agonists: structure-activity studies of a series of substituted N-[3-(1-methyl-4-piperidinyl)-1H-pyrrolo[3,2-b]pyridin-5-yl]amides.
Nodulisporic acid A is a compound with a defined pharmacophore that is integral to its potent insecticidal activity, specifically flea efficacy.
Chemical modification of nodulisporic acid A: preliminary structure-activity relationships.

Aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexanedicarboxylic acid (ABHxD-I) is a constrained analogue of ACPD that acts as a potent agonist at all eight tested mGluR subtypes, with greater potency than ACPD and glutamate at most subtypes.
Synthesis and biology of the conformationally restricted ACPD analogue, 2-aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexane-2,5-dicarboxylic acid-I, a potent mGluR agonist.

(S)-N-(1-benzylpyrrolidin-3-yl)-4-bromo-1-methoxy-2-naphthamide analogues bind with higher affinity at both D2 and D3 dopamine receptors and exhibit higher selectivity for D3 receptors compared to (R)-series analogues.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of naphthamides as dopamine D3 receptor ligands.
Pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine derivatives exhibit a wide range of pharmacological activities, including analgesic, sedative, antidiabetic, antiviral, antimycobacterial, and anticancer effects.
An Overview of the Biological Activity of Pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine Derivatives.
Morpholino phosphorodiamidate anti-sense oligonucleotides (MPOs) are used for gene-specific knockdown and have potential applications in both therapeutic and functional genomics studies.
Morpholino phosphorodiamidate oligonucleotides in zebrafish: a recipe for functional genomics?
The oxo-manganese-calcium (Mn4O5Ca) centre is a key component of the natural photosystem II (PSII) that efficiently catalyzes light-driven water oxidation and water splitting at ambient temperatures.
Calcium-manganese oxides as structural and functional models for active site in oxygen evolving complex in photosystem II: lessons from simple models.

Asymmetric synthesis of (2R,3R)-heptane-2,3-diol via enantioconvergent biohydrolysis of (±)-cis-2,3-epoxyheptane using Nocardia EH1-epoxide hydrolases affords 79% yield and 91% enantiomeric excess at 100% conversion.
Epoxide hydrolase-mediated enantioconvergent bioconversions to prepare chiral epoxides and alcohols.
The oxo-manganese-calcium (Mn4O5Ca) centre is a key component of the natural photosystem II (PSII) that efficiently catalyzes light-driven water oxidation and water splitting at ambient temperatures.
Manganese Compounds as Water-Oxidizing Catalysts: From the Natural Water-Oxidizing Complex to Nanosized Manganese Oxide Structures.
(-)-2-(6-[ 18 F] fluoro-2,3′-bipyridin-5′-yl)-7-methyl-7-aza-bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane ([ 18 F]AZAN) is a radioligand antagonist of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nAChR) developed for brain imaging.
Development of radioligands with optimized imaging properties for quantification of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors by positron emission tomography.

(-)-2-(6-[ 18 F]fluoro-2,3′-bipyridin-5′yl)-7-methyl-7-aza-bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane ([ 18 F]AZAN) is a highly specific nAChR radioligand with rapid brain kinetics, high blood-to-brain ratio (BP), and high uptake (%SUV) in PET imaging.
Development of radioligands with optimized imaging properties for quantification of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors by positron emission tomography.
Arachidic acid ethyl ester, a saturated fatty acid ethyl ester, induces sustained, concentration-dependent increases in cytosolic Ca 2؉ and causes cell death in pancreatic acinar cells.
Role of Ca2+ in pancreatic cell death induced by alcohol metabolites.
Arachidic acid ethyl ester (20:0) elevates intracellular Ca 2+ in a concentration-dependent manner, contributing to ethanol-induced pancreatic injury.
Role of Ca2+ in pancreatic cell death induced by alcohol metabolites.

Amide derivatives of 8-[4-[[carboxymethyl]oxy]phenyl]-1,3-di-(n-propyl)xanthine act as selective A(2B) adenosine receptor antagonists, with para-substituted anilides showing high affinity (1–3 nM) and selectivity for human A(2B) receptors.
Structure-affinity relationships of adenosine A2B receptor ligands.
mitoFP is a chemical probe that enables visualization of S-palmitoyltransferase (APTs) activity and allows discrimination of S-depalmitoylase activities in live cells.
Activity-Based Sensing of S-Depalmitoylases: Chemical Technologies and Biological Discovery.

Trivinylborane, as a boron-activated alkene, acts as a highly reactive dienophile in Diels-Alder reactions, with computed transition structures adopting classical [4 + 2] character due to steric and electronic effects.
Boron and Silicon-Substituted 1,3-Dienes and Dienophiles and Their Use in Diels-Alder Reactions.

The (R)-2-aminopentane derivative of lysergamide has comparable affinity for the ketanserin-labeled 5-HT2A receptor to LSD, but its in vivo activity in the drug discrimination assay drops off compared to LSD.
Stereoselective pharmacological effects of lysergic acid amides possessing chirality in the amide substituent.
A 3-methoxypropyl-substituted xanthine derivative has been developed as a potent and selective antagonist of the A2B adenosine receptor.
Structure-affinity relationships of adenosine A2B receptor ligands.
The thermolysis of disodium 1,4-naphthalenedipropanoate produces singlet oxygen (1 O2) that reacts with dGuo to yield spiroiminodihydantoin nucleoside diastereoisomers and 8-oxodGuo.
Singlet oxygen oxidation of isolated and cellular DNA: product formation and mechanistic insights.

Asymmetric synthesis of (2R,3R)-heptane-2,3-diol via enantioconvergent biohydrolysis of (±)-cis-2,3-epoxyheptane using Nocardia EH1-epoxide hydrolases affords 79% yield and 91% enantiomeric excess at 100% conversion.
Molecular engineering of epoxide hydrolase and its application to asymmetric and enantioconvergent hydrolysis.
Coproheme III is decarboxylated by HemQ, an ironcoproporphyrin (coproheme) decarboxylase, into protoheme IX during heme biosynthesis.
HemQ: An iron-coproporphyrin oxidative decarboxylase for protoheme synthesis in Firmicutes and Actinobacteria.

Tetrahydrothiopyrano[2,3-b]thiopyran serves as a cis-fused bicyclic template that enables highly diastereoselective formation of six out of eleven stereocenters in complex molecule synthesis, including erythromycin A analogs.
The thiopyran route to polypropionates.
Methyl-aminolevulinate is a photosensitizer approved by European regulatory authorities for the photodynamic treatment of actinic keratosis and basal cell carcinoma.
Photodynamic therapy: update 2006. Part 2: Clinical results.
6-iodoquinazolin-4(3H)-one serves as a substrate in Suzuki-Miyaura cross-coupling reactions with various boronic acids to synthesize quinazolinone derivatives.
Advances in metal-catalyzed cross-coupling reactions of halogenated quinazolinones and their quinazoline derivatives.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

Disodium 1-[2-(carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a low molecular weight nitric oxide donor that, when incorporated into electrospun polymer fibers, enables nitric oxide release upon immersion in aqueous solutions at physiological pH (7.4, 37°C).
Nitric oxide-releasing electrospun polymer microfibers.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
Manadosterol A is a dimeric sterol isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa that exhibits inhibitory activity against the formation of the Ubc13-Uev1A complex.
Manadosterols A and B, sulfonated sterol dimers inhibiting the Ubc13-Uev1A interaction, isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa.
(R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine functions as a pharmacophore moiety that confers moderate inhibitory activity for DPP-4 and is associated with good pharmacokinetic properties.
Discovery of new chemotype dipeptidyl peptidase IV inhibitors having (R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine as a pharmacophore.

Bis-chelated gold(I) diphosphine complexes, such as [Au(d2pypp)2]Cl, selectively induce apoptosis in breast cancer cells at submicromolar concentrations by targeting mitochondria and inhibiting the thioredoxin/thioredoxin reductase system.
A gold(I) phosphine complex selectively induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells: implications for anticancer therapeutics targeted to mitochondria.
Adenosine 3'-phosphonate acts as an inhibitor of the MurMN/FemABX family of tRNA-dependent ligases, which are implicated in high-level penicillin resistance in gram-positive bacteria.
Adenosine phosphonate inhibitors of lipid II: alanyl tRNA ligase MurM from Streptococcus pneumoniae.

In the crystal, 5-(5,7-dihydroxy-4-oxo-4H-chromenyl)-2-hydroxybenzenesulfonic acid dihydrate molecules are linked by classical O–H···O and C–H···O intra- and intermolecular hydrogen bonds and aromatic pi–pi stacking interactions, with a centroid-centroid distance of 3.4523 Å between the 1,4-pyranone rings and benzene rings and 3.6337 Å between the benzene rings.
Genistein-3'-sulfonic acid dihydrate.
A 3-methoxypropyl-substituted xanthine derivative has been developed as a potent and selective antagonist of the A2B adenosine receptor.
Synthesis of novel 1-alkyl-8-substituted-3-(3-methoxypropyl) xanthines as putative A(2B) receptor antagonists.

When covalently linked to bacteriophage 75 and delivered to Staphylococcus aureus, tin(IV) chlorin e6 (SnCe6) enables selective killing of methicillin- and vancomycin-intermediate strains with low concentrations (1.5 µg/ml) and low light doses (21 J/cm2), while sparing human epithelial cells.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.

Ferric hydrogensulfate acts as a catalyst in the multi-component, one-pot condensation reaction for synthesizing amidoalkyl naphthols from beta-naphthol, aromatic aldehydes, and acetamide under solvent, solvent-free, and microwave conditions.
An efficient, simple and expedition synthesis of 1-amidoalkyl-2-naphthols as 'drug like' molecules for biological screening.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.

Arformoterol tartrate is chemically and physically compatible with commercially available nebulized formulations of ipratropium bromide, acetylcysteine, and budesonide, showing no visible changes or unacceptable pH shifts (pH 4.82–6.40 at T0, pH 4.82–6.40 at T30, assay 98.3%–101.4% at both T0 and T30) when mixed in the tested concentrations.
Compatibility of arformoterol tartrate inhalation solution with three nebulized drugs.
ll-Azi-DAP, the (2S,4S)-diastereomer of 2-(4-amino-4-carboxybutyl)aziridine-2-carboxylic acid, selectively binds to Cys-73 of the diaminopimelic acid epimerase active site.
The stereoselective synthesis of aziridine analogues of diaminopimelic acid (DAP) and their interaction with dap epimerase.

His-Gly-Ile, a peptide from soybean 11S glycinin hydrolysate, produces a less reduction in intracellular Ca(2+) concentration compared to other vasoactive peptides in angiotensin II-stimulated vascular smooth muscle cells.
Identification of peptides from soybean protein, glycinin, possessing suppression of intracellular Ca2+ concentration in vascular smooth muscle cells.
Ala-Ser-Leu is a novel angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide identified from silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein hydrolysate, with an IC50 value of 102.15 µM.

A novel angiotensin-І converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide from gastrointestinal protease hydrolysate of silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein: Biochemical characterization and molecular docking study.

2′-deoxyguanosine-5′-tetraphosphate can be efficiently incorporated into DNA during polymerase reactions when modified with four different length coumarin-PEG tags at the 5′-terminal phosphate, allowing for single-molecule discrimination of the tags by nanopore ionic current blockade.
PEG-labeled nucleotides and nanopore detection for single molecule DNA sequencing by synthesis.

(1S)-1-(trans-4-{[(4-trifluoromethoxyphenyl)sulfonyl]amino}cyclohexyl)-2-[(3S)-3-fluoropyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-oxoethanaminium chloride (MRL-A) is a DPP-IV inhibitor analog containing a fluoropyrrolidine moiety that undergoes metabolic activation in rat liver microsomes, primarily catalyzed by CYP3A1 and CYP3A2.
Metabolic activation of fluoropyrrolidine dipeptidyl peptidase-IV inhibitors by rat liver microsomes.

alpha-(2-pyridyl)glycine (2Pyg) can be incorporated into tripeptides via solid-phase Ugi reaction, and its presence enables the formation of unique peptide conformations with intramolecular hydrogen bonding between 2Pyg-NH and a pyridine nitrogen.

Studies on intramolecular hydrogen bonding between the pyridine nitrogen and the amide hydrogen of the peptide: synthesis and conformational analysis of tripeptides containing novel amino acids with a pyridine ring.
Both (2S,3R)- and (2R,3S)-hydroxycitric acid enantiomers produced by Bacillus megaterium G45C and Streptomyces sp. U121 are optically pure.
Absolute configuration of hydroxycitric acid produced by microorganisms.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
Sodium 2-(1-imidazolylmethyl)-4,5-dihydrobenzo[b]thiophene-6-carboxylate is a potent and specific inhibitor of thromboxane A synthetase in vitro and in vivo.
Synthesis and thromboxane synthetase inhibitory activity of di- or tetrahydrobenzo[b]thiophenecarboxylic acid derivatives.

Aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexanedicarboxylic acid (ABHxD-I) is a constrained analogue of ACPD that acts as a potent agonist at all eight tested mGluR subtypes, with greater potency than ACPD and glutamate at most subtypes.
Synthesis and biology of the conformationally restricted ACPD analogue, 2-aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexane-2,5-dicarboxylic acid-I, a potent mGluR agonist.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) inhibits apoptosis and reduces infarct size in myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury by activating the PI3K/Akt pathway and reducing inflammation.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) reduces rat myocardial apoptosis against ischemia and reperfusion injury by activation of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/Akt signaling and anti-inflammatory action in vivo.
Sodium 2-(1-imidazolylmethyl)-4,5-dihydrobenzo[b]thiophene-6-carboxylate is a potent and specific inhibitor of thromboxane A2 synthetase in vitro.
Synthesis and thromboxane synthetase inhibitory activity of di- or tetrahydrobenzo[b]thiophenecarboxylic acid derivatives.
Aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexane dicarboxylic acid (ABHxD-I) is a conformationally constrained analogue of glutamate that acts as a potent agonist at group I, II, and III mGluR subtypes.
Synthesis and biology of the conformationally restricted ACPD analogue, 2-aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexane-2,5-dicarboxylic acid-I, a potent mGluR agonist.

The R-(-) isomer of methyl 1-(2,2-dimethylindan-1-yl)imidazole-5-carboxylate (CGA 214372) is a strong uncompetitive inhibitor of P450-dependent obtusifoliol 14 alpha-demethylase (P450OBT.14DM) with an I50 of 8 x 10^-9 M and I50/Km of 5 x 10^-5 in maize microsomal assays.
Plant sterol biosynthesis: novel potent and selective inhibitors of cytochrome P450-dependent obtusifoliol 14 alpha-methyl demethylase.
10 beta-hydroperoxy-1,4-estradien-3,17-dione is the only product formed after photosensitization of oestrone with hematoporphyrin and is responsible for the strong interaction with DNA.
Interaction of the photosensitization products of oestrone with DNA: comparison with the horseradish peroxidase catalyzed reaction.

6-Chloro-(4S)-cyclopropyl-3,4-dihydro-4-((2-pyridyl)-ethynyl)quinazol in- 2(1H)-one (L-738,372) is a reversible, nonnucleoside inhibitor of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase that acts as a mixed noncompetitive inhibitor against poly(rC).oligo(dG) as primer-template.

Inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by a quinazolinone and comparison with inhibition by pyridinones. Differences in the rates of inhibitor binding and in synergistic inhibition with nucleoside analogs.
Aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexanedicarboxylic acid (ABHxD-I) expresses a fully extended glutamate conformation and shows little subtype selectivity at mGluRs despite its fixed conformation.
Synthesis and biology of the conformationally restricted ACPD analogue, 2-aminobicyclo[2.1.1]hexane-2,5-dicarboxylic acid-I, a potent mGluR agonist.

Irisolidone, an aglycone of isoflavones from Pueraria thunbergiana, is the most potent inhibitor among tested aglycones and inhibits Helicobacter pylori growth with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 12.5–25 µg/ml.
In vitro anti-Helicobacter pylori activity of irisolidone isolated from the flowers and rhizomes of Pueraria thunbergiana.

trans-3,4-dihydro-3,4-dihydroxy-7-methylbenz[a]anthracene, a dihydrodiol derived from polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, induces up to a 6-fold increase in the frequency of sister-chromatid exchanges in Chinese hamster ovary cells.

The relationship between the levels of DNA-hydrocarbon adducts and the formation of sister-chromatid exchanges in Chinese hamster ovary cells treated with derivatives of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.

N-(piperidin-1-yl)-5-(4-chlorophenyl)-4-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carboxyamide (SR 141716A) is a cannabinoid receptor antagonist that fails to block the development of rapid tolerance to both ethanol and Delta(9)-tetrahydrocannabinol in mice when administered at 2 mg/kg intraperitoneally.
Rapid tolerance to Delta(9)-tetrahydrocannabinol and cross-tolerance between ethanol and Delta(9)-tetrahydrocannabinol in mice.

3-Phenyl-5-(2-piperidinoethyl)-isoxazole citrate (31245) elevates blood glucose levels in intact and hypophysectomized rats but not in adrenalectomized or adrenal-demedullated rats after subcutaneous injection.
Drug-induced adrenaline release and blood glucose in rats: 3-phenyl-5(2-piperidinoethyl)-isoxazole citrate.

5-amino-6,7,8-trifluoro-1-[(1R,2S)-2-fluorocyclopropan-1-yl]-4-oxo-1,4-dihydro-quinoline-3-carboxylic acid is a 6-fluoro-1-[(1R,2S)-2-fluorocyclopropan-1-yl]-4-oxoquinoline-3-carboxylic acid derivative with a cyclopropane-fused 2-amino-8-azabicyclo[4.3.0]nonan-8-yl substituent at the C-7 position, designed for potent antibacterial activity and inhibition of bacterial DNA topoisomerase IV.

Synthesis and antibacterial activity of novel 6-fluoro-1-[(1R,2S)-2-fluorocyclopropan-1-yl]-4-oxoquinoline-3-carboxylic acids bearing cyclopropane-fused 2-amino-8-azabicyclo[4.3.0]nonan-8-yl substituents at the C-7 position.

6-Chloro-(4S)-cyclopropyl-3,4-dihydro-4-((2-pyridyl)-ethynyl)quinazol in- 2(1H)-one (L-738,372) binds to HIV-1 reverse transcriptase at a site overlapping with other nonnucleoside inhibitor binding sites.

Inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by a quinazolinone and comparison with inhibition by pyridinones. Differences in the rates of inhibitor binding and in synergistic inhibition with nucleoside analogs.
1,4-phenanthrenequinone derivatives can exhibit virucidal activity against equine infectious anemia virus, a retrovirus, without requiring light exposure.
Phenanthrenequinone antiretroviral agents.
3,4-diphenylcoumarin derivatives were synthesized and studied for their receptor binding affinities to human estrogen receptors ERa and ERb.
Synthesis and binding affinity to human alpha and beta estrogen receptors of various 7-hydroxycoumarins substituted at 4- and 3,4- positions.

Perhexiline exerts partially endothelium-dependent coronary vasodilator effects in the rat, predominantly on small coronary arteries, and these effects are independent of nitric oxide, prostacyclin, and the endothelium-derived hyperpolarising factor (EDHF) released by bradykinin.
The effects of perhexiline on the rat coronary vasculature.

6-Chloro-(4S)-cyclopropyl-3,4-dihydro-4-((2-pyridyl)-ethynyl)quinazol in- 2(1H)-one (L-738,372) inhibits the azidothymidine-resistant form of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase (D67N, K70R, T215Y, K219Q) with 2-3-fold greater potency than the wild-type enzyme.

Inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by a quinazolinone and comparison with inhibition by pyridinones. Differences in the rates of inhibitor binding and in synergistic inhibition with nucleoside analogs.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.
(R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine functions as a pharmacophore moiety that confers moderate inhibitory activity for DPP-4 and is associated with good pharmacokinetic properties.
Discovery of new chemotype dipeptidyl peptidase IV inhibitors having (R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine as a pharmacophore.

3-aminomethyl cyclohexane carboxylic acid, when incorporated as the P2 amino acid in peptidomimetic BACE-1 inhibitors, enables a novel binding mode with the S3 pocket of BACE-1, forming a hydrogen bond interaction pattern distinct from previous designs.
Structure-based design and synthesis of novel P2/P3 modified, non-peptidic beta-secretase (BACE-1) inhibitors.
N-Mannich bases derived from 5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamide sulfonamides are more potent antibacterials than the parent compound.
Synthesis and in vitro study of novel Mannich bases as antibacterial agents.
1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated as nonsteroidal progesterone receptor modulators with antiprogestant activity.
Development of progesterone receptor antagonists from 1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline agonist pharmacophore.
6-hydroxy-tetrahydroquinoline serves as a scaffold for a new series of estrogen receptor ligands with high binding affinity and functional activity in MCF-7 cells.
Tetrahydroquinoline-based selective estrogen receptor modulators (SERMs).
A pyridazin-4-one fragment acts as a cathepsin S inhibitor with an IC50 of 170 µM, serving as a template for designing more potent CatS inhibitors.
Diazinones as P2 replacements for pyrazole-based cathepsin S inhibitors.
Bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene derivatives can be synthesized by reacting cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes in the presence of a rhenium catalyst, [ReBr(CO)(3)(thf)](2).
Rhenium-catalyzed formation of bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene frameworks by a reaction of cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes.
18F-labeled RGD peptide generated by tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation demonstrates prominent tumor uptake in vivo and confirmed receptor specificity.
Tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation for the rapid construction of integrin αvβ₃ targeted PET tracer based on a cyclic RGD peptide.

7-amino-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline is used as a fragment in the synthesis of novel fibrinogen receptor antagonists that inhibit platelet aggregation and block FITC-Fg binding to alpha(IIbγ)3 on human platelets.
Derivatives of 7-amino-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline and isophthalic acids as novel fibrinogen receptor antagonists.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives exhibit anti-MERS-CoV activity in Vero cells.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.

(R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine is a chemical moiety that confers potent DPP-4 inhibitory activity and favorable bioavailability and pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) profiles when incorporated into compounds.
Discovery of new chemotype dipeptidyl peptidase IV inhibitors having (R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine as a pharmacophore.

2-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)tryptamine derivatives can act as antagonists at the gonadotropin releasing hormone receptor, with potency influenced by para-substituents on the 4-phenylbutyl side chain attached to the tryptamine nitrogen.
2-(3,5-Dimethylphenyl)tryptamine derivatives that bind to the GnRH receptor.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives have been shown to possess high anti-MERS-CoV activity.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.

2-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)tryptamine derivatives act as potent antagonists of the gonadotropin releasing hormone (GnRH) receptor, with the most potent compound exhibiting an IC50 of 16 nM in a rat GnRH receptor assay.
Heterocyclic derivatives of 2-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)tryptamine as GnRH receptor antagonists.

The C-terminally fluorinated peptide desGly10-LH-RH-2,2,2-trifluoroethylamide is about nine times more effective than LH-RH in releasing luteinizing hormone (LH) in immature male rats over a prolonged period.
Polyfluoroalkylamine derivatives of luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone.
Oximino-piperidino-piperidine derivatives act as CCR5 antagonists and exhibit antiviral activity against HIV-1, with small alkyl substitutions at the oxime moiety favoring CCR5 antagonism.
Spirodiketopiperazine-based CCR5 antagonists: Improvement of their pharmacokinetic profiles.
Actinopolymorphol A is a natural product that preferentially induces ERα/β heterodimerization over homodimerization in live cells.
Discovery and total synthesis of a new estrogen receptor heterodimerizing actinopolymorphol A from Actinopolymorpha rutilus.
(+)-3-[2-(benzo[b]thiophen-2-yl)-2oxoethyl]-1-azabicyclo[2.2.2]octane is a potent agonist for the a7 receptor.
(+)-3-[2-(Benzo[b]thiophen-2-yl)-2-oxoethyl]-1-azabicyclo[2.2.2]octane as potent agonists for the alpha7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor.
2-ethoxy-N-((1-(4-isopropylpiperazin-1-yl)cyclohexyl)methyl)benzamide is a potent and selective blocker of the HCN1 cyclic nucleotide-modulated channel.
Discovery of a novel series of selective HCN1 blockers.

ChloraPrep, a solution containing 2% chlorhexidine gluconate and 70% isopropyl alcohol, is the most effective surgical skin-preparation solution for eliminating bacteria from the hallux and toes in the foot and ankle prior to surgery.
Efficacy of surgical preparation solutions in foot and ankle surgery.

1-(4-chloro-benzyl)-4-[(4-fluoro-phenyl)-hydrazono]-5-thioxo-imidazolidin-2-one (LPSF/PT-11) is an imidazolidine derivative that demonstrates schistosomicidal activity against adult Schistosoma mansoni worms in vitro, causing significant ultrastructural alterations and cell death.
Antischistosomal action of thioxo-imidazolidine compounds: an ultrastructural and cytotoxicity study.
5-bromodeoxycytidine 5'-monophosphate is produced from 5-bromodeoxycytidine in extracts of herpes simplex virus-infected cells but not in uninfected cells.
Phosphorylation of 5-bromodeoxycytidine in cells infected with herpes simplex virus.

6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) enhances natural host resistance against Pseudomonas aeruginosa in cyclophosphamide-treated mice in a dose-dependent manner.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.
D0870 is a bis-triazole derivative that prevents death and induces parasitological cure in 70 to 90% of animals in acute and chronic murine models of Chagas' disease.
In vivo activity of the bis-triazole D0870 against drug-susceptible and drug-resistant strains of the protozoan parasite Trypanosoma cruzi.
5-bromodeoxycytidine-5'-phosphate (BrdCMP) is formed from 5-bromodeoxycytidine in extracts of herpes simplex virus-infected cells but not in uninfected cells.
Phosphorylation of 5-bromodeoxycytidine in cells infected with herpes simplex virus.

Heronapyrrole A is a structurally unique 2-nitropyrroloterpene produced by Streptomyces sp. (CMB-M0423) and shows significant activity against Gram-positive bacteria without adverse effects on mammalian cell lines.
Heronapyrroles A-C: farnesylated 2-nitropyrroles from an Australian marine-derived Streptomyces sp.

Proteomic analysis suggests that 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) targets proteins such as a putative vacuolar ATP synthase subunit C and a class I S-adenosylmethionine-dependent methyltransferase in Toxoplasma gondii.
Evaluation of anti-coccidial effects of 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one and identification of its potential target proteins in Toxoplasma gondii.

Oral administration of 3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) at doses ≥10 mg/kg in MCMV-infected mice produces antiviral effects comparable to ganciclovir, as measured by survival and viral DNA levels in organs.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.
The pharmacokinetics of atazanavir can be significantly affected by coadministration with acid-suppressing agents such as H 2-receptor antagonists or tenofovir disoproxil fumarate.
Effects of the H2-receptor antagonist famotidine on the pharmacokinetics of atazanavir-ritonavir with or without tenofovir in HIV-infected patients.
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives exhibit low toxicity and are effective against T. cruzi amastigote forms.
In vitro and in vivo trypanocidal activities of 8-methoxy-3-(4-nitrobenzoyl)-6-propyl-2H-cromen-2-one, a new synthetic coumarin of low cytotoxicity against mammalian cells.
1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) is a synthetic compound with anti-coccidial activity, showing lower EC50 values for anti-Toxoplasma activity in vitro compared to pyrimethamine.
Evaluation of anti-coccidial effects of 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one and identification of its potential target proteins in Toxoplasma gondii.
6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) is substantially more protective against Pseudomonas aeruginosa than its alpha anomer.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.

Bis-(3-oxoundecyl) tetrasulfide is identified as the major compound in the A7 fraction of Bryopsis pennata chloroform extract, which exhibits strong larvicidal activity against Aedes aegypti (LC50 = 4.7 µg/mL) and Aedes albopictus (LC50 = 5.3 µg/mL).
Mosquitocidal and Oviposition Repellent Activities of the Extracts of Seaweed Bryopsis pennata on Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus.
5-bromodeoxycytidine monophosphate can be deaminated by HSV-infected cells, and its phosphorylated derivatives may serve as substrates for deoxyribonucleotide kinases and DNA polymerases.
Phosphorylation of 5-bromodeoxycytidine in cells infected with herpes simplex virus.
Oral administration of 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) at 100 mg/kg for 4 days reduces the number of tachyzoites in Eimeria tenella-infected chickens.
Evaluation of anti-coccidial effects of 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one and identification of its potential target proteins in Toxoplasma gondii.
5-bromodeoxycytidine 5'-monophosphate can be deaminated to 5-bromodeoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate and incorporated into DNA in both infected and uninfected cells.
Phosphorylation of 5-bromodeoxycytidine in cells infected with herpes simplex virus.
Cefovecin sodium is non-inferior to amoxicillin/clavulanic acid for the treatment of superficial pyoderma, deep pyoderma, and wounds/abscesses in dogs when administered every 14 days.
Clinical efficacy and safety of cefovecin in the treatment of canine pyoderma and wound infections.

Carbocyclic 3-deazaadenosine is an adenosine analogue that inhibits Ebola virus replication in vitro, likely by blocking S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine hydrolase and thereby limiting methylation of the 5' cap of viral messenger RNA.
Treatment of lethal Ebola virus infection in mice with a single dose of an S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine hydrolase inhibitor.
D0870 is a bis-triazole derivative that inhibits the parasite's sterol C14␣ demethylase and induces parasitological cure in murine models of both acute and chronic Chagas' disease.
In vivo activity of the bis-triazole D0870 against drug-susceptible and drug-resistant strains of the protozoan parasite Trypanosoma cruzi.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene derivatives can be synthesized by reacting cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes in the presence of a rhenium catalyst, [ReBr(CO)(3)(thf)](2).
Rhenium-catalyzed formation of bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene frameworks by a reaction of cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes.

Acid-promoted de-tert-butylation of N-nitroso-N-tert-butylamine generates a diazonium cation in situ, which can be used for cyclo-etherification to produce substituted benzofuran-3(2H)-ones and P-stereogenic chiral dihydrobenzo[d][1,3]oxaphosphole 3-oxides.
Metal-Free Cycloetherification by in Situ Generated P-Stereogenic α-Diazanium Intermediates: A Convergent Synthesis of Enantiomerically Pure Dihydrobenzooxaphospholes.
5-nitrothiazole derivatives bearing a diethylmethylene malonate group can undergo reduction of the nitro group followed by cyclization to yield bicyclic 2-pyridones.
Synthesis of new potentially bioactive bicyclic 2-pyridones.

Aristolactam BIII is a natural aristolactam that can be synthesized efficiently using a one-pot Suzuki-Miyaura coupling/aldol condensation cascade reaction of isoindolin-1-one with 2-formylphenylboronic acid.
Total synthesis of aristolactams via a one-pot suzuki-miyaura coupling/aldol condensation cascade reaction.
Militarinone A is a novel pyridone alkaloid isolated from Paecilomyces militaris and exhibits neuritogenic activity on PC-12 cells.
Militarinone A, a neurotrophic pyridone alkaloid from Paecilomyces militaris.

The computationally derived conformer leading to the asynchronous transition structure in squalene cyclization closely resembles the analogous region of 2-azasqualene encapsulated by squalene-hopene cyclase.
Concerted nature of AB ring formation in the enzymatic cyclization of squalene to hopenes.

Epoxide ring opening, functional group interconversion, and deprotection of (2RS,3SR)-N(1)-benzyl-2-chloromethyl-3-benzyloxy-4,5-epoxypiperidine enable the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
An oxidation and ring contraction approach to the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
(E)-1-decenyl(phenyl)-λ3-iodane undergoes vinylic S–N2 reactions with thioureas to yield (Z)-S-vinylisothiouronium salts with inversion of configuration.
Stereoselective synthesis of (Z)-enethiols and their derivatives: vinylic S(N)2 reaction of (E)-alkenyl(phenyl)-lambda3-iodanes with thioamides.

Bis-(3-oxoundecyl) tetrasulfide is identified as the major compound in the A7 fraction of Bryopsis pennata chloroform extract, which exhibits strong larvicidal activity against Aedes aegypti (LC50 = 4.7 µg/mL) and Aedes albopictus (LC50 = 5.3 µg/mL).
Mosquitocidal and Oviposition Repellent Activities of the Extracts of Seaweed Bryopsis pennata on Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus.
8-(1-decynyl)benzolactam-V8 is a PKC modulator that exhibits improved potency and selectivity for classical PKC isozymes.
Synthesis and modeling study of (2S,5R,6R)- and (2S,5R,6S)-6-hydroxy- 8-(1-decynyl)-benzolactam-V8 as protein kinase C modulators.

The rate of transesterification of 2-hydroxypropyl-p-nitrophenyl phosphate is increased by 50- to 5000-fold in acetonitrile, 1,4-dioxane, methanol, ethanol, N,N-dimethylformamide, or dimethyl sulfoxide compared to water.
Acceleration of acid-catalyzed transesterification of 2-hydroxypropyl-p-nitrophenyl phosphate by organic solvents.
L-proline-malic acid (L-Pro-Maa) is a natural deep eutectic solvent that enhances the extraction and oral bioavailability of puerarin from Radix Pueraria.
Screening, Optimization, and Bioavailability Research of Natural Deep Eutectic Solvent Extracts from Radix Pueraria.

3-(hydroxymethyl)benzophenone undergoes a highly efficient (Φ ∼ 0.6) intramolecular redox reaction in acidic (pH < 3) aqueous solution to form 3-formylbenzhydrol (95%) with trace amounts of the fully oxidized 3-formylbenzophenone (5%).
Formal intramolecular photoredox chemistry of meta-substituted benzophenones.

The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be constructed via intramolecular Diels-Alder (DA) reactions of halogenated 2-pyrones, including methods using base- or acid-induced equilibration of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.

4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol (SMBD) inhibits LPS-induced production of nitric oxide and prostaglandin E2 in BV2 microglia by suppressing gene expression of inducible NO synthase (iNOS) and cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), respectively, at non-toxic concentrations.
Anti-inflammatory potential of newly synthesized 4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated BV2 microglia.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) reduces erythema intensity, thickness, and desquamation, as measured by PASI, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model when administered topically (1% w/w) or orally (125 mg/kg) for 14 days.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.
3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin functions as a fluorescent probe capable of detecting small-molecule biothiols cysteine, homocysteine, and glutathione in dual fluorescence channels.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
KF-Al2O3 acts as a catalyst in the Claisen-Schmidt condensation of acetophenone with aromatic aldehydes, yielding chalcones with 83–98% yield in alcoholic solvent under ultrasound irradiation.
Improved synthesis of chalcones under ultrasound irradiation.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
Reversible photoligation of DNA via 5-vinyldeoxyuridine.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
3,4-diamino-3-cyclobutene-1,2-dione functions as an amino acid isostere when incorporated into VLA-4 integrin antagonists to improve the rate of clearance.
Squaric acid derivatives as VLA-4 integrin antagonists.

2-phenylpyrido[1,2-e]purin-4(3H)-one derivatives with piperazinyl sulfonamide substituents have been developed as potential PDE5 inhibitors for the treatment of erectile dysfunction, exhibiting improved selectivity over PDE1 and PDE6.
Synthesis and phosphodiesterase 5 inhibitory activity of novel pyrido[1,2-e]purin-4(3H)-one derivatives.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.
5-nitrothiazole derivatives bearing a diethylmethylene malonate group can undergo reduction of the nitro group followed by cyclization to yield bicyclic 2-pyridones.
Synthesis of new potentially bioactive bicyclic 2-pyridones.
4-amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) derivatives represent a novel class of anti-cancer agents with activity influenced by the nature of substituents at the R1, R2, and R3 positions.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.
1′,3′-dihydrospiro[azetidine-3,2′-indene] is a substituted azetidine that is a suitable P2 moiety for constructing potent HCV NS3 protease inhibitors.
Azetidines and spiro azetidines as novel P2 units in hepatitis C virus NS3 protease inhibitors.
Substitution of isoxazoline C-5 position with piperazyl-carbamate improves anti-tubercular activity in 3,5-disubstituted isoxazoline esters.
Synthesis, optimization and structure-activity relationships of 3,5-disubstituted isoxazolines as new anti-tuberculosis agents.
Substitution of isoxazoline C-5 position with piperazyl-carbamate improves anti-tubercular activity in 3,5-disubstituted isoxazoline esters.
Synthesis, optimization and structure-activity relationships of 3,5-disubstituted isoxazolines as new anti-tuberculosis agents.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).
5-Substituted 2-aminothiophenes as A1 adenosine receptor allosteric enhancers.

4-(2-(benzhydryloxy)ethyl)-1-((R)-2-hydroxy-2-phenylethyl)-piperidin-3-ol derivatives exhibit high affinity for the dopamine transporter (DAT) and moderate to high affinity for the norepinephrine transporter (NET) in vitro.
Synthesis and biological characterization of (3R,4R)-4-(2-(benzhydryloxy)ethyl)-1-((R)-2-hydroxy-2-phenylethyl)-piperidin-3-ol and its stereoisomers for activity toward monoamine transporters.

SN56 (3-(2-(azepan-1-yl)ethyl)-6propylbenzo[d]thiazol-2(3H)-one) is a novel 2(3H)-benzothiazolone compound with sub-nanomolar affinity and over 1,000-fold selectivity for the σ 1 subtype of the sigma receptor relative to σ 2.
