Synthesis and characterization of [³H]-SN56, a novel radioligand for the σ₁ receptor.

In the crystal structures of isopropyl 2-(4-chlorophenoxy)-2-methylpropanoate (fenofibrate), the dihedral angle between the hydrogen atom on the secondary C atom of the isopropyl group and the C atom of the carboxyl group is 2.74° in polymorph I, 45.94° in polymorph II, and -30.9° in the isopropyl clofibrate crystal structure.
Biomolecular chemistry of isopropyl fibrates.

4-phenylethynylthiophene derivatives of 2-amino-3-(p-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene exhibit similar allosteric modulatory activity at the A1 adenosine receptor regardless of whether the 4-position is substituted with a phenyl, methyl, or hydrogen group.
5-Substituted 2-aminothiophenes as A1 adenosine receptor allosteric enhancers.
The symmetrical 1,3-disubstituted ureas derived from 1-(isocyanatomethyl)-3,5-dimethyladamantane are promising human soluble epoxide hydrolase inhibitors.
Adamantyl thioureas as soluble epoxide hydrolase inhibitors.
2,6-difluorostyryl is a subunit motif in fluorinated N,N-dialkylaminostilbenes (FIDAS agents) that confers high activity against the catalytic subunit of methionine S-adenosyl transferase-2 (MAT2A).
2',6'-Dihalostyrylanilines, pyridines, and pyrimidines for the inhibition of the catalytic subunit of methionine S-adenosyltransferase-2.
The 7,8-dihydroxy-5-phenyl-substituted octahydrobenzo[h]isoquinoline derivative acts as an extremely potent, high-affinity, full D1 dopamine receptor-selective agonist.
Facile synthesis of octahydrobenzo[h]isoquinolines: novel and highly potent D1 dopamine agonists.

N2-(1-benzylpiperidin-4-yl)-N4-(3,4-dimethoxybenzyl)pyrimidine-2,4-diamine (compound 7h) binds to BuChE with a molecular volume of 299.4 Å³, is 4 Å from the PAS residues, and inhibits AChE-induced Ab-aggregation by 59% at 100 lM, showing superior activity compared to donepezil.
Design, synthesis and structure-activity relationship (SAR) studies of 2,4-disubstituted pyrimidine derivatives: dual activity as cholinesterase and Aβ-aggregation inhibitors.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).
3- and 6-Substituted 2-amino-4,5,6,7-tetrahydrothieno[2,3-c]pyridines as A1 adenosine receptor allosteric modulators and antagonists.
Piperazyl-carbamate substitution at the C-5 position of the isoxazoline ring yields compounds with enhanced anti-tuberculosis activity.
Synthesis, optimization and structure-activity relationships of 3,5-disubstituted isoxazolines as new anti-tuberculosis agents.
1,2,3,4-tetrahydrobenzo[b][1,6]naphthyridine serves as a scaffold for novel phosphodiesterase 5 inhibitors (PDE5Is) with potential therapeutic application in Alzheimer's disease.
Identification of a Novel 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrobenzo[b][1,6]naphthyridine Analogue as a Potent Phosphodiesterase 5 Inhibitor with Improved Aqueous Solubility for the Treatment of Alzheimer's Disease.

2-iodophenyl-[1-(1-methylpiperidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indol-3-yl]methanone (AM2233) is an indole derivative with a 2-iodophenyl group at the 1-position and a 3-position substituted with a N-methylpiperidin-2-ylmethyl group, and it is the most potent compound among a series of indole derivatives synthesized for binding studies at the CB1 receptor.
Potent cannabinergic indole analogues as radioiodinatable brain imaging agents for the CB1 cannabinoid receptor.
2',4'-difluorobiphenyl-4-yloxy acetic acid (dfbp-o), an analog of levosimendan, binds to cNTnC in complex with the cTnI 147-163 switch peptide, as shown by the NMR structure (PDB: 2L1R).
A structural and functional perspective into the mechanism of Ca2+-sensitizers that target the cardiac troponin complex.
Hydrogen-bonding groups at the 7-position of 1,2,3,4-tetrahydrobenz[h]isoquinoline inhibitors are predicted to increase potency against phenethanolamine N-methyltransferase.
Exploring the active site of phenylethanolamine N-methyltransferase with 1,2,3,4-tetrahydrobenz[h]isoquinoline inhibitors.
6-Cyclohexamidomanzamine A shows improved activity against M. intracellulare with an IC50 of 1.485 μM, similar to the antimalarial activity profile for position 6 amides.
Structure-activity relationship studies of manzamine A: amidation of positions 6 and 8 of the beta-carboline moiety.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Identification of 3-sulfonylindazole derivatives as potent and selective 5-HT(6) antagonists.
Ala-Ser-Leu is a novel angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide identified from silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein hydrolysate, with an IC50 value of 102.15 µM.

A novel angiotensin-І converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide from gastrointestinal protease hydrolysate of silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein: Biochemical characterization and molecular docking study.
3-(phenylsulfonyl)thieno[2,3-e][1,2,3]triazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine derivatives serve as promising ligands for the 5-HT 6 receptor, particularly when substituted with amino groups at position 5.
Synthesis and biological study of 3-(phenylsulfonyl)thieno[2,3-e][1,2,3]triazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidines as potent and selective serotonin 5-HT6 receptor antagonists.

3-(benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole ([18F]-IND1) is a positron emission tomography radiotracer structurally related to the acetylcholinesterase inhibitor CP126,998, designed for noninvasive detection of acetylcholinesterase changes in Alzheimer's disease patients.
3-(Benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole: a PET radiotracer to measure acetylcholinesterase in brain.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives with alkylamino side chains act as highly selective ligands for binding to telomeric G-quadruplex DNA, effectively stabilizing the G-quadruplex structure and showing significant selectivity over duplex DNA.
Disubstituted 2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives as a new type of highly selective ligands for telomeric G-quadruplex DNA.

Pyridoxal 4-N-(1-benzylpiperidin-4-yl) thiosemicarbazone (PBPT) is a thiosemicarbazone derivative designed to target five Alzheimer's disease markers: low acetylcholinesterase levels, autophagy dysfunction, metal homeostasis disruptions, protein aggregation, and oxidative stress.
A novel class of thiosemicarbazones show multi-functional activity for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

Pyridoxal 4-N-(1-benzylpiperidin-4-yl)thiosemicarbazone (PBPT) is a thiosemicarbazone compound designed to target five major Alzheimer's disease hallmarks: low acetylcholine levels, dysfunctional autophagy, metal dys-homeostasis, protein aggregation, and oxidative stress.
A novel class of thiosemicarbazones show multi-functional activity for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

N-(2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl)benzamide derivatives can be structurally optimized as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with compound 8r showing strong inhibition of FLT3 and FLT3 mutants relevant to AML.
Rational design and synthesis of 2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole derivatives as inhibitors targeting FMS-like tyrosine kinase 3 (FLT3) and its mutants.
1-methyl-1 H-pyrazole-5-carboxamide derivatives inhibit the development of Haemonchus contortus larvae, with the first identified compound showing an IC50 of 0.29 µM.
Optimization of Novel 1-Methyl-1 H-Pyrazole-5-carboxamides Leads to High Potency Larval Development Inhibitors of the Barber's Pole Worm.
Pyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives act as potent GPR119 agonists, with some derivatives exhibiting in vitro EC50 values less than 100 nM.
Optimisation of novel 4, 8-disubstituted dihydropyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives as potent GPR 119 agonists.

4-(1H-pyrazol-1-yl)-3-pyridinesulfonamide derivatives exhibit potent inhibition of human carbonic anhydrase IX (hCA IX) with K(I) values ranging from 19.5 to 48.6 nM, comparable or more effective than clinically used sulfonamides such as AAZ, MZA, EZA, DCP, and IND (K(I) = 24–50 nM).

Carbonic anhydrase inhibitors. Synthesis, molecular structures, and inhibition of the human cytosolic isozymes I and II and transmembrane isozymes IX, XII (cancer-associated) and XIV with novel 3-pyridinesulfonamide derivatives.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a new series of 2-amino-3-aroyl thiophene derivatives as agonist allosteric modulators of the A1 adenosine receptor. A position-dependent effect study.
Schiff bases of valproylglycine hydrazide exhibit strong antiangiogenic and anticancer activities in developing zebrafish embryos.
Synthesis and biological activity of Schiff base series of valproyl, N-valproyl glycinyl, and N-valproyl-4-aminobenzoyl hydrazide derivatives.

Indenopyridinone derivatives with meta-phenolic groups at the 4-position and para-phenolic groups at the 2- and/or 4-position exhibit strong topo IIalpha-selective inhibition and anti-proliferative activity against human cervix (HeLa) and colorectal (HCT15) cancer cell lines.
A new phenolic series of indenopyridinone as topoisomerase inhibitors: Design, synthesis, and structure-activity relationships.

4-(1H-pyrazol-1-yl)-3-pyridinesulfonamide derivatives exhibit potent inhibition of human carbonic anhydrase IX (hCA IX) with K(I) values ranging from 19.5 to 48.6 nM, comparable or more effective than clinically used sulfonamides such as AAZ, MZA, EZA, DCP, and IND (K(I) = 24–50 nM).

Carbonic anhydrase inhibitors. Synthesis of heterocyclic 4-substituted pyridine-3-sulfonamide derivatives and their inhibition of the human cytosolic isozymes I and II and transmembrane tumor-associated isozymes IX and XII.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).

Structure-activity relationships of 2-amino-3-aroyl-4-[(4-arylpiperazin-1-yl)methyl]thiophenes. Part 2: Probing the influence of diverse substituents at the phenyl of the arylpiperazine moiety on allosteric enhancer activity at the A₁ adenosine receptor.
Ethyl (2-amino-3-fluoro-4-((4-(trifluoromethyl)benzyl)amino)phenyl)carbamate (RL648_81) is a synthetic modification of retigabine designed to enhance selectivity and potency for KCNQ2/3 channels.
Synthesis and Evaluation of Potent KCNQ2/3-Specific Channel Activators.

Arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate (AGHMB) promotes anastomotic wound healing in immunosuppressed rats with tacrolimus, as evidenced by similar neovascularization and inflammatory/fibroblast responses to honey.
Effect of a honey and arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate mixture on the healing of colon anastomosis in rats immunosuppressed with tacrolimus.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) attenuates the oxidative stress and inflammatory response in 661W cells exposed to lipoteichoic acid, including decreased pro-inflammatory factors IL-1beta, IL-12, and TNFalpha, and inhibition of nuclear factor kappa B and Egr1, Fosl1, and Lox12 gene expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.
4-acetoxy-3,5,3',4'-tetramethoxybibenzyl is a bibenzyl derivative isolated from Schefflera arboricola that displays moderate nuclear factor-kappaB (NF-κB) inhibitory activity in vitro.
A new bibenzyl derivative with nuclear factor-kappaB inhibitory activity from Schefflera arboricola (Araliaceae).
Cladosporilactam A is a new bicyclic lactam isolated from a gorgonian-derived Cladosporium sp. fungus and exhibits potent anticancer activity against HeLa cells with an IC50 value of 0.76 µM.
Bioactive 7-Oxabicyclic[6.3.0]lactam and 12-Membered Macrolides from a Gorgonian-Derived Cladosporium sp. Fungus.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives exhibit anti-MERS-CoV activity in Vero cells.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.
3-methoxy-2',4,4'-trihydroxy-2-prenylchalcone is a chalcone isolated from Shuteria sinensis that exhibits effects on glucose consumption in insulin-resistant HepG2 cells.
Two new chalcones from Shuteria sinensis.

Indenopyridinone derivatives with meta-phenolic groups at the 4-position and para-phenolic groups at the 2- and/or 4-position exhibit strong topo IIalpha-selective inhibition and anti-proliferative activity against human cervix (HeLa) and colorectal (HCT15) cancer cell lines.
A new phenolic series of indenopyridinone as topoisomerase inhibitors: Design, synthesis, and structure-activity relationships.
Tetrahydrothieno[2,3-b]quinoline derivatives have been evaluated for antiproliferative activity against HCT116, MDA-MB-468, and MDA-MB-231 human cancer cell lines.
Synthesis and cytotoxicity of thieno[2,3-b]quinoline-2-carboxamide and cycloalkyl[b]thieno[3,2-e]pyridine-2-carboxamide derivatives.

4-[4-(dimethylamino)phenyl]-N-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-6-methyl-2-oxo-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-5-pyrimidinecarboxamide (BTI-A-404) is a novel antagonist of hGPR43 that acts as an inverse agonist, increasing intracellular cAMP levels in a dose-dependent manner with an IC50 of 9.5 μM in the presence of 1 mM propionate and 2.1 μM in the absence of propionate in GPR43-overexpressing Glosensor cells.
Selective novel inverse agonists for human GPR43 augment GLP-1 secretion.

4-((4-(2-chlorophenyl)thiazole-2-yl)amino)-4oxo-3-phenylbutanoic acid (2CTAP) is an inverse agonist of the FFA2 receptor that inhibits constitutive calcium ion (Ca2+) levels and antagonises acetate-induced phosphorylated ERK1/2 (pERK1/2), but does not affect cAMP.
Characterisation of small molecule ligands 4CMTB and 2CTAP as modulators of human FFA2 receptor signalling.
N-acetyl-S-[1-(5-amino-5carboxylpentyl)-1H-pyrrol-3-yl]-L-cysteine is a downstream urinary metabolite derived from the reaction of cysteine with cis-2-Butene-1,4-dial (BDA) in furan-treated rats.
Biomonitoring of heat-induced food contaminants: Quantitative analysis of furan dependent glutathione- and lysine-adducts in rat urine as putative biomarkers of exposure.
4-chloro-3-nitro-N-butylbenzenesulfonamide (SMD2) is a reversible, use-dependent blocker of K V 3.1 channels, with an IC50 of approximately 10 µM and a Hill coefficient of around 2 in L-929 cells.
4-Chloro-3-nitro-N-butylbenzenesulfonamide acts on KV3.1 channels by an open-channel blocker mechanism.

Sulfur sodium hydride (NaHS) is a donor of gaseous hydrogen sulfide and induces concentration-dependent contractions in rat coronary artery, reaching approximately 65% of 60 mM KCl-induced contraction.
Hydrogen sulphide induces vasoconstriction of rat coronary artery via activation of Ca(2+) influx.

Tetrahydropyrido[4,3-d]pyrimidine-2,4-dione is a core chemical structure for small molecule anticancer agents that can inhibit phosphorylation of AKT and ERK, induce dephosphorylation of Foxo3a, promote TRAIL expression, and enhance ATF4 in PC-3 cells.
Development of Novel Anticancer Agents with a Scaffold of Tetrahydropyrido[4,3-d]pyrimidine-2,4-dione.

Ethyl 4-oxo-2-phenyl-1,4-dihydroquinoline-6-carboxylate (AS1712) can overcome P-glycoprotein efflux pump and beta-tubulin alterations that confer resistance to microtubule-targeting drugs in cancer cells.
4(1H)-quinolone derivatives overcome acquired resistance to anti-microtubule agents by targeting the colchicine site of β-tubulin.

4-aminoquinazoline-2-carboxamide derivatives function as PAK4 inhibitors with high selectivity (346-fold vs PAK1) and can potently inhibit migration and invasion of A549 tumor cells by regulating PAK4-directed downstream signaling pathways in vitro.
Structure-Based Design of 6-Chloro-4-aminoquinazoline-2-carboxamide Derivatives as Potent and Selective p21-Activated Kinase 4 (PAK4) Inhibitors.
N-acetyl-S-[1-(5-amino-5carboxylpentyl)-1H-pyrrol-3-yl]-L-cysteine is a downstream urinary metabolite derived from the reaction of cysteine with cis-2-Butene-1,4-dial (BDA) in furan-treated rats.
Abundant Rodent Furan-Derived Urinary Metabolites Are Associated with Tobacco Smoke Exposure in Humans.
Thiazolidinone-pyrazole hybrids exhibit anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma and MDA-MB231 human breast cancer cell lines, as well as moderate antimicrobial activity.
Synthesis and evaluation of thiazolidinone-pyrazole conjugates as anticancer and antimicrobial agents.

(S)-7-amino-2,3,4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[b][1,4]oxazepin-3-ol is a chiral compound that effectively inhibits the activity of phospholipase C (PC-PLC) derived from Bacillus cereus and human umbilical vein endothelial cells (HUVECs).
Discovery of novel PC-PLC activity inhibitors.

6-bromo-N-(1H-indazol-6-yl)quinolin-4-amine is a potent and selective cell-active chemical probe for cyclin G-associated kinase (GAK), with an IC50 of 1.4 µM in cells and improved metabolic stability in liver microsomes.
Towards the Development of an In vivo Chemical Probe for Cyclin G Associated Kinase (GAK).

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Trans-3,4-methano-L-syn-arginine acts as a good substrate for nitric oxide synthase isoforms with potency close to L-arginine and similar isoform selectivities.
Synthesis of a series of 3,4-methanoarginines as side-chain conformationally restricted analogues of arginine.
S-arachidonoyl-2-thioglycerol is the thioester analog of the endocannabinoid 2-arachidonoylglycerol and serves as a substrate for assays of hMAGL and human brain membrane MAG hydrolase activity.
S-Arachidonoyl-2-thioglycerol synthesis and use for fluorimetric and colorimetric assays of monoacylglycerol lipase.
3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin functions as a fluorescent probe capable of detecting small-molecule biothiols cysteine, homocysteine, and glutathione in dual fluorescence channels.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.
2-deoxy-2-[18F]fluoroglucose azide is used in click chemistry for the concomitant fluorination and glycosylation of peptides.
Recent trends in bioorthogonal click-radiolabeling reactions using fluorine-18.
(R)-9-hydroperoxy-(10E, 12Z)-10, 12-octadecadienoic acid is formed from linoleic acid by a crude enzyme from Ulva conglobata with high enantiomeric excess (>99%).
Enantioselective formation of (R)-9-HPODE and (R)-9-HPOTrE in marine green alga Ulva conglobata.
3-(2'-nitrovinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin functions as a fluorescent probe capable of distinguishing between Cys/Hcy and GSH by detecting these biothiols in dual fluorescence channels.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.
Methylaspartase exhibits a primary deuterium isotope effect for the removal of the C-3 proton from (2S,3S)-3-ethylaspartic acid, but not for (2S)-aspartic acid.

The 3-methylaspartase reaction probed using 2H- and 15N-isotope effects for three substrates: a flip from a concerted to a carbocationic amino-enzyme elimination mechanism upon changing the C-3 stereochemistry in the substrate from R to S.

5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-methoxyphenyl-3-sulfonatophenyl) porphyrin (AgTMPPS) exhibits strong antibacterial activity against MRSA and can be used for non-invasive bacterial sterilization via photodynamic therapy involving metal ion and photodynamic effects.
Synthesis of a Rare Water-Soluble Silver(II)/Porphyrin and Its Multifunctional Therapeutic Effect on Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus.

3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin (also known as CouSeNO2/CouSNO2) functions as a fluorescent probe for distinguishing between Cys/Hcy and GSH by detecting the nucleophilic substitution of its 4-phenylselenium group with thiol groups from these biothiols.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.

3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin enables dual fluorescence channel detection of Cys/Hcy and GSH by employing a first-step nucleophilic substitution reaction with the thiol group of each biothiol.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.
Samarium perchlorate acts as an efficient catalyst for the condensation reaction of ethyl acetoacetate, 2-phenyl-1,2,3-triazole-4-carbaldehyde, and urea in ethanol under reflux conditions.
Ultrasound-assisted synthesis of novel 4-(2-phenyl-1,2,3-triazol-4-yl)-3,4-dihydropyrimidin-(1H)-(thio)ones catalyzed by Sm(ClO(4))(3).
1-O-beta-D-malylglucose serves as an acyl donor substrate for anthocyanin malyltransferase (AMalT) activity in carnation.
The role of acyl-glucose in anthocyanin modifications.
2-trichloromethylquinazoline can serve as a substrate for the SRN1+ERC1 reaction with nitroalkanes to prepare vinylic chloride derivatives of quinazolines.
Access to original vinylic chlorides in the quinazoline series via a monoelectronic transfer reaction approach.
Hydroxycinnamoylglucoses participate in sugar acylation of anthocyanidin glycosides.
The role of acyl-glucose in anthocyanin modifications.
(E)-4-(2-(2-naphthyl)vinyl)pyridine forms synHT dimers with high stereoselectivity upon irradiation with a 250 W high-pressure mercury lamp in acidic solution.
[2 + 2] Photodimerization of Naphthylvinylpyridines through Cation-π Interactions in Acidic Solution.
Bis(acrylate)-bis(2-ethylimidazole)-copper(II) exists in both trans and cis isomeric forms, with the trans isomer being more active in antimicrobial activity compared to the cis isomer.
X-ray Crystal Structure, Geometric Isomerism, and Antimicrobial Activity of New Copper(II) Carboxylate Complexes with Imidazole Derivatives.
Triazolylphosphines are highly selective reaction partners that can covalently elabrate with azides incorporated into cellular glycans, enabling the attachment of biological probes to glycoproteins.
Applications of azide-based bioorthogonal click chemistry in glycobiology.

Bis(acrylate)-bis(2-ethylimidazole)-copper(II) demonstrates broad in vitro antimicrobial activity against Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria and fungal strains of both clinical and ecological importance.
X-ray Crystal Structure, Geometric Isomerism, and Antimicrobial Activity of New Copper(II) Carboxylate Complexes with Imidazole Derivatives.
4-nitrophenyl galactofuranoside serves as a glycosyl donor in transglycosylation reactions catalyzed by ΢-L-arabinofuranosidase, enabling the preparation of O-hexofuranosides and difucofuranosides.
Thiodisaccharides with galactofuranose or arabinofuranose as terminal units: synthesis and inhibitory activity of an exo beta-D-galactofuranosidase.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
Tomenphantin B is a cytotoxic germacranolide isolated from Elephantopus tomentosus L. and its structure and absolute stereochemistry have been determined.
Antitumor agents. 190. Absolute stereochemistry of the cytotoxic germacranolides, tomenphantins A and B, from Elephantopus tomentosus.

Plagionicin A is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.

Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate) is essentially inactive compared to ryanodine for insecticidal activity against Musca domestica adults and Tribolium castaneum larvae, for toxicity to mice, and for competition with [3H]ryanodine at the Ca(2+)-ryanodine receptor complex of skeletal muscle.
Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate): a novel ryanoid from Ryania insecticide.
Zooxanthellamide D (ZAD-D, C54H83NO19) is a long-chain polyhydroxy polyene amide isolated from a cultured marine dinoflagellate of the genus Symbiodinium.
Zooxanthellamide D, a polyhydroxy polyene amide from a marine dinoflagellate, and chemotaxonomic perspective of the Symbiodinium polyols.

Plagioneurin B is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.

Plagioneurin D is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.

Karuquinone A is a dihydronaphthoquinone derivative isolated from Fusarium solani that exhibits strong cytotoxicity against human cancer cell lines and induces apoptosis in cancer cells by generating reactive oxygen species (ROS).
Bioactive dihydronaphthoquinone derivatives from Fusarium solani.

Nanoparticles containing 5,6-bis(4 ′ -(diphenylamino)-[1,1 ′ -biphenyl]-4-yl)pyrazine-2,3-dicarbonitrile (DCDPP-2TPA) demonstrate photo-cytotoxicity towards tumor cells under two-photon photodynamic therapy (PDT).
Utilizing a Pyrazine-Containing Aggregation-Induced Emission Luminogen as an Efficient Photosensitizer for Imaging-Guided Two-Photon Photodynamic Therapy.

Kansuinin D is a jatrophane-type diterpene isolated from the root of Euphorbia kansui that shows no induction of cell cleavage arrest in Xenopus cells at the blastular stage at concentrations up to 0.5 µg/ml.
Diterpenes from the roots of Euphorbia kansui and their in vitro effects on the cell division of Xenopus.
Hydroxytyrosol decanoate exhibits a skin permeation profile increased 2.2-fold compared to hydroxytyrosol, reaching 2672.05 μg/cm²h⁻¹ after 12 hours.
Lipophilic hydroxytyrosol esters: fatty acid conjugates for potential topical administration.
22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A is a newly identified 3-alkylpiperidine alkaloid isolated from the marine sponge Amphimedon sp. and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P388 murine leukemia cells.
Tetradehydrohalicyclamine A and 22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A, new cytotoxic bis-piperidine alkaloids from a marine sponge Amphimedon sp.

Chloropupukeanolide E is a highly functionalized secondary metabolite with a novel spiroketal skeleton derived from chlorinated tricyclo-[4.3.1.0(3, 7)]-decane and 2,6-dihydroxy-4-methylbenzoic acid, isolated from Pestalotiopsis fici.
Chloropupukeanolides C-E: cytotoxic pupukeanane chlorides with a spiroketal skeleton from Pestalotiopsis fici.
Sarcotin A is a furanosesterterpene isolated from the marine sponge Sarcotragus sp. (family Thorectidae, order Dictyoceratida), and its C-21 configuration was revised based on NMR and CD data.
Cytotoxic pyrrolo- and furanoterpenoids from the sponge sarcotragus species.

Chloropupukeanolide E is a highly functionalized secondary metabolite with a novel spiroketal skeleton derived from chlorinated tricyclo-[4.3.1.0(3,7)]-decane and 2,6-dihydroxy-4-methylbenzoic acid, isolated from Pestalotiopsis fici.
Chloropupukeanolides C-E: cytotoxic pupukeanane chlorides with a spiroketal skeleton from Pestalotiopsis fici.

Riisein B is a steroidal glycoside isolated from the Brazilian telestacean octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei, with the structure 3beta-O-(4'-O-acetyl-beta-D-arabinopyranosyl)-25xi-choles tan e-3beta, 5alpha,6beta,26-tetrol-26-acetate.
New cytotoxic sterol glycosides from the octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei.

Kibdelone A is a novel bioactive heterocyclic polyketide produced by the actinomycete Kibdelosporangium sp. (MST-108465) and exhibits potent and selective cytotoxicity against human tumor cell lines, as well as significant antibacterial and nematocidal activity.
Kibdelones: novel anticancer polyketides from a rare Australian actinomycete.

(19Z)-halichondramide is an oxazole-containing compound isolated from the marine sponge Chondrosia corticata that exhibits significant cytotoxicity and antifungal activity against human leukemia K562 cells and Candida albicans, respectively.
New macrolides from the sponge Chondrosia corticata.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the aqueous extract of the lithistid demosponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis and is responsible for the observed anti-tumor activity in HCT-116 human colon carcinoma cells.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.
Ineleganene is a cytotoxic lobane diterpene isolated from the Formosan soft coral Sinularia inelegans.
A cytotoxic lobane diterpene from the formosan soft coral Sinularia inelegans.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

Ferrocenium boronic acid salt activates allylic alcohols to generate transient carbocations, enabling the formation of acyclic products with methyl-aryl quaternary carbon centers and high enantioselectivity (up to 90% yield, 97:3 enantiomeric ratio) under noble-metal-free conditions.
Dual Catalysis Using Boronic Acid and Chiral Amine: Acyclic Quaternary Carbons via Enantioselective Alkylation of Branched Aldehydes with Allylic Alcohols.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

(1R,1 ′ R,2R,2 ′ R)-N 1 ,N 1 ′ -(anthracene-1,8-diylbis(methylene))bis(N 2 ,N 2 -bis(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine) (R-ABDC, 4a) is an optically active dinuclear ligand designed to position a Fe(II) center at the 1- and 8-positions of an anthracene bridge, facilitating DNA intercalation and DNA-cleaving activity in the presence of FeSO 4 ⋅7H 2 O and excess sodium ascorbate.
Design and synthesis of an anthranyl bridged optically active dinuclear iron(II)-ligand and evaluation of DNA-cleaving activity.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.
3,4-diamino-3-cyclobutene-1,2-dione functions as an amino acid isostere when incorporated into VLA-4 integrin antagonists to improve the rate of clearance.
Squaric acid derivatives as VLA-4 integrin antagonists.

3-aminomethyl cyclohexane carboxylic acid, when incorporated as the P2 amino acid in peptidomimetic BACE-1 inhibitors, enables a novel binding mode with the S3 pocket of BACE-1, forming a hydrogen bond interaction pattern distinct from previous designs.
Structure-based design and synthesis of novel P2/P3 modified, non-peptidic beta-secretase (BACE-1) inhibitors.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.
Adenosine 3'-phosphonate acts as an inhibitor of the MurMN/FemABX family of tRNA-dependent ligases, which are implicated in high-level penicillin resistance in gram-positive bacteria.
Adenosine phosphonate inhibitors of lipid II: alanyl tRNA ligase MurM from Streptococcus pneumoniae.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.

2-phenylpyrido[1,2-e]purin-4(3H)-one derivatives with piperazinyl sulfonamide substituents have been developed as potential PDE5 inhibitors for the treatment of erectile dysfunction, exhibiting improved selectivity over PDE1 and PDE6.
Synthesis and phosphodiesterase 5 inhibitory activity of novel pyrido[1,2-e]purin-4(3H)-one derivatives.
4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one analogs with certain substitutions exhibit cytotoxic and antitumor activity against various tumor and cancer cell lines, with ED50 values ranging from 0.01 to 2.1 µM.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.
NSC 373989 is a pyrimido-quinoline compound that potently inhibits in vitro processive DNA synthesis mediated by KSHV rPOL8/rPF8 and significantly reduces lytic KSHV DNA replication in cells.
Chemical library screen for novel inhibitors of Kaposi's sarcoma-associated herpesvirus processive DNA synthesis.

(-)-(2R,4R)-1-(2-Hydroxymethyl-1,3-dioxolan-4-yl)thymine (DOT) demonstrates potent in vitro activity against wild-type HIV and a broad range of NRTI-resistant HIV strains, including those with K65R, L74V, M184V, T215Y, M184V/T215Y, and D67N/K70R/T215Y/K219Q mutations.
Mechanism of action of (-)-(2R,4R)-1-(2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-dioxolan-4-yl) thymine as an anti-HIV agent.

7-O-acetyl-19,20-epoxycytochalasin D is a 19,20-epoxycytochalasan isolated from Xylaria cf. curta, an endophyte associated with Solanum tuberosum, and shows cytotoxicity against HL-60 and MCF-7 cancer cell lines with IC50s in the range of 13.31–37.16 µM, while remaining inactive against A-549, SMMC-7721, and SW480 cell lines.
Sesquiterpenes and other constituents of Xylaria sp. NC1214, a fungal endophyte of the moss Hypnum sp.
Cysteinol derivatives are effective inhibitors of kinesin spindle protein (KSP) and induce a monopolar spindle phenotype in cells.
Synthesis and characterization of tritylthioethanamine derivatives with potent KSP inhibitory activity.

Ferroptocide is a novel compound that induces rapid ferroptotic death of cancer cells and inhibits thioredoxin, with a mechanism of ferroptotic induction distinct from and complementary to existing ferroptosis inducers.
Diverse compounds from pleuromutilin lead to a thioredoxin inhibitor and inducer of ferroptosis.
Cyclopropeniminium ions (CpN+) function as bioorthogonal reagents capable of reacting with cellular nucleophiles such as thiols and amines.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

4-deoxy-4-fluoro-xyloside derivatives can be synthesized by deprotection of benzoylated intermediates and evaluated for their ability to selectively inhibit heparan sulfate and chondroitin sulfate/derman sulfate biosynthesis in endothelial cells.
4-deoxy-4-fluoro-xyloside derivatives as inhibitors of glycosaminoglycan biosynthesis.
2-[(pyridine-2-ylmethyl)-amino]-phenol (2-PMAP) affects APP steady-state levels and Abeta production in CHO APP751SW cells.
Modulation of amyloid precursor protein expression reduces β-amyloid deposition in a mouse model.

Thiadiazole carbamate compound 12 is the most potent inhibitor of lysosomal acid lipase (LAL) activity in eliciting decreases in cholesterol accumulation in NPC1 mutant cells and increases in neutral lipid accumulation in normal cells among the tested compounds.
Thiadiazole carbamates: potent inhibitors of lysosomal acid lipase and potential Niemann-Pick type C disease therapeutics.
2-amino benzo[b]thiophene derivatives with a 3,4,5-trimethoxybenzoyl group form the core structure of a series of antitubulin agents (compounds 14-16) with cytotoxic activity comparable to CA-4.
Synthesis and preliminary biological evaluation of new anti-tubulin agents containing different benzoheterocycles.
CH3Hg-Hcy is a transportable substrate of hOAT1, as shown by its uptake in MDCK II cells expressing hOAT1.
Handling of the homocysteine S-conjugate of methylmercury by renal epithelial cells: role of organic anion transporter 1 and amino acid transporters.

N-salicylidene-L-tryptophanate (sal-L-tryp) serves as a ligand for vanadium complexes with anti-proliferative activity against colon cancer cells and reduced cytotoxicity towards non-cancerous colonic myofibroblasts.
Preliminary anti-cancer photodynamic therapeutic in vitro studies with mixed-metal binuclear ruthenium(II)-vanadium(IV) complexes.
Tetrahydroquinoline-pyrimidine exhibits strong anti-proliferative activity with IC50 values ranging from 5.6 to 18.3 nM via tubulin polymerization inhibition.
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.

Indenopyridinone derivatives with meta-phenolic groups at the 4-position and para-phenolic groups at the 2- and/or 4-position exhibit strong topo IIalpha-selective inhibition and anti-proliferative activity against human cervix (HeLa) and colorectal (HCT15) cancer cell lines.
Optimization of the indenone ring of indenoisoquinoline topoisomerase I inhibitors.

The BRL37344-induced hyperpolarization of freshly isolated detrusor smooth muscle cells is eliminated by iberiotoxin or ryanodine, indicating involvement of BK channels and ryanodine receptors in its mechanism of action.
BRL37344, a β3-adrenergic receptor agonist, decreases nerve-evoked contractions in human detrusor smooth muscle isolated strips: role of BK channels.
N 6 -(2,4-dimethoxybenzyl)adenosine shows no detectable toxicity to uninfected host cells at concentrations up to 50 μM, indicating favorable potency and selectivity as an anti-toxoplasma agent.
Synthesis, biological evaluation and molecular modeling studies of N6-benzyladenosine analogues as potential anti-toxoplasma agents.
2,5-DMA selectively induces PLC-IP accumulation in cells expressing 5-HT 2C or 5-HT 2A receptors.
Functional selectivity of hallucinogenic phenethylamine and phenylisopropylamine derivatives at human 5-hydroxytryptamine (5-HT)2A and 5-HT2C receptors.

Aminopyrimido[4,5-c]isoquinolinequinone derivatives display increased cytotoxic potency against cancer cells compared to their precursors, with the nature of the nitrogen substituent and the methyl group at the 6-position influencing antitumor activity.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.

N-salicylidene-L-tryptophanate (sal-L-tryp) serves as a ligand for vanadium complexes with anti-proliferative activity against colon cancer cells and reduced cytotoxicity towards non-cancerous colonic myofibroblasts.
Titanium(IV) complexes: cytotoxicity and cellular uptake of titanium(IV) complexes on caco-2 cell line.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
Pyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives can act as GPR119 agonists and have been optimized for anti-type 2 diabetes mellitus activity.
Optimisation of novel 4, 8-disubstituted dihydropyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives as potent GPR 119 agonists.
Benzimidazole-1,2,3-triazole compounds can be synthesized via a click reaction in DMF/H2O and exhibit antidiabetic activity, including inhibition of α-amylase and α-glucosidase.
Synthesis and antidiabetic evaluation of benzimidazole-tethered 1,2,3-triazoles.

Molybdenum oxyfluoride is a phase formed in fluorinated MoO2 anodes for lithium-ion batteries, contributing to improved conversion reaction kinetics, increased capacity, higher coulombic efficiency, and better cycling stability compared to nonfluorinated anodes.
Synthesizing High-Capacity Oxyfluoride Conversion Anodes by Direct Fluorination of Molybdenum Dioxide (MoO2 ).

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.
Ginsenoside Rh7 suppresses the expression of ILF3-AS1, which competitively binds with miR-212, resulting in down-regulation of SMAD1 and suppression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) progression.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

Ginsenoside Rh7 can regulate the expression of ILF3-AS1, and by competitively binding with miR-212, it can enhance the miR-212-Smad1 axis, thereby significantly inhibiting the proliferation, migration, and invasion of NSCLC cells.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.

Pipoxide chlorohydrin is a halogenated plant metabolite that shows the greatest affinity and potency for binding to chikungunya virus nsP2pro, as demonstrated by molecular docking, MD simulation, and MMPBSA studies.
Halogenated Secondary Metabolites from Higher Plants: Potent Drug Candidates for Chikungunya Using in silico Approaches.
Pyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives act as potent GPR119 agonists, with some derivatives exhibiting in vitro EC50 values less than 100 nM.
Optimisation of novel 4, 8-disubstituted dihydropyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives as potent GPR 119 agonists.

Iodide-2-thiouracil binary clusters show that low energy electron attachment leads to the formation of anion states with both dipole bound and valence bound character, with the dipole bound state decaying rapidly (~50 fs) after excitation near the vertical detachment energy.
Electron attachment dynamics following UV excitation of iodide-2-thiouracil complexes.

(1R,1 ′ R,2R,2 ′ R)-N 1 ,N 1 ′ -(anthracene-1,8-diylbis(methylene))bis(N 2 ,N 2 -bis(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine) (R-ABDC, 4a) is an optically active dinuclear ligand designed to position a Fe(II) center at the 1- and 8-positions of an anthracene bridge, facilitating DNA intercalation and DNA-cleaving activity in the presence of FeSO 4 ⋅7H 2 O and excess sodium ascorbate.
Design and synthesis of an anthranyl bridged optically active dinuclear iron(II)-ligand and evaluation of DNA-cleaving activity.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.
Benzoylazepan-2-one reacts with amines in the presence of DTBP and TBAI to produce transamidated products under aqueous conditions with good functional group tolerance.
Metal-free transamidation of benzoylpyrrolidin-2-one and amines under aqueous conditions.

A specific thiazole-naphthalene derivative (compound 5b) inhibits tubulin polymerisation with an IC50 of 3.3 µM and shows antiproliferative activity with IC50 values of 0.48 ± 0.03 µM in MCF-7 and 0.97 ± 0.13 µM in A549 cancer cells.
Design, synthesis and biological evaluation of novel thiazole-naphthalene derivatives as potential anticancer agents and tubulin polymerisation inhibitors.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been designed and synthesized as inhibitors of Aurora A kinase.
Design, synthesis, biological evaluation of 6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives as novel anticancer agents with Aurora kinase inhibition.
3-fluorochromanone serves as a substrate for ruthenium-catalyzed asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to yield enantioenriched cis-3-fluoro-chroman-4-ol derivatives.
Ru(II)-Catalyzed Asymmetric Transfer Hydrogenation of 3-Fluorochromanone Derivatives to Access Enantioenriched cis-3-Fluorochroman-4-ols through Dynamic Kinetic Resolution.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) reduces erythema intensity, thickness, and desquamation, as measured by PASI, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model when administered topically (1% w/w) or orally (125 mg/kg) for 14 days.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.

N-(2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl)benzamide derivatives can be structurally optimized as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with compound 8r showing strong inhibition of FLT3 and FLT3 mutants relevant to AML.
Rational design and synthesis of 2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole derivatives as inhibitors targeting FMS-like tyrosine kinase 3 (FLT3) and its mutants.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) is a prodrug of 2-(4-Chlorophenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetic acid (CBA) that inhibits psoriasis-associated inflammatory symptoms, including erythema, thickness, desquamation, hyperkeratosis, and hyperplasia, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.

4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs act as inhibitors of Walker carcinosarcoma 256 cells, with inhibitory activity dependent on the length of the aliphatic side chain and substitution at the terminal positions.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.
Sodium 2-(1-imidazolylmethyl)-4,5-dihydrobenzo[b]thiophene-6-carboxylate is a potent and specific inhibitor of thromboxane A2 synthetase in vitro.
Synthesis and thromboxane synthetase inhibitory activity of di- or tetrahydrobenzo[b]thiophenecarboxylic acid derivatives.
Butyl alpha-d-mannose binds to FimH, following a hydrophobic trail through the tyrosine gate and interacting via Manalpha1,3Man and Manbeta1,4GlcNAc linkages.
Study of the structural and dynamic effects in the FimH adhesin upon α-d-heptyl mannose binding.

Methyl 3-[(4-methoxyphenyl)thio]-5-chloro-1H-indole-2-carboxylate is a novel indole derivative that inhibits tubulin polymerization, colchicine binding, and the growth of MCF-7 human breast carcinoma cells.
Arylthioindoles, potent inhibitors of tubulin polymerization.

1-fluoro-D-glucopyranosyl fluoride is hydrolyzed by inverting amyloglucosidase II from A. niger according to Michaelis-Menten kinetics, but with a catalytic efficiency 2700-fold lower than that of a-glucopyranosyl fluoride.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of glycosidase inhibitors: gem-difluoromethylenated nojirimycin analogues.

7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) is a potent tubulin-polymerization inhibitor with high in vitro cytotoxic activity (GI50 1.9–3.2 nM), significant tubulin assembly inhibition (IC50 0.77 µM), and substantial inhibition of colchicine binding (99% at 5 µM).
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.

2-hydroxy-4-methoxycarbonylisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-dione is a substituted analogue of 2-hydroxyisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-dione with an electron-withdrawing group at the 4-position, and its tautomeric forms, magnesium complex stoichiometry, and antiintegrase, anti-RNase H, and antiviral activities are studied.

Magnesium chelating 2-hydroxyisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-diones, as inhibitors of HIV-1 integrase and/or the HIV-1 reverse transcriptase ribonuclease H domain: discovery of a novel selective inhibitor of the ribonuclease H function.

4-aminoquinazoline-2-carboxamide derivatives function as PAK4 inhibitors with high selectivity (346-fold vs PAK1) and can potently inhibit migration and invasion of A549 tumor cells by regulating PAK4-directed downstream signaling pathways in vitro.
Structure-Based Design of 6-Chloro-4-aminoquinazoline-2-carboxamide Derivatives as Potent and Selective p21-Activated Kinase 4 (PAK4) Inhibitors.
5-bromo-2,3-dimethoxy-6-phytyl-1,4-benzoquinone is less inhibitory than the 5-hydroxy derivative against mitochondrial NADHoxidase and succinoxidase systems.
Specificity of inhibition of coenzyme Q-enzyme systems by lipoidal benzoquinone derivatives.
7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) causes cell arrest in the G2/M phase, disrupts microtubule formation, and competes at the colchicine site on tubulin.
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.

4,6-bisbenzylthio-2-methylthiopyrimidine-5-carboxylic acid acts as a partial agonist of peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPARgamma) and shows weaker adipocyte differentiation in 3T3-L1 cells compared to rosiglitazone.

Design, synthesis, and structure-activity relationship studies of novel 2,4,6-trisubstituted-5-pyrimidinecarboxylic acids as peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPARgamma) partial agonists with comparable antidiabetic efficacy to rosiglitazone.
2-(3′,4′,5′-Trimethoxybenzoyl)-3-amino-5-phenyl thiophene exhibits antiproliferative activity and induces G2/M cell cycle arrest and apoptosis in HL-60 cells.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of 2-(3',4',5'-trimethoxybenzoyl)-3-amino 5-aryl thiophenes as a new class of tubulin inhibitors.
Hydroxymethoxynitrobenzaldehyde derivatives, except for one, do not inhibit catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) at a concentration of 5 × 10⁻¹ M in vitro.
Dihydroxynitrobenzaldehydes and hydroxymethoxynitrobenzaldehydes: synthesis and biological activity as catechol-O-methyltransferase inhibitors.

(R)-4-methyl-8-ethyl-2-phenyl-imidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-one is a highly potent and selective antagonist for human A(3) adenosine receptors, exhibiting a K(i) value of 2.3 nM and high selectivity over all other adenosine receptor subtypes.
New 2-heterocyclyl-imidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-one derivatives as potent and selective human A3 adenosine receptor antagonists.
2,3-dimethoxy-5-hydroxy-6-n-pentadecyl-1,4-benzoquinone is a more potent inhibitor in DPNH-oxidase than the corresponding 2,3-ethylenedioxyquinone analog.
Synthesis and inhibitory activity of new ethylenedioxyquinones as analogs of coenzyme Q.
7-phenoxy-4-imidazolylmethylcoumarin derivatives exhibit very high selectivity for CYP19 (aromatase) over CYP17 (17β-dehydrogenase).
Design, synthesis, and biological evaluation of imidazolyl derivatives of 4,7-disubstituted coumarins as aromatase inhibitors selective over 17-α-hydroxylase/C17-20 lyase.

3-(3-oxo-1-propenyl)thymidine is a 3'-azido analogue of pyrimidine deoxyribonucleosides that shows significant antiviral activity against HIV-1 in human peripheral blood mononuclear cells, with an EC50 value of 0.01 µM.
Synthesis and antiviral activity of various 3'-azido analogues of pyrimidine deoxyribonucleosides against human immunodeficiency virus (HIV-1, HTLV-III/LAV).

diethylenetriamine pentaacetic acid-deoxyglucose (DTPA-DG) can be radiolabeled with (188)Re and shows high tumor uptake in mammary tumor-bearing mice, with mean tumoral percent injected dosages per gram (% ID/g) of 1.98 ± 0.29 % ID/g at 3 hours, 2.89 ± 0.43 % ID/g at 12 hours, and 0.42 ± 0.06 % ID/g at 24 hours after intravenous injection.
Evaluation of 188Re-DTPA-deoxyglucose as a potential cancer radiopharmaceutical.

(4S)-4-(3-fluoro-4-fluorophenyl)-L-glutamate (18F-FSPG, or BAY 94-9392) is a PET tracer designed to assess system xC transporter activity and can visualize tumors in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma.
(4S)-4-(3-18F-fluoropropyl)-L-glutamate for imaging of xC transporter activity in hepatocellular carcinoma using PET: preclinical and exploratory clinical studies.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.

N-(chloro-3-[11C]methoxyphenyl)-2-picolinamide is a carbon-11 labeled PET radioligand that selectively binds to mGlu4 receptors in the brain, showing fast brain uptake and accumulation in mGlu4-rich regions.
Radiosynthesis and evaluation of an 18F-labeled positron emission tomography (PET) radioligand for metabotropic glutamate receptor subtype 4 (mGlu4).

3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionate is a catabolic product of phenylalanine metabolism by Clostridium species and is significantly higher in urine of ASDs children compared with age-matched controls.
Urinary 3-(3-Hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionic Acid, 3-Hydroxyphenylacetic Acid, and 3-Hydroxyhippuric Acid Are Elevated in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorders.
5-bromodeoxycytidine 5'-monophosphate is produced from 5-bromodeoxycytidine in extracts of herpes simplex virus-infected cells but not in uninfected cells.
Phosphorylation of 5-bromodeoxycytidine in cells infected with herpes simplex virus.

5-(3-[ 18 F]-fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan ([ 18 F]-L-FPTP) is a [ 18 F]-labeled 5-hydroxy-L-tryptophan tracer developed for clinical amino acid PET imaging, primarily transported by LAT2 in tumor cells, and shows high tumor uptake in model mice.
Radiosynthesis and biological evaluation of 5-(3-[18F]fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan for tumor PET imaging.
Parenteral copper supplementation of the dam by copper edetate in late gestation has no effect on the liver copper concentration of the foal at birth.
Changes in liver copper concentration of thoroughbred foals from birth to 160 days of age and the effect of prenatal copper supplementation of their dams.
4-carbomethoxy-2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole is a simplified analog of UK-1 that displays cytotoxic activity against cancer cell lines and forms complexes with metal ions such as Mg2+ and Zn2+.
Synthesis, metal ion binding, and biological evaluation of new anticancer 2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole analogs of UK-1.

In human breast invasive ductal carcinoma tissues, the initial velocity (Vc,6) of 14CO2 production from [6-14C]glucose increases with cancer stage, showing Vc,6 values of 1.34–1.90 (SBR I), 1.33–1.41 (SBR II), and 0.72–0.79 (SBR III).
Rate of 14CO2 production from variously labeled forms of [14C]glucose in human breast invasive ductal carcinoma tissues.
14S,22-diHDHA (Maresin-L1) acts as an autacrine or paracrine factor produced by leukocytes and platelets that diminishes diabetic impairment of macrophage reparative functions.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.
Actinopolymorphol A is a natural product that preferentially induces ERα/β heterodimerization over homodimerization in live cells.
Identification and characterization of a novel estrogenic ligand actinopolymorphol A.

1-benzyl-4',5'-dihydrospiro[piperidine-4,7'-thieno[2,3-c]pyran]-2'-carboxamide is a scaffold identified through virtual and binding assays as a starting point for optimization targeting RORgammat in the Th17 pathway.
Defining Target Engagement Required for Efficacy In Vivo at the Retinoic Acid Receptor-Related Orphan Receptor C2 (RORγt).

p-[ 18 F]fluorohippurate ([ 18 F]PFH) is a potential renal tubular PET agent that provides quantitative imaging useful for estimating effective renal plasma flow and produces high-quality tomographic images in dynamic studies.
Single-step radiosynthesis and in vivo evaluation of a novel fluorine-18 labeled hippurate for use as a PET renal agent.
5-bromodeoxycytidine-5'-phosphate (BrdCMP) is formed from 5-bromodeoxycytidine in extracts of herpes simplex virus-infected cells but not in uninfected cells.
Phosphorylation of 5-bromodeoxycytidine in cells infected with herpes simplex virus.

trans-3,4-dihydro-3,4-dihydroxy-7-methylbenz[a]anthracene, a dihydrodiol derived from polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, induces up to a 6-fold increase in the frequency of sister-chromatid exchanges in Chinese hamster ovary cells.

The relationship between the levels of DNA-hydrocarbon adducts and the formation of sister-chromatid exchanges in Chinese hamster ovary cells treated with derivatives of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
In zebrafish (Danio rerio) exposed to 100 µg/l bisphenol A for 7 days, the total body steady state concentration of bisphenol A glucuronic acid is 12,600 ng/g fish after 24 hours.
Metabolism of bisphenol A in zebrafish (Danio rerio) and rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss) in relation to estrogenic response.

[2-3H]glycerol-3-phosphate is incorporated into lipids during all stages of development in Bufo arenarum embryos, up to gill circulation, indicating its role as a precursor in embryonic lipid synthesis.
Lipid biosynthesis in amphibian oocyte and embryonic cell-free preparations.

Under the described protocol, [3-13C]serine contributes approximately 100% of the methyl groups used for total body homocysteine remethylation, while accounting for only about 2.8% of total serine flux.
Tracer-derived total and folate-dependent homocysteine remethylation and synthesis rates in humans indicate that serine is the main one-carbon donor.
2'(3')-O-Bromoacetyluridine reacts rapidly and selectively with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A at pH 5.5 and 25°, forming N-3-carboxymethylhistidine-12 ribonuclease A as the product.
The kinetics and specificity of the reaction of 2'(3')-O-bromoacetyluridine with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

(Z)-5-(4-methoxybenzylidene)-2-(naphthalen-2-yl)-5H-thiazol-4-one (ST1237) is a potent direct 5-lipoxygenase (5-LO) inhibitor with IC50 values of 0.08–0.03 µM in the cell-free S100 assay and 0.12–0.02 µM in intact polymorphonuclear leukocytes (PMNL).
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a class of 5-benzylidene-2-phenyl-thiazolinones as potent 5-lipoxygenase inhibitors.
N-(2-morpholinoethyl)nicotinamide derivatives are potent and selective inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) over MAO-B.
Design of novel nicotinamides as potent and selective monoamine oxidase a inhibitors.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.

(R)-4-methyl-8-ethyl-2-phenyl-imidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-one is a highly potent and selective antagonist for human A(3) adenosine receptors, exhibiting a K(i) value of 2.3 nM and high selectivity over all other adenosine receptor subtypes.
2-Phenylimidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-ones: structure-activity relationships and characterization of potent and selective inverse agonists at Human A3 adenosine receptors.
4-carbomethoxy-2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole is a simplified analog of UK-1 that displays cytotoxic activity against cancer cell lines and forms complexes with metal ions such as Mg2+ and Zn2+.
Synthesis, metal ion binding, and biological evaluation of new anticancer 2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole analogs of UK-1.
2'(3')-O-Bromoacetyluridine inactivates ribonuclease A with a rate 3100 times greater than with L-histidine.
The kinetics and specificity of the reaction of 2'(3')-O-bromoacetyluridine with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A.
p-amidinophenylphosphinic acid (AMPA) forms hydrogen bonds with catalytic residues at the oxyanion hole of trypsin, resulting in improved activity compared to benzamidine.
An oxyanion-hole selective serine protease inhibitor in complex with trypsin.

(Z)-5-(4-methoxybenzylidene)-2-(naphthalen-2-yl)-5H-thiazol-4-one is a potent, direct, non-cytotoxic 5-lipoxygenase inhibitor with IC50 values of 0.08 µM in cell-free assay and 0.12 µM in intact cells.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a class of 5-benzylidene-2-phenyl-thiazolinones as potent 5-lipoxygenase inhibitors.
Manadosterol A is a dimeric sterol isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa that exhibits inhibitory activity against the formation of the Ubc13-Uev1A complex.
Manadosterols A and B, sulfonated sterol dimers inhibiting the Ubc13-Uev1A interaction, isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa.

1,2-bis(heptanylcarbamoyl)-rac-glycerol 3-sulfate binds directly to porcine pancreatic phospholipase A2 and its zymogen, as detected by changes in the zero-field splitting of Trp3 residue in optical detection of triplet-state magnetic resonance spectroscopy.
Optically detected magnetic resonance studies of porcine pancreatic phospholipase A2 binding to a negatively charged substrate analogue.

O-phenoxycarbonyl-N-(R)-[(4-amino-4-carboxy-1-butyl)oxycarbonyl]hydroxylamine is an effective inactivator of Class C beta-lactamases, requiring a hydrophobic element and a negatively charged group (carboxylate) for high inhibitory activity.
Specificity of extended O-aryloxycarbonyl hydroxamates as inhibitors of a class C β-lactamase.
O-phenoxycarbonyl-N-(R)-[(4-amino-4-carboxy-1-butyl)oxycarbonyl]hydroxylamine inactivates Class C beta-lactamases by forming an inert hydroxamoyl derivative rather than a carbonyl cross-link.
Specificity of extended O-aryloxycarbonyl hydroxamates as inhibitors of a class C β-lactamase.
Tetrahydrothieno[2,3-b]quinoline derivatives have been evaluated for antiproliferative activity against HCT116, MDA-MB-468, and MDA-MB-231 human cancer cell lines.
Synthesis and cytotoxicity of thieno[2,3-b]quinoline-2-carboxamide and cycloalkyl[b]thieno[3,2-e]pyridine-2-carboxamide derivatives.

6-(4-chlorophenoxy)-4-(4-methoxyphenyl) morpholin-3-one inhibits the growth of A549 lung cancer cells in a dose-dependent manner, causing partial G1 cell cycle arrest, inducing apoptosis, and elevating P53 and Fas protein levels at 40 µg/ml.
Novel morpholin-3-one derivatives induced apoptosis and elevated the level of P53 and Fas in A549 lung cancer cells.

Fiscpropionate D is a polypropionate derivative from Aspergillus fischeri FS452 that inhibits Mycobacterium tuberculosis protein tyrosine phosphatase B (MptpB) with an IC50 of 11 µM via a noncompetitive mechanism.
Polypropionate Derivatives with Mycobacterium tuberculosis Protein Tyrosine Phosphatase B Inhibitory Activities from the Deep-Sea-Derived Fungus Aspergillus fischeri FS452.

N-(N-hydroxysulfamoyl)phenylalanine is a transition state analogue inhibitor of carboxypeptidase A, binding in a manner reminiscent of a transition state and exhibiting a competitive inhibition with a Ki in the micromolar range.
Sulfamide derivatives as transition state analogue inhibitors for carboxypeptidase A.

5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate (IP-dUMP) is a mechanism-based, irreversible inactivator of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase, covalently modifying Tyr94 at the enzyme's active site.
Modification of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase at tyrosine-94 by 5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate.
2-Geranylgeranyl-1,4-dihydroxybenzene, isolated from Lactarius lignyotus, shows strong inhibitory activity on DNA, RNA, and protein synthesis in HeLa and HL-60 cell lines.
A bioactive tetraprenylphenol from Lactarius lignyotus.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) enhances natural host resistance against Pseudomonas aeruginosa in cyclophosphamide-treated mice in a dose-dependent manner.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

(E)-4-(4-[(5-methyl-2-phenyl-1, 3-oxazol-4-yl)methoxy]benzyloxyimino)-4-phenylbutyric acid (TAK-559) exhibits marked glucose and lipid lowering activity in KKA(y) mice without significant body weight gain and demonstrates favorable pharmacokinetic properties with good absorption and duration.
Studies on non-thiazolidinedione antidiabetic agents. 2. Novel oxyiminoalkanoic acid derivatives as potent glucose and lipid lowering agents.
EED226 is a potent inhibitor of PRC2 activity that binds to the H3K27me3 pocket of EED and induces regression of tumor xenograft in vivo.
An allosteric PRC2 inhibitor targeting the H3K27me3 binding pocket of EED.

Repetitive intratesticular injection of 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline complex of oxovanadium(IV) at 7.5 mg/kg testis induces germ cell apoptosis, decreased sperm counts, and reduced testicular weights in vivo.
Bis(4,7-dimethyl and 5-dinitro-1,10-phenanthroline) sulfato-oxovanadium(IV)-mediated in vivo male germ cell apoptosis.
Tasuldine (2-[(3-pyridinylmethyl)thio]pyrimidine) increases tracheobronchial secretion in mice, as indicated by increased excretion of phenol red into the trachea.
2-[(3-Pyridinylmethyl)thio]pyrimidine derivatives: new bronchosecretolytic agents.

1,4-butanediol di-2,2,2-trifluoroethanesulfonate (BFS) is a fluorine-containing busulfan analog that, when administered as five daily intraperitoneal treatments, significantly increases survival and reduces toxicity in sarcoma 180-bearing mice compared to busulfan.
Antitumor activity and neutrophil-selective hematopoietic toxicity of busulfan analogs in mice.
N-benzyl-D-glucamine dithiocarbamate forms cadmium complexes that are taken up into tubular cells by an organic anion transport system through the basolateral membrane in mice.
Structure-effect relationship in the mobilization of cadmium in mice by several dithiocarbamates.

Tropifexor (LJN452) is a highly potent agonist of the farnesoid X receptor (FXR) that induces FXR target genes in vivo in rodent models of nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH) and primary biliary cholangitis (PBC).
Discovery of Tropifexor (LJN452), a Highly Potent Non-bile Acid FXR Agonist for the Treatment of Cholestatic Liver Diseases and Nonalcoholic Steatohepatitis (NASH).
N-benzyl-D-glucamine dithiocarbamate is an N,N-disubstituted dithiocarbamate that effectively mobilizes cadmium from hepatic and renal tissue without promoting redistribution to the brain.
Optimization of chelating agent structure for the mobilization of aged renal and hepatic cadmium deposits: sodium N-benzyl-4-O-(beta-D-galactopyranosyl)-D-glucamine-N-carbodithioate.

Substitution of the N-terminal amino group in the enkephalin analogue H-Dmt-D-Ala-Gly-Phe-Leu-NH2 with (2S)-2-methyl-3-(2,6-dimethyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acid results in a potent δ opioid antagonist with a K e of 28.1 nM in the mouse vas deferens assay.
[2',6'-Dimethyltyrosine]dynorphin A(1-11)-NH2 analogues lacking an N-terminal amino group: potent and selective kappa opioid antagonists.

Substitution of the N-terminal amino group in the enkephalin analogue H-Dmt-D-Ala-Gly-Phe-Leu-NH2 with (2S)-2-methyl-3-(2,6-dimethyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acid results in a potent δ opioid antagonist with a K e of 28.1 nM in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Conversion of delta-, kappa- and mu-receptor selective opioid peptide agonists into delta-, kappa- and mu-selective antagonists.

Stilbene isothiocyanate can be used to label the N-terminus of thymopentin analogues, resulting in a mono-labeled analogue (SITS-TP-5) that retains the ability to induce Thy 1.2 surface marker expression on nude mouse prothymocytes and inhibit radiolabeled thymopentin binding to human lymphocytes.
Synthesis of two biologically active fluorescent probes of thymopentin.
4,6-bisbenzylthio-2-methylthiopyrimidine-5-carboxylic acid displays antidiabetic efficacy comparable to rosiglitazone in db/db mice, though with 10-fold lower potency.

Design, synthesis, and structure-activity relationship studies of novel 2,4,6-trisubstituted-5-pyrimidinecarboxylic acids as peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPARgamma) partial agonists with comparable antidiabetic efficacy to rosiglitazone.

Intracerebroventricular injection of N-[(RS)-2-carboxy-3-phenylpropanoyl]-L-leucine in mice produces high potentiation of the analgesic effect of D-Ala2-Met-enkephalin, indicating in vivo inhibition of enkephalinase.
New carboxyalkyl inhibitors of brain enkephalinase: synthesis, biological activity, and analgesic properties.
4-amino-3-phenylquinolin-2(1H)-one is a potent ligand at the NMDA receptor glycine site, with high binding affinity and in vivo activity in the DBA/2 mouse audiogenic seizure test.
4-substituted-3-phenylquinolin-2(1H)-ones: acidic and nonacidic glycine site N-methyl-D-aspartate antagonists with in vivo activity.

Quinuclidine-cyclohexanone derivatives function as muscarinic receptor antagonists with tissue-dependent potency, showing equipotent molar ratios with respect to atropine ranging from 10,000–1,000 in isolated guinea pig ileum to 100–10 in mouse salivary gland, cat superior cervical ganglion, and mouse CNS.
A study of muscarinic receptor heterogeneity with weak antagonists.
Thiazolobenzodiazepine derivatives exhibit anticonvulsant activity in DBA/2 mice subjected to sound-induced seizures and in vivo clonus induced by pentylenetetrazol.
Molecular requirement for anticonvulsant activity in a series of thiazolo-1,4-benzodiazepine derivatives and comparison with classical benzodiazepines.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

When covalently linked to bacteriophage 75 and delivered to Staphylococcus aureus, tin(IV) chlorin e6 (SnCe6) enables selective killing of methicillin- and vancomycin-intermediate strains with low concentrations (1.5 µg/ml) and low light doses (21 J/cm2), while sparing human epithelial cells.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

Oleyl oleate (36 carbon atoms) can be produced by esterification of long-chain fatty acids in n-hexane catalyzed by nylon-immobilized lipase from Candida rugosa, achieving maximum reaction rates of approximately 60 mmol h⁻¹ g⁻¹ immobilized enzyme at an initial substrate concentration of 0.6 mol l⁻¹ and equimolar substrate proportions.
Esterification of fatty acids using nylon-immobilized lipase in n-hexane: kinetic parameters and chain-length effects.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
Concise means for accessing an advanced precursor to 1-deoxypaclitaxel.
