ChloraPrep, a solution containing 2% chlorhexidine gluconate and 70% isopropyl alcohol, is the most effective surgical skin-preparation solution for eliminating bacteria from the hallux and toes in the foot and ankle prior to surgery.
Efficacy of surgical preparation solutions in foot and ankle surgery.

The SnCe6–bacteriophage conjugate is a more effective bactericide against vancomycin-intermediate Staphylococcus aureus strains than unconjugated SnCe6, and killing is not dependent on bacterial growth phase.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.
Mixed esters of ascorbic acid synthesized from palm oil methyl ester and soybean oil methyl ester contain 42.59% ascorbyl oleate, 45.89% ascorbyl palmitate, and 10.1% ascorbyl linoleate.
Synthesis of mixed esters of ascorbic acid using methyl esters of palm and soybean oils.

Dodecyl-alpha-D-maltoside is a non-ionic detergent that stabilizes and enables crystallization of the human nuclear receptor hRARgamma ligand-binding domain by mediating a crystal contact in the coactivator binding region.
Structural role of a detergent molecule in retinoic acid nuclear receptor crystals.

Chloromaloside A is a cytotoxic steroidal glycoside isolated from Chlorophytum malayense Ridley and characterized as neohecogenin 3-O-beta-D-glucopyranosyl(1→2)-[beta-D-xylopyranosyl(1→3)]-beta-D-glucopyranosyl(1→4)-beta-D-galactopyranoside.
Isolation and characterization of cytotoxic saponin chloromaloside A from Chlorophytum malayense.

Oral administration of 3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) at doses ≥10 mg/kg in MCMV-infected mice produces antiviral effects comparable to ganciclovir, as measured by survival and viral DNA levels in organs.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.
erectquione C is a newly isolated polyprenylated phloroglucinol derivative from the whole plant of Hypericum erectum.
Polyprenylated phloroglucinol derivatives from Hypericum erectum.
Militarinone A is a novel pyridone alkaloid isolated from Paecilomyces militaris and exhibits neuritogenic activity on PC-12 cells.
Militarinone A, a neurotrophic pyridone alkaloid from Paecilomyces militaris.

5,10,15,20-Tetra(4-methoxyphenyl)porphyrin is a non-toxic molecule in the dark that induces cell death upon illumination within 30 minutes in Hep-2 carcinoma cells via a photodynamic mechanism involving apoptosis.
Expression of Fas antigen and apoptosis caused by 5,10,15, 20-tetra(4-methoxyphenyl)porphyrin (TMP) on carcinoma cells: implication for photodynamic therapy.
22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A is a newly identified 3-alkylpiperidine alkaloid isolated from the marine sponge Amphimedon sp. and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P388 murine leukemia cells.
Tetradehydrohalicyclamine A and 22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A, new cytotoxic bis-piperidine alkaloids from a marine sponge Amphimedon sp.

Riisein B is a steroidal glycoside isolated from the Brazilian telestacean octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei, with the structure 3beta-O-(4'-O-acetyl-beta-D-arabinopyranosyl)-25xi-choles tan e-3beta, 5alpha,6beta,26-tetrol-26-acetate.
New cytotoxic sterol glycosides from the octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei.

Repeated intramuscular administration of D-alpha-tocopheryl acid succinate (24 I.U./kg, three times per week for 1 month) exerts a rapid neuroprotective effect on nigrostriatal dopaminergic neurons in the early unilateral model of Parkinson's disease in rats.
Neuroprotective effect of vitamin E on the early model of Parkinson's disease in rat: behavioral and histochemical evidence.
Nodulisporic acid A is a compound with a defined pharmacophore that is integral to its potent insecticidal activity, specifically flea efficacy.
Chemical modification of nodulisporic acid A: preliminary structure-activity relationships.

Cholesterol-lysine, when used to construct a lipopeptide analog, does not induce strong T cell-dependent protective immunity against lethal HSV-1 infection in mice compared to Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine.

Lipopeptide epitopes extended by an Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine moiety increase uptake and maturation of dendritic cells through a Toll-like receptor-2 pathway and trigger a Th1-dependent protective immunity.

Hesperetin 7-O-glucuronide is a bioavailable form of hesperetin that protects against UV-A-induced oxidative stress in cultured human foreskin-derived fibroblasts (FEK4) by interacting with the external plasma membrane and evoking an adaptive response.
Hesperetin glucuronide, a photoprotective agent arising from flavonoid metabolism in human skin fibroblasts.

The mean AUC of 3'-hydroxydiclofenac after a single 150 mg oral dose of diclofenac is significantly different between healthy subjects and patients with alcoholic cirrhosis, but not between healthy subjects and chronic active hepatitis patients.
Pharmacokinetics of diclofenac sodium in chronic active hepatitis and alcoholic cirrhosis.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene (trans-B[a]P-4,5-diol) is a K-region dihydrodiol metabolite of benzo[a]pyrene that can be metabolized by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to six major products: two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:7,8-tetrahydroxy-4,5:7,8-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:9,10-tetrahydroxy-4,5:9,10-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, and two diastereomers of trans,trans-1,2:4,5-tetrahydroxy-1,2:4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.
MPP+ (the active metabolite of MPTP) at 10⁻⁶ M and 10⁻⁵ M potently and rapidly inhibits DOPAC output and stimulates dopamine (DA) release in the rat corpus striatum in a dose-dependent manner.
The mechanism of action of MPTP and MPP+ on endogenous dopamine release from the rat corpus striatum superfused in vitro.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene can be metabolically activated by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to form stable polar DNA adducts, with the trans,trans-B[a]P-4,5:7,8-bis-diols serving as metabolic intermediates in this process.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-methoxyethanamine (PCMEA) is a phencyclidine-derived designer drug that undergoes metabolism in rats to common metabolites through N-dealkylation, O-dealkylation, oxidation, and hydroxylation of the cyclohexyl ring, with all metabolites partially excreted in conjugated form.

New designer drugs N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-ethoxyethanamine (PCEEA) and N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-methoxyethanamine (PCMEA): Studies on their metabolism and toxicological detection in rat urine using gas chromatographic/mass spectrometric techniques.

2-[4-(4-Chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide (PPPA) is a novel, broad-spectrum Na(+) channel blocker that is approximately 1000 times more potent, has 2000-fold faster binding kinetics, and at least 10-fold higher state dependence than carbamazepine and lamotrigine in recombinant rat Na(v)1.2 channels and native rat dorsal root ganglion neurons.
Pharmacology of 2-[4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide: a potent, broad-spectrum state-dependent sodium channel blocker for treating pain states.

Repetitive intratesticular injection of 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline complex of oxovanadium(IV) at 7.5 mg/kg testis induces germ cell apoptosis, decreased sperm counts, and reduced testicular weights in vivo.
Bis(4,7-dimethyl and 5-dinitro-1,10-phenanthroline) sulfato-oxovanadium(IV)-mediated in vivo male germ cell apoptosis.

3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-dibenz[a,h]anthracene is a distal bisdihydrodiol metabolite of dibenz[a,h]anthracene that forms highly polar DNA adducts contributing 38% ± 12 pmol/mg DNA to adducts in Aroclor 1254-treated rat liver microsome incubations.
Characterization of highly polar DNA adducts derived from dibenz[A,H]anthracene (DBA), 3,4-dihydroxy-3,4-dihydro-DBA, and 3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-DBA.
23-COOH-24,25,26,27-tetranor D3 is an end product of 25OHD3 metabolism through the C-24 oxidation pathway in rat kidney.

23-carboxy-24,25,26,27-tetranorvitamin D3 (calcioic acid) and 24-carboxy-25,26,27-trinorvitamin D3 (cholacalcioic acid): end products of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 metabolism in rat kidney through C-24 oxidation pathway.

Quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt exerts an antagonistic effect on cadmium intoxication by counteracting cadmium-induced decreases in superoxide dismutase (SOD) and glutathione (GSH) levels in the mouse liver.
Influence of water-soluble flavonoids, quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt and morin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt, on antioxidant parameters in the subacute cadmium intoxication mouse model.

ChloraPrep, a solution containing 2% chlorhexidine gluconate and 70% isopropyl alcohol, is the most effective surgical skin-preparation solution for eliminating bacteria from the hallux and toes in the foot and ankle prior to surgery.
Efficacy of surgical preparation solutions in foot and ankle surgery.

1-(2-pyrenyl)ethanol is a benzylic alcohol derived from polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons that is efficiently activated to a mutagen in Salmonella typhimurium TA98 by hepatic cytosol from both male and female rats, with similar efficiency between sexes.
Substance-dependent sex differences in the activation of benzylic alcohols to mutagens by hepatic sulfotransferases of the rat.

After metabolic activation by rat liver microsomes, 3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydrodibenz[a,h]anthracene forms DNA adducts that are 38% of the total 14C-labelled adducts in the fraction and 25% in the bay region after incubation with calf thymus DNA.
Characterization of highly polar DNA adducts derived from dibenz[A,H]anthracene (DBA), 3,4-dihydroxy-3,4-dihydro-DBA, and 3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-DBA.

1-ethyl-6-fluoro-1,4-dihydro-7-(2-aminopropyl-amino)-4-oxo-3-quinolinecarboxylic acid (APA) is a photoproduct generated from Lomefloxacin under UV-A (310–360 nm) exposure in PBS pH 7.4, whole blood in vitro, and in the blood of rats exposed to UV-A and Lomefloxacin.
Photochemical decomposition of lomefloxacin in vitro and in vivo.

4-benzoyl-3-hydroxyfuran-2(5H)one is a lipophilic ascorbic acid analogue with antioxidant properties, as evaluated by DPPH radical assay, superoxide-anion scavenging assay, and lipid peroxidation assay.
Antioxidant properties of novel lipophilic ascorbic acid analogues.

5alpha-cyprinol, a component of grass carp bile juice, causes short-term toxicity in rats as evidenced by increased liver and kidney weights, altered blood parameters, and pathological liver and kidney lesions after oral administration for 19 days.
Short-term toxicity of grass carp bile powder, 5alpha-cyprinol and 5alpha-cyprinol sulfate in rats.

Repeated intramuscular administration of D-alpha-tocopheryl acid succinate (24 I.U./kg, three times per week for 1 month) exerts a rapid neuroprotective effect on nigrostriatal dopaminergic neurons in the early unilateral model of Parkinson's disease in rats.
Neuroprotective effect of vitamin E on the early model of Parkinson's disease in rat: behavioral and histochemical evidence.
3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydrodibenz[a,h]anthracene is metabolized by rat liver microsomes to form highly polar DNA adducts, with a DNA binding yield of 38 ± 12 pmol/mg DNA in vitro.
Characterization of highly polar DNA adducts derived from dibenz[A,H]anthracene (DBA), 3,4-dihydroxy-3,4-dihydro-DBA, and 3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-DBA.

Oral administration of 3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) at doses ≥10 mg/kg in MCMV-infected mice produces antiviral effects comparable to ganciclovir, as measured by survival and viral DNA levels in organs.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.
Phosphatoquinone B is a quinone isolated from Streptomyces sp. TA-0363 that inhibits PTP activity with an IC50 of 2.9 µM.
Phosphatoquinones A and B, novel tyrosine phosphatase inhibitors produced by Streptomyces sp.

When covalently linked to bacteriophage 75 and delivered to Staphylococcus aureus, tin(IV) chlorin e6 (SnCe6) enables selective killing of methicillin- and vancomycin-intermediate strains with low concentrations (1.5 µg/ml) and low light doses (21 J/cm2), while sparing human epithelial cells.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.

(S)-7-amino-2,3,4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[b][1,4]oxazepin-3-ol (S-7ABO) is a novel inhibitor of phosphatidylcholine-specific phospholipase C (PC-PLC) activity derived from Bacillus cereus and human umbilical vein endothelial cells.
Discovery of novel PC-PLC activity inhibitors.

N(4)-octyl-6,6-dimethyl-N(2)-(4-methylbenzyl)-1,6-dihydro-1,3,5-triazine-2,4-diamine (HM-242) is a synthetic compound with potent antimicrobial and virucidal activity, efficiently killing Gram-positive bacteria including methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus and vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus faecalis, and showing virucidal activity against enveloped viruses such as influenza virus and herpes simplex virus.
In vitro evaluation of the antimicrobial activity of HM-242, a novel antiseptic compound.

1-(4-chlorophenyl)-4,4,4-trifluoro-3-hydroxy-2-buten-1-one is a protonophore with potent in vitro and in vivo antibacterial activity against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), showing a MIC of 4 µg/ml and reduced intracellular MRSA in macrophages.
Activity of a novel protonophore against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus.
4,6,11-trihydroxy-9-propyltetracene-5,12-dione is an anthracycline anticancer compound isolated from Micromonospora sp. (BRA006) associated with the tunicate Eudistoma vannamei.
Anthracyclinones from Micromonospora sp.

2,4-bis((Z)-2-(1-(pyridin-2-yl)ethylidene)hydrazinyl)pyrimidine has been used for sensitive fluorescence-based detection of zinc ions in biological samples such as bacterial cells, mammalian cell imaging, and real samples including liquid milk, tomato juice, banana stem juice, and commercial fruit juice.
An efficient, Schiff-base derivative for selective fluorescence sensing of Zn²⁺ ions: quantum chemical calculation appended by real sample application and cell imaging study.

Heronapyrrole A is a structurally unique 2-nitropyrroloterpene produced by Streptomyces sp. (CMB-M0423) and shows significant activity against Gram-positive bacteria without adverse effects on mammalian cell lines.
Heronapyrroles A-C: farnesylated 2-nitropyrroles from an Australian marine-derived Streptomyces sp.
Spiroindimicin A is a cytotoxic bisindole alkaloid with a [5, 6] or [5, 5] spiro-ring skeleton, isolated from a deep-sea-derived Streptomyces sp. SCSIO 03032.
Indimicins A-E, Bisindole Alkaloids from the Deep-Sea-Derived Streptomyces sp. SCSIO 03032.
5-dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives, when used as 3'- and 5'-modified compounds, exhibit antimycobacterial activity and cytotoxicity against M. tuberculosis strains H37Rv and MS-115.
Inhibition of Mycobacterium tuberculosis strains H37Rv and MDR MS-115 by a new set of C5 modified pyrimidine nucleosides.

Langkocycline A1 is an angucycline antibiotic produced by Streptomyces sp. Acta 3034 from the rhizospheric soil of Clitorea sp., exhibiting biological activity against Gram-positive bacteria and moderate inhibitory activity against several human tumor cell lines.
Langkocyclines: novel angucycline antibiotics from Streptomyces sp. Acta 3034(*).

Indeno[2,1-c]quinoline is a class of conformationally-constrained compounds designed to minimize conformational flexibility across C4-C2' and improve free-energy of stabilization in complex formation with target proteins, resulting in enhanced anti-mycobacterial activity.
Conformationally-constrained indeno[2,1-c]quinolines--a new class of anti-mycobacterial agents.
5-Dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives with various substituents on the sugar moiety exhibit antimycobacterial activity depending on the length of the substituent at the C5 position.
Design and studies of novel 5-substituted alkynylpyrimidine nucleosides as potent inhibitors of mycobacteria.
Thioamycolamide E is a sulfur-containing cytotoxic cycliclipopeptidyl metabolite isolated from the actinomycete Amycolatopsis sp. 26-4.
Thioamycolamides A-E, Sulfur-Containing Cycliclipopeptides Produced by the Rare Actinomycete Amycolatopsis sp.

3-bromo-N-(5-nitrothiazol-2-yl)-4-propoxybenzamide is an analog of nitazoxanide and exhibits antibacterial activity against Mycobacterium smegmatis by repressing the nfnB gene, which encodes the nitroreductase NfnB.
Nitazoxanide Analogs Require Nitroreduction for Antimicrobial Activity in Mycobacterium smegmatis.

(E)-1-(furan-3-yl)-3-(1H-indol-3-yl)prop-2-en-1-one exhibits binding modes similar to FAS-II inhibitors such as isoniazid (INH) or Thiolactomycin against the KasA protein of Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
Indole chalcones: Design, synthesis, in vitro and in silico evaluation against Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

5-amino-6,7,8-trifluoro-1-[(1R,2S)-2-fluorocyclopropan-1-yl]-4-oxo-1,4-dihydro-quinoline-3-carboxylic acid is a 6-fluoro-1-[(1R,2S)-2-fluorocyclopropan-1-yl]-4-oxoquinoline-3-carboxylic acid derivative with a cyclopropane-fused 2-amino-8-azabicyclo[4.3.0]nonan-8-yl substituent at the C-7 position, designed for potent antibacterial activity and inhibition of bacterial DNA topoisomerase IV.

Synthesis and antibacterial activity of novel 6-fluoro-1-[(1R,2S)-2-fluorocyclopropan-1-yl]-4-oxoquinoline-3-carboxylic acids bearing cyclopropane-fused 2-amino-8-azabicyclo[4.3.0]nonan-8-yl substituents at the C-7 position.

5-dodecyloxymethyl-2'-deoxyuridine is a C-5 modified nucleoside derivative with a dodecyloxymethyl substituent that exhibits significant antitubercular activity against M. tuberculosis H37Rv and drug-resistant MS-115 strains, with a MIC of 20 µg/mL.
Inhibition of Mycobacterium tuberculosis strains H37Rv and MDR MS-115 by a new set of C5 modified pyrimidine nucleosides.
5-Dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives with various substituents on the sugar moiety exhibit antimycobacterial activity depending on the length of the substituent at the C5 position.
Inhibition of Mycobacterium tuberculosis strains H37Rv and MDR MS-115 by a new set of C5 modified pyrimidine nucleosides.
Seconeolitsine is a phenanthrene alkaloid that inhibits Streptococcus pneumoniae DNA-topoisomerase I (TopA) activity and bacterial cell growth at concentrations around 17 µM.
New alkaloid antibiotics that target the DNA topoisomerase I of Streptococcus pneumoniae.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

An octasaccharide sequence within heparin corresponds to the minimum oligosaccharide required for binding to annexin V, with a Kd of approximately 1 µM and a binding stoichiometry of 1:1 as determined by isothermal titration calorimetry.
Interaction of heparin with annexin V.

Nanoliposomal irinotecan treatment results in greater accumulation of irinotecan in intracranial tumor tissue compared to free drug, providing a significant therapeutic advantage in preclinical studies.
Pharmacokinetics, tumor accumulation and antitumor activity of nanoliposomal irinotecan following systemic treatment of intracranial tumors.

N-(deoxyadenosine-8-yl)-6-aminochrysene is a major DNA adduct formed from 6-nitrochrysene metabolism and is repaired with similar efficiency to N-(deoxyguanosin-8-yl)-6-aminochrysene in Hela cell extracts.
Adenine-DNA adduct derived from the nitroreduction of 6-nitrochrysene is more resistant to nucleotide excision repair than guanine-DNA adducts.
The formation clearance ratio of harmol glucuronide in rat liver tissue decreases as the rate of oxygen delivery decreases, with the effect being more pronounced in the fasted state.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.

Preincubation with 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyquin azoline (ACP) followed by photolysis and washing reduces the concentration of [3H]prazosin binding sites on rat brain membrane alpha 1-adrenoceptors without altering KD.
A new photoaffinity probe, 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyqu inazoline, for alpha 1-adrenoceptors.

The specificity of 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyquin azoline (ACP) for alpha 1-adrenoceptors is demonstrated by its inability to compete with [3H]dihydroalprenolol and [3H]yohimbine binding in rat brain membrane preparations.
A new photoaffinity probe, 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyqu inazoline, for alpha 1-adrenoceptors.

N'-(5-Chloro-2-oxoindolin-3-ylidene)-2-(6-nitrobenzothiazol-2-ylamino)acetohydrazide is a highly potent and selective inhibitor of human monoamine oxidase B (MAO-B), with an IC50 of 1.8 ± 0.3 nM and a selectivity index of 766.67, acting as a competitive-type reversible inhibitor.
Design, Synthesis, and Evaluation of 2-Amino-6-nitrobenzothiazole-Derived Hydrazones as MAO Inhibitors: Role of the Methylene Spacer Group.
N6-POB-dAdo is a DNA adduct formed by NNK and N 6 -[4-(3-pyridyl)-4hydroxy-1-butyl]-2′-deoxyadenosine (N6-PHB-dAdo) in rat lung DNA.
Identification of an N'-Nitrosonornicotine-Specific Deoxyadenosine Adduct in Rat Liver and Lung DNA.

[1-14C]glucose 6-phosphate is rapidly released from vanadate-treated rat liver microsomal vesicles pre-equilibrated with 0.1 mM or 1.0 mM glucose 6-phosphate when diluted in a glucose 6-phosphate-free medium, with efflux largely prevented by the anion-channel blocker DIDS.
Permeability of rat liver microsomal membrane to glucose 6-phosphate.

N'-(5-Chloro-2-oxoindolin-3-ylidene)-2-(6-nitrobenzothiazol-2-ylamino)acetohydrazide is a highly potent and selective inhibitor of monoamine oxidase B (MAO-B) with an IC50 of 1.8 ± 0.3 nM and a selectivity index of 766.67, acting as a competitive-type reversible inhibitor of MAO-B.
Design, Synthesis, and Evaluation of 2-Amino-6-nitrobenzothiazole-Derived Hydrazones as MAO Inhibitors: Role of the Methylene Spacer Group.
N-(deoxyadenosin-8-yl)-4-amino-3-chlorotoluene is a DNA adduct formed in vitro by reaction of N-hydroxy-MOCA with DNA and is also a major adduct in rat tissues after MOCA exposure.
Characterization of 4,4'-methylenebis(2-chloroaniline)--DNA adducts formed in vivo and in vitro.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene (BM 225) is a glibenclamide isostere that increases 45Ca outflow and insulin release in rat pancreatic islets at 10 and 25 µM in the presence of 8.3 mM D-glucose.
In vitro and in vivo effects of new insulin releasing agents.

In native and N-ethylmaleimide-treated rat liver microsomes, purified microsomal glutathione S-transferase, rat liver 9000g supernatant, and isolated rat hepatocytes, (2S)-S-(2-chloro-1,1,2-trifluoroethyl)glutathione is formed with 75–81% yield.
Stereochemistry of the microsomal glutathione S-transferase catalyzed addition of glutathione to chlorotrifluoroethene.

Glypondin, when administered to rats at doses of 20, 30, and 40 mg/kg for 21 days in drinking water, increases the concentration of TCA-soluble glycogen in the liver but does not alter labile or glycogen content in the myocardium or gastrocnemius muscle.
The effect of glypondin on glycogen and protein accumulation in rat organs.

4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyquin azoline (ACP) is a specific and sensitive photoprobe for alpha 1-adrenoceptors, binding with high affinity (KD = 1.05 nM) and reversibility in the dark.
A new photoaffinity probe, 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyqu inazoline, for alpha 1-adrenoceptors.

N-(2'-deoxyguanosine-8-yl)-2-phenylindole (N-OH-PhIP) undergoes acCoA-dependent metabolism by cytosolic enzymes from various rat organs in the presence of dG, producing metabolites such as PhIP, dG-C8-PhIP, G-C8-PhIP, and 5-OH-PhIP after 1 h incubation.

N-acetyltransferase-dependent activation of 2-hydroxyamino-1-methyl-6-phenylimidazo[4,5-b]pyridine: formation of 2-amino-1-methyl-6-(5-hydroxy)phenylimidazo [4,5-b]pyridine, a possible biomarker for the reactive dose of 2-amino-1-methyl-6-phenylimidazo[4,5-b]pyridine.
Photolysis of 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyquin azoline (ACP) does not reduce the concentration of beta- or alpha 2-adrenoceptors in rat brain membrane preparations.
A new photoaffinity probe, 4-amino-2-[4-(4-azidocinnamoyl)piperazino]-6,7-dimethoxyqu inazoline, for alpha 1-adrenoceptors.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
5'-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N6-substitution function as dual-acting agonists at human A1 AR and antagonists at human A3 AR.
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

Dodecyl-alpha-D-maltoside is a non-ionic detergent that stabilizes and enables crystallization of the human nuclear receptor hRARgamma ligand-binding domain by mediating a crystal contact in the coactivator binding region.
Structural role of a detergent molecule in retinoic acid nuclear receptor crystals.
The hexapeptide Arg-Glu-Leu-Leu-Lys-Met, when displayed on a filamentous phage, is recognized by autoantibodies from patient ITP-2 that target the GPIIb/IIIa complex.
Characterization of autoantigenic epitopes on platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa using random peptide libraries.
N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol enhances residual beta-galactosidase activities in fibroblasts.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

When covalently linked to bacteriophage 75 and delivered to Staphylococcus aureus, tin(IV) chlorin e6 (SnCe6) enables selective killing of methicillin- and vancomycin-intermediate strains with low concentrations (1.5 µg/ml) and low light doses (21 J/cm2), while sparing human epithelial cells.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.
5′-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N 6 -cycloalkyl or N 6 -3-halobenzyl substituents show subnanomolar affinity at hA 3 AR (K i = 0.34–0.58 nM).
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

RC-3095 acetate, a bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide (GRP) antagonist, administered subcutaneously twice daily at 10 micrograms for 4 weeks suppresses tumor growth in xenograft models of MCF-7 MIII human breast cancer in nude mice.
Inhibitory effect of bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide (GRP) antagonists RC-3950-II and RC-3095 on MCF-7 MIII human breast cancer xenografts in nude mice.

The anti-endosialin-MC-VC-PABC-MMAE antibody-drug conjugate is selectively cytotoxic to endosialin-positive cells in vitro and produces profound and durable antitumor efficacy in preclinical human tumor xenograft models of endosialin-positive sarcomas and neuroblastomas.
Anti-Endosialin Antibody-Drug Conjugate: Potential in Sarcoma and Other Malignancies.
SNIPER(ER)-110 is a novel SNIPER compound incorporating an IAP ligand with higher binding affinity, which induces degradation of ER␣, cIAP1, and XIAP more potently than SNIPER(ER)-87.
Derivatization of inhibitor of apoptosis protein (IAP) ligands yields improved inducers of estrogen receptor α degradation.

Thr-Met-Lys-Ile-Ile-Pro-Phe-Asn-Arg-Leu-Thr-Ile-Gly-Gly (TMKIIPFNRLTIGG) is a peptide ligand for Mac-1, a beta(2) integrin, and promotes robust neutrophil adhesion and spreading when incorporated into PEG hydrogels.
Integrin interactions with immobilized peptides in polyethylene glycol diacrylate hydrogels.

Fmoc-arginine-glycine-aspartic acid (Fmoc-RGD) is an integrin-binding peptide ligand that, when incorporated into a nanofibrous hydrogel with Fmoc-FF, supports the maintenance and function of primary human hepatocytes.
Peptide-based scaffolds for the culture and maintenance of primary human hepatocytes.
2'-(18)F-fluorofolic acid demonstrates high and specific uptake in folate receptor-positive KB tumors, enabling clear-cut visualization by PET.
A new 18F-labeled folic acid derivative with improved properties for the PET imaging of folate receptor-positive tumors.
3'-Azido-2',3'-dideoxyuridine-5'-triphosphate is a potent and highly selective inhibitor of human immunodeficiency virus type 1 and simian immunodeficiency virus reverse transcriptases.
Phosphorylation of 3'-azido-2',3'-dideoxyuridine and preferential inhibition of human and simian immunodeficiency virus reverse transcriptases by its 5'-triphosphate.
Ghesperidin down-regulates the assembly of apo B-containing lipoproteins by reducing cellular cholesterol ester synthesis augmented with oleate and suppresses excess apo B secretion from HepG2 cells.
Suppression of apolipoprotein B secretion from HepG2 cells by glucosyl hesperidin.

Chloromaloside A is a cytotoxic steroidal glycoside isolated from Chlorophytum malayense Ridley and characterized as neohecogenin 3-O-beta-D-glucopyranosyl(1→2)-[beta-D-xylopyranosyl(1→3)]-beta-D-glucopyranosyl(1→4)-beta-D-galactopyranoside.
Isolation and characterization of cytotoxic saponin chloromaloside A from Chlorophytum malayense.

Pentagalloylglucopyranosyl, when sulfated at positions 2 through 6, forms an allosteric inhibitor (SPGG) that binds to multiple anion-binding sites on factor XIa, contributing nearly 87% of binding energy through nonionic interactions.
Designing allosteric inhibitors of factor XIa. Lessons from the interactions of sulfated pentagalloylglucopyranosides.

In the crystal structure of hRARgamma ligand-binding domain, the dodecyl moiety of dodecyl-alpha-D-maltoside is buried in a hydrophobic channel, while its maltoside head group forms hydrogen bonds with water molecules and polar residue side chains.
Structural role of a detergent molecule in retinoic acid nuclear receptor crystals.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol (SMBD) inhibits LPS-induced production of nitric oxide and prostaglandin E2 in BV2 microglia by suppressing gene expression of inducible NO synthase (iNOS) and cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), respectively, at non-toxic concentrations.
Anti-inflammatory potential of newly synthesized 4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated BV2 microglia.
3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin functions as a fluorescent probe capable of detecting small-molecule biothiols cysteine, homocysteine, and glutathione in dual fluorescence channels.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.

(E)-4-(hept-1-enyl)-7-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)-2,3-dihydrobenzofuran-2,5-diol is a new benzyl derivative isolated from Eurotium repens and exhibits good binding affinity for human opioid or cannabinoid receptors.
Benzyl derivatives with in vitro binding affinity for human opioid and cannabinoid receptors from the fungus Eurotium repens.
2-Geranylgeranyl-1,4-dihydroxybenzene, isolated from Lactarius lignyotus, shows strong inhibitory activity on DNA, RNA, and protein synthesis in HeLa and HL-60 cell lines.
A bioactive tetraprenylphenol from Lactarius lignyotus.
Manadosterol A is a dimeric sterol isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa that exhibits inhibitory activity against the formation of the Ubc13-Uev1A complex.
Manadosterols A and B, sulfonated sterol dimers inhibiting the Ubc13-Uev1A interaction, isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa.

6-bromo-N-(1H-indazol-6-yl)quinolin-4-amine is a potent and selective cell-active chemical probe for cyclin G-associated kinase (GAK), with an IC50 of 1.4 µM in cells and improved metabolic stability in liver microsomes.
Towards the Development of an In vivo Chemical Probe for Cyclin G Associated Kinase (GAK).
(E)-4-(2-(2-naphthyl)vinyl)pyridine forms synHT dimers with high stereoselectivity upon irradiation with a 250 W high-pressure mercury lamp in acidic solution.
[2 + 2] Photodimerization of Naphthylvinylpyridines through Cation-π Interactions in Acidic Solution.
2-Hydroxysorangiadenosine is a sorangiadenosine derivative isolated from the myxobacterial producer Vitiosangium cumulatum MCy10943T, exhibiting cytotoxic and antibacterial activities.
2-Hydroxysorangiadenosine: Structure and Biosynthesis of a Myxobacterial Sesquiterpene-Nucleoside.
3-(2'-nitrovinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin functions as a fluorescent probe capable of distinguishing between Cys/Hcy and GSH by detecting these biothiols in dual fluorescence channels.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.

Alstiphyllanine B is an alkaloid isolated from Alstonia macrophylla that shows moderate antiplasmodial activity against Plasmodium falciparum and vasorelaxant activity against phenylephrine-induced contraction of isolated rat aorta.
Alstiphyllanines A-D, indole alkaloids from Alstonia macrophylla.

3,4-dihydroxyclerodan-13Z-en-15-oic acid is a clerodane diterpene isolated from Detarium microcarpum fruit pulp extract that shows both antifungal activity against Cladosporium cucumerinum and inhibition of acetylcholinesterase.
Bioactive diterpenes from the fruits of Detarium microcarpum.
4-phenyl-1-[(1-methyl-4-nitroimidazol-2-yl)carbonyl]thiosemicarbazide is a thiosemicarbazide derivative with anthelmintic activity against Rhabditis sp. nematodes.
Synthesis and Anthelmintic Activity of New Thiosemicarbazide Derivatives-A Preliminary Study.

Fiscpropionate D is a polypropionate derivative from Aspergillus fischeri FS452 that inhibits Mycobacterium tuberculosis protein tyrosine phosphatase B (MptpB) with an IC50 of 11 µM via a noncompetitive mechanism.
Polypropionate Derivatives with Mycobacterium tuberculosis Protein Tyrosine Phosphatase B Inhibitory Activities from the Deep-Sea-Derived Fungus Aspergillus fischeri FS452.

(E)-4-(hept-1-enyl)-7-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)-2,3-dihydrobenzofuran-2,5-diol is a benzyl derivative isolated from Eurotium repens that exhibits binding affinity for opioid and cannabinoid receptors in a receptor binding assay.
Benzyl derivatives with in vitro binding affinity for human opioid and cannabinoid receptors from the fungus Eurotium repens.

hypoxylonol E is a benzo[j]fluoranthene derivative isolated from Hypoxylon truncatum that exhibits antiproliferative activity against HUVECs (human umbilical vein endothelial cells) and HUAECs (human umbilical artery endothelial cells).
Hypoxylonols C-F, benzo[j]fluoranthenes from Hypoxylon truncatum.

3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin (also known as CouSeNO2/CouSNO2) functions as a fluorescent probe for distinguishing between Cys/Hcy and GSH by detecting the nucleophilic substitution of its 4-phenylselenium group with thiol groups from these biothiols.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.

3-(2'-nitro vinyl)-4-phenylselenyl coumarin enables dual fluorescence channel detection of Cys/Hcy and GSH by employing a first-step nucleophilic substitution reaction with the thiol group of each biothiol.
Theoretical Investigation of a Coumarin Fluorescent Probe for Distinguishing the Detection of Small-Molecule Biothiols.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Searching for Multi-Targeting Neurotherapeutics against Alzheimer's: Discovery of Potent AChE-MAO B Inhibitors through the Decoration of the 2H-Chromen-2-one Structural Motif.
Chrysotrinone B is an acylcyclopentenedione isolated from Hygrophorus chrysodon that exhibits antifungal activity against Fusarium verticillioides.
Antifungal acylcyclopentenediones from fruiting bodies of Hygrophorus chrysodon.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Dibutyliodotin hydride (Bu2 SnIH) is effective as a catalyst for the coupling of vinylcyclopropanes with aldehydes to produce homoallylic alcohols in the presence of hydrosilane as a hydride source.
Transition-metal-free coupling reaction of vinylcyclopropanes with aldehydes catalyzed by tin hydride.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.
5-nitrothiazole derivatives bearing a diethylmethylene malonate group can undergo reduction of the nitro group followed by cyclization to yield bicyclic 2-pyridones.
Synthesis of new potentially bioactive bicyclic 2-pyridones.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.

4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs act as inhibitors of Walker carcinosarcoma 256 cells, with inhibitory activity dependent on the length of the aliphatic side chain and substitution at the terminal positions.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.
4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs exhibit antitumor activity against Walker carcinosarcoma 256, with the nitroalkene side chain being essential for this activity.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.
Hexane-bisammonium-type compounds containing lateral phthalimide moieties have high affinity for the allosteric site of muscarinic M2 receptors.
Structure-activity relationships in a series of bisquaternary bisphthalimidine derivatives modulating the muscarinic M(2)-receptor allosterically.

6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) enhances natural host resistance against Pseudomonas aeruginosa in cyclophosphamide-treated mice in a dose-dependent manner.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
5'-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N6-substitution function as dual-acting agonists at human A1 AR and antagonists at human A3 AR.
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.
1-methyl-1 H-pyrazole-5-carboxamide derivatives inhibit the development of Haemonchus contortus larvae, with the first identified compound showing an IC50 of 0.29 µM.
Optimization of Novel 1-Methyl-1 H-Pyrazole-5-carboxamides Leads to High Potency Larval Development Inhibitors of the Barber's Pole Worm.

4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol (SMBD) inhibits LPS-induced production of nitric oxide and prostaglandin E2 in BV2 microglia by suppressing gene expression of inducible NO synthase (iNOS) and cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), respectively, at non-toxic concentrations.
Anti-inflammatory potential of newly synthesized 4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated BV2 microglia.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.

Amide derivatives of 8-[4-[[carboxymethyl]oxy]phenyl]-1,3-di-(n-propyl)xanthine act as selective A(2B) adenosine receptor antagonists, with para-substituted anilides showing high affinity (1–3 nM) and selectivity for human A(2B) receptors.
Anilide derivatives of an 8-phenylxanthine carboxylic congener are highly potent and selective antagonists at human A(2B) adenosine receptors.
Sodium 2-(1-imidazolylmethyl)-4,5-dihydrobenzo[b]thiophene-6-carboxylate is a potent and specific inhibitor of thromboxane A synthetase in vitro and in vivo.
Synthesis and thromboxane synthetase inhibitory activity of di- or tetrahydrobenzo[b]thiophenecarboxylic acid derivatives.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.
1,6-di(pyrid-2-yl)pyrene is a substrate that can be functionalized at the 2- and 2,7-positions via Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H bond activation.
The synthesis of 2- and 2,7-functionalized pyrene derivatives through Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H activation.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Diphenylphosphinylallenes serve as substrates in Rh-catalyzed intermolecular hydroalkoxylation with phenols to produce vinyl ethers in high yields and enantioselectivities.
Rhodium-catalyzed asymmetric hydroalkoxylation and hydrosulfenylation of diphenylphosphinylallenes.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.
6-hydroxy-tetrahydroquinoline serves as a scaffold for a new series of estrogen receptor ligands with high binding affinity and functional activity in MCF-7 cells.
Tetrahydroquinoline-based selective estrogen receptor modulators (SERMs).
Dibutyliodotin hydride (Bu2 SnIH) is effective as a catalyst for the coupling of vinylcyclopropanes with aldehydes to produce homoallylic alcohols in the presence of hydrosilane as a hydride source.
Transition-metal-free coupling reaction of vinylcyclopropanes with aldehydes catalyzed by tin hydride.
(S)-3-cyanophenylalanine reacts with NaN3 in the presence of H2O and Lewis acids (ZnCl2 and CdCl2) to form homochiral 3D coordination networks.
Homochiral Zn and Cd coordination polymers containing amino acid-tetrazole ligands.
Bicyclo[3.2.0]heptenone serves as a substrate for Baeyer-Villiger monooxygenase (BVMO) activity assays in fungal strains from the Ascomycota, Basidiomycota, and Zygomycota phyla.
Prevalence and specificity of Baeyer-Villiger monooxygenases in fungi.

Allyl selenocyanate is used as an allylating agent for cysteine, generating S-allyl cysteine-modified proteins that display the same cross-metathesis reactivity as those allylated with allyl chloride.
Enabling olefin metathesis on proteins: chemical methods for installation of S-allyl cysteine.

1-Phenylsulfanyl[1,2,4]triazole functions as an electrophilic sulfur transfer reagent for the organocatalytic alpha-sulfenylation of substituted piperazine-2,5-diones under mild conditions, providing excellent product yields.
Efficient Organocatalytic alpha-Sulfenylation of Substituted Piperazine-2,5-diones.
Methyltrioctylammonium trifluoroacetate functions as an ionic liquid catalyst in the lipase-catalyzed butanolysis of triolein.
Lipase-catalyzed butanolysis of triolein in ionic liquid and selective extraction of product using supercritical carbon dioxide.

2-[bis-(3,5-bis-trifluoromethyl-phenyl)trimethylsilanyloxy-methyl]-pyrrolidine acts as an organocatalyst for the asymmetric epoxidation of a,b-unsaturated aldehydes in water-alcohol solutions using hydrogen peroxide as the oxidant.
Organocatalytic asymmetric epoxidation reactions in water-alcohol solutions.

(R)-4-methyl-8-ethyl-2-phenyl-imidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-one is a highly potent and selective antagonist for human A(3) adenosine receptors, exhibiting a K(i) value of 2.3 nM and high selectivity over all other adenosine receptor subtypes.
2-Phenylimidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-ones: structure-activity relationships and characterization of potent and selective inverse agonists at Human A3 adenosine receptors.
Ferric tetramesitylporphyrin can be oxidized by m-CPBA in butyronitrile at -78°C to generate six-coordinate oxoiron(IV) tetramesitylporphyrin complexes with a halide ion as the second axial ligand.

Generation of oxoiron (IV) tetramesitylporphyrin pi-cation radical complexes by m-CPBA oxidation of ferric tetramesitylporphyrin derivatives in butyronitrile at - 78 degrees C. Evidence for the formation of six-coordinate oxoiron (IV) tetramesitylporphyrin pi-cation radical complexes FeIV = O(tmp*)X (X = Cl-, Br-), by Mössbauer and X-ray absorption spectroscopy.

3-(hydroxymethyl)benzophenone undergoes a highly efficient (Φ ∼ 0.6) intramolecular redox reaction in acidic (pH < 3) aqueous solution to form 3-formylbenzhydrol (95%) with trace amounts of the fully oxidized 3-formylbenzophenone (5%).
Formal intramolecular photoredox chemistry of meta-substituted benzophenones.
8-octanolactone can undergo enzymatic ring-opening polymerization catalyzed by CALB, resulting in oligomer formation when the enzyme is encapsulated in PS-PIAT polymersomes.
Polymersome nanoreactors for enzymatic ring-opening polymerization.
N-ferrocenylmaleimide is a redox-active label that can be coupled to the single surface cysteine mutant SCF-K344C of cytochrome P450cam for electrochemical studies.
Surface-modified mutants of cytochrome P450cam: enzymatic properties and electrochemistry.

Tetrahydropyrido [4,3-d]pyrimidine-2,4-dione serves as a core scaffold for small molecule antagonists targeting the human GnRH receptor, with analogs designed around this core achieving binding affinity below 10 nM in vitro.
Selection, synthesis, and structure-activity relationship of tetrahydropyrido[4,3-d]pyrimidine-2,4-diones as human GnRH receptor antagonists.
4-hydroxy-2-chlorocinnamic acid is a novel compound produced by cinnamate 4-hydroxylase (C4H) from a substrate analogue with a 2-chloro aromatic side chain.
Non-natural cinnamic acid derivatives as substrates of cinnamate 4-hydroxylase.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

Disodium 1-[2-(carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a low molecular weight nitric oxide donor that, when incorporated into electrospun polymer fibers, enables nitric oxide release upon immersion in aqueous solutions at physiological pH (7.4, 37°C).
Nitric oxide-releasing electrospun polymer microfibers.

2′-deoxyguanosine-5′-tetraphosphate can be efficiently incorporated into DNA during polymerase reactions when modified with four different length coumarin-PEG tags at the 5′-terminal phosphate, allowing for single-molecule discrimination of the tags by nanopore ionic current blockade.
PEG-labeled nucleotides and nanopore detection for single molecule DNA sequencing by synthesis.

At high concentrations, methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) induces aberrant HBV capsid protein assembly, favoring hexagonal arrays and promoting dissociation of metastable capsids by scavenging capsid protein and redirecting assembly into hexamer-rich structures.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid is a histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands the pool of renal progenitor cells in zebrafish embryos in a proliferation-dependent manner.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.
N-propargyloxycarbamate-glucosamine is a chemical reporter that displays unique labeling efficiencies and can be metabolically converted to enter the O-GlcNAcylation pathway.
N-Propargyloxycarbamate monosaccharides as metabolic chemical reporters of carbohydrate salvage pathways and protein glycosylation.

N-(4'-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) binds to the α-site of tryptophan synthase and its CF3 groups provide 19F NMR signals that sample the channel microenvironment at the α-β subunit interface, allowing distinction between open and closed β-subunit conformations.
Allostery and substrate channeling in the tryptophan synthase bienzyme complex: evidence for two subunit conformations and four quaternary states.

Pentagalloylglucopyranosyl, when sulfated at positions 2 through 6, forms an allosteric inhibitor (SPGG) that binds to multiple anion-binding sites on factor XIa, contributing nearly 87% of binding energy through nonionic interactions.
Designing allosteric inhibitors of factor XIa. Lessons from the interactions of sulfated pentagalloylglucopyranosides.

The pharmacokinetic profile of N-(3-(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propyl)-5-(3-chloro-4-methoxyphenyl)oxazole-4-carboxamide (PF-367) is suitable for developing GSK-3-targeted radiopharmaceuticals for the central nervous system.
Discovery of a Highly Selective Glycogen Synthase Kinase-3 Inhibitor (PF-04802367) That Modulates Tau Phosphorylation in the Brain: Translation for PET Neuroimaging.
23-COOH-24,25,26,27-tetranor D3 is an end product of 25OHD3 metabolism through the C-24 oxidation pathway in rat kidney.

23-carboxy-24,25,26,27-tetranorvitamin D3 (calcioic acid) and 24-carboxy-25,26,27-trinorvitamin D3 (cholacalcioic acid): end products of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 metabolism in rat kidney through C-24 oxidation pathway.

Nothospondin is a quassinoid isolated from Nothospondias staudtii that potently and dose-dependently inhibits the oncogenic transcription factor activator protein-1 (AP-1) at noncytotoxic concentrations.
Nothospondin, a new AP-1 inhibitory quassinoid from the Cameroonian plant Nothospondias staudtii.
1,1,3,3-tetramethyl-1,3-disiloxanediol (HOSiMe2OSiMe2OH) reacts with Ta(OEt)5 to form siloxy complexes where it acts as a bridging ligand, replacing terminal ethoxide groups.
Synthesis, structural and spectroscopic characterization, catalytic properties, and thermal transformations of new cyclic di- and trisiloxanediolato tantalum complexes.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.
14S,22-diHDHA (Maresin-L1) acts as an autacrine or paracrine factor produced by leukocytes and platelets that diminishes diabetic impairment of macrophage reparative functions.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.

1-(4-chlorophenyl)-4,4,4-trifluoro-3-hydroxy-2-buten-1-one is a protonophore with potent in vitro and in vivo antibacterial activity against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), showing a MIC of 4 µg/ml and reduced intracellular MRSA in macrophages.
Activity of a novel protonophore against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus.

Enzymatic dephosphorylation of resveratrol triphosphate to resveratrol occurs in the stratum corneum of pigskin, as indicated by the absence of the 995 cm⁻¹ resveratrol band in Raman spectra after heat treatment.
Tracking the dephosphorylation of resveratrol triphosphate in skin by confocal Raman microscopy.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid (PTBA) is a small molecule histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands embryonic kidney progenitor cells (EKPCs) in zebrafish embryos.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.

Pharmacologic inhibition of arginase with N-hydroxy-N-(1-iminoethyl)-L-ornithine protects against hepatic ischemia/reperfusion injury, as evidenced by decreased serum ALT and reduced liver injury markers in mice.
Arginase blockade protects against hepatic damage in warm ischemia-reperfusion.

3-(methoxyamino)propylamine (MAPA) is a multifunctional fluorescent linker that can be used for glycan derivatization, enabling efficient conjugation with both synthetic and natural glycans under mild conditions.
Novel Reversible Fluorescent Glycan Linker for Functional Glycomics.

(1R,1 ′ R,2R,2 ′ R)-N 1 ,N 1 ′ -(anthracene-1,8-diylbis(methylene))bis(N 2 ,N 2 -bis(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine) (R-ABDC, 4a) is an optically active dinuclear ligand designed to position a Fe(II) center at the 1- and 8-positions of an anthracene bridge, facilitating DNA intercalation and DNA-cleaving activity in the presence of FeSO 4 ⋅7H 2 O and excess sodium ascorbate.
Design and synthesis of an anthranyl bridged optically active dinuclear iron(II)-ligand and evaluation of DNA-cleaving activity.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
Concise means for accessing an advanced precursor to 1-deoxypaclitaxel.
Tomenphantin B is a cytotoxic germacranolide isolated from Elephantopus tomentosus L. and its structure and absolute stereochemistry have been determined.
Antitumor agents. 190. Absolute stereochemistry of the cytotoxic germacranolides, tomenphantins A and B, from Elephantopus tomentosus.
The anti-microtubule activity of phenylahistin is dependent on a uniplanar pseudo-three-ring structure formed by hydrogen bonding between diketopiperazine and imidazole rings.
Synthesis and biological activities of phenylahistin derivatives.
Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds are inhibitors of mitochondrial threonine dehydrogenase (TDH) that impede cell growth and induce autophagy in mouse embryonic stem (ES) cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.
A thiophene S-oxide intermediate is implicated as a possible species involved in the inactivation of CYP 2C19 and CYP 2C9 by ticlopidine and tienilic acid, respectively.
Ticlopidine as a selective mechanism-based inhibitor of human cytochrome P450 2C19.

Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds selectively inhibit TDH-mediated threonine catabolism in mouse ES cells, failing to affect the growth of HeLa cells at concentrations 300 times higher than those required to kill ES cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.
9-methoxy-9-borabicyclo[3.3.1]nonane reacts with amino acids to form boron-containing complexes that can irreversibly accumulate in melanoma cells.
Synthesis and evaluation of novel boron-containing complexes of potential use for the selective treatment of malignant melanoma.
9-methoxy-9-borabicyclo[3.3.1]nonane reacts with amino acids to form boron-containing complexes that can irreversibly accumulate in melanoma cells.
Synthesis and evaluation of novel boron-containing complexes of potential use for the selective treatment of malignant melanoma.

3-formyl-4-phenyl-1,2,5-oxadiazole N2-oxide is a derivative of 1,2,5-oxadiazole N-oxide that demonstrates higher cytotoxicity and DNA-binding affinity compared to other tested derivatives in both oxia and hypoxia.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of 1,2,5-oxadiazole N-oxide derivatives as potential hypoxic cytotoxins and DNA-binders.

Plagionicin A is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.

Phenylahistin is an anti-microtubule agent whose activity depends on the structure of its isoprenylated dehydrohistidine component, specifically the rigid, planar pseudo-three-ring structure formed by diketopiperazine and imidazole rings.
Synthesis and biological activities of phenylahistin derivatives.
Calicheamicin T binds in the minor groove of DNA and cleaves all nucleotides comparably, indicating that its cyclization kinetics do not significantly influence its cleavage selectivity.
Cleavage behavior of calicheamicin gamma 1 and calicheamicin T.

N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-methoxyethanamine (PCMEA) is a phencyclidine-derived designer drug that undergoes metabolism in rats to common metabolites through N-dealkylation, O-dealkylation, oxidation, and hydroxylation of the cyclohexyl ring, with all metabolites partially excreted in conjugated form.

New designer drugs N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-ethoxyethanamine (PCEEA) and N-(1-phenylcyclohexyl)-2-methoxyethanamine (PCMEA): Studies on their metabolism and toxicological detection in rat urine using gas chromatographic/mass spectrometric techniques.
Nodulisporic acid A is a compound with a defined pharmacophore that is integral to its potent insecticidal activity, specifically flea efficacy.
Chemical modification of nodulisporic acid A: preliminary structure-activity relationships.

N 6 -(2-hydroxy-3,4-epoxybut-1-yl)-2′-deoxyadenosine (N 6 -HEB-dA) is a modified nucleoside that, when incorporated into oligonucleotides, leads to preferential formation of exocyclic derivatives rather than DNA-DNA cross-linking or hydrolysis, even in the presence of nucleophilic agents such as L-cysteine or 2′-dG.
Site specific N6-(2-hydroxy-3,4-epoxybut-1-yl)adenine oligodeoxynucleotide adducts of 1,2,3,4-diepoxybutane: synthesis and stability at physiological pH.
Glufosinate can be separated and determined using micellar electrokinetic chromatography with laser-induced fluorescence detection after derivatization with fluorescein isothiocyanate isomer I.
Simultaneous determination of phosphorus-containing amino acid-herbicides by nonionic surfactant micellar electrokinetic chromatography with laser-induced fluorescence detection.
Benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives can be synthesized via a microwave-assisted one-pot four-component approach and exhibit cytotoxic and antimicrobial activities.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of novel benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives.

3-nitro-10-methylbenzothiazolo [3,2-a] quinolinium chloride (NBQ-91) is a water-soluble DNA binding compound with nitro-functionality that exhibits increased DNA binding affinity under N2-saturated (hypoxic) conditions due to reduction of the nitro group, resulting in covalent adduct formation with 2'-dG.
Role of the nitro functionality in the DNA binding of 3-nitro-10-methylbenzothiazolo[3,2-a]quinolinium chloride.

2-acetylpyrazine thiosemicarbazone forms complexes with gallium(III) and iron(III) that display different effects on cytotoxicity, with gallium(III) enhancing and iron(III) attenuating antiproliferative activity.
Gallium(III) and iron(III) complexes of alpha-N-heterocyclic thiosemicarbazones: synthesis, characterization, cytotoxicity, and interaction with ribonucleotide reductase.
12-epi-fischerindole U is a substrate that can be monochlorinated at an unactivated sp3 aliphatic carbon by the nonheme iron enzyme WelO5 in the welwitindolinone biosynthetic pathway.
A new family of iron-dependent halogenases acts on freestanding substrates.

(S)-7-amino-2,3,4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[b][1,4]oxazepin-3-ol (S-7ABO) is a novel inhibitor of phosphatidylcholine-specific phospholipase C (PC-PLC) activity derived from Bacillus cereus and human umbilical vein endothelial cells.
Discovery of novel PC-PLC activity inhibitors.

The R(+) isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate (5–20 mg/kg orally) causes delayed neuropathy in adult hens by inhibiting neural transaminase (NTE), with the effect associated with high NTE inhibition in brain, spinal cord, and sciatic nerve, peaking at 6–24 hours.
The R-(+)isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate causes delayed neuropathy in hens after generation of a form of inhibited neuropathy target esterase (NTE) which can be reactivated ex vivo.
5-deoxy-5-phosphono-D-arabinonate is a phosphonate analogue that shows good selectivity as a competitive inhibitor of 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase from Trypanosoma brucei.
Sugar derivatives as new 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase inhibitors selective for the parasite Trypanosoma brucei.

Oxalyl derivatives of beta-d-glucopyranosylamine act as competitive inhibitors of rabbit muscle glycogen phosphorylase (GPb) with respect to alpha-d-glucose-1-phosphate, exhibiting K(i) values of 0.2–1.4 mM for compounds 1–4.
Binding of oxalyl derivatives of beta-d-glucopyranosylamine to muscle glycogen phosphorylase b.

dl-Azi-DAP, the (2S,4R)-diastereomer of 2-(4-amino-4-carboxybutyl)aziridine-2-carboxylic acid, binds to Cys-217 of diaminopimelic acid epimerase via attack of its sulfhydryl group on the methylene of the aziridine ring.
The stereoselective synthesis of aziridine analogues of diaminopimelic acid (DAP) and their interaction with dap epimerase.
Albogrisin D is a 4H-pyrroloquinolinone alkaloid isolated from Streptomyces albogriseolus MGR072 and is a new indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase 1 inhibitor.
Characterization and Nonenzymatic Transformation of Three Types of Alkaloids from Streptomyces albogriseolus MGR072 and Discovery of Inhibitors of Indoleamine 2,3-Dioxygenase.

2-propyl methylphosphonochloridate (IMCl) reacts with chymotrypsin to form phosphonate ester adducts that produce a nearly 6 ppm downfield shift of the 31P NMR signal compared to IMN, indicating a different chemical environment upon inhibition.
Diastereomeric phosphonate ester adducts of chymotrypsin: 31P-NMR measurements.
The trans isomer of m-nitrophenyl-tetrahydro-b-carboline exhibits potent trypanocidal activity against epimastigote forms of T. cruzi and is less cytotoxic compared to other tested derivatives.

Comparative study of the trypanocidal activity of the methyl 1-nitrophenyl-1,2,3,4-9H-tetrahydro-beta-carboline-3-carboxylate derivatives and benznidazole using theoretical calculations and cyclic voltammetry.

In the brain, spinal cord, and sciatic nerve of hens, the R(+) isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate at doses of 5, 10, 15, and 20 mg/kg causes residual NTE activity of 13%, 10%, 10%, and 6% respectively, 24 hours after dosing.
The R-(+)isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate causes delayed neuropathy in hens after generation of a form of inhibited neuropathy target esterase (NTE) which can be reactivated ex vivo.

Radioiodinated 2-iodophenyl-[1-(1-methylpiperidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indol-3-yl]methanone binds selectively to brain CB1 receptors in mouse and rat brain sections, with little to no specific binding to CB1 receptor knockout mice.
Potent cannabinergic indole analogues as radioiodinatable brain imaging agents for the CB1 cannabinoid receptor.
5-deoxy-5-phosphono-d-arabinonate is a potent inhibitor of T. brucei 6PGDH and exhibits high selectivity for the parasite enzyme over the sheep liver enzyme.
Sugar derivatives as new 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase inhibitors selective for the parasite Trypanosoma brucei.

Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate can undergo enantioselective benzylic hydroxylation by P450 BM3, yielding products with enantiomeric excesses (ee) ranging from 16% to 76% depending on the enzyme variant and reaction conditions.
P450 BM3-Catalyzed Regio- and Stereoselective Hydroxylation Aiming at the Synthesis of Phthalides and Isocoumarins.
2-(4-amino-4-carboxybutyl)aziridine-2-carboxylic acid (ll-azi-DAP and dl-azi-DAP) are irreversible inhibitors of diaminopimelic acid epimerase.
The stereoselective synthesis of aziridine analogues of diaminopimelic acid (DAP) and their interaction with dap epimerase.
Thiophen-2-yl-1,2,4-oxadiazoles are inhibitors of the mycobacterial regulator EthR, acting as 10-fold ethionamide activity boosters.
Ethionamide boosters. 2. Combining bioisosteric replacement and structure-based drug design to solve pharmacokinetic issues in a series of potent 1,2,4-oxadiazole EthR inhibitors.
3-pentyn-2-ol is oxidized by a purified NAD(+)-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase from Nocardia fusca AKU 2123 in a (S)-specific manner, catalyzing stereoinversion to produce (R)-3-pentyn-2-ol.
NAD(+)-dependent (S)-specific secondary alcohol dehydrogenase involved in stereoinversion of 3-pentyn-2-ol catalyzed by Nocardia fusca AKU 2123.

3-(3-methyl-2,5-dioxo-3-phenylpyrrolidin-1-yl)propanamide derivatives (compounds 5-17) were screened for anticonvulsant activity, with some compounds showing activity and others being non-neurotoxic in acute neurological toxicity tests in mice.
Primary amino acid derivatives: compounds with anticonvulsant and neuropathic pain protection activities.
12-epi-fischerindole U is a substrate for WelO5, which catalyzes its regio- and stereoselective chlorination with a turnover rate five to tenfold higher than that of PhyH-type halogenases.
A new family of iron-dependent halogenases acts on freestanding substrates.

(RS)-2-amino-3-(2-(2-carboxyethyl)-5-chloro-4-nitrophenyl)propionic acid is the most active compound among tested compounds in modulating AMPA receptors expressed in Xenopus laevis oocytes, as determined by two-electrode voltage clamp electrophysiology.
Heteroaryl analogues of AMPA. 2. Synthesis, absolute stereochemistry, photochemistry, and structure-activity relationships.

(1S)-1-(trans-4-{[(4-trifluoromethoxyphenyl)sulfonyl]amino}cyclohexyl)-2-[(3S)-3-fluoropyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-oxoethanaminium chloride (MRL-A) is a potent and selective DPP-IV inhibitor with good pharmacokinetic properties and significant activity in an oral glucose tolerance test in lean mice.

(2S,3S)-3-Amino-4-(3,3-difluoropyrrolidin-1-yl)-N,N-dimethyl-4-oxo-2-(4-[1,2,4]triazolo[1,5-a]-pyridin-6-ylphenyl)butanamide: a selective alpha-amino amide dipeptidyl peptidase IV inhibitor for the treatment of type 2 diabetes.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.
Cholest-8(14)-en-3-ol is formed in significant amounts from cholesta-7,14-dien-3-ol under anaerobic incubation with a 10,000 x g supernatant fraction of rat liver homogenate.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.
3-thienylalanine is identified as an especially good substrate for phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase (PheRS) based on computational predictions using the HierDock protocol.
Virtual screening for binding of phenylalanine analogues to phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase.

[5,6-3H]arachidonic acid can be enzymically converted to PGI2 by swine aortic microsomes, with tritium loss at the C-6 position, and the rate of tritium release can be used to monitor the enzymatic rearrangement.
Synthesis of [5,6-3H]arachidonic acid and its use in development of a sensitive assay for prostacyclin synthetase.

In porcine gonadal P450arom, efficient estrogen synthesis is more dependent on P450 reductase levels than in the placental isozyme, resulting in higher release of 19-oxoandrostenedione under sub-optimal P450 reductase support.
Porcine gonadal and placental isozymes of aromatase cytochrome P450: sub-cellular distribution and support by NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase.
Cholest-8(14)-en-3-ol can be formed from cholest-8,14-dien-3-ol under anaerobic conditions during incubation with rat liver homogenate preparations.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.
Substituted 3,4-diphenyl-1,3-thiazols represent a new class of potent monoamine transporter inhibitors.
Discovery of substituted 3,4-diphenyl-thiazoles as a novel class of monoamine transporter inhibitors through 3-D pharmacophore search using a new pharmacophore model derived from mazindol.

3 beta-(p-chlorophenyl)tropan-2 beta-carboxylic acid 2-[p-(bromoacetamido)phenyl]ethyl ester is a potentially irreversible ligand for the cocaine receptor site on the dopamine transporter and exhibits the highest apparent potency among tested compounds in wash-resistant inhibition of [3H]WIN 35,428 binding.
Probes for the cocaine receptor. Potentially irreversible ligands for the dopamine transporter.

Single or double substitutions involving F295L, F297I, and Y337A in acetylcholinesterase enhance the reactivation rate of S P -phosphonylated acetylcholinesterase mutants with bulky methylphosphonates such as 3,3-dimethylbutyl methylphosphonate.
Mutant cholinesterases possessing enhanced capacity for reactivation of their phosphonylated conjugates.

3-quinuclidinyl 4-bromobenzilate is a muscarinic cholinergic receptor ligand whose affinity is not significantly altered by replacing a phenyl ring with 2-furyl, 3-furyl, 2-thienyl, or 3-thienyl moieties.
Synthesis and receptor affinities of new 3-quinuclidinyl alpha-heteroaryl-alpha-aryl-alpha-hydroxyacetates.

Dibenzo[c,h]cinnoline analogues are aza analogues of benzo[i]phenanthridines and exhibit more potent topoisomerase I-targeting activity and cytotoxicity compared to their benzo[i]phenanthridine counterparts.
Substituted dibenzo[c,h]cinnolines: topoisomerase I-targeting anticancer agents.

1-(2-pyrenyl)ethanol is a benzylic alcohol derived from polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons that is efficiently activated to a mutagen in Salmonella typhimurium TA98 by hepatic cytosol from both male and female rats, with similar efficiency between sexes.
Substance-dependent sex differences in the activation of benzylic alcohols to mutagens by hepatic sulfotransferases of the rat.
12-iodo-trans-9-octadecenoic acid does not cause inactivation of soybean lipoxygenase 1.
Inactivation of soybean lipoxygenase 1 by 12-iodo-cis-9-octadecenoic acid.
12-iodo-trans-9-octadecenoic acid does not cause inactivation of soybean lipoxygenase 1.
Inactivation of soybean lipoxygenase 1 by 12-iodo-cis-9-octadecenoic acid.

(+)-bornyl p-coumarate is an antibacterial agent isolated from the crude chloroform bark extract of Piper caninum and exhibits activity against Gram-positive bacteria such as Bacillus cereus, Staphylococcus aureus, and Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Antibacterial hydroxycinnamic esters from Piper caninum from Paluma, north Queensland, Australia. The crystal and molecular structure of (+)-bornyl coumarate.
8-(1-decynyl)benzolactam-V8 is a PKC modulator that exhibits improved potency and selectivity for classical PKC isozymes.
Synthesis and modeling study of (2S,5R,6R)- and (2S,5R,6S)-6-hydroxy- 8-(1-decynyl)-benzolactam-V8 as protein kinase C modulators.

Oligonucleotides modified with N,N-diethylethylenediamine can inhibit the expression of a reporter plasmid in Xenopus oocytes when a triplex is preformed prior to injection, but unmodified oligonucleotides do not achieve this effect.
Understanding oligonucleotide-mediated inhibition of gene expression in Xenopus laevis oocytes.

The C-terminally fluorinated peptide desGly10-LH-RH-2,2,2-trifluoroethylamide is about nine times more effective than LH-RH in releasing luteinizing hormone (LH) in immature male rats over a prolonged period.
Polyfluoroalkylamine derivatives of luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone.

5alpha, 14beta-cholest-7-en-3beta-ol can be enzymically converted in rat liver homogenate under aerobic conditions to produce 5alpha-cholest-7-en-3beta-ol, 5alpha-cholest-8(14)-en-3beta-ol, and cholesterol, indicating inversion of the C/D cis ring junction.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.
In rats, the rate of transport of [14C]-D-lactate into liver in vivo is 1.8 µmol·min⁻¹·g⁻¹, with a volume of distribution of 0.71 ± 0.07 mL/g and an extraction of 91% ± 9% for unidirectional influx.
Kinetics of lactate transport into rat liver in vivo.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
