A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Miniaturization of a liquid membrane sensor for the determination of bile acids.

Iodide-2-thiouracil binary clusters show that low energy electron attachment leads to the formation of anion states with both dipole bound and valence bound character, with the dipole bound state decaying rapidly (~50 fs) after excitation near the vertical detachment energy.
Electron attachment dynamics following UV excitation of iodide-2-thiouracil complexes.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
Reversible photoligation of DNA via 5-vinyldeoxyuridine.

1,3-bis(N'-benzoylthioureido)benzene functions as an ionophore in PVC membrane ion-selective electrodes for Pb(II) ion detection, providing a Nernstian response in the concentration range 4.0×10⁻⁶ to 1.0×10⁻⁲ M with a slope of 31.5 ± 1.6 mV/dec, a response time of t90% = 14 s, and operational stability for at least 10 weeks.
Lead(II) ion selective electrodes with PVC membranes based on two bis-thioureas as ionophores: 1,3-bis(N'-benzoylthioureido)benzene and 1,3-bis(N'-furoylthioureido)benzene.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

S9947 is a novel Kv1.5 channel blocker that inhibits cloned hKv1.5 channels in Xenopus oocytes and CHO cells, and blocks I Kur currents in human and rat cardiomyocytes in a concentration-dependent manner.
Characterization of a novel Kv1.5 channel blocker in Xenopus oocytes, CHO cells, human and rat cardiomyocytes.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.

Cyrtominetin inhibits the hemolytic activity of α-hemolysin (α-HL) by binding to the 'Loop' region of the protein, blocking the conformational transition from monomer to oligomer and thereby inhibiting protein activity, as shown by molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations.
Molecular insight into the inhibition mechanism of cyrtominetin to α-hemolysin by molecular dynamics simulation.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
The 6-O-pivaloyl group of methyl 2,6-di-O-pivaloyl-alpha-D-glucopyranoside is selectively removed by esterase II from rabbit serum.
Partial purification of esterases from rabbit serum and their use in regioselective deacylations of sugars.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.
Isocoumarin phosphorus analogues (phosphaisocoumarins) can act as reversible competitive inhibitors of pancreatic cholesterol esterase (CEase), with some compounds showing potent inhibitory activity.
Phosphaisocoumarins as a new class of potent inhibitors for pancreatic cholesterol esterase.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

Methyl 3-methyl-2H-azirine-2-carboxylate (MMAC) is an aliphatic 2H-azirine compound whose structure and photochemical behavior have been investigated using matrix-isolation IR spectroscopy and DFT calculations.
Methyl 3-methyl-2H-azirine-2-carboxylate photochemistry studied by matrix-isolation FTIR and DFT calculations.

Bis-chelated gold(I) diphosphine complexes, such as [Au(d2pypp)2]Cl, selectively induce apoptosis in breast cancer cells at submicromolar concentrations by targeting mitochondria and inhibiting the thioredoxin/thioredoxin reductase system.
A gold(I) phosphine complex selectively induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells: implications for anticancer therapeutics targeted to mitochondria.

2-(butyloxy)-4-[4-(methylsulfonyl)phenyl]-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyrimidine is a potent and selective cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitor with a favorable pharmacokinetic profile, high brain penetration, and good efficacy in rat models of hypersensitivity.
Identification of [4-[4-(methylsulfonyl)phenyl]-6-(trifluoromethyl)-2-pyrimidinyl] amines and ethers as potent and selective cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitors.
Pyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives can act as GPR119 agonists and have been optimized for anti-type 2 diabetes mellitus activity.
Optimisation of novel 4, 8-disubstituted dihydropyrimido[5,4-b][1,4]oxazine derivatives as potent GPR 119 agonists.

Aristolactam BIII is a natural aristolactam that can be synthesized efficiently using a one-pot Suzuki-Miyaura coupling/aldol condensation cascade reaction of isoindolin-1-one with 2-formylphenylboronic acid.
Total synthesis of aristolactams via a one-pot suzuki-miyaura coupling/aldol condensation cascade reaction.

2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide exhibits significantly greater antileishmanial activity than sodium antimony gluconate in reducing parasite load in the spleen and liver of hamster models at much lower concentrations.
Synthesis of a novel quinoline derivative, 2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide--a potential antileishmanial agent.
Schiff bases of valproylglycine hydrazide exhibit strong antiangiogenic and anticancer activities in developing zebrafish embryos.
Synthesis and biological activity of Schiff base series of valproyl, N-valproyl glycinyl, and N-valproyl-4-aminobenzoyl hydrazide derivatives.

In the lipids of both tumor and muscle tissues, radioactivity from cis-9-[1-14C]octadecenol incorporation occurs predominantly in the acyl moieties of diradylglycerophosphocholines, diradylglycerophosphoethanolamines, and triradylglycerols, rather than in the alkyl or alk-1-enyl moieties.
Radioactive labelling of lipids in rat neurosarcoma by intravenous injection of [1-14C]-octadecenol.

Quercetin-5',8-disulfonate is a synthetic sulfated derivative of quercetin that, when administered orally at 100, 200, and 500 mg/kg bw prior to acute CCl4 oxidative damage in mice, attenuates serum ALT, AST, and LDH activities and hepatic malondialdehyde levels, and suppresses CCl4-induced depletion of glutathione peroxidase and total superoxide dismutase.
Protective effects of quercetin and quercetin-5',8-disulfonate against carbon tetrachloride-caused oxidative liver injury in mice.

Repetitive intratesticular injection of 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline complex of oxovanadium(IV) at 7.5 mg/kg testis induces germ cell apoptosis, decreased sperm counts, and reduced testicular weights in vivo.
Bis(4,7-dimethyl and 5-dinitro-1,10-phenanthroline) sulfato-oxovanadium(IV)-mediated in vivo male germ cell apoptosis.

3-amino-N-(4-chlorobenzyl)-6-(3-methoxyphenyl)thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide (SKLB703) is a small molecule that induces apoptosis in human hepatocellular carcinoma cells via the mitochondrial apoptotic pathway and inhibits tumor growth.
Small molecular anticancer agent SKLB703 induces apoptosis in human hepatocellular carcinoma cells via the mitochondrial apoptotic pathway invitro and inhibits tumor growth invivo.

(4S)-4-(3-fluoro-4-fluorophenyl)-L-glutamate (18F-FSPG, or BAY 94-9392) is a PET tracer designed to assess system xC transporter activity and can visualize tumors in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma.
(4S)-4-(3-18F-fluoropropyl)-L-glutamate for imaging of xC transporter activity in hepatocellular carcinoma using PET: preclinical and exploratory clinical studies.
In human bladder cancer T24 cells, 5, 10, 15, 20-tetrakis (5-(pyrrolidin-1-yl) pentyl) porphin (TPPP) shows low dark toxicity and high phototoxicity under photodynamic therapy.
Antitumor activity evaluation of meso-tetra (pyrrolidine substituted) pentylporphin-mediated photodynamic therapy in vitro and in vivo.

3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionate is a catabolic product of phenylalanine metabolism by Clostridium species and is significantly higher in urine of ASDs children compared with age-matched controls.
Urinary 3-(3-Hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionic Acid, 3-Hydroxyphenylacetic Acid, and 3-Hydroxyhippuric Acid Are Elevated in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorders.
Tyrosine-glutamate urea, when radioiodinated, serves as a PSMA inhibitor with high affinity and selective binding to PSMA-positive cells.
A series of halogenated heterodimeric inhibitors of prostate specific membrane antigen (PSMA) as radiolabeled probes for targeting prostate cancer.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene (trans-B[a]P-4,5-diol) is a K-region dihydrodiol metabolite of benzo[a]pyrene that can be metabolized by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to six major products: two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:7,8-tetrahydroxy-4,5:7,8-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:9,10-tetrahydroxy-4,5:9,10-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, and two diastereomers of trans,trans-1,2:4,5-tetrahydroxy-1,2:4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

5-(3-[ 18 F]-fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan ([ 18 F]-L-FPTP) is a [ 18 F]-labeled 5-hydroxy-L-tryptophan tracer developed for clinical amino acid PET imaging, primarily transported by LAT2 in tumor cells, and shows high tumor uptake in model mice.
Radiosynthesis and biological evaluation of 5-(3-[18F]fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan for tumor PET imaging.

1-(biphenyl-4-ylmethyl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline-6,7-diol (EDL-155) is selectively cytotoxic to C6 rat glioma cells in vitro with an EC50 of 1.5 µmol/l, compared to 27.4 µmol/l in cultured neonatal rat astrocytes.
Effects, in an in-vivo model system, of 1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline on glioma.

In HepG2 cells, 3-amino-N-(4-chlorobenzyl)-6-(3-methoxyphenyl)thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide (SKLB703) induces apoptosis via the mitochondrial pathway, involving upregulation of Bax, downregulation of Bcl-2, cytochrome c release, and changes in mitochondrial transmembrane potential.
Small molecular anticancer agent SKLB703 induces apoptosis in human hepatocellular carcinoma cells via the mitochondrial apoptotic pathway invitro and inhibits tumor growth invivo.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene can be metabolically activated by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to form stable polar DNA adducts, with the trans,trans-B[a]P-4,5:7,8-bis-diols serving as metabolic intermediates in this process.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.
When administered intravenously to rats bearing neurosarcoma, cis-9-[1-14C]octadecenol is preferentially incorporated into tumor tissue compared to muscle tissue.
Radioactive labelling of lipids in rat neurosarcoma by intravenous injection of [1-14C]-octadecenol.

3-amino-N-(4-chlorobenzyl)-6-(3-methoxyphenyl)thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide (SKLB703) induces apoptosis and inhibits proliferation in human cancer cell lines, with HepG2 cells being the most sensitive.
Small molecular anticancer agent SKLB703 induces apoptosis in human hepatocellular carcinoma cells via the mitochondrial apoptotic pathway invitro and inhibits tumor growth invivo.

The induction of apoptosis by 4-[3-(1-heptyl-4,4-dimethyl-2-oxo-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroquinolin-6-yl)-3-oxo-E-propenyl] benzoic acid (AGN193198) is caspase-mediated and independent of retinoic acid receptors (RARs) and retinoid X receptors (RXRs).
A retinoid-related molecule that does not bind to classical retinoid receptors potently induces apoptosis in human prostate cancer cells through rapid caspase activation.

Combined treatment with 5, 10, 15, 20-tetrakis (5-(pyrrolidin-1-yl) pentyl) porphin (TPPP) and 100 J/cm^2 (650 nm, 180 mW/cm^2) laser irradiation significantly inhibits tumor growth and induces tumor cell shrinkage and necrosis in T24 tumor nude mice.
Antitumor activity evaluation of meso-tetra (pyrrolidine substituted) pentylporphin-mediated photodynamic therapy in vitro and in vivo.
Arg-Gly-Asp-D-Phe-Cys (RGD) peptides can be conjugated to gold nanorods via PAMAM dendrimer modification to enable tumor targeting in photothermal therapy.
Dendrimer-Templated Ultrasmall and Multifunctional Photothermal Agents for Efficient Tumor Ablation.

Temocapril hydrochloride is an angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor that markedly decreases tumor tissue blood flow in the LY80 tumor subline of Yoshida sarcoma in rats, reducing flow from above 20 ml/min/100 g to less than 3 ml/min/100 g.
Tumor-selective blood flow decrease induced by an angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor, temocapril hydrochloride.

[11C]-Busulfan rapidly crosses the blood-brain barrier in vivo, with brain radioactivity peaking in the cortex and white matter at a ratio of 1.25 at 3 min and declining to approximately 1 after 30 min in cynomolgus monkeys.
In vivo distribution of [11C]-busulfan in cynomolgus monkey and in the brain of a human patient.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
A pig microsomal enzyme catalyzes the 7a-hydroxylation of 3a-hydroxy-5-cholestenoic acid, but not cholesterol.
7 alpha-hydroxylation of 26-hydroxycholesterol, 3 beta-hydroxy-5-cholestenoic acid and 3 beta-hydroxy-5-cholenoic acid by cytochrome P-450 in pig liver microsomes.

In the lipids of both tumor and muscle tissues, radioactivity from cis-9-[1-14C]octadecenol incorporation occurs predominantly in the acyl moieties of diradylglycerophosphocholines, diradylglycerophosphoethanolamines, and triradylglycerols, rather than in the alkyl or alk-1-enyl moieties.
Radioactive labelling of lipids in rat neurosarcoma by intravenous injection of [1-14C]-octadecenol.

4-(3,7,11,15-tetramethyl-6,10,14-hexadecatrienoyl)morpholine (E-0712) protects against liver injury induced by D-galactosamine and carbon tetrachloride in rats, as evidenced by reduced SGPT values and prothrombin time prolongation.
Protective effect of 4-(3,7,11,15-tetramethyl-6,10,14-hexadecatrienoyl)morpholine on liver injury induced by hepatotoxins in rats.
The hydroxystyryl-acrylonitrile compound LS104 inhibits proliferation and induces potent cytotoxic effects in FLT3-expressing leukemic cells in vitro.
The kinase inhibitor LS104 induces apoptosis, enhances cytotoxic effects of chemotherapeutic drugs and is targeting the receptor tyrosine kinase FLT3 in acute myeloid leukemia.

24 alpha-ethyl-5 beta-cholestan-3 alpha-ol is a substrate for 7 alpha-hydroxylation by NADPH-dependent cholesterol-and cholestanol-7 alpha-hydroxylase enzymes, producing minute amounts of 7 beta-hydroxy derivatives.
7-Hydroxylation of 3-oxygenated C27-, C28-, and C29-steroids in rat liver 18,000 g supernate.

UniPR1331 is a protein-protein interaction inhibitor that blocks Eph-ephrin signaling and mitigates intestinal and lung injury, reduces gut edema formation, and counteracts increases in pro-inflammatory cytokine levels following mesenteric ischemia-reperfusion in mice.
Protection by the Eph-Ephrin System Against Mesenteric Ischemia-Reperfusion Injury.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.

Cholesterol-lysine, when used to construct a lipopeptide analog, does not induce strong T cell-dependent protective immunity against lethal HSV-1 infection in mice compared to Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine.

Lipopeptide epitopes extended by an Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine moiety increase uptake and maturation of dendritic cells through a Toll-like receptor-2 pathway and trigger a Th1-dependent protective immunity.

The induction of apoptosis by 4-[3-(1-heptyl-4,4-dimethyl-2-oxo-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroquinolin-6-yl)-3-oxo-E-propenyl] benzoic acid (AGN193198) is caspase-mediated and independent of retinoic acid receptors (RARs) and retinoid X receptors (RXRs).
A retinoid-related molecule that does not bind to classical retinoid receptors potently induces apoptosis in human prostate cancer cells through rapid caspase activation.

5alpha-cyprinol, a component of grass carp bile juice, causes short-term toxicity in rats as evidenced by increased liver and kidney weights, altered blood parameters, and pathological liver and kidney lesions after oral administration for 19 days.
Short-term toxicity of grass carp bile powder, 5alpha-cyprinol and 5alpha-cyprinol sulfate in rats.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
Synthesis of trimethylhydroquinone derivatives as anti-allergic agents with anti-oxidative actions.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.

Temocapril hydrochloride is an angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor that markedly decreases tumor tissue blood flow in the LY80 tumor subline of Yoshida sarcoma in rats, reducing flow from above 20 ml/min/100 g to less than 3 ml/min/100 g.
Tumor-selective blood flow decrease induced by an angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor, temocapril hydrochloride.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).
23-COOH-24,25,26,27-tetranor D3 is an end product of 25OHD3 metabolism through the C-24 oxidation pathway in rat kidney.

23-carboxy-24,25,26,27-tetranorvitamin D3 (calcioic acid) and 24-carboxy-25,26,27-trinorvitamin D3 (cholacalcioic acid): end products of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 metabolism in rat kidney through C-24 oxidation pathway.
Pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyrazole derivatives act as first-in-class inhibitors of p53-MDM2 interaction and the NF-kappaB pathway.
Double-edged swords as cancer therapeutics: novel, orally active, small molecules simultaneously inhibit p53-MDM2 interaction and the NF-κB pathway.
EED226 is a potent inhibitor of PRC2 activity that binds to the H3K27me3 pocket of EED and induces regression of tumor xenograft in vivo.
Discovery of First-in-Class, Potent, and Orally Bioavailable Embryonic Ectoderm Development (EED) Inhibitor with Robust Anticancer Efficacy.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

The (S)-enantiomer of 5-amino-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole exhibits moderate antagonist activity at the 5-HT 6 receptor, while many (R)-enantiomer derivatives function as full agonists with EC50 values of 1 nM or less.
Discovery of 5-arylsulfonamido-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole derivatives as potent, selective 5-HT6 receptor agonists and antagonists.
Hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic acid act as highly specific and potent reversible inactivators of copper-dependent amine oxidases, with greater affinity for plasma amine oxidase.
Inhibition of copper-dependent amine oxidases by some hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic and pyrrol-1-ylphenylacetic acids.

4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs act as inhibitors of Walker carcinosarcoma 256 cells, with inhibitory activity dependent on the length of the aliphatic side chain and substitution at the terminal positions.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.
4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs exhibit antitumor activity against Walker carcinosarcoma 256, with the nitroalkene side chain being essential for this activity.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Discovery of 5-arylsulfonamido-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole derivatives as potent, selective 5-HT6 receptor agonists and antagonists.
5'-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N6-substitution function as dual-acting agonists at human A1 AR and antagonists at human A3 AR.
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.

N1-substituted derivatives of cis-piperazine-2,3-dicarboxylic acid act as competitive antagonists at the N-methyl-D-aspartate receptor (NMDAR), displaying improved relative affinity for GluN2C and GluN2D over GluN2A and GluN2B.
Synthesis and pharmacology of N1-substituted piperazine-2,3-dicarboxylic acid derivatives acting as NMDA receptor antagonists.
Sodium 2-(1-imidazolylmethyl)-4,5-dihydrobenzo[b]thiophene-6-carboxylate is a potent and specific inhibitor of thromboxane A synthetase in vitro and in vivo.
Synthesis and thromboxane synthetase inhibitory activity of di- or tetrahydrobenzo[b]thiophenecarboxylic acid derivatives.
Imidazolyl nitrones are a class of compounds evaluated as neuroprotective agents in vivo, with their neuroprotective profile influenced by ClogP and HOMO energy level.
Synthesis, structure, and neuroprotective properties of novel imidazolyl nitrones.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.
Tasuldine (2-[(3-pyridinylmethyl)thio]pyrimidine) increases tracheobronchial secretion in mice, as indicated by increased excretion of phenol red into the trachea.
2-[(3-Pyridinylmethyl)thio]pyrimidine derivatives: new bronchosecretolytic agents.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.

3-aza-9-hydroxyfluoren-9(R)-ylcarbonyl-l-prolyl-2-aminomethyl-5-chlorobenzylamide is a potent thrombin inhibitor with a K(i) of 0.40 nM and 2x APTT of 0.18 µM, excellent pharmacokinetic properties (F = 55%, T1/2 = 14 h in dogs), and complete efficacy in inhibiting FeCl3-induced vessel occlusions in a rat in vivo thrombosis model at 10 µg/kg/min intravenous infusion.
9-hydroxyazafluorenes and their use in thrombin inhibitors.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.

In the lipids of both tumor and muscle tissues, radioactivity from cis-9-[1-14C]octadecenol incorporation occurs predominantly in the acyl moieties of diradylglycerophosphocholines, diradylglycerophosphoethanolamines, and triradylglycerols, rather than in the alkyl or alk-1-enyl moieties.
Radioactive labelling of lipids in rat neurosarcoma by intravenous injection of [1-14C]-octadecenol.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) inhibits apoptosis and reduces infarct size in myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury by activating the PI3K/Akt pathway and reducing inflammation.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) reduces rat myocardial apoptosis against ischemia and reperfusion injury by activation of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/Akt signaling and anti-inflammatory action in vivo.

1,3-dihydro-7,8-dimethyl-2H- imidazo[4,5-b]quinolin-2-one (BMY 20844) is a potent inhibitor of cAMP hydrolysis and platelet aggregation, with favorable hemodynamic properties, and was selected for toxicological and clinical evaluation based on its in vitro platelet inhibitory activity and efficacy in animal models of thrombosis.
Pharmacology of a potent, new antithrombotic agent, 1,3-dihydro-7,8-dimethyl-2H-imidazo[4,5-b]quinolin-2-one (BMY-20844).

[5-oxo-5,6-dihydroindolo-(1,2-a)quinazolin-7-yl]acetic acid (IQA) is a CK2 inhibitor structurally unrelated to previously described CK2 inhibitors and is superior to TBB, emodin, and apigenin in terms of selectivity and efficiency both in vitro and in vivo.
Biochemical and three-dimensional-structural study of the specific inhibition of protein kinase CK2 by [5-oxo-5,6-dihydroindolo-(1,2-a)quinazolin-7-yl]acetic acid (IQA).

A single intraperitoneal dose of 60 mg/kg 5-(4-chlorophenyl)-1-(2,4-dichlorophenyl)-3-hexyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole (LH-21) suppresses overnight food intake and body weight gain in both wild-type and CB1 receptor knockout mice.
Pharmacological evaluation of LH-21, a newly discovered molecule that binds to cannabinoid CB1 receptor.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.
Topically applied liposome-encapsulated copper palmitate provides significant protection against both Photofrin- and aminolevulinic acid-induced protoporphyrin IX photosensitivity in animal skin.
Topical use of liposomal copper palmitate formulation blocks porphyrin-induced photosensitivity in rats.

1,3-dihydro-7,8-dimethyl-2H- imidazo[4,5-b]quinolin-2-one (BMY 20844) is a potent inhibitor of cAMP hydrolysis and platelet aggregation, with favorable hemodynamic properties, and was selected for toxicological and clinical evaluation based on its in vitro platelet inhibitory activity and efficacy in animal models of thrombosis.
1,3-Dihydro-2H-imidazo[4,5-b]quinolin-2-ones--inhibitors of blood platelet cAMP phosphodiesterase and induced aggregation.
EED226 is a potent inhibitor of PRC2 activity that binds to the H3K27me3 pocket of EED and induces regression of tumor xenograft in vivo.
Discovery of First-in-Class, Potent, and Orally Bioavailable Embryonic Ectoderm Development (EED) Inhibitor with Robust Anticancer Efficacy.
4-amino-3-phenylquinolin-2(1H)-one is a potent ligand at the NMDA receptor glycine site, with high binding affinity and in vivo activity in the DBA/2 mouse audiogenic seizure test.
4-substituted-3-phenylquinolin-2(1H)-ones: acidic and nonacidic glycine site N-methyl-D-aspartate antagonists with in vivo activity.

3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide (MPCA) induces dose-dependent antinociception in the neurogenic and inflammatory phases of the formalin test in mice, with no effect on locomotion or motor performance in the rotarod test.
3-Methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception.

(E)-4-(4-[(5-methyl-2-phenyl-1, 3-oxazol-4-yl)methoxy]benzyloxyimino)-4-phenylbutyric acid (TAK-559) exhibits marked glucose and lipid lowering activity in KKA(y) mice without significant body weight gain and demonstrates favorable pharmacokinetic properties with good absorption and duration.
Studies on non-thiazolidinedione antidiabetic agents. 2. Novel oxyiminoalkanoic acid derivatives as potent glucose and lipid lowering agents.
Uridine monophosphorus, when administered as a conjugate targeted to hepatocytes, protects hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
Targeted protection of hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
3-[2-{[(3-methyl-1-phenylbutyl)amino]carbonyl}-4-(phenoxymethyl)phenyl]propanoic acid is a potent and selective EP3 antagonist with a higher in vivo potency (F = 3.9%) than its analog 1.
3-(2-Aminocarbonylphenyl)propanoic acid analogs as potent and selective EP3 receptor antagonists. part 2: optimization of the side chains to improve in vitro and in vivo potencies.
NGB 2904 is a novel D(3)-selective dopamine receptor antagonist that attenuates cocaine reward and relapse in preclinical models of addiction.
The novel dopamine D3 receptor antagonist NGB 2904 inhibits cocaine's rewarding effects and cocaine-induced reinstatement of drug-seeking behavior in rats.

Zinc L-carnosine administration in gastric mucosa increases HSP72 expression, with relative densities of 109.2 α 5.5% (76 mg/kg L-carnosine), 135.0 α 11.2% (30 mg/kg zinc sulfate), 150.3 α 13.7% (100 mg/kg zinc sulfate), 156.6 α 7.5% (30 mg/kg zinc L-carnosine), and 191.9 α 15.5% (100 mg/kg zinc L-carnosine).
Induction of a 72-kDa heat-shock protein in cultured rat gastric mucosal cells and rat gastric mucosa by zinc L-carnosine.
The antinociceptive effect of 3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide (MPCA) in the formalin test is not mediated by opioid receptors, as naloxone does not prevent its effect.
3-Methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception.
Sodium 2-(1-imidazolylmethyl)-4,5-dihydrobenzo[b]thiophene-6-carboxylate is a potent TXAz sflthetase inhibitor, as demonstrated in ex vivo and in vivo assays.
Synthesis and thromboxane synthetase inhibitory activity of di- or tetrahydrobenzo[b]thiophenecarboxylic acid derivatives.

1,3-dihydro-7,8-dimethyl-2H-imidazo[4,5-b]quinolin-2-one (BMY 20844) was selected for further development based on its in vitro platelet inhibitory properties, efficacy in preventing thrombus formation in animal models of thrombosis, and favorable hemodynamic profile.
Pharmacology of a potent, new antithrombotic agent, 1,3-dihydro-7,8-dimethyl-2H-imidazo[4,5-b]quinolin-2-one (BMY-20844).
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
4'-ethenylthymidine exhibits potent anti-HSV-1 and anti-HIV-1 activities with no significant cytotoxicity.
Nucleosides and nucleotides. 183. Synthesis of 4'alpha-branched thymidines as a new type of antiviral agent.
Landomycin A inhibits the uptake of [3H]thymidine into DNA in murine smooth muscle cells, indicating decreased DNA synthesis.
Landomycin A inhibits DNA synthesis and G1/S cell cycle progression.
Regioisomeric 2-phenyl thiazole analogs display a striking disparity in COX-1 and COX-2 inhibitory activity.
Design and synthesis of sulfonyl-substituted 4,5-diarylthiazoles as selective cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitors.
1,4-phenanthrenequinone derivatives can exhibit virucidal activity against equine infectious anemia virus, a retrovirus, without requiring light exposure.
Phenanthrenequinone antiretroviral agents.
Replacement of d-Phe at the P3 position with d-diphenylalanine in peptidic thrombin inhibitors results in a dramatic increase in potency.
Structure-based design and synthesis of novel thrombin inhibitors based on phosphinic peptide mimetics.
1,4-dibenzyl-pyrazol-5-ol is the minimum structural requirement for endothelin receptor binding affinity.
Nonpeptide endothelin antagonists: from lower affinity pyrazol-5-ols to higher affinity pyrazole-5-carboxylic acids.
N-acetyl-carboxyethyl-phosphotyrosine binds to the SH2 domain of Lck, as shown by chemical shift changes in specific Lck residues during NMR analysis.
NMR-based discovery of phosphotyrosine mimetics that bind to the Lck SH2 domain.
N-benzensulfonyl 2-(2-pyridylethyl) -benzimidazole derivatives have been synthesized and studied for their antiviral activity.
Synthesis and antiviral activity of some N-benzenesulphonylbenzimidazoles.
3-guanylpiperidinylalanine is used as a rigid arginine surrogate in the design of potent and selective thrombin inhibitors by enhancing cation-p interactions in the S3 pocket of factor Xa.
Guanylpiperidine peptidomimetics: potent and selective bis-cation inhibitors of factor Xa.
3-guanylpiperidinylalanine scaffolds exhibit high potency as factor Xa inhibitors and function as efficient arginine surrogates.
Guanylpiperidine peptidomimetics: potent and selective bis-cation inhibitors of factor Xa.
4,5,6,7-tetrahydropyrrolo[3,2-c]pyridines are inhibitors of ADP-stimulated platelet aggregation in vitro, with their antiplatelet activity related to their lipophilicity.
Pyrrolo[3,2-c]pyridine derivatives as inhibitors of platelet aggregation.
Nodulisporic acid A is a compound with a defined pharmacophore that is integral to its potent insecticidal activity, specifically flea efficacy.
Chemical modification of nodulisporic acid A: preliminary structure-activity relationships.
Replacement of d-Phe at the P3 position with d-diphenylalanine in peptidic thrombin inhibitors results in a dramatic increase in potency.
The design of potent and selective inhibitors of thrombin utilizing a piperazinedione template: part 1.
cis-8-(4-isopropyl-cyclohexyl)-1-phenyl-1,3,8-triaza-spiro [4.5]decan-4-one is a potent agonist of the opioid receptor subtype ORL1, with selectivity over mu (m) and kappa (k) opioid receptors.
ORL1 receptor ligands: structure-activity relationships of 8-cycloalkyl-1-phenyl-1,3,8-triaza-spiro[4.5]decan-4-ones.
Diphenylthiazole-thiazolidinone derivatives have been developed and screened for in vitro and in vivo anti-inflammatory activity, including inhibition of COX-I and COX-II enzymes.
Design and synthesis of sulfonyl-substituted 4,5-diarylthiazoles as selective cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitors.
Arachidonyl amides with sterically undemanding amide substituents act as effective gap junction inhibitors.
Arachidonic acid amide inhibitors of gap junction cell-cell communication.
Analogues of 4,6,7,8-tetrahydro-5H-thieno[3,2-b]azepine have been reported as potent orally active antagonists of the arginine vasopressin (AVP) receptor.
The synthesis and vasopressin (AVP) antagonist activity of a novel series of N-aroyl-2,4,5,6-tetrahydropyrazolo[3,4-d]thieno[3,2-b]azepines.

4-nitrobenzylcarbamate prodrugs exhibit high potency against NTR-transfected cells via nitroreductase-mediated bioreductive activation, but require oxygen for the cytotoxicity of the released enediyne effectors, especially in repair-competent cells.
Nitrobenzyl carbamate prodrugs of enediynes for nitroreductase gene-directed enzyme prodrug therapy (GDEPT).
Lead(IV) acetate acts as a reagent that converts 7-amino-9-ethylguaninium chloride and 1-amino-3-methylbenzimidazolium chloride into their corresponding heterocyclic derivatives in methanol.
Reactions of chloride salts of 7-amino-9-ethylguanine and 1-amino-3-methylbenzimidazoles with lead(IV) acetate: formation of 8-aza-9-ethylguanine and 1-methyl-1H-benzotriazoles.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

Fluorescently-labeled risedronate and related analogs can be synthesized by cross-linking risedronate with aromatic succinimidyl esters using 3-amino-2-hydroxypropyl as a linker, and these conjugates have been characterized spectroscopically and tested for photodynamic inactivation, minimum inhibitory concentrations (MIC), and fluorescence labeling.
Novel fluorescent risedronates: synthesis, photodynamic inactivation and imaging of Bacillus subtilis.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

4-hydroxycinnamic ethyl ester derivatives, both monomeric and dimeric, exhibit antioxidant activity against copper-catalyzed human LDL oxidation, with lower oxidation potential and IC50 values for the monomeric series: sinapate > ferulate > p-coumarate.
4-hydroxycinnamic ethyl ester derivatives and related dehydrodimers: Relationship between oxidation potential and protective effects against oxidation of low-density lipoproteins.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
1,6-di(pyrid-2-yl)pyrene is a substrate that can be functionalized at the 2- and 2,7-positions via Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H bond activation.
The synthesis of 2- and 2,7-functionalized pyrene derivatives through Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H activation.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

p-nitrocinnamate can be used as a pendant group to modify gelatin, enabling the formation of cross-linked hydrogels upon exposure to low-intensity 365 nm UV light without the need for photoinitiators or catalysts.
Nitrocinnamate-functionalized gelatin: synthesis and "smart"hydrogel formation via photo-cross-linking.
At 3 hours post-injection, the specific binding of CS2 sulphur is approximately 31% higher in the neurofilament fraction of control animals than in the phenobarbitone-pretreated group.
CS2 binding to rat spinal neurofilaments.

N-carbobenzoxyglycine vinyl ester inhibits pregnancy in mice when administered intravaginally and also inhibits acrosin enzymatic activity as measured by N-alpha-benzoyl-L-arginine ethyl ester and azocasein hydrolysis assays.
Antifertility activity of N-protected glycine activated esters.
In grass carp exposed to emodin combined with DZN, the major ABC transporter gene responsible for DZN transport is ABCG2, which shows higher expression than other ABC transporter genes.
Effects of emodin on ABC transporter gene expression in grass carp (Ctenopharyngodon idellus) exposed to diazinon.

Repetitive intratesticular injection of 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline complex of oxovanadium(IV) at 7.5 mg/kg testis induces germ cell apoptosis, decreased sperm counts, and reduced testicular weights in vivo.
Bis(4,7-dimethyl and 5-dinitro-1,10-phenanthroline) sulfato-oxovanadium(IV)-mediated in vivo male germ cell apoptosis.
3-methyl-3-piperidinooxindole exhibits antiarrhythmic activity against chloroform-induced arrhythmias in mice, with efficacy equal to lidocaine but with only one-third the acute toxicity.
Antiarrhythmic activity of 3-amino-3-methyloxindoles.
Landomycin A inhibits the uptake of [3H]thymidine into DNA in murine smooth muscle cells, indicating decreased DNA synthesis.
Landomycin A inhibits DNA synthesis and G1/S cell cycle progression.
Parenteral copper supplementation of the dam by copper edetate in late gestation has no effect on the liver copper concentration of the foal at birth.
Changes in liver copper concentration of thoroughbred foals from birth to 160 days of age and the effect of prenatal copper supplementation of their dams.

Stilbene isothiocyanate can be used to label the N-terminus of thymopentin analogues, resulting in a mono-labeled analogue (SITS-TP-5) that retains the ability to induce Thy 1.2 surface marker expression on nude mouse prothymocytes and inhibit radiolabeled thymopentin binding to human lymphocytes.
Synthesis of two biologically active fluorescent probes of thymopentin.

The R(+) isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate (5–20 mg/kg orally) causes delayed neuropathy in adult hens by inhibiting neural transaminase (NTE), with the effect associated with high NTE inhibition in brain, spinal cord, and sciatic nerve, peaking at 6–24 hours.
The R-(+)isomer of O-n-hexyl S-methyl phosphorothioamidate causes delayed neuropathy in hens after generation of a form of inhibited neuropathy target esterase (NTE) which can be reactivated ex vivo.

1,4-butanediol di-2,2,2-trifluoroethanesulfonate (BFS) is a fluorine-containing busulfan analog that, when administered as five daily intraperitoneal treatments, significantly increases survival and reduces toxicity in sarcoma 180-bearing mice compared to busulfan.
Antitumor activity and neutrophil-selective hematopoietic toxicity of busulfan analogs in mice.

Rubiyunnanin C is a newly isolated cyclopeptide from Rubia yunnanensis roots, and its isolation, structure, cytotoxic activities, and inhibitory activities against nitric oxide production in LPS- and IFNγ-induced RAW 264.7 murine macrophages have been described.
Rubiyunnanins C-H, cytotoxic cyclic hexapeptides from Rubia yunnanensis inhibiting nitric oxide production and NF-κB activation.
Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds are inhibitors of mitochondrial threonine dehydrogenase (TDH) that impede cell growth and induce autophagy in mouse embryonic stem (ES) cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.

Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds selectively inhibit TDH-mediated threonine catabolism in mouse ES cells, failing to affect the growth of HeLa cells at concentrations 300 times higher than those required to kill ES cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.

Titanocene bis(p-aminothiophenolate) bis(hydrochloride) produces considerable and stable tumor growth inhibition to less than 50% of control values in small cell lung carcinoma xenograft models when administered by the Q2D X 5 schedule at LD10 doses.
Tumor inhibition by titanocene complexes: influence upon two xenografted human lung carcinomas.
7-Hydroxymethyl-12-methylbenzanthracene 3,4-diol is more tumorigenic than 7,12-Dimethylbenzanthracene in causing lung adenomas when injected into newborn mice.
Tumorigenicity of 7,12-dimethylbenz[a]anthracene, its hydroxymethylated derivatives and selected dihydrodiols in the newborn mouse.

4,4'-dichlorodiphenyl ether increases hepatic oxidative stress and alters metal homeostasis in mouse liver after 28 days of oral exposure at 1.20 mg/kg/day, as shown by decreased SOD, CAT, GPx activities, GSH levels, and increased malondialdehyde content.
Subacute oral toxicity of BDE-15, CDE-15, and HODE-15 in ICR male mice: assessing effects on hepatic oxidative stress and metals status and ascertaining the protective role of vitamin E.
22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A is a newly identified 3-alkylpiperidine alkaloid isolated from the marine sponge Amphimedon sp. and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P388 murine leukemia cells.
Tetradehydrohalicyclamine A and 22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A, new cytotoxic bis-piperidine alkaloids from a marine sponge Amphimedon sp.
9-ethynylphenanthrene inactivates P450 2B1 via apoprotein adduction.
Potent mechanism-based inactivation of cytochrome P450 2B4 by 9-ethynylphenanthrene: implications for allosteric modulation of cytochrome P450 catalysis.
Erythrocytes from all three species metabolize o-DNB to S-(2-nitrophenyl)glutathione conjugates.
Metabolism and toxicity of dinitrobenzene isomers in erythrocytes from Fischer-344 rats, rhesus monkeys and humans.

Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate) is essentially inactive compared to ryanodine for insecticidal activity against Musca domestica adults and Tribolium castaneum larvae, for toxicity to mice, and for competition with [3H]ryanodine at the Ca(2+)-ryanodine receptor complex of skeletal muscle.
Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate): a novel ryanoid from Ryania insecticide.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
A 3-methoxypropyl-substituted xanthine derivative has been developed as a potent and selective antagonist of the A2B adenosine receptor.
Synthesis of novel 1-alkyl-8-substituted-3-(3-methoxypropyl) xanthines as putative A(2B) receptor antagonists.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
The identification of a novel lead class for phosphodiesterase 2 inhibition by fragment-based drug design.
N-(2-morpholinoethyl)nicotinamide derivatives are potent and selective inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) over MAO-B.
Design of novel nicotinamides as potent and selective monoamine oxidase a inhibitors.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
2-pyrazoline-1-ethanone derivatives can act as selective human monoamine oxidase A (hMAO-A) inhibitors.
Design and synthesis of novel 2-pyrazoline-1-ethanone derivatives as selective MAO inhibitors.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.
Substitution of the t-butylcarboxamide substituent with a trifluoroethylamide moiety in HIV protease inhibitors increases potency against both wild-type and PI-resistant HIV.
Bis tertiary amide inhibitors of the HIV-1 protease generated via protein structure-based iterative design.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
The N1 substituent of 2-pyrazoline-1-ethanone derivatives influences hMAO-A inhibitory activity, with bromo > piperidinyl > morpholinyl > imidazolyl in terms of contribution to potency.
Design and synthesis of novel 2-pyrazoline-1-ethanone derivatives as selective MAO inhibitors.
Ptns (phenylthionorstatine) is a norstatine derivative containing a hydroxymethylcarbonyl (HMC) isostere and serves as a suitable P(1) moiety for potent BACE1 inhibitor design.
Design and synthesis of BACE1 inhibitors containing a novel norstatine derivative (2R,3R)-3-amino-2-hydroxy-4-(phenylthio)butyric acid.
(6S)-6-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzyl)-4-[(4-chlorophenyl)sulfonyl]-1,4-diazepane-2,5-dione is a potent human chymase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.027 µM.
Identification of 6-substituted 4-arylsulfonyl-1,4-diazepane-2,5-diones as a novel scaffold for human chymase inhibitors.
Substitution of the t-butylcarboxamide substituent with a trifluoroethylamide moiety in HIV protease inhibitors increases potency against both wild-type and PI-resistant HIV.
Synthesis and activity of novel HIV protease inhibitors with improved potency against multiple PI-resistant viral strains.
Cysteinol derivatives are effective inhibitors of kinesin spindle protein (KSP) and induce a monopolar spindle phenotype in cells.
Synthesis and characterization of tritylthioethanamine derivatives with potent KSP inhibitory activity.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Identification of 3-sulfonylindazole derivatives as potent and selective 5-HT(6) antagonists.
A 3-methoxypropyl-substituted xanthine derivative has been developed as a potent and selective antagonist of the A2B adenosine receptor.
Synthesis of novel 1-alkyl-8-substituted-3-(3-methoxypropyl) xanthines as putative A(2B) receptor antagonists.
N-(2-morpholinoethyl)nicotinamide derivatives are potent and selective inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) over MAO-B.
Design of novel nicotinamides as potent and selective monoamine oxidase a inhibitors.

Amide derivatives of 8-[4-[[carboxymethyl]oxy]phenyl]-1,3-di-(n-propyl)xanthine act as selective A(2B) adenosine receptor antagonists, with para-substituted anilides showing high affinity (1–3 nM) and selectivity for human A(2B) receptors.
Synthesis of novel 1-alkyl-8-substituted-3-(3-methoxypropyl) xanthines as putative A(2B) receptor antagonists.

3Beta-(4-Methylphenyl)-2beta-[3'-(4-fluorobenzyl)isoxazol-5-yl]tropane exhibits high selectivity for the dopamine transporter (IC50 = 5.9 nM, Ki at NET = 454 nM, Ki at 5-HTT = 113 nM) among tested analogs.
Synthesis and monoamine transporter binding properties of 2beta-[3'-(substituted benzyl)isoxazol-5-yl]- and 2beta-[3'-methyl-4'-(substituted phenyl)isoxazol-5-yl]-3beta-(substituted phenyl)tropanes.
2-amino-5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole compounds act as benzodiazepine analogs and exhibit superior binding affinity toward GABA A receptors compared with diazepam.

Synthesis, receptor affinity and effect on pentylenetetrazole-induced seizure threshold of novel benzodiazepine analogues: 3-Substituted 5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-4H-1,2,4-triazoles and 2-amino-5-(phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazoles.
1,5,6,10b-tetrahydro-5-oxo-pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline-2-carboxylates demonstrate good binding affinities to Gly/NMDA and/or AMPA receptors.
1-Substituted pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazolines as novel Gly/NMDA receptor antagonists: synthesis, biological evaluation, and molecular modeling study.

Indenopyridinone derivatives with meta-phenolic groups at the 4-position and para-phenolic groups at the 2- and/or 4-position exhibit strong topo IIalpha-selective inhibition and anti-proliferative activity against human cervix (HeLa) and colorectal (HCT15) cancer cell lines.
A new phenolic series of indenopyridinone as topoisomerase inhibitors: Design, synthesis, and structure-activity relationships.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).

Structure-activity relationships of 2-amino-3-aroyl-4-[(4-arylpiperazin-1-yl)methyl]thiophenes. Part 2: Probing the influence of diverse substituents at the phenyl of the arylpiperazine moiety on allosteric enhancer activity at the A₁ adenosine receptor.

(Z)-5-(4-methoxybenzylidene)-2-(naphthalen-2-yl)-5H-thiazol-4-one (ST1237) is a potent direct 5-lipoxygenase (5-LO) inhibitor with IC50 values of 0.08–0.03 µM in the cell-free S100 assay and 0.12–0.02 µM in intact polymorphonuclear leukocytes (PMNL).
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a class of 5-benzylidene-2-phenyl-thiazolinones as potent 5-lipoxygenase inhibitors.

(R)-4-methyl-8-ethyl-2-phenyl-imidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-one is a highly potent and selective antagonist for human A(3) adenosine receptors, exhibiting a K(i) value of 2.3 nM and high selectivity over all other adenosine receptor subtypes.
2-Phenylimidazo[2,1-i]purin-5-ones: structure-activity relationships and characterization of potent and selective inverse agonists at Human A3 adenosine receptors.
Tetrahydrothieno[2,3-b]quinoline derivatives have been evaluated for antiproliferative activity against HCT116, MDA-MB-468, and MDA-MB-231 human cancer cell lines.
Synthesis and cytotoxicity of thieno[2,3-b]quinoline-2-carboxamide and cycloalkyl[b]thieno[3,2-e]pyridine-2-carboxamide derivatives.

N-(2-Aminopyridin-4-ylmethyl)nicotinamide derivatives, such as compound 23h, are potent and orally bioavailable inhibitors of reverse Na/Ca exchange (NCX) and are promising candidates for antiarrhythmic drugs in the setting of ischemia-reperfusion and prevention of heart failure or arrhythmia.
Discovery of an N-(2-aminopyridin-4-ylmethyl)nicotinamide derivative: a potent and orally bioavailable NCX inhibitor.
The anti-microtubule activity of phenylahistin is dependent on a uniplanar pseudo-three-ring structure formed by hydrogen bonding between diketopiperazine and imidazole rings.
Synthesis and biological activities of phenylahistin derivatives.

6-(4-methoxybenzyl)-2,11-dimethyl-6H,11H-indeno[1,2-c]isoquinolin-5-one (IIQ-16), an indeno[1,2-c]isoquinoline derivative, exhibits the highest differentiation-enhancing activity among tested compounds when combined with low-dose ATRA, via a pathway involving protein kinase C, extracellular signal-regulated kinase, and c-Jun N-terminal kinase.
Indeno[1,2-c]isoquinolines as enhancing agents on all-trans retinoic acid-mediated differentiation of human myeloid leukemia cells.

O-phenoxycarbonyl-N-(R)-[(4-amino-4-carboxy-1-butyl)oxycarbonyl]hydroxylamine is an effective inactivator of Class C beta-lactamases, requiring a hydrophobic element and a negatively charged group (carboxylate) for high inhibitory activity.
Specificity of extended O-aryloxycarbonyl hydroxamates as inhibitors of a class C β-lactamase.
O-phenoxycarbonyl-N-(R)-[(4-amino-4-carboxy-1-butyl)oxycarbonyl]hydroxylamine inactivates Class C beta-lactamases by forming an inert hydroxamoyl derivative rather than a carbonyl cross-link.
Specificity of extended O-aryloxycarbonyl hydroxamates as inhibitors of a class C β-lactamase.

N-(2-aminopyridin-4-ylmethyl)nicotinamide derivatives, such as compound 23h, are potent, orally bioavailable inhibitors of reverse sodium-calcium exchange (NCX) and are promising candidates for antiarrhythmic drugs in the ischemia-reperfusion setting and as prophylactic anti-arrhythmic agents.
Discovery of an N-(2-aminopyridin-4-ylmethyl)nicotinamide derivative: a potent and orally bioavailable NCX inhibitor.
N-(2-aminopyridin-4-ylmethyl)nicotinamide derivatives have been designed and synthesized as sodium-calcium exchanger (NCX) inhibitors targeting Ca(2+) overload in myocardial cells.
Discovery of an N-(2-aminopyridin-4-ylmethyl)nicotinamide derivative: a potent and orally bioavailable NCX inhibitor.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.
Both (2S,3R)- and (2R,3S)-hydroxycitric acid enantiomers produced by Bacillus megaterium G45C and Streptomyces sp. U121 are optically pure.
Absolute configuration of hydroxycitric acid produced by microorganisms.

2-[4-(4-Chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide (PPPA) is a novel, broad-spectrum Na(+) channel blocker that is approximately 1000 times more potent, has 2000-fold faster binding kinetics, and at least 10-fold higher state dependence than carbamazepine and lamotrigine in recombinant rat Na(v)1.2 channels and native rat dorsal root ganglion neurons.
Pharmacology of 2-[4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide: a potent, broad-spectrum state-dependent sodium channel blocker for treating pain states.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid is a histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands the pool of renal progenitor cells in zebrafish embryos in a proliferation-dependent manner.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid (PTBA) is a small molecule histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands embryonic kidney progenitor cells (EKPCs) in zebrafish embryos.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.

Cortisol 21-sulfate restores normal circadian rhythmicity of food intake in CRH1RÀ/À mutant mice, with both wildtype and mutant mice consuming 27 ± 2.5% and 26 ± 5.3% of their total food intake per 24 hours during the light period after CORT 21-sulfate administration.
Disruption of feeding behavior in CRH receptor 1-deficient mice is dependent on glucocorticoids.
Benzothiazolylcysteine is a nontoxic cysteine S-conjugate that acts as a substrate for hOAT1, as shown by preloading and trans-stimulation of PAH uptake.
Interaction of cysteine conjugates with human and rabbit organic anion transporter 1.

[N-(4-chlorophenyl)]-alpha-[(4-chlorophenyl)-aminomethylene]-3-methyl-5-isoxazoleacet-amide (CCMI) is a type I positive allosteric modulator of alpha7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors that does not affect cell integrity or cell viability in PC12 cells or primary rat cortical neuronal cultures under conditions measuring alpha7 nAChR activity.
Positive allosteric modulation of alpha7 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptors: lack of cytotoxicity in PC12 cells and rat primary cortical neurons.
Morpholino phosphorodiamidate anti-sense oligonucleotides (MPOs) are used for gene-specific knockdown and have potential applications in both therapeutic and functional genomics studies.
Morpholino phosphorodiamidate oligonucleotides in zebrafish: a recipe for functional genomics?

Carbocyclic 3-deazaadenosine is an adenosine analogue that inhibits Ebola virus replication in vitro, likely by blocking S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine hydrolase and thereby limiting methylation of the 5' cap of viral messenger RNA.
Treatment of lethal Ebola virus infection in mice with a single dose of an S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine hydrolase inhibitor.

Quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt exerts an antagonistic effect on cadmium intoxication by counteracting cadmium-induced decreases in superoxide dismutase (SOD) and glutathione (GSH) levels in the mouse liver.
Influence of water-soluble flavonoids, quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt and morin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt, on antioxidant parameters in the subacute cadmium intoxication mouse model.

2-[4-(4-Chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide (PPPA) is a novel structural analog of the state-dependent Naϩ channel blocker V102862, designed to have higher in vitro potency, faster binding kinetics, and more pronounced state dependence than existing Naϩ channel blockers.
Pharmacology of 2-[4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide: a potent, broad-spectrum state-dependent sodium channel blocker for treating pain states.

PPPA {2-[4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]pyrimidine-4-carboxamide} is an analog of Naϩ channel blockers with enhanced state-dependent (inactivated versus resting channel) selectivity and a greater protective index against rotarod toxicity compared to carbamazepine and lamotrigine.
Pharmacology of 2-[4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide: a potent, broad-spectrum state-dependent sodium channel blocker for treating pain states.

N-(4-methylpiperazinyl)phenylacridine-9-amine is a functionalized monoacridine that reduces or clears PrP Sc in various prion-infected cell models at sub-micromolar concentrations without toxicity to host cells.
Antiprion activity of functionalized 9-aminoacridines related to quinacrine.
Benzyloxycarbonyl-L-phenylalanine cyanomethyl ester is a mildly activated amino acid ester that undergoes aminolysis when reacted with glycine esters in the presence of catechol.
Catechol as a nucleophilic catalyst of peptide bond formation.

1,2-dipentanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine forms protonated oligomeric clusters ([D5PCn + H]+, n = 5, 6, and 14) in mass spectrometry when subjected to electrospray ionization from fresh 10 mM ammonium acetate solution.
Sources of artefacts in the electrospray ionization mass spectra of saturated diacylglycerophosphocholines: from condensed phase hydrolysis reactions through to gas phase intercluster reactions.

The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be constructed via intramolecular Diels-Alder (DA) reactions of halogenated 2-pyrones, including methods using base- or acid-induced equilibration of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.
Benzothienothiadiazine derivatives retain antiviral activity against human cytomegalovirus (HCMV) and, when modified with a fused ring and a sulfamide moiety, show potency comparable to ganciclovir.
Benzothiadiazine dioxide human cytomegalovirus inhibitors: synthesis and antiviral evaluation of main heterocycle modified derivatives.
