Oral administration of 3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) at doses ≥10 mg/kg in MCMV-infected mice produces antiviral effects comparable to ganciclovir, as measured by survival and viral DNA levels in organs.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.

Tetra(3-hydroxyphenyl)porphyrin is a lipophilic photosensitizer that, when combined with light, induces a rapid apoptotic response in MDCK II cells and triggers a bystander effect, where neighboring cells are also induced to apoptosis.
The bystander effect in photodynamic inactivation of cells.
Calicheamicin T binds in the minor groove of DNA and cleaves all nucleotides comparably, indicating that its cyclization kinetics do not significantly influence its cleavage selectivity.
Cleavage behavior of calicheamicin gamma 1 and calicheamicin T.

2-naphthylglucose is transported by human SGLT1 with lower affinity (K0.5 = 0.5 mM) and by pig SGLT3 (SAAT1) with higher affinity (K0.5 = 0.2 mM), suggesting more extensive hydrophobic/aromatic interaction in pig SGLT3 than in human SGLT1.
Glycoside binding and translocation in Na(+)-dependent glucose cotransporters: comparison of SGLT1 and SGLT3.
D-glycero-D-gulo-heptose does not inhibit D-glucose phosphorylation by low-Km hexokinase isoenzymes or liver B-cell glucokinase.

Effects of D-mannoheptose and D-glycero-D-gulo-heptose upon D-glucose metabolism and insulinotropic action in rat pancreatic islets and D-glucose phosphorylation by hexokinase isoenzymes: comparison with D-mannoheptulose.

ChloraPrep, a solution containing 2% chlorhexidine gluconate and 70% isopropyl alcohol, is the most effective surgical skin-preparation solution for eliminating bacteria from the hallux and toes in the foot and ankle prior to surgery.
Efficacy of surgical preparation solutions in foot and ankle surgery.

In humans, 3-fucosyl-N-acetyllactosamine (CD15) shows strong immunoreactivity on reactive glial cells at the periphery of brain tumors and in freshly demyelinated lesions of central pontine myelinolysis.
The subcellular localization of the carbohydrate epitope 3-fucosyl-N-acetyllactosamine is different in normal and reactive astrocytes.

In n-3 PUFA adequate rats, the loss half-life of [4,5-3H]DHA in phospholipids ranged from 23 days in phosphatidylcholine (PC) to 58 days in phosphatidylethanolamine (PS), as determined by slope analysis.
Half-lives of docosahexaenoic acid in rat brain phospholipids are prolonged by 15 weeks of nutritional deprivation of n-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids.
The Bergman cyclization rate of calicheamicin T is insensitive to local variations in minor groove conformation and sequence.
Cleavage behavior of calicheamicin gamma 1 and calicheamicin T.
The binding affinity of 2-phenyl-5-bromobenzylglutathione, a G- and H-site competitive inhibitor, is diminished in the I219A mutant of hGST A1-1 compared to wildtype.
Residue 219 impacts on the dynamics of the C-terminal region in glutathione transferase A1-1: implications for stability and catalytic and ligandin functions.
Gly-Pro-Arg-Gly-Pro-Pro inhibits fibrinogen/thrombin clotting but does not inhibit the activity of thrombin.
Inhibitory effects of enzymatic hydrolysates of collagen and collagen-related synthetic peptides on fibrinogen/thrombin clotting.
19-hydroxy-Resolvin E1 is a major metabolic product of Resolvin E1 in isolated human PMN and murine spleen and lung, but less so in human blood, kidney, and liver.
Resolvin E1 metabolome in local inactivation during inflammation-resolution.

Gadolinium tetrasulfophenylporphyrin (GdTPPS) produces strong and long-lasting T1 reduction in tumors and also strongly affects T2 relaxation in tumors, with the effect retained for days and no influence on normal brain tissue relaxation times.
Quantitative magnetic resonance imaging of rat brain tumors: in vivo NMR relaxometry for the discrimination of normal and pathological tissues.

DNJ is absorbed from mulberry leaf extract and purified compound in Caco-2 human intestinal cell monolayers in a dose-dependent manner, with higher absorption from purified DNJ than from the extract after 2 hours of incubation.
Comparison of absorption of 1-deoxynojirimycin from mulberry water extract in rats.
Oligopeptides containing the Asp-Gly-Arg (DGR) sequence do not inhibit cell adhesion to microtiter plates coated with fibrin, fibrinogen, or fibronectin in RTG-2 cells.
Integrin-like substrate adhesion in RTG-2 cells, a fibroblastic cell line derived from rainbow trout.
2-MeSADP binds to its P2Y receptor on the basolateral membrane and inhibits Na+/H+ exchange activity via the adenylate cyclase/PKA pathway.
Extracellular adenine nucleotides regulate Na+/H+ exchanger NHE3 activity in A6-NHE3 transfectants by a cAMP/PKA-dependent mechanism.
The rhenium oxo dimethylglycyl-L-seryl-L-cysteinyl-glycine segment does not significantly affect the binding affinity of TKPPR to the tuftsin receptor.
Tuftsin receptor-binding peptide labeled with technetium: chemistry and preliminary in vitro receptor-binding study.
In hSGLT1, 2-NaphGlu is transported with high affinity, and its recognition as a substrate is influenced by hydrophobic and H-bonding interactions.
Glycoside binding and translocation in Na(+)-dependent glucose cotransporters: comparison of SGLT1 and SGLT3.

2-naphthylglucose binds to SGLT1 with an apparent affinity that is approximately 30-fold higher than that of glucose, suggesting the naphthyl moiety lowers the binding free energy in the SGLT1 binding site.
Glycoside binding and translocation in Na(+)-dependent glucose cotransporters: comparison of SGLT1 and SGLT3.

A stable analytical method for mycophenolic acid acyl glucuronide has been developed and applied to human plasma, urine, and whole blood samples, with stability observed for at least 8 hours at 1–4°C and at least 11 months at ∈22°C.
Determination of the acyl glucuronide metabolite of mycophenolic acid in human plasma by HPLC and Emit.

Gadofluorine M is a contrast agent that produces very high and homogeneous signal intensities in functional rabbit lymph node tissue within 15 minutes after intravenous injection, with enhancement lasting at least 120 minutes as observed by T1-weighted gradient-echo MR imaging.
Early MR lymphography with gadofluorine M in rabbits.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

When covalently linked to bacteriophage 75 and delivered to Staphylococcus aureus, tin(IV) chlorin e6 (SnCe6) enables selective killing of methicillin- and vancomycin-intermediate strains with low concentrations (1.5 µg/ml) and low light doses (21 J/cm2), while sparing human epithelial cells.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.
Cladosporilactam A is a new bicyclic lactam isolated from a gorgonian-derived Cladosporium sp. fungus and exhibits potent anticancer activity against HeLa cells with an IC50 value of 0.76 µM.
Bioactive 7-Oxabicyclic[6.3.0]lactam and 12-Membered Macrolides from a Gorgonian-Derived Cladosporium sp. Fungus.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
6-(substituted methylene)penems, potent broad spectrum inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamase. IV. Kidney stability, serum binding and additional biological evaluation of racemic derivatives.
Congeners bearing an (E)-cyclooct-2-enylidenemethyl group exhibit more potent type I fatty acid synthase (FAS) inhibitory activity than (S)-3-demethylthiolactomycin with an unnatural configuration.
Synthesis and biological activity of enantiomeric pairs of 5-[(E)-cycloalk-2-enylidenemethyl]thiolactomycin congeners.
1-methyl-1 H-pyrazole-5-carboxamide derivatives inhibit the development of Haemonchus contortus larvae, with the first identified compound showing an IC50 of 0.29 µM.
Optimization of Novel 1-Methyl-1 H-Pyrazole-5-carboxamides Leads to High Potency Larval Development Inhibitors of the Barber's Pole Worm.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.

Methyl substitution of 1,10-phenanthroline to 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline in oxovanadium complexes enhances anti-leukemic activity, as seen in bis(4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline)sulfatooxovanadium(IV) which induces reactive oxygen species production, G1-arrest, and inhibits clonogenic growth in human leukemia cells at nanomolar concentrations.
Synthesis, X-ray structure, and anti-leukemic activity of oxovanadium(IV) complexes.
Substituents at positions 5 and 7 of the 3-phenyl-4-hydroxycoumarin ring influence its inhibitory potency against HIV-1 protease.
Structure-activity relationships of some 3-substituted-4-hydroxycoumarins as HIV-1 protease inhibitors.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.

5-hydroxy-8-chloro-N1-methylbenz[f]-indazole-4,9-quinone shows growth-inhibitory activity against Cryptosporidium parvum in a T-cell receptor alpha (TCR-alpha)-deficient mouse model of chronic cryptosporidiosis.
Evaluation of in vitro and in vivo activity of benzindazole-4,9-quinones against Cryptosporidium parvum.

16-(3-methoxyphenyl)-9,10,11,12,13,16-hexahydro-15H-benzo [5',6']chromeno [2',3':4,5]pyrimido [1,2-a]azepin-15-imine is a benzochromene derivative identified as a potential multitarget-directed ligand for Alzheimer's disease therapy, acting as a human acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.36 ± 0.02 mM, exhibiting strong antioxidant activity (3.61 mmol Troloxe equivalents), and moderate beta-amyloid aggregation inhibition (40.3%).
Synthesis and Biological Assessment of Racemic Benzochromenopyrimidinimines as Antioxidant, Cholinesterase, and Aβ1-42 Aggregation Inhibitors for Alzheimer's Disease Therapy.
(6S)-6-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzyl)-4-[(4-chlorophenyl)sulfonyl]-1,4-diazepane-2,5-dione is a potent human chymase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.027 µM.
Identification of 6-substituted 4-arylsulfonyl-1,4-diazepane-2,5-diones as a novel scaffold for human chymase inhibitors.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.
Ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives can inhibit the growth of A549 lung cancer cells in a dose-dependent manner by inducing cell cycle arrest.
Synthesis, single-crystal characterization and preliminary biological evaluation of novel ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives.
1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated as nonsteroidal progesterone receptor modulators with antiprogestant activity.
Development of progesterone receptor antagonists from 1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline agonist pharmacophore.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate (IP-dUMP) is a mechanism-based, irreversible inactivator of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase, covalently modifying Tyr94 at the enzyme's active site.
Modification of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase at tyrosine-94 by 5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate.
Apicidin A is a cyclic tetrapeptide that acts as a reversible inhibitor of histone deacetylase (HDAC), exhibiting potent antiprotozoal activity.
Structure, histone deacetylase, and antiprotozoal activities of apicidins B and C, congeners of apicidin with proline and valine substitutions.
Integramycin is a complex natural product with significant and novel activity against HIV-1 integrase, making it a promising candidate for new anti-HIV therapy.
Structure, stereochemistry, and biological activity of integramycin, a novel hexacyclic natural product produced by Actinoplanes sp. that inhibits HIV-1 integrase.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.

Epoxide ring opening, functional group interconversion, and deprotection of (2RS,3SR)-N(1)-benzyl-2-chloromethyl-3-benzyloxy-4,5-epoxypiperidine enable the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
An oxidation and ring contraction approach to the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
Bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene derivatives can be synthesized by reacting cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes in the presence of a rhenium catalyst, [ReBr(CO)(3)(thf)](2).
Rhenium-catalyzed formation of bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene frameworks by a reaction of cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes.
8-(Methylsulfonyl)FAD reacts with a single cysteine residue (Cys293) in the flavin domain of Escherichia coli DNA photolyase to form a covalent 8-(cysteinyl)FAD derivative.
Affinity probing of flavin binding sites. 2. Identification of a reactive cysteine in the flavin domain of Escherichia coli DNA photolyase.

Covalent modification of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase by 5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate increases the enzyme's mass by 412 amu, as determined by electrospray ionization-mass spectrometry.
Modification of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase at tyrosine-94 by 5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate.
Shornephine A is a diketomorpholine (DKM) natural product isolated from an Australian marine sediment-derived Aspergillus sp. (CMB-M081F).
Shornephine A: structure, chemical stability, and P-glycoprotein inhibitory properties of a rare diketomorpholine from an Australian marine-derived Aspergillus sp.

N-(4'-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) binds to the α-site of tryptophan synthase and its CF3 groups provide 19F NMR signals that sample the channel microenvironment at the α-β subunit interface, allowing distinction between open and closed β-subunit conformations.
Allostery and substrate channeling in the tryptophan synthase bienzyme complex: evidence for two subunit conformations and four quaternary states.
5-imidazolylpropynyl-2′-deoxyuridine 5′-monophosphate (IP-dUMP) is an irreversible inactivator of L. casei thymidylate synthase (TS), covalently modifying the active site residue Tyr94.
Modification of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase at tyrosine-94 by 5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate.
Quinolinium dichromate in water oxidizes vicinal and nonvicinal diols to their corresponding alpha-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
Quinolinium Dichromate Oxidation of Diols: A Kinetic Study.

1,2-bis(heptanylcarbamoyl)-rac-glycerol 3-sulfate binds directly to porcine pancreatic phospholipase A2 and its zymogen, as detected by changes in the zero-field splitting of Trp3 residue in optical detection of triplet-state magnetic resonance spectroscopy.
Optically detected magnetic resonance studies of porcine pancreatic phospholipase A2 binding to a negatively charged substrate analogue.

6-deoxy-6-fluoro-alpha-D-glucopyranosyl phosphate acts as a competitive inhibitor of phosphoryl transfer by phosphoglucomutase, with its ground-state inhibition constant (Ki) providing insight into specific interactions with the sugar hydroxyls.
Binding energy and catalysis: deoxyfluoro sugars as probes of hydrogen bonding in phosphoglucomutase.
Albogrisin D is a 4H-pyrroloquinolinone alkaloid isolated from Streptomyces albogriseolus MGR072 and is a new indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase 1 inhibitor.
Characterization and Nonenzymatic Transformation of Three Types of Alkaloids from Streptomyces albogriseolus MGR072 and Discovery of Inhibitors of Indoleamine 2,3-Dioxygenase.
Integramycin inhibits HIV-1 integrase-mediated coupled and strand transfer reactions with IC50 values of 3 µM and 4 µM, respectively.
Structure, stereochemistry, and biological activity of integramycin, a novel hexacyclic natural product produced by Actinoplanes sp. that inhibits HIV-1 integrase.

Fluoromalonyl tyrosine is a phosphotyrosine mimetic that, when incorporated into a cyclic hexapeptide and bound to PTP1B, reveals the mechanism of phosphotyrosine mimicry and interacts within the catalytic site of the enzyme.
Structural basis for inhibition of the protein tyrosine phosphatase 1B by phosphotyrosine peptide mimetics.
N-benzyl 3-hydroxypyrrolidine can be synthesized in optically active form via regio- and stereoselective hydroxylation of N-benzylpyrrolidine using biocatalysts such as Sphingomonas sp. HXN-200.
Preparation of (R)- and (S)-N-protected 3-hydroxypyrrolidines by hydroxylation with Sphingomonas sp. HXN-200, a highly active, regio- and stereoselective, and easy to handle biocatalyst.

[5,6-3H]arachidonic acid can be enzymically converted to PGI2 by swine aortic microsomes, with tritium loss at the C-6 position, and the rate of tritium release can be used to monitor the enzymatic rearrangement.
Synthesis of [5,6-3H]arachidonic acid and its use in development of a sensitive assay for prostacyclin synthetase.
The hexapeptide Arg-Glu-Leu-Leu-Lys-Met, when displayed on a filamentous phage, is recognized by autoantibodies from patient ITP-2 that target the GPIIb/IIIa complex.
Characterization of autoantigenic epitopes on platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa using random peptide libraries.
12-(4-azido-2-nitrophenoxy)-stearoyl[1-14C]glucosamine is a glycolipid photoprobe capable of undergoing transbilayer flip-flop and intermembrane transfer between liposomes.
Transbilayer migration (flip-flop) of 12-(4-azido-2-nitrophenoxy)stearoyl glucosamine in large unilamellar phospholipid vesicles.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Isonucleosides incorporating universal bases.

Samarium(III) triflate (Sm(OTf)3) is used to activate benzylated glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones, enabling them to react with simple and carbohydrate alcohols to yield β-mixtures of glycosides in moderate to excellent yield.
A glycosylation protocol based on activation of glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones with samarium triflate.

(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid inhibits leukotriene B4-induced degranulation and superoxide anion generation in human leukocytes in a dose-dependent manner without affecting cyclic AMP levels.
Relationship of cyclic-AMP levels in leukotriene B4-stimulated leukocytes to lysosomal enzyme release and the generation of superoxide anions.

Thr-Met-Lys-Ile-Ile-Pro-Phe-Asn-Arg-Leu-Thr-Ile-Gly-Gly (TMKIIPFNRLTIGG) is a peptide ligand for Mac-1, a beta(2) integrin, and promotes robust neutrophil adhesion and spreading when incorporated into PEG hydrogels.
Integrin interactions with immobilized peptides in polyethylene glycol diacrylate hydrogels.
The presence of tyrosyl iodide in thyroid hormone analogs enhances their inhibition of 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase (5'-ITD) activity in rat liver homogenates.
Thyroid hormone analog inhibition of hepatic 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase activity.
9-(2-deoxy-beta-D-erythro-pentofuranosyl)-6-methylpurine can be synthesized in high yield using a (Ph3P)4Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling reaction of CH3ZnBr with 6-chloropurine derivatives.
Convenient syntheses of 6-methylpurine and related nucleosides.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.
Tomenphantin B is a cytotoxic germacranolide isolated from Elephantopus tomentosus L. and its structure and absolute stereochemistry have been determined.
Antitumor agents. 190. Absolute stereochemistry of the cytotoxic germacranolides, tomenphantins A and B, from Elephantopus tomentosus.

At high concentrations, methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) induces aberrant HBV capsid protein assembly, favoring hexagonal arrays and promoting dissociation of metastable capsids by scavenging capsid protein and redirecting assembly into hexamer-rich structures.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.

5-Iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) is converted to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine (IUdR) by aldehyde oxidase in the liver, a reaction that does not require exogenous cofactors and is not catalyzed by mixed-function oxidases, xanthine oxidase, or many other oxido-reductases.
Conversion of 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine by aldehyde oxidase. Implication in hepatotropic drug design.

An octasaccharide sequence within heparin corresponds to the minimum oligosaccharide required for binding to annexin V, with a Kd of approximately 1 µM and a binding stoichiometry of 1:1 as determined by isothermal titration calorimetry.
Interaction of heparin with annexin V.

The mean AUC of 3'-hydroxydiclofenac after a single 150 mg oral dose of diclofenac is significantly different between healthy subjects and patients with alcoholic cirrhosis, but not between healthy subjects and chronic active hepatitis patients.
Pharmacokinetics of diclofenac sodium in chronic active hepatitis and alcoholic cirrhosis.
Cholest-8(14)-en-3-ol is formed in significant amounts from cholesta-7,14-dien-3-ol under anaerobic incubation with a 10,000 x g supernatant fraction of rat liver homogenate.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.

Amide derivatives of 8-[4-[[carboxymethyl]oxy]phenyl]-1,3-di-(n-propyl)xanthine act as selective A(2B) adenosine receptor antagonists, with para-substituted anilides showing high affinity (1–3 nM) and selectivity for human A(2B) receptors.
Anilide derivatives of an 8-phenylxanthine carboxylic congener are highly potent and selective antagonists at human A(2B) adenosine receptors.
3-thienylalanine is identified as an especially good substrate for phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase (PheRS) based on computational predictions using the HierDock protocol.
Virtual screening for binding of phenylalanine analogues to phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase.

5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) enables radiosensitization in both MMR-proficient and MMR-deficient human colon cancer xenografts, with a sensitizer enhancement ratio (SER) of 1.48 at 2 Gy per fraction and 1.41 at 4 Gy per fraction in MMR-deficient tumors, and 1.21 at 2 Gy and 1.20 at 4 Gy in MMR-proficient tumors.
Differential radiosensitization in DNA mismatch repair-proficient and -deficient human colon cancer xenografts with 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose.

The thermolytic deprotection of 4-methylthio-1-butyl phosphate groups occurs via cyclodeesterification, resulting in the formation of a sulfonium salt side product that is inert to DNA nucleobases and does not cause significant desulfurization of phosphorothioate diesters.
Thermolytic 4-methylthio-1-butyl group for phosphate/thiophosphate protection in solid-phase synthesis of DNA oligonucleotides.
3-fluorochromanone serves as a substrate for ruthenium-catalyzed asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to yield enantioenriched cis-3-fluoro-chroman-4-ol derivatives.
Ru(II)-Catalyzed Asymmetric Transfer Hydrogenation of 3-Fluorochromanone Derivatives to Access Enantioenriched cis-3-Fluorochroman-4-ols through Dynamic Kinetic Resolution.
Nitrobenzisoselenazolone derivatives with N-substituents at position 7 can be synthesized and show GPx-like activities and cytoprotective effects.
Nitro-, Azo-, and Amino Derivatives of Ebselen: Synthesis, Structure, and Cytoprotective Effects.
2-iodoselenophene can undergo copper-catalyzed coupling reactions with amides to form 2-nitrogenselenophene derivatives under mild conditions without the need for additional additives.
Copper-promoted carbon-nitrogen bond formation with 2-iodo-selenophene and amides.
Cyano-aci-nitroacetate, in its dianionic form, acts as a safe synthetic equivalent for nitroacetonitrile in Michael addition reactions to produce 5-acylated 3-cyanoisoxazoles.
Synthesis of Functionalized 3-Cyanoisoxazoles Using a Dianionic Reagent.

Iron(II) tris-bipyridine complexes can exhibit diastereoselectivity in their solid-state structure, influenced by noncovalent interactions and solvent polarity, due to the chiral nature of the L-amino acid ligands.
Induction of diastereoselectivity in Fe(II) tris(amino acid-bipyridine) complexes.

1-chloromethylbenzotriazole reacts with 2-aminopyridines and 1-aminoisoquinoline to produce 2-amino-1-[alpha-benzotriazol-1-ylmethyl]pyridinium chlorides and 1-amino-2-(alpha-benzotriazol-1-ylmethyl)isoquinolinium++ + chloride, respectively.
Efficient syntheses of imidazolo[1,2-a]pyridines and -[2, 1-a]isoquinolines.
Quinolinium dichromate in water oxidizes vicinal and nonvicinal diols to their corresponding alpha-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
Quinolinium Dichromate Oxidation of Diols: A Kinetic Study.

3-fluorochromanone derivatives with electron-withdrawing substituents such as fluoro, bromo, and nitro groups on the aryl ring are efficiently reduced by asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to give cis-fluorohydrins with high diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity.
Ru(II)-Catalyzed Asymmetric Transfer Hydrogenation of 3-Fluorochromanone Derivatives to Access Enantioenriched cis-3-Fluorochroman-4-ols through Dynamic Kinetic Resolution.
The (hydroxylamino)pyrazolone (HAPY) class of HNO donors features a pyrazolone ring that is expected to be resistant to hydrolysis and rearrangement.
"Catch-and-release" of HNO with pyrazolones.
2-iodo-selenophene can participate in a copper-catalyzed carbon-nitrogen bond formation reaction, likely involving an amidation process analogous to that of aryl iodide.
Copper-promoted carbon-nitrogen bond formation with 2-iodo-selenophene and amides.
Lactosyl bromide can be used as a glycosyl donor for the S-glycosylation of homocysteine and cysteine containing peptides under two-phase conditions with sodium carbonate as base.
Synthesis of S-linked glycopeptides in aqueous solution.
(E)-dimethyl but-2-enoylphosphonate acts as a nucleophile in the enantioselective conjugate addition reaction to 2-hydroxy-1,4-naphthoquinone, catalyzed by cinchonine-based thioureas.
Organocatalyzed enantioselective michael addition of 2-hydroxy-1,4-naphthoquinone to β,γ-unsaturated α-ketophosphonatesθ.
Hydroxylaminopyrazolone (HAPY) compounds are nitric oxide (HNO) donors that are important for vasorelaxation and myocardial contractility.
"Catch-and-release" of HNO with pyrazolones.

The imidazole-catalyzed decomposition of bis(2,4-dinitrophenyl) oxalate follows a first-order dependence on imidazole concentration and has an observed positive activation energy of 12.0 ± 0.6 kJ/mol.
Kinetics and Mechanism of the Nucleophilic Substitution Reaction of Imidazole with Bis(2,4,6-trichlorophenyl) Oxalate and Bis(2,4-dinitrophenyl) Oxalate.
2-tert-butyl-pyrene undergoes Ir-catalyzed monoborylation at the 7-position in nearly 100% yield when reacted with B2pin2 and [Ir(COD)Cl]2 in hexane at 80°C for 48 h.
Ir-catalyzed direct borylation at the 4-position of pyrene.

5,6-dihydro-2(1H)-pyridinone undergoes arylation with arylboroxines such as 4-fluorophenylboroxine and 4-chlorophenylboroxine to yield (R)-4-aryl-2-piperidinones with high enantioselectivity (around 98% ee) and high yield when 1 equivalent of water is used.
Rhodium-catalyzed asymmetric 1,4-addition of organoboron reagents to 5,6-dihydro-2(1H)-pyridinones. Asymmetric synthesis of 4-aryl-2-piperidinones.

Benzyl carbonate derivatives (compounds 5a–5c) have been designed as fumagillin analogues to optimize hydrophobic interactions and van der Waals contacts with the hydrophobic valley and pocket of human MetAP-2.
MetAP-2 inhibitors based on the fumagillin structure. Side-chain modification and ring-substituted analogues.
Nitrobenzisoselenazolone derivatives with N-substituents at position 7 can be synthesized and show GPx-like activities and cytoprotective effects.
Effect of a Bromo Substituent on the Glutathione Peroxidase Activity of a Pyridoxine-like Diselenide.
(-)-triptolide is a natural product whose tricyclic skeleton can be constructed via Mn(OAc)3-mediated oxidative free radical cyclization using (+)-8-phenylmenthol as a chiral auxiliary.
Enantioselective total synthesis of (-)-triptolide, (-)-triptonide, (+)-triptophenolide, and (+)-triptoquinonide.
Bis(2,4-dinitrophenyl) oxalate undergoes imidazole-catalyzed decomposition producing 1,1'-oxalyldiimidazole and two molecules of substituted phenol.
Kinetics and Mechanism of the Nucleophilic Substitution Reaction of Imidazole with Bis(2,4,6-trichlorophenyl) Oxalate and Bis(2,4-dinitrophenyl) Oxalate.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

3-chloro-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-5-carboxylic acid (CMPA) is an effective inducer of systemic acquired resistance (SAR) in tobacco, inducing PR gene expression and broad-range disease resistance without antibacterial activity.
Pyrazolecarboxylic acid derivative induces systemic acquired resistance in tobacco.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Isonucleosides incorporating universal bases.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.
Substitution of isoxazoline C-5 position with piperazyl-carbamate improves anti-tubercular activity in 3,5-disubstituted isoxazoline esters.
Synthesis, optimization and structure-activity relationships of 3,5-disubstituted isoxazolines as new anti-tuberculosis agents.
Substitution of isoxazoline C-5 position with piperazyl-carbamate improves anti-tubercular activity in 3,5-disubstituted isoxazoline esters.
Synthesis, optimization and structure-activity relationships of 3,5-disubstituted isoxazolines as new anti-tuberculosis agents.
1-methyl-1 H-pyrazole-5-carboxamide derivatives inhibit the development of Haemonchus contortus larvae, with the first identified compound showing an IC50 of 0.29 µM.
Optimization of Novel 1-Methyl-1 H-Pyrazole-5-carboxamides Leads to High Potency Larval Development Inhibitors of the Barber's Pole Worm.
N-Mannich bases derived from 5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamide sulfonamides are more potent antibacterials than the parent compound.
Synthesis and in vitro study of novel Mannich bases as antibacterial agents.
1,6-di(pyrid-2-yl)pyrene is a substrate that can be functionalized at the 2- and 2,7-positions via Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H bond activation.
The synthesis of 2- and 2,7-functionalized pyrene derivatives through Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H activation.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

At high concentrations, methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) induces aberrant HBV capsid protein assembly, favoring hexagonal arrays and promoting dissociation of metastable capsids by scavenging capsid protein and redirecting assembly into hexamer-rich structures.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.
Quinolinium dichromate in water oxidizes vicinal and nonvicinal diols to their corresponding alpha-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
Quinolinium Dichromate Oxidation of Diols: A Kinetic Study.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.

1,4-bis[(6-hydroxymethyl-2-pyridyl)-2-oxapropyl]benzene forms a 1:1 complex with Cu(II) or Zn(II) that acts as the active catalytic species for the hydrolysis of p-nitrophenyl phosphate in CTAB micellar solution at 30°C.
Metallomicellar catalysis: hydrolysis of phosphate monoester and phosphodiester by Cu(II), Zn(II) complexes of pyridyl ligands in CTAB micellar solution.
The formation clearance ratio of harmol glucuronide in rat liver tissue decreases as the rate of oxygen delivery decreases, with the effect being more pronounced in the fasted state.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.
Ferric tetramesitylporphyrin can be oxidized by m-CPBA in butyronitrile at -78°C to generate six-coordinate oxoiron(IV) tetramesitylporphyrin complexes with a halide ion as the second axial ligand.

Generation of oxoiron (IV) tetramesitylporphyrin pi-cation radical complexes by m-CPBA oxidation of ferric tetramesitylporphyrin derivatives in butyronitrile at - 78 degrees C. Evidence for the formation of six-coordinate oxoiron (IV) tetramesitylporphyrin pi-cation radical complexes FeIV = O(tmp*)X (X = Cl-, Br-), by Mössbauer and X-ray absorption spectroscopy.

2-(1-benzyl-1H-indol-3-yl)-2-oxo-N-phenylacetamide (KI-7) is a positive allosteric modulator of the A2B adenosine receptor in mesenchymal stem cells, potentiating the effects of adenosine and synthetic A2B AR agonists on osteoblast differentiation in vitro.
Osteoblast differentiation and survival: A role for A2B adenosine receptor allosteric modulators.
5-deoxystrigol, isolated from root exudates of Lotus japonicus, induces hyphal branching morphogenesis in arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi, a critical step in host recognition.
Plant sesquiterpenes induce hyphal branching in arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi.

An octasaccharide sequence within heparin corresponds to the minimum oligosaccharide required for binding to annexin V, with a Kd of approximately 1 µM and a binding stoichiometry of 1:1 as determined by isothermal titration calorimetry.
Interaction of heparin with annexin V.

Samarium(III) triflate (Sm(OTf)3) is used to activate benzylated glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones, enabling them to react with simple and carbohydrate alcohols to yield β-mixtures of glycosides in moderate to excellent yield.
A glycosylation protocol based on activation of glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones with samarium triflate.
8-(Methylsulfonyl)FAD reacts with a single cysteine residue (Cys293) in the flavin domain of Escherichia coli DNA photolyase to form a covalent 8-(cysteinyl)FAD derivative.
Affinity probing of flavin binding sites. 2. Identification of a reactive cysteine in the flavin domain of Escherichia coli DNA photolyase.
Quinolinium dichromate-mediated oxidation of diols proceeds almost twice as fast in D2O than in H2O.
Quinolinium Dichromate Oxidation of Diols: A Kinetic Study.

N-(anilinomethyl)-p-isopropoxyphenylsuccinimide (AMIPPS) at 37.5 mg/kg significantly enhances the anticonvulsant activity of phenobarbital and valproate in the maximal electroshock seizure test in mice.
N-(anilinomethyl)-p-isopropoxyphenylsuccinimide potentiates the anticonvulsant action of phenobarbital and valproate in the mouse maximal electroshock-induced seizure model.

6-(5-Cholesten-3 beta-yloxy)hexyl 1-thio-beta-D-mannopyranoside (L-644,257) enhances natural host resistance against Pseudomonas aeruginosa in cyclophosphamide-treated mice in a dose-dependent manner.
Glycolipids as host resistance stimulators.

2-[bis-(3,5-bis-trifluoromethyl-phenyl)trimethylsilanyloxy-methyl]-pyrrolidine acts as an organocatalyst for the asymmetric epoxidation of a,b-unsaturated aldehydes in water-alcohol solutions using hydrogen peroxide as the oxidant.
Organocatalytic asymmetric epoxidation reactions in water-alcohol solutions.

2,6-bis(((2-methoxyethyl)-(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)amino)methyl)-4-methylphenol acts as a ligand forming zinc(II) complexes that function as competent catalysts for the hydrolysis of bis(2,4-dinitrophenyl) phosphate (BDNPP), with catalytic activities of k(cat) = 5.70 ± 0.04 × 10⁻⁳ s⁻¹ and k(cat) = 3.60 ± 0.04 × 10⁻⁳ s⁻¹ for [Zn(2)(CH(3)L2)]+ and [Zn(2)(CH(3)L3)]+, respectively, and exhibit a catalytically relevant pKa of 6.7 for [Zn(2)(CH(3)L2)]+ and 7.7 for [Zn(2)(CH(3)L3)].
The role of Zn-OR and Zn-OH nucleophiles and the influence of para-substituents in the reactions of binuclear phosphatase mimetics.

N-(anilinomethyl)-p-isopropoxyphenylsuccinimide (AMIPPS) at 18.75 mg/kg does not affect the antiseizure action of phenobarbital and valproate against maximal electroshock seizure-induced seizures in mice.
N-(anilinomethyl)-p-isopropoxyphenylsuccinimide potentiates the anticonvulsant action of phenobarbital and valproate in the mouse maximal electroshock-induced seizure model.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

Disodium 1-[2-(carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a low molecular weight nitric oxide donor that, when incorporated into electrospun polymer fibers, enables nitric oxide release upon immersion in aqueous solutions at physiological pH (7.4, 37°C).
Nitric oxide-releasing electrospun polymer microfibers.

Pentagalloylglucopyranosyl, when sulfated at positions 2 through 6, forms an allosteric inhibitor (SPGG) that binds to multiple anion-binding sites on factor XIa, contributing nearly 87% of binding energy through nonionic interactions.
Designing allosteric inhibitors of factor XIa. Lessons from the interactions of sulfated pentagalloylglucopyranosides.

At high concentrations, methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) induces aberrant HBV capsid protein assembly, favoring hexagonal arrays and promoting dissociation of metastable capsids by scavenging capsid protein and redirecting assembly into hexamer-rich structures.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.

2′-deoxyguanosine-5′-tetraphosphate can be efficiently incorporated into DNA during polymerase reactions when modified with four different length coumarin-PEG tags at the 5′-terminal phosphate, allowing for single-molecule discrimination of the tags by nanopore ionic current blockade.
PEG-labeled nucleotides and nanopore detection for single molecule DNA sequencing by synthesis.

N-(4'-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) binds to the α-site of tryptophan synthase and its CF3 groups provide 19F NMR signals that sample the channel microenvironment at the α-β subunit interface, allowing distinction between open and closed β-subunit conformations.
Allostery and substrate channeling in the tryptophan synthase bienzyme complex: evidence for two subunit conformations and four quaternary states.

Tetra(4-fluorophenyl) phosphonium (F4-TPP+) binds to EmrE and, in phospholipid bilayers, its structure revealed that it lies closer to the proton-binding residue E14 in one subunit than the other subunit in the dimer, explaining the asymmetric protonation observed in NMR studies.
Structure and dynamics of the drug-bound bacterial transporter EmrE in lipid bilayers.
N-propargyloxycarbamate-glucosamine is a chemical reporter that displays unique labeling efficiencies and can be metabolically converted to enter the O-GlcNAcylation pathway.
N-Propargyloxycarbamate monosaccharides as metabolic chemical reporters of carbohydrate salvage pathways and protein glycosylation.

Fmoc-arginine-glycine-aspartic acid (Fmoc-RGD) is an integrin-binding peptide ligand that, when incorporated into a nanofibrous hydrogel with Fmoc-FF, supports the maintenance and function of primary human hepatocytes.
Peptide-based scaffolds for the culture and maintenance of primary human hepatocytes.
4'-ethynylflavone (4′EF) is a time-dependent inhibitor (TDI) of P450 1A1, docking into the enzyme's active site with its acetylene group oriented toward the heme.
Ethynylflavones, highly potent, and selective inhibitors of cytochrome P450 1A1.
18F-labeled RGD peptide generated by tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation demonstrates prominent tumor uptake in vivo and confirmed receptor specificity.
Tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation for the rapid construction of integrin αvβ₃ targeted PET tracer based on a cyclic RGD peptide.
Aza-asparaginyl Michael acceptors are inhibitors specific for asparaginyl endopeptidases (legumains), exhibiting lowest IC50 values in the picomolar range across multiple parasite species.
Aza-peptidyl Michael acceptors. A new class of potent and selective inhibitors of asparaginyl endopeptidases (legumains) from evolutionarily diverse pathogens.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid is a histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands the pool of renal progenitor cells in zebrafish embryos in a proliferation-dependent manner.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.
Nicotinoyl azide is a small molecule that, upon light activation, enables measurement of solvent accessibility of purine nucleobases in RNA.
Light-activated chemical probing of nucleobase solvent accessibility inside cells.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.
5-azidoneuraminic acid (Neu5N3) can be incorporated into α2-8-linked sialosides via a chemoenzymatic α2-8-sialylation system using Neu5Acα2-3GalβpNP as an acceptor.
Parallel chemoenzymatic synthesis of sialosides containing a C5-diversified sialic acid.

3-(methoxyamino)propylamine (MAPA) is a multifunctional fluorescent linker that can be used for glycan derivatization, enabling efficient conjugation with both synthetic and natural glycans under mild conditions.
Novel Reversible Fluorescent Glycan Linker for Functional Glycomics.

Enzymatic dephosphorylation of resveratrol triphosphate to resveratrol occurs in the stratum corneum of pigskin, as indicated by the absence of the 995 cm⁻¹ resveratrol band in Raman spectra after heat treatment.
Tracking the dephosphorylation of resveratrol triphosphate in skin by confocal Raman microscopy.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.
23-COOH-24,25,26,27-tetranor D3 is an end product of 25OHD3 metabolism through the C-24 oxidation pathway in rat kidney.

23-carboxy-24,25,26,27-tetranorvitamin D3 (calcioic acid) and 24-carboxy-25,26,27-trinorvitamin D3 (cholacalcioic acid): end products of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 metabolism in rat kidney through C-24 oxidation pathway.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

3-aminomethyl cyclohexane carboxylic acid, when incorporated as the P2 amino acid in peptidomimetic BACE-1 inhibitors, enables a novel binding mode with the S3 pocket of BACE-1, forming a hydrogen bond interaction pattern distinct from previous designs.
Structure-based design and synthesis of novel P2/P3 modified, non-peptidic beta-secretase (BACE-1) inhibitors.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives with alkylamino side chains act as highly selective ligands for binding to telomeric G-quadruplex DNA, effectively stabilizing the G-quadruplex structure and showing significant selectivity over duplex DNA.
Disubstituted 2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives as a new type of highly selective ligands for telomeric G-quadruplex DNA.
Phosphorylation of the pThr-Pro peptide bond in the cytoplasmic tail of amyloid precursor protein (APP) leads to prolyl isomerization, with 9% of the cis isomer present after phosphorylation at T20.
Phosphorylation-induced structural changes in the amyloid precursor protein cytoplasmic tail detected by NMR.
A pyridazin-4-one fragment acts as a cathepsin S inhibitor with an IC50 of 170 µM, serving as a template for designing more potent CatS inhibitors.
Diazinones as P2 replacements for pyrazole-based cathepsin S inhibitors.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid serves as the central core structure in a new series of nonpeptidic bacterial peptide deformylase (PDF) inhibitors.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.

The thermolytic deprotection of 4-methylthio-1-butyl phosphate groups occurs via cyclodeesterification, resulting in the formation of a sulfonium salt side product that is inert to DNA nucleobases and does not cause significant desulfurization of phosphorothioate diesters.
Thermolytic 4-methylthio-1-butyl group for phosphate/thiophosphate protection in solid-phase synthesis of DNA oligonucleotides.
(S)-3-cyanophenylalanine reacts with NaN3 in the presence of H2O and Lewis acids (ZnCl2 and CdCl2) to form homochiral 3D coordination networks.
Homochiral Zn and Cd coordination polymers containing amino acid-tetrazole ligands.
Pyridazinopyridinone compounds have been designed as novel p38 kinase inhibitors with demonstrated potency in enzyme and human whole blood assays.
Discovery of pyridazinopyridinones as potent and selective p38 mitogen-activated protein kinase inhibitors.
N-(2-morpholinoethyl)nicotinamide derivatives are potent and selective inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) over MAO-B.
Design of novel nicotinamides as potent and selective monoamine oxidase a inhibitors.

3-isobutyl-4H-1,2,4-benzothiadiazine 1,1-dioxide compounds have been synthesized and their activity on pancreatic β-cells (insulin release inhibition) and vascular and uterine smooth muscle tissues (myorelaxant effects) has been compared to other K ATP channel openers.
Impact of the nature of the substituent at the 3-position of 4H-1,2,4-benzothiadiazine 1,1-dioxides on their opening activity toward ATP-sensitive potassium channels.
Trans-3,4-methano-L-syn-arginine acts as a good substrate for nitric oxide synthase isoforms with potency close to L-arginine and similar isoform selectivities.
Synthesis of a series of 3,4-methanoarginines as side-chain conformationally restricted analogues of arginine.
3-thienylalanine is identified as an especially good substrate for phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase (PheRS) based on computational predictions using the HierDock protocol.
Virtual screening for binding of phenylalanine analogues to phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase.

4-(1H-pyrazol-1-yl)-3-pyridinesulfonamide derivatives exhibit potent inhibition of human carbonic anhydrase IX (hCA IX) with K(I) values ranging from 19.5 to 48.6 nM, comparable or more effective than clinically used sulfonamides such as AAZ, MZA, EZA, DCP, and IND (K(I) = 24–50 nM).

Carbonic anhydrase inhibitors. Synthesis, molecular structures, and inhibition of the human cytosolic isozymes I and II and transmembrane isozymes IX, XII (cancer-associated) and XIV with novel 3-pyridinesulfonamide derivatives.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives have been studied for their ability to inhibit telomerase and their cellular effects, with compound 11b identified as a promising telomerase inhibitor and telomeric G-quadruplex binding ligand for potential cancer treatment.
Disubstituted 2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives as a new type of highly selective ligands for telomeric G-quadruplex DNA.

2-(2-pyridyl)imidazo[4,5- f][1,10]-phenanthroline (obip) serves as a ligand in the dinuclear Ru(II) complex Ru2(obip)L4, which stabilizes and promotes the formation of human telomeric repeat AG3(T2AG3)3 quadruplex DNA structures.
Promoting the formation and stabilization of G-quadruplex by dinuclear RuII complex Ru2(obip)L4.
Hexane-bisammonium-type compounds containing lateral phthalimide moieties have high affinity for the allosteric site of muscarinic M2 receptors.
Structure-activity relationships in a series of bisquaternary bisphthalimidine derivatives modulating the muscarinic M(2)-receptor allosterically.

Disodium 1-[(2-Carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a nitric oxide donor incorporated into nanofiber gels that reduces neointimal hyperplasia by 77% (intima/media area ratio, 0.19 ± 0.03) and inhibits vascular smooth muscle cell proliferation in vivo by 62% (1.06 ± 0.30 vs. injury alone 2.02 ± 0.20, P < 0.05) in a rat carotid artery injury model.
Nitric oxide and nanotechnology: a novel approach to inhibit neointimal hyperplasia.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

At high concentrations, methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) induces aberrant HBV capsid protein assembly, favoring hexagonal arrays and promoting dissociation of metastable capsids by scavenging capsid protein and redirecting assembly into hexamer-rich structures.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid is a histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands the pool of renal progenitor cells in zebrafish embryos in a proliferation-dependent manner.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.

Tetra(4-fluorophenyl) phosphonium (F4-TPP+) binds to EmrE and, in phospholipid bilayers, its structure revealed that it lies closer to the proton-binding residue E14 in one subunit than the other subunit in the dimer, explaining the asymmetric protonation observed in NMR studies.
Structure and dynamics of the drug-bound bacterial transporter EmrE in lipid bilayers.
(-)-2-(6-[ 18 F] fluoro-2,3′-bipyridin-5′-yl)-7-methyl-7-aza-bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane ([ 18 F]AZAN) is a radioligand antagonist of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nAChR) developed for brain imaging.
Development of radioligands with optimized imaging properties for quantification of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors by positron emission tomography.

(-)-2-(6-[ 18 F]fluoro-2,3′-bipyridin-5′yl)-7-methyl-7-aza-bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane ([ 18 F]AZAN) is a highly specific nAChR radioligand with rapid brain kinetics, high blood-to-brain ratio (BP), and high uptake (%SUV) in PET imaging.
Development of radioligands with optimized imaging properties for quantification of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors by positron emission tomography.

7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) is a potent tubulin-polymerization inhibitor with high in vitro cytotoxic activity (GI50 1.9–3.2 nM), significant tubulin assembly inhibition (IC50 0.77 µM), and substantial inhibition of colchicine binding (99% at 5 µM).
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid (PTBA) is a small molecule histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands embryonic kidney progenitor cells (EKPCs) in zebrafish embryos.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.
2- and 12-biphenyl dibenzo[a,g]quinolizin-7-ium derivatives act as enhancers of FtsZ self-polymerization in purified Staphylococcus aureus FtsZ.
Antibacterial activity of substituted dibenzo[a,g]quinolizin-7-ium derivatives.
18F-labeled RGD peptide generated by tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation demonstrates prominent tumor uptake in vivo and confirmed receptor specificity.
Tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation for the rapid construction of integrin αvβ₃ targeted PET tracer based on a cyclic RGD peptide.

methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) is a heteroaryldihydropyrimidine antiviral that inhibits hepatitis B virus (HBV) production by enhancing the rate and extent of capsid protein assembly in vitro.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.

Tetra(4-fluorophenyl) phosphonium (F4-TPP+) binds to the EmrE transporter in phospholipid bilayers, with its binding site dominated by aromatic residues such as W63 and Y60, and it lies closer to the proton-binding residue E14 in subunit A than in subunit B, resulting in asymmetric protonation of the protein.
Structure and dynamics of the drug-bound bacterial transporter EmrE in lipid bilayers.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.

3Beta-(4-Methylphenyl)-2beta-[3'-(4-fluorobenzyl)isoxazol-5-yl]tropane exhibits high selectivity for the dopamine transporter (IC50 = 5.9 nM, Ki at NET = 454 nM, Ki at 5-HTT = 113 nM) among tested analogs.
Synthesis and monoamine transporter binding properties of 2beta-[3'-(substituted benzyl)isoxazol-5-yl]- and 2beta-[3'-methyl-4'-(substituted phenyl)isoxazol-5-yl]-3beta-(substituted phenyl)tropanes.
23-COOH-24,25,26,27-tetranor D3 is an end product of 25OHD3 metabolism through the C-24 oxidation pathway in rat kidney.

23-carboxy-24,25,26,27-tetranorvitamin D3 (calcioic acid) and 24-carboxy-25,26,27-trinorvitamin D3 (cholacalcioic acid): end products of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 metabolism in rat kidney through C-24 oxidation pathway.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.

Upon capturing hydroxyl radicals, 17beta-butoxy-1,3,5(10)-estratrien-3-ol undergoes a chemical transformation in which its phenolic A-ring becomes 10beta-hydroxy-17beta-butoxy-1,3,5(10)-estratrien-3-one, a non-aromatic para-quinol, resulting in complete loss of estrogen-receptor affinity and antioxidant activity.
Mechanistic investigations on the antioxidant action of a neuroprotective estrogen derivative.
4-amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) derivatives represent a novel class of anti-cancer agents with activity influenced by the nature of substituents at the R1, R2, and R3 positions.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).
5-Substituted 2-aminothiophenes as A1 adenosine receptor allosteric enhancers.
7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) causes cell arrest in the G2/M phase, disrupts microtubule formation, and competes at the colchicine site on tubulin.
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.
2-deoxy-D-glucosyl fluoride acts as a glycosyl donor, providing excellent β-selectivity and high yields in glycosylation reactions with various nucleophiles, including 2-deoxy-D-galactosyl fluoride.
Glycosyl disulfides: novel glycosylating reagents with flexible aglycon alteration.
2-deoxyglucosyl fluoride is tolerated in C-glycosylation reactions with potassium phenylacetylene trifluoroborate, yielding the R-isomer product in 92% yield as a single product.
Direct C-glycosylation of organotrifluoroborates with glycosyl fluorides and its application to the total synthesis of (+)-varitriol.

4,4-dimethyl-2-oxovaleric acid as a substrate in reductive amination with [Rh[(R,R)-Deguphos](COD)]BF 4 yields N-benzylated amino acids in good to excellent yields and high enantiomeric excess within 2–3 hours.
The first highly enantioselective homogeneously catalyzed asymmetric reductive amination: synthesis of alpha-N-benzylamino acids.
2-deoxy-D-glucosyl fluoride acts as a glycosyl donor, providing excellent β-selectivity and high yields in glycosylation reactions with various nucleophiles, including 2-deoxy-D-galactosyl fluoride.
Diastereoselective synthesis of beta-Aryl-C-nucleosides from 1,2-anhydrosugars.
2-deoxyglucosyl fluoride is tolerated in C-glycosylation reactions with potassium phenylacetylene trifluoroborate, yielding the R-isomer product in 92% yield as a single product.
Diastereoselective synthesis of beta-Aryl-C-nucleosides from 1,2-anhydrosugars.
2-deoxy-D-glucosyl fluoride acts as a glycosyl donor, providing excellent β-selectivity and high yields in glycosylation reactions with various nucleophiles, including 2-deoxy-D-galactosyl fluoride.
Glycosylation with 2-Acetamido-2-deoxyglycosyl Donors at a Low Temperature: Scope of the Non-Oxazoline Method.

Reaction of 1,2-orthoesters with HF-pyridine: a method for the preparation of partly unprotected glycosyl fluorides and their use in saccharide synthesis.

Replacement of the C-3 hydroxy group with an amino group in 2-amino-D-glucose-6-phosphate maintains substrate activity toward DOIS, demonstrating that the C-3 amino group acts as a hydrogen bond donor.

4,6-O-[1-cyano-2-(2-iodophenyl)ethylidene] acetals. improved second-generation acetals for the stereoselective formation of beta-D-mannopyranosides and regioselective reductive radical fragmentation to beta-D-rhamnopyranosides. scope and limitations.
2-deoxy-D-glucosyl fluoride acts as a glycosyl donor, providing excellent β-selectivity and high yields in glycosylation reactions with various nucleophiles, including 2-deoxy-D-galactosyl fluoride.
Trimethylsilylnitrate-trimethylsilyl azide: a novel reagent system for the synthesis of 2-deoxyglycosyl azides from glycals. Application in the synthesis of 2-deoxy-beta-N-glycopeptides.

The 4,7-dioxoheptanoic acid 9-fluorenylmethyl ester reacts with lysyl ε-amino groups to generate CEP esters that can be deprotected with 1,8-diazabicyclo [5.4.0]undec-7-ene (DBU) without causing protein denaturation.
Protein Modification of Lysine with 2-(2-Styrylcyclopropyl)ethanal.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
Concise means for accessing an advanced precursor to 1-deoxypaclitaxel.
Landomycin A inhibits the uptake of [3H]thymidine into DNA in murine smooth muscle cells, indicating decreased DNA synthesis.
Landomycin A inhibits DNA synthesis and G1/S cell cycle progression.

An octasaccharide sequence within heparin corresponds to the minimum oligosaccharide required for binding to annexin V, with a Kd of approximately 1 µM and a binding stoichiometry of 1:1 as determined by isothermal titration calorimetry.
Interaction of heparin with annexin V.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.

Octathymidylate, when covalently linked to an intercalating agent and complementary to a crucial A-T-rich region in the SV40 origin of replication, inhibits replication of SV40 viral DNA in CV1 cells by binding to open regions or forming local triple helices.
Inhibition of simian virus 40 DNA replication in CV-1 cells by an oligodeoxynucleotide covalently linked to an intercalating agent.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.

2-naphthylethyl-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (THI 53) inhibits LPS-mediated iNOS protein induction by reducing phosphorylation of tyrosine kinases, especially Tyk2 and STAT-1, and by affecting the JAK2/STAT-1 pathway.

Regulation of lipopolysaccharide-induced inducible nitric-oxide synthase expression through the nuclear factor-kappaB pathway and interferon-beta/tyrosine kinase 2/Janus tyrosine kinase 2-signal transducer and activator of transcription-1 signaling cascades by 2-naphthylethyl-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (THI 53), a new synthetic isoquinoline alkaloid.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Miniaturization of a liquid membrane sensor for the determination of bile acids.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) inhibits apoptosis and reduces infarct size in myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury by activating the PI3K/Akt pathway and reducing inflammation.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) reduces rat myocardial apoptosis against ischemia and reperfusion injury by activation of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/Akt signaling and anti-inflammatory action in vivo.
Integramycin is a complex natural product with significant and novel activity against HIV-1 integrase, making it a promising candidate for new anti-HIV therapy.
Structure, stereochemistry, and biological activity of integramycin, a novel hexacyclic natural product produced by Actinoplanes sp. that inhibits HIV-1 integrase.

Tetra(3-hydroxyphenyl)porphyrin is a lipophilic photosensitizer that, when combined with light, induces a rapid apoptotic response in MDCK II cells and triggers a bystander effect, where neighboring cells are also induced to apoptosis.
The bystander effect in photodynamic inactivation of cells.

3,6-diamino-5-cyano-4-phenyl-thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxylic acid (4-bromophenyl)amide (TEP) is a competitive ATP inhibitor of histidine kinase autophosphorylation and inhibits autophosphorylation of histidine kinases from four diverse bacterial species without inhibiting a panel of 10 human Serine/Threonine kinases.
New class of competitive inhibitor of bacterial histidine kinases.
Octathymidylate can be covalently linked to an acridine derivative and, upon UV irradiation, forms a local triple helix with viral DNA at A-T rich regions within the viral origin of replication.
Inhibition of simian virus 40 DNA replication in CV-1 cells by an oligodeoxynucleotide covalently linked to an intercalating agent.

3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-dibenz[a,h]anthracene is a distal bisdihydrodiol metabolite of dibenz[a,h]anthracene that forms highly polar DNA adducts contributing 38% ± 12 pmol/mg DNA to adducts in Aroclor 1254-treated rat liver microsome incubations.
Characterization of highly polar DNA adducts derived from dibenz[A,H]anthracene (DBA), 3,4-dihydroxy-3,4-dihydro-DBA, and 3,4,10,11-tetrahydroxy-3,4,10,11-tetrahydro-DBA.
The formation clearance ratio of harmol glucuronide in rat liver tissue decreases as the rate of oxygen delivery decreases, with the effect being more pronounced in the fasted state.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.

3-aza-9-hydroxyfluoren-9(R)-ylcarbonyl-l-prolyl-2-aminomethyl-5-chlorobenzylamide is a potent thrombin inhibitor with a K(i) of 0.40 nM and 2x APTT of 0.18 µM, excellent pharmacokinetic properties (F = 55%, T1/2 = 14 h in dogs), and complete efficacy in inhibiting FeCl3-induced vessel occlusions in a rat in vivo thrombosis model at 10 µg/kg/min intravenous infusion.
9-hydroxyazafluorenes and their use in thrombin inhibitors.
(24S)-2α,3α,5α,22,23-pentahydroxystigmastan-6-one is a synthetic brassinosteroid derivative that inhibits HSV-1 and arenavirus replication in cell culture.
Antiviral effect of brassinosteroids against herpes virus and arenaviruses.
8-(Methylsulfonyl)FAD reacts with a single cysteine residue (Cys293) in the flavin domain of Escherichia coli DNA photolyase to form a covalent 8-(cysteinyl)FAD derivative.
Affinity probing of flavin binding sites. 2. Identification of a reactive cysteine in the flavin domain of Escherichia coli DNA photolyase.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

4-aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione is a core structural component of isoniazid-based triads that, when combined with isoniazid and isoniazid, confers promising anti-mycobacterial activity against the mc26230 strain of M. tuberculosis with MIC values in the range of 5.1–11.9 µM and non-cytotoxicity to Vero cells.
Design and synthesis of 4-Aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione-isoniazid triads as potential anti-mycobacterials.

Vanillin semicarbazone exhibits potent anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma cells in mice, with efficacy comparable to bleomycin at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg (i.p.), when administered at 10 mg/kg (i.p.).
In vivo anticancer activity of vanillin semicarbazone.

Vanillin semicarbazone exhibits potent anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma cells in mice, with efficacy comparable to bleomycin at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg (i.p.), when administered at 10 mg/kg (i.p.).
In vivo anticancer activity of vanillin semicarbazone.

3,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid N-oxide (H2-pydcao) serves as an organic linker in the synthesis of the three-dimensional metal-organic framework [Zn(pydcao)(DMF)], where it acts as the ligand coordinating to Zn(II) centers.
A Zn(ii) metal-organic framework with dinuclear [Zn2(N-oxide)2] secondary building units.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.

Citronellyl 6-(dimethylamino)hexanoate is a hybrid terpene-amino acid ester that acts as an outstanding skin penetration enhancer, increasing epidermal cidofovir concentration by 15-fold and showing up to 82-fold enhancement in drug delivery with low cellular toxicity.
Esters of terpene alcohols as highly potent, reversible, and low toxic skin penetration enhancers.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.

Ethyl 4-oxo-2-phenyl-1,4-dihydroquinoline-6-carboxylate (AS1712) can overcome P-glycoprotein efflux pump and beta-tubulin alterations that confer resistance to microtubule-targeting drugs in cancer cells.
4(1H)-quinolone derivatives overcome acquired resistance to anti-microtubule agents by targeting the colchicine site of β-tubulin.

2,5-dimethyl-1H-pyrrole-3,4-dicarbaldehyde is proposed as a new derivatizing reagent for HPLC analysis of amino acids, with structural analogy to OPA and the potential to react rapidly and selectively with amino functions under mild conditions.
2,5-Dimethyl-1H-pyrrole-3,4-dicarbaldehyde as a precolumn derivatization reagent for HPLC/UV detection of amino acids.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.
