4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid is a histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands the pool of renal progenitor cells in zebrafish embryos in a proliferation-dependent manner.
Inhibition of histone deacetylase expands the renal progenitor cell population.

Nothospondin is a quassinoid isolated from Nothospondias staudtii that potently and dose-dependently inhibits the oncogenic transcription factor activator protein-1 (AP-1) at noncytotoxic concentrations.
Nothospondin, a new AP-1 inhibitory quassinoid from the Cameroonian plant Nothospondias staudtii.
In human bladder cancer T24 cells, 5, 10, 15, 20-tetrakis (5-(pyrrolidin-1-yl) pentyl) porphin (TPPP) shows low dark toxicity and high phototoxicity under photodynamic therapy.
Antitumor activity evaluation of meso-tetra (pyrrolidine substituted) pentylporphin-mediated photodynamic therapy in vitro and in vivo.

4-(tridecyloxy)benzaldehyde oxime is non-cytotoxic to keratinocyte cells at concentrations used to enhance ascorbyl palmitate stability in microemulsions, although cytotoxicity increases with higher microemulsion concentrations.
A new approach for increasing ascorbyl palmitate stability by addition of non-irritant co-antioxidant.

2-ethylamino-curcumin derivatives with substituted triazole moieties act as TRAIL sensitizers in TRAIL-resistant human CRT-MG astroglioma cells, exhibiting sensitization effects without or with slight cytotoxicity.
Synthesis of diethylamino-curcumin mimics with substituted triazolyl groups and their sensitization effect of TRAIL against brain cancer cells.
Imidazole-1-sulfonyl azide tetrafluoroborate salt is used to catalyze a DNA-compatible diazo-transfer reaction that converts primary amines into azides in good to excellent yields.
DNA-Compatible Diazo-Transfer Reaction in Aqueous Media Suitable for DNA-Encoded Chemical Library Synthesis.

Cytarabine ocfosfate hydrate is an oral chemotherapeutic agent that can induce persistent molecular complete remission in elderly patients with relapsed acute myeloid leukemia (AML) harboring an inv(16)(p13.1q22) genetic abnormality.
Persistent molecular remission of refractory acute myeloid leukemia with inv(16)(p13.1q22) in an elderly patient induced by cytarabine ocfosfate hydrate.

Calcium phthalocyanine (CAPC) induces DNA damage, including alkali-labile lesions and both Proteinase K-sensitive and Proteinase K-resistant cross-links, in Chinese hamster V79 cells after 18 hours of incubation with 1 µM CAPC and red light exposure.
Induction of DNA-protein cross-links in Chinese hamster cells by the photodynamic action of chloroaluminum phthalocyanine and visible light.

Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate) is essentially inactive compared to ryanodine for insecticidal activity against Musca domestica adults and Tribolium castaneum larvae, for toxicity to mice, and for competition with [3H]ryanodine at the Ca(2+)-ryanodine receptor complex of skeletal muscle.
Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate): a novel ryanoid from Ryania insecticide.

RC-3095 acetate, a bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide (GRP) antagonist, administered subcutaneously twice daily at 10 micrograms for 4 weeks suppresses tumor growth in xenograft models of MCF-7 MIII human breast cancer in nude mice.
Inhibitory effect of bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide (GRP) antagonists RC-3950-II and RC-3095 on MCF-7 MIII human breast cancer xenografts in nude mice.
H-7 induces rapid changes in astrocyte morphology that are similar to those elicited by PMA.
Astrocyte morphology altered by 1-(5-isoquinolinylsulfonyl) 2-methyl piperazine (H-7) and other protein kinase inhibitors.
Uridine monophosphorus, when administered as a conjugate targeted to hepatocytes, protects hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
Targeted protection of hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
Photolabile application of tyrosyl-butanoyl-spermine analogues in the presence of neural stimulation or L-glutamate results in complete and irreversible antagonism of muscle twitch, EPSC, and qGluR.
The action of philanthotoxin-343 and photolabile analogues on locust (Schistocerca gregaria) muscle.
Azido galactosamine (GalNAz) allows for subsequent glycan-profiling and visualization of glycoproteins.
Metabolic oligosaccharide engineering as a tool for glycobiology.
Azido galactosamine can be used for cell surface labeling in cancer cells when combined with isonitrile-labelled sugars for dual metabolic labeling.
Metabolic oligosaccharide engineering as a tool for glycobiology.

Roritoxin B is a macrocyclic trichothecene mycotoxin that is among the most potent compounds evaluated for immunotoxic activity in mice, stimulating mitogen-induced lymphocyte blastogenesis in splenic cells.
Effects of macrocyclic trichothecene congeners on the viability and mitogenesis of murine splenic lymphocytes.
7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D exhibits superior antitumor activity against CCRF-CEM, P388, and L1210 cell lines, attributed to its ability to covalently attach to DNA.
Carbon-7 substituted actinomycin D analogues as improved antitumor agents: synthesis and DNA-binding and biological properties.
Among N 6 -phenyladenosine derivatives, 4-chloro- and 4-bromo-N 6 phenyladenosines exhibit EC 50 values of about 0.5 µM at the A 2B receptor in a cyclic AMP production assay with CHO-A 2B cells.
2-Substituted adenosine derivatives: affinity and efficacy at four subtypes of human adenosine receptors.
7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D retains DNA binding property and enhances in vivo tumor-inhibiting activity compared to actinomycin D analogues.
Carbon-7 substituted actinomycin D analogues as improved antitumor agents: synthesis and DNA-binding and biological properties.
D-Phe-Cys-Tyr-D-Trp-Orn-Thr-Pen-Thr-NH(2) is a mu-selective opioid receptor antagonist that reduces alcohol consumption in a limited access model in alcohol-preferring C57BL/6 mice.
The effect of antagonists selective for mu- and delta-opioid receptor subtypes on alcohol consumption in C57BL/6 mice.
N-benzyl-D-glucamine dithiocarbamate is an N,N-disubstituted dithiocarbamate that effectively mobilizes cadmium from hepatic and renal tissue without promoting redistribution to the brain.
Optimization of chelating agent structure for the mobilization of aged renal and hepatic cadmium deposits: sodium N-benzyl-4-O-(beta-D-galactopyranosyl)-D-glucamine-N-carbodithioate.
In mouse K-Cl cotransport systems containing HbC or HbS and HbF, the sensitivity to DIOA is increased, indicating that DIOA acts as an inhibitor of the cotransport in these modified systems.
Expression of HbC and HbS, but not HbA, results in activation of K-Cl cotransport activity in transgenic mouse red cells.

Cholesterol-lysine, when used to construct a lipopeptide analog, does not induce strong T cell-dependent protective immunity against lethal HSV-1 infection in mice compared to Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine.

Lipopeptide epitopes extended by an Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine moiety increase uptake and maturation of dendritic cells through a Toll-like receptor-2 pathway and trigger a Th1-dependent protective immunity.
Satratoxin F, a Type D trichothecene, does not increase COX-2 promoter activity in transfected cells at concentrations equivalent to its toxicity.
Relationship of trichothecene structure to COX-2 induction in the macrophage: selective action of type B (8-keto) trichothecenes.

1-deoxy-1-propyl-amino-fructose (Amadori product) at 1 mM concentration does not significantly affect AGE-binding to galectin-3 in anti-Galectin-3 immunoprecipitated RAW 264.7 cell lysates, as measured by ligand blot analysis with 125I-AGE-BSA.
Identification of galectin-3 as a high-affinity binding protein for advanced glycation end products (AGE): a new member of the AGE-receptor complex.
19-oxodesacetylcinobufagin is a bufadienolide isolated from Ch'an Su that exhibits significant inhibitory activity against the KB and HL-60 cancer cell lines.
Isolation and structure of five new cancer cell growth inhibitory bufadienolides from the Chinese traditional drug Ch'an Su.
19-oxodesacetylcinobufagin is a bufadienolide isolated from Ch'an Su that exhibits cancer cell growth inhibitory activity against KB, HL-60, and MH-60 cancer cell lines.
Isolation and structure of five new cancer cell growth inhibitory bufadienolides from the Chinese traditional drug Ch'an Su.
N-(3,5-difluorophenyl)-2- pyridinecarbothioamide is a potent selective mucosal protectant that is equipotent with ranitidine against gastric injury caused by stress in rats.
N-phenyl-2-pyridinecarbothioamides as gastric mucosal protectants.
N-(3,5-difluorophenyl)-2- pyridinecarbothioamide is more potent than sucralfate and ranitidine in protecting against ethanol-induced gastric lesions in rats.
N-phenyl-2-pyridinecarbothioamides as gastric mucosal protectants.
POM-l-BHDU-MP is a phosphate ester prodrug of l-BHDU monophosphate developed to improve the pharmacokinetic profile and antiviral potency of l-BHDU against varicella-zoster virus (VZV).
Prodrug Strategies for the Development of β-l-5-((E)-2-Bromovinyl)-1-((2S,4S)-2-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-(dioxolane-4-yl))uracil (l-BHDU) against Varicella Zoster Virus (VZV).

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Discovery of 5-arylsulfonamido-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole derivatives as potent, selective 5-HT6 receptor agonists and antagonists.

2-hydroxy-4-methoxycarbonylisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-dione is a substituted analogue of 2-hydroxyisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-dione with an electron-withdrawing group at the 4-position, and its tautomeric forms, magnesium complex stoichiometry, and antiintegrase, anti-RNase H, and antiviral activities are studied.

Magnesium chelating 2-hydroxyisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-diones, as inhibitors of HIV-1 integrase and/or the HIV-1 reverse transcriptase ribonuclease H domain: discovery of a novel selective inhibitor of the ribonuclease H function.

4-aminoquinazoline-2-carboxamide derivatives function as PAK4 inhibitors with high selectivity (346-fold vs PAK1) and can potently inhibit migration and invasion of A549 tumor cells by regulating PAK4-directed downstream signaling pathways in vitro.
Structure-Based Design of 6-Chloro-4-aminoquinazoline-2-carboxamide Derivatives as Potent and Selective p21-Activated Kinase 4 (PAK4) Inhibitors.
Pyridazinopyridinone compounds have been designed as novel p38 kinase inhibitors with demonstrated potency in enzyme and human whole blood assays.
Discovery of pyridazinopyridinones as potent and selective p38 mitogen-activated protein kinase inhibitors.
Quinoline-3-carboxamido-based compounds show selectivity for ABCG2 over ABCB1 by about 100–500 fold and are inactive at ABCC2 (MRP2).
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 4-Anilino-quinazolines and -quinolines as Inhibitors of Breast Cancer Resistance Protein (ABCG2).
Tasuldine (2-[(3-pyridinylmethyl)thio]pyrimidine) increases tracheobronchial secretion in mice, as indicated by increased excretion of phenol red into the trachea.
2-[(3-Pyridinylmethyl)thio]pyrimidine derivatives: new bronchosecretolytic agents.
3-acetylphenyloxazolidinone functions as a highly potent P2 ligand in protease inhibitor design, contributing to compounds with high affinity and antiviral activity.
Structure-based design, synthesis, and structure-activity relationship studies of HIV-1 protease inhibitors incorporating phenyloxazolidinones.

3-methylquinoxaline-2-carboxylate 1,4-dioxide derivatives with a chlorine group in position 7 of the benzene moiety display antitubercular activity with EC90/MIC values between 0.01 and 2.30 in vitro.
Synthesis of new quinoxaline-2-carboxylate 1,4-dioxide derivatives as anti-Mycobacterium tuberculosis agents.

1-[(2-hydroxyethoxy)methyl]uracil derivatives with 5-halo substitutions (analogue 17 and 18) exhibit potent and selective inhibition of duck HBV, wild-type human HBV, and lamivudine-resistant HBV replication in vitro.
Inhibition of hepatitis B virus (HBV) replication by pyrimidines bearing an acyclic moiety: effect on wild-type and mutant HBV.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

1-[(2-hydroxyethoxy)methyl]uracil derivatives with 5-halo substitutions (analogue 17 and 18) exhibit potent and selective inhibition of duck HBV, wild-type human HBV, and lamivudine-resistant HBV replication in vitro.
Effect of various pyrimidines possessing the 1-[(2-hydroxy-1-(hydroxymethyl)ethoxy)methyl] moiety, able to mimic natural 2'-deoxyribose, on wild-type and mutant hepatitis B virus replication.
1,3,2-benzodithiazole S-oxide analogs exhibit in vitro antifungal activity against several Candida strains.
Synthesis and antifungal activity of 1,3,2-benzodithiazole S-oxides.
The iron complex of 5-trifluoromethyl-2-formylpyridine thiosemicarbazone can completely prevent tumor growth in Ehrlich ascites tumor models.
Inhibition of tumor cell transplantability by iron and copper complexes of 5-substituted 2-formylpyridine thiosemicarbazones.
Thiophen-2-yl-1,2,4-oxadiazoles are inhibitors of the mycobacterial regulator EthR, acting as 10-fold ethionamide activity boosters.
Ethionamide boosters: synthesis, biological activity, and structure-activity relationships of a series of 1,2,4-oxadiazole EthR inhibitors.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs exhibit antitumor activity against Walker carcinosarcoma 256, with the nitroalkene side chain being essential for this activity.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be obtained in optically pure form with a yield greater than 99% and enantiomeric excess (ee) of 96% (R) via chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger asymmetric oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone using Candida antarctica lipase B and H2O2.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.
Isocoumarin phosphorus analogues (phosphaisocoumarins) can act as reversible competitive inhibitors of pancreatic cholesterol esterase (CEase), with some compounds showing potent inhibitory activity.
Phosphaisocoumarins as a new class of potent inhibitors for pancreatic cholesterol esterase.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.
12-epi-fischerindole U is a substrate that can be monochlorinated at an unactivated sp3 aliphatic carbon by the nonheme iron enzyme WelO5 in the welwitindolinone biosynthetic pathway.
A new family of iron-dependent halogenases acts on freestanding substrates.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene (trans-B[a]P-4,5-diol) is a K-region dihydrodiol metabolite of benzo[a]pyrene that can be metabolized by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to six major products: two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:7,8-tetrahydroxy-4,5:7,8-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:9,10-tetrahydroxy-4,5:9,10-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, and two diastereomers of trans,trans-1,2:4,5-tetrahydroxy-1,2:4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene can be metabolically activated by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to form stable polar DNA adducts, with the trans,trans-B[a]P-4,5:7,8-bis-diols serving as metabolic intermediates in this process.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

Aristolactam BIII is a natural aristolactam that can be synthesized efficiently using a one-pot Suzuki-Miyaura coupling/aldol condensation cascade reaction of isoindolin-1-one with 2-formylphenylboronic acid.
Total synthesis of aristolactams via a one-pot suzuki-miyaura coupling/aldol condensation cascade reaction.
ll-Azi-DAP, the (2S,4S)-diastereomer of 2-(4-amino-4-carboxybutyl)aziridine-2-carboxylic acid, selectively binds to Cys-73 of the diaminopimelic acid epimerase active site.
The stereoselective synthesis of aziridine analogues of diaminopimelic acid (DAP) and their interaction with dap epimerase.
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives have been studied as VLA-4 antagonists using 3-D-QSAR (CoMFA and CoMSIA) models.
3-D-QSAR CoMFA and CoMSIA studies on tetrahydrofuroyl-L-phenylalanine derivatives as VLA-4 antagonists.
(R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine functions as a pharmacophore moiety that confers moderate inhibitory activity for DPP-4 and is associated with good pharmacokinetic properties.
Discovery of new chemotype dipeptidyl peptidase IV inhibitors having (R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine as a pharmacophore.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate serves as a substrate for P450 BM3-catalyzed biotransformation, yielding optically active 3methylphthalides with 7% yield and 31% enantiomeric excess.
P450 BM3-Catalyzed Regio- and Stereoselective Hydroxylation Aiming at the Synthesis of Phthalides and Isocoumarins.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
Concise means for accessing an advanced precursor to 1-deoxypaclitaxel.
3-fluorochromanone serves as a substrate for ruthenium-catalyzed asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to yield enantioenriched cis-3-fluoro-chroman-4-ol derivatives.
Ru(II)-Catalyzed Asymmetric Transfer Hydrogenation of 3-Fluorochromanone Derivatives to Access Enantioenriched cis-3-Fluorochroman-4-ols through Dynamic Kinetic Resolution.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Isonucleosides incorporating universal bases.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
Zinc triethanolamine complex inhibits rat brain monoamine oxidase-B (MAO-B) in a time-dependent and reversible manner, with lower potency compared to cadmium triethanolamine complex.
Effect of cadmium and zinc ethanolamine complexes on rat brain monoamine oxidase-B activity in vitro.
(1R,6S)-6-methoxycarbonyl-3-cyclohexene-1-carboxylic acid is a versatile chiral synthon that can be obtained by enantioselective hydrolysis of the corresponding meso diester with pig liver esterase.
Chiral synthon obtained with pig liver esterase: introduction of chiral centers into cyclohexene skeleton.

Epoxide ring opening, functional group interconversion, and deprotection of (2RS,3SR)-N(1)-benzyl-2-chloromethyl-3-benzyloxy-4,5-epoxypiperidine enable the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
An oxidation and ring contraction approach to the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.
Ginsenoside Rh7 suppresses the expression of ILF3-AS1, which competitively binds with miR-212, resulting in down-regulation of SMAD1 and suppression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) progression.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

Ginsenoside Rh7 can regulate the expression of ILF3-AS1, and by competitively binding with miR-212, it can enhance the miR-212-Smad1 axis, thereby significantly inhibiting the proliferation, migration, and invasion of NSCLC cells.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.

Pipoxide chlorohydrin is a halogenated plant metabolite that shows the greatest affinity and potency for binding to chikungunya virus nsP2pro, as demonstrated by molecular docking, MD simulation, and MMPBSA studies.
Halogenated Secondary Metabolites from Higher Plants: Potent Drug Candidates for Chikungunya Using in silico Approaches.

Cyrtominetin inhibits the hemolytic activity of α-hemolysin (α-HL) by binding to the 'Loop' region of the protein, blocking the conformational transition from monomer to oligomer and thereby inhibiting protein activity, as shown by molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations.
Molecular insight into the inhibition mechanism of cyrtominetin to α-hemolysin by molecular dynamics simulation.

3,4,5-trihydroxy-N-(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)benzamide (SG-HQ2) is a synthetic analogue of gallic acid that attenuates histamine release by reducing intracellular calcium in human and primary peritoneal mast cells.
SG-HQ2 inhibits mast cell-mediated allergic inflammation through suppression of histamine release and pro-inflammatory cytokines.

1,3-bis(N'-benzoylthioureido)benzene functions as an ionophore in PVC membrane ion-selective electrodes for Pb(II) ion detection, providing a Nernstian response in the concentration range 4.0×10⁻⁶ to 1.0×10⁻⁲ M with a slope of 31.5 ± 1.6 mV/dec, a response time of t90% = 14 s, and operational stability for at least 10 weeks.
Lead(II) ion selective electrodes with PVC membranes based on two bis-thioureas as ionophores: 1,3-bis(N'-benzoylthioureido)benzene and 1,3-bis(N'-furoylthioureido)benzene.

4-aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione is a core structural component of isoniazid-based triads that, when combined with isoniazid and isoniazid, confers promising anti-mycobacterial activity against the mc26230 strain of M. tuberculosis with MIC values in the range of 5.1–11.9 µM and non-cytotoxicity to Vero cells.
Design and synthesis of 4-Aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione-isoniazid triads as potential anti-mycobacterials.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been designed and synthesized as inhibitors of Aurora A kinase.
Design, synthesis, biological evaluation of 6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives as novel anticancer agents with Aurora kinase inhibition.

4-((4-(2-chlorophenyl)thiazole-2-yl)amino)-4oxo-3-phenylbutanoic acid (2CTAP) is a functionally selective allosteric modulator of human free fatty acid receptor 2 (FFA2) that inhibits constitutive Ca2+ levels, antagonizes acetate-induced pERK1/2 phosphorylation, and prevents damage in intestinal ischemia reperfusion injury (IRI).
Characterisation of small molecule ligands 4CMTB and 2CTAP as modulators of human FFA2 receptor signalling.

N-(2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl)benzamide derivatives can be structurally optimized as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with compound 8r showing strong inhibition of FLT3 and FLT3 mutants relevant to AML.
Rational design and synthesis of 2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole derivatives as inhibitors targeting FMS-like tyrosine kinase 3 (FLT3) and its mutants.
GW427353 (solabegron) is a novel, selective ␤ 3-adrenergic receptor (AR) agonist investigated for its effects on bladder function in dogs.
GW427353 (solabegron), a novel, selective beta3-adrenergic receptor agonist, evokes bladder relaxation and increases micturition reflex threshold in the dog.
14S,22-diHDHA (Maresin-L1) acts as an autacrine or paracrine factor produced by leukocytes and platelets that diminishes diabetic impairment of macrophage reparative functions.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.
2-(2-fluoro-6-nitrobenzylsulfanyl)pyridine-4-carbothioamide acts as an elicitor in Trifolium pratense L. suspension culture, increasing the production of isoflavonoids and flavonoids.
New synthetic pyridine derivate as potential elicitor in production of isoflavonoids and flavonoids in Trifolium pratense L. suspension culture.

Bis(7-acetoxy-2-ethyl-5-methylheptyl) phthalate, a novel benzoic acid derivative from Lonicera quinquelocularis, inhibits acetylcholinesterase (AChE) and butyrylcholinesterase (BChE) with IC50 values of 1.65 µM and 5.98 µM, respectively.
New cholinesterase inhibitory constituents from Lonicera quinquelocularis.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) attenuates the oxidative stress and inflammatory response in 661W cells exposed to lipoteichoic acid, including decreased pro-inflammatory factors IL-1beta, IL-12, and TNFalpha, and inhibition of nuclear factor kappa B and Egr1, Fosl1, and Lox12 gene expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.

Plagionicin A is a monotetrahydrofuran acetogenin isolated from Disepalum plagioneurum leaves that exhibits significant in vitro cytotoxicity against the KB cancer cell line with an IC50 in the nanomolar range.
Cytotoxic monotetrahydrofuran acetogenins from Disepalum plagioneurum.

Pyridoxal 4-N-(1-benzylpiperidin-4-yl) thiosemicarbazone (PBPT) is a thiosemicarbazone derivative designed to target five Alzheimer's disease markers: low acetylcholinesterase levels, autophagy dysfunction, metal homeostasis disruptions, protein aggregation, and oxidative stress.
A novel class of thiosemicarbazones show multi-functional activity for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene (trans-B[a]P-4,5-diol) is a K-region dihydrodiol metabolite of benzo[a]pyrene that can be metabolized by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to six major products: two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:7,8-tetrahydroxy-4,5:7,8-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, two diastereomers of trans,trans-4,5:9,10-tetrahydroxy-4,5:9,10-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene, and two diastereomers of trans,trans-1,2:4,5-tetrahydroxy-1,2:4,5-tetrahydrobenzo[a]pyrene.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

2-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)tryptamine derivatives can act as antagonists at the gonadotropin releasing hormone receptor, with potency influenced by para-substituents on the 4-phenylbutyl side chain attached to the tryptamine nitrogen.
2-(3,5-Dimethylphenyl)tryptamine derivatives that bind to the GnRH receptor.

trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4, 5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene can be metabolically activated by beta-naphthoflavone-induced rat liver microsomes to form stable polar DNA adducts, with the trans,trans-B[a]P-4,5:7,8-bis-diols serving as metabolic intermediates in this process.
Biotransformation of trans-4,5-dihydroxy-4,5-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene to benzo[a]pyrene bis-diols and DNA adducts by induced rat liver microsomes.

2-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)tryptamine derivatives act as potent antagonists of the gonadotropin releasing hormone (GnRH) receptor, with the most potent compound exhibiting an IC50 of 16 nM in a rat GnRH receptor assay.
Heterocyclic derivatives of 2-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)tryptamine as GnRH receptor antagonists.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.

4-(1H-indol-3-yl)-6-(4-methylphenyl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydropyrimidin-2-one demonstrates significant anti-inflammatory activity and shows reduced ulcerogenic potential and lipid peroxidation compared to ibuprofen.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of some 4-(1H-indol-3-yl)-6-phenyl-1,2,3,4-tetrahydropyrimidin-2-ones/thiones as potent anti-inflammatory agents.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.

In the lipids of both tumor and muscle tissues, radioactivity from cis-9-[1-14C]octadecenol incorporation occurs predominantly in the acyl moieties of diradylglycerophosphocholines, diradylglycerophosphoethanolamines, and triradylglycerols, rather than in the alkyl or alk-1-enyl moieties.
Radioactive labelling of lipids in rat neurosarcoma by intravenous injection of [1-14C]-octadecenol.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.

2-(butyloxy)-4-[4-(methylsulfonyl)phenyl]-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyrimidine is a potent and selective cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitor with a favorable pharmacokinetic profile, high brain penetration, and good efficacy in rat models of hypersensitivity.
Identification of [4-[4-(methylsulfonyl)phenyl]-6-(trifluoromethyl)-2-pyrimidinyl] amines and ethers as potent and selective cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitors.

3-aza-9-hydroxyfluoren-9(R)-ylcarbonyl-l-prolyl-2-aminomethyl-5-chlorobenzylamide is a potent thrombin inhibitor with a K(i) of 0.40 nM and 2x APTT of 0.18 µM, excellent pharmacokinetic properties (F = 55%, T1/2 = 14 h in dogs), and complete efficacy in inhibiting FeCl3-induced vessel occlusions in a rat in vivo thrombosis model at 10 µg/kg/min intravenous infusion.
9-hydroxyazafluorenes and their use in thrombin inhibitors.

5-Iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) is converted to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine (IUdR) by aldehyde oxidase in the liver, a reaction that does not require exogenous cofactors and is not catalyzed by mixed-function oxidases, xanthine oxidase, or many other oxido-reductases.
Conversion of 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine by aldehyde oxidase. Implication in hepatotropic drug design.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) inhibits apoptosis and reduces infarct size in myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury by activating the PI3K/Akt pathway and reducing inflammation.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) reduces rat myocardial apoptosis against ischemia and reperfusion injury by activation of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/Akt signaling and anti-inflammatory action in vivo.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.

S9947 is a novel Kv1.5 channel blocker that inhibits cloned hKv1.5 channels in Xenopus oocytes and CHO cells, and blocks I Kur currents in human and rat cardiomyocytes in a concentration-dependent manner.
Characterization of a novel Kv1.5 channel blocker in Xenopus oocytes, CHO cells, human and rat cardiomyocytes.

Amide derivatives of 8-[4-[[carboxymethyl]oxy]phenyl]-1,3-di-(n-propyl)xanthine act as selective A(2B) adenosine receptor antagonists, with para-substituted anilides showing high affinity (1–3 nM) and selectivity for human A(2B) receptors.
Anilide derivatives of an 8-phenylxanthine carboxylic congener are highly potent and selective antagonists at human A(2B) adenosine receptors.
Phosphorylation of the pThr-Pro peptide bond in the cytoplasmic tail of amyloid precursor protein (APP) leads to prolyl isomerization, with 9% of the cis isomer present after phosphorylation at T20.
Phosphorylation-induced structural changes in the amyloid precursor protein cytoplasmic tail detected by NMR.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Isonucleosides incorporating universal bases.
[1-14C]glycyl-L-proline is transported into purified human small intestinal brush border membrane vesicles via a carrier-mediated, Na+-independent process.
Transport of glycyl-L-proline by human intestinal brush border membrane vesicles.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.

5-Iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) is converted to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine (IUdR) by aldehyde oxidase in the liver, a reaction that does not require exogenous cofactors and is not catalyzed by mixed-function oxidases, xanthine oxidase, or many other oxido-reductases.
Conversion of 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine by aldehyde oxidase. Implication in hepatotropic drug design.

The mean AUC of 3'-hydroxydiclofenac after a single 150 mg oral dose of diclofenac is significantly different between healthy subjects and patients with alcoholic cirrhosis, but not between healthy subjects and chronic active hepatitis patients.
Pharmacokinetics of diclofenac sodium in chronic active hepatitis and alcoholic cirrhosis.
p-amidinophenylphosphinic acid (AMPA) forms hydrogen bonds with catalytic residues at the oxyanion hole of trypsin, resulting in improved activity compared to benzamidine.
An oxyanion-hole selective serine protease inhibitor in complex with trypsin.

(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid inhibits leukotriene B4-induced degranulation and superoxide anion generation in human leukocytes in a dose-dependent manner without affecting cyclic AMP levels.
Relationship of cyclic-AMP levels in leukotriene B4-stimulated leukocytes to lysosomal enzyme release and the generation of superoxide anions.
The cytotoxic action of 1-beta-D-arabinosyl-5-azacytosine in CEM cells is mediated by the accumulation of its triphosphate anabolite, ara-ACTP, and its incorporation into nucleic acids.
Cellular metabolism of 1-beta-D-arabinofuranosyl-5-azacytosine and incorporation into DNA and RNA of human lymphoid CEM/0 and CEM/dCk(-) cells.

A 1:1 mixture of L-prolyl-L-leucyl-(-)-thiazolidine-2-carboxamide and L-prolyl-L-leucyl-(+)-thiazolidine-2-carboxamide shows marked activity in the Dopa potentiation test but does not antagonize oxotremorine-induced tremors.
New dopaminergic and potential anti-parkinson compounds, N,N-disubstituted beta-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)ethylamines.

N-(3-pyrrolidinyl)benzamide derivatives with specific 4-substituent and N-substituent modifications on the pyrrolidine ring enhance selectivity for D 3 and D 4 dopamine receptor subtypes over D 2 receptors.
Design and synthesis of [(2,3-dichlorophenyl)piperazin-1-yl]alkylfluorenylcarboxamides as novel ligands selective for the dopamine D3 receptor subtype.
Some 5-amino-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole derivatives are highly selective agonists for the 5-HT 6 receptor over several other 5-HT and dopamine receptors.
2-Substituted tryptamines: agents with selectivity for 5-HT(6) serotonin receptors.

N,N-Diethylaminoethanol 2,2-diphenyl-2-ethylthioacetate methyl iodide acts as a muscarinic receptor antagonist that displays competitive inhibition at low concentrations and allosteric inhibition at higher concentrations in CHO cells expressing human m2 and m3 muscarinic receptors.
Cardioselective derivatives of 2,2-diphenyl-2-ethylthioacetate do not discriminate between m2 and m3 muscarinic receptors expressed in CHO cells.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
2-Substituted tryptamines: agents with selectivity for 5-HT(6) serotonin receptors.

5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) enables radiosensitization in both MMR-proficient and MMR-deficient human colon cancer xenografts, with a sensitizer enhancement ratio (SER) of 1.48 at 2 Gy per fraction and 1.41 at 4 Gy per fraction in MMR-deficient tumors, and 1.21 at 2 Gy and 1.20 at 4 Gy in MMR-proficient tumors.
Differential radiosensitization in DNA mismatch repair-proficient and -deficient human colon cancer xenografts with 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose.

5-methoxy-b-carboline analogues are the highest affinity methoxy-substituted derivatives for 5-HT 2C receptors, with K i =75 nM, and show enhanced affinity compared to their unsubstituted parent compounds.
Binding of beta-carbolines at 5-HT(2) serotonin receptors.

Dibenzo[c,h]cinnoline analogues are aza analogues of benzo[i]phenanthridines and exhibit more potent topoisomerase I-targeting activity and cytotoxicity compared to their benzo[i]phenanthridine counterparts.
Substituted dibenzo[c,h]cinnolines: topoisomerase I-targeting anticancer agents.
2beta-Carbomethoxy-3beta-[4'-((Z)-2-iodoethenyl)phenyl]tropane (ZIET) displays high affinity for human SERT with a Ki of 0.11 nM in cells expressing SERT.

Synthesis, radiosynthesis, and biological evaluation of carbon-11 and iodine-123 labeled 2beta-carbomethoxy-3beta-[4'-((Z)-2-haloethenyl)phenyl]tropanes: candidate radioligands for in vivo imaging of the serotonin transporter.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.
3-thienylalanine is identified as an especially good substrate for phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase (PheRS) based on computational predictions using the HierDock protocol.
Virtual screening for binding of phenylalanine analogues to phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

Oral administration of 3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) at doses ≥10 mg/kg in MCMV-infected mice produces antiviral effects comparable to ganciclovir, as measured by survival and viral DNA levels in organs.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives have been studied as VLA-4 antagonists using 3-D-QSAR (CoMFA and CoMSIA) models.
3-D-QSAR CoMFA and CoMSIA studies on tetrahydrofuroyl-L-phenylalanine derivatives as VLA-4 antagonists.

5,5-dimethyl-3-(3-fluorophenyl)-4-(4-methylsulphonyl) phenyl-2(5H)-furanone (DFU) is a selective COX-2 inhibitor that potently inhibits KCl-, oxytocin-, and PGF(2alpha)-stimulated contractions of isolated pregnant human myometrial strips, with a more potent inhibitory effect than nimesulide on these contraction types.
Comparison of the effects of nimesulide and 5,5-dimethyl-3-(3-fluorophenyl)-4-(4-methylsulphonyl) phenyl-2(5H)-furanone (DFU) on contractions of isolated pregnant human myometrium.

The rate of transesterification of 2-hydroxypropyl-p-nitrophenyl phosphate is increased by 50- to 5000-fold in acetonitrile, 1,4-dioxane, methanol, ethanol, N,N-dimethylformamide, or dimethyl sulfoxide compared to water.
Acceleration of acid-catalyzed transesterification of 2-hydroxypropyl-p-nitrophenyl phosphate by organic solvents.

Methylamphetamine sulphate is a chemical associated with clandestine drug laboratories and its metabolic transformation by soil microorganisms is important for linking discarded residues to drug manufacturing methods and assessing environmental impact.
Microbial degradation of illicit drugs, their precursors, and manufacturing by-products: implications for clandestine drug laboratory investigation and environmental assessment.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide exhibits significantly greater antileishmanial activity than sodium antimony gluconate in reducing parasite load in the spleen and liver of hamster models at much lower concentrations.
Synthesis of a novel quinoline derivative, 2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide--a potential antileishmanial agent.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

When covalently linked to bacteriophage 75 and delivered to Staphylococcus aureus, tin(IV) chlorin e6 (SnCe6) enables selective killing of methicillin- and vancomycin-intermediate strains with low concentrations (1.5 µg/ml) and low light doses (21 J/cm2), while sparing human epithelial cells.
Development of a novel targeting system for lethal photosensitization of antibiotic-resistant strains of Staphylococcus aureus.
Parenteral copper supplementation of the dam by copper edetate in late gestation has no effect on the liver copper concentration of the foal at birth.
Changes in liver copper concentration of thoroughbred foals from birth to 160 days of age and the effect of prenatal copper supplementation of their dams.

Samarium(III) triflate (Sm(OTf)3) is used to activate benzylated glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones, enabling them to react with simple and carbohydrate alcohols to yield β-mixtures of glycosides in moderate to excellent yield.
A glycosylation protocol based on activation of glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones with samarium triflate.
4-nitrophenyl beta-D-N,N',N''-triacetylchitotriose is a substrate hydrolyzed by a chitinase encoded by the SL0018 (chiA) gene in Salmonella enterica Typhimurium SL1344.
Characterization of a novel Salmonella Typhimurium chitinase which hydrolyzes chitin, chitooligosaccharides and an N-acetyllactosamine conjugate.

1-(2-nitro-4,5-dimethoxyphenyl)-N,N,N',N'-tetrakis[(oxycarbonyl)methyl]-1,2-ethanediamine (DM-nitrophen) is a caged Ca2+ compound that, upon two-photon excitation photolysis, induces local and sustained increases in intracellular Ca2+] in rat ventricular myocytes.
Calcium waves in skinned cardiac myocytes evoked by two-photon excitation photolysis of caged calcium.
Adenosine 3'-phosphonate acts as an inhibitor of the MurMN/FemABX family of tRNA-dependent ligases, which are implicated in high-level penicillin resistance in gram-positive bacteria.
Adenosine phosphonate inhibitors of lipid II: alanyl tRNA ligase MurM from Streptococcus pneumoniae.

Quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt exerts an antagonistic effect on cadmium intoxication by counteracting cadmium-induced decreases in superoxide dismutase (SOD) and glutathione (GSH) levels in the mouse liver.
Influence of water-soluble flavonoids, quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt and morin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt, on antioxidant parameters in the subacute cadmium intoxication mouse model.

Nickel Gluconate, when administered as part of a Heel-Potentised Swine Organ Preparation (D10/D30/D200), is an effective primary treatment for pediatric ranula and intraoral mucocele, with 89% of ranulas and 67% of labial mucoceles responding completely to this homotoxicological therapy.
Nickel Gluconate-Mercurius Heel-Potentised Swine Organ Preparations: a new therapeutical approach for the primary treatment of pediatric ranula and intraoral mucocele.

In rat jejunum, the mucosal efflux of 2,5-dinitro-1,6-naphthyridine sulfonate-glutathione (DNP-SG) is primarily mediated by MRP2, as shown by higher efflux and inhibition by probenecid compared to the ileum.
Site-specific bidirectional efflux of 2,4-dinitrophenyl-S-glutathione, a substrate of multidrug resistance-associated proteins, in rat intestine and Caco-2 cells.

Pyridine-2-carboxylate gold(III) acts as a catalyst for the cycloisomerization of optically active 1-cyclopropyl propargyl acetate, affording cyclopentanone with up to 61% enantiomeric excess under standard conditions.
Mechanistic insights in gold-stabilized nonclassical carbocations: gold-catalyzed rearrangement of 3-cyclopropyl propargylic acetates.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.

diethylenetriamine pentaacetic acid-deoxyglucose (DTPA-DG) can be radiolabeled with (188)Re and shows high tumor uptake in mammary tumor-bearing mice, with mean tumoral percent injected dosages per gram (% ID/g) of 1.98 ± 0.29 % ID/g at 3 hours, 2.89 ± 0.43 % ID/g at 12 hours, and 0.42 ± 0.06 % ID/g at 24 hours after intravenous injection.
Evaluation of 188Re-DTPA-deoxyglucose as a potential cancer radiopharmaceutical.
5'-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N6-substitution function as dual-acting agonists at human A1 AR and antagonists at human A3 AR.
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

Quercetin-5',8-disulfonate is a synthetic sulfated derivative of quercetin that, when administered orally at 100, 200, and 500 mg/kg bw prior to acute CCl4 oxidative damage in mice, attenuates serum ALT, AST, and LDH activities and hepatic malondialdehyde levels, and suppresses CCl4-induced depletion of glutathione peroxidase and total superoxide dismutase.
Protective effects of quercetin and quercetin-5',8-disulfonate against carbon tetrachloride-caused oxidative liver injury in mice.
4-carboxymethoxy-5,7-dinitroindolinyl-L-glutamate (CDNI-Glu) uncages in living brain slices at low power (ca. 10 mW at 720 nm) and low concentrations (0.40–1.35 mM).
Two-photon uncaging of gamma-aminobutyric acid in intact brain tissue.

Alstiphyllanine B is an alkaloid isolated from Alstonia macrophylla that shows moderate antiplasmodial activity against Plasmodium falciparum and vasorelaxant activity against phenylephrine-induced contraction of isolated rat aorta.
Alstiphyllanines A-D, indole alkaloids from Alstonia macrophylla.

ChloraPrep, a solution containing 2% chlorhexidine gluconate and 70% isopropyl alcohol, is the most effective surgical skin-preparation solution for eliminating bacteria from the hallux and toes in the foot and ankle prior to surgery.
Efficacy of surgical preparation solutions in foot and ankle surgery.
D0870 is a bis-triazole derivative that prevents death and induces parasitological cure in 70 to 90% of animals in acute and chronic murine models of Chagas' disease.
In vivo activity of the bis-triazole D0870 against drug-susceptible and drug-resistant strains of the protozoan parasite Trypanosoma cruzi.

Cytarabine ocfosfate hydrate is an oral chemotherapeutic agent that can induce persistent molecular complete remission in elderly patients with relapsed acute myeloid leukemia (AML) harboring an inv(16)(p13.1q22) genetic abnormality.
Persistent molecular remission of refractory acute myeloid leukemia with inv(16)(p13.1q22) in an elderly patient induced by cytarabine ocfosfate hydrate.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
3-amino-N-(4-bromophenyl)-4-methoxybenzamide exhibits anti-EV 71 activity with IC50 values ranging from 5.7 ± 0.8 to 12 ± 1.2 µM in vitro.
Synthesis and antiviral activity of N-phenylbenzamide derivatives, a novel class of enterovirus 71 inhibitors.

Pentagalloylglucopyranosyl, when sulfated at positions 2 through 6, forms an allosteric inhibitor (SPGG) that binds to multiple anion-binding sites on factor XIa, contributing nearly 87% of binding energy through nonionic interactions.
Designing allosteric inhibitors of factor XIa. Lessons from the interactions of sulfated pentagalloylglucopyranosides.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Identification of 3-sulfonylindazole derivatives as potent and selective 5-HT(6) antagonists.
6-(4-fluorophenyl)-4-hydroxypyridazin-3(2H)-one is a 6-aza analogue of 5-(4-fluorophenyl)-3-hydroxypyridin-2(1H)-one that retains modest inhibitory activity against influenza endonuclease.
Phenyl substituted 4-hydroxypyridazin-3(2H)-ones and 5-hydroxypyrimidin-4(3H)-ones: inhibitors of influenza A endonuclease.
Pyridazinopyridinone compounds have been designed as novel p38 kinase inhibitors with demonstrated potency in enzyme and human whole blood assays.
Discovery of pyridazinopyridinones as potent and selective p38 mitogen-activated protein kinase inhibitors.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.
3-amino-N-(4-bromophenyl)-4-methoxybenzamide is a representative N-aryl benzamide compound that exerts moderate anti-EV-A71 activity against multiple virus strains.
Synthesis and antiviral activity of N-phenylbenzamide derivatives, a novel class of enterovirus 71 inhibitors.
3-amino-N-(4-bromophenyl)-4-methoxybenzamide is a representative N-aryl benzamide compound that exhibits moderate anti-EV-A71 activity against multiple virus strains.
Synthesis and antiviral activity of N-phenylbenzamide derivatives, a novel class of enterovirus 71 inhibitors.

4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol (SMBD) inhibits LPS-induced production of nitric oxide and prostaglandin E2 in BV2 microglia by suppressing gene expression of inducible NO synthase (iNOS) and cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), respectively, at non-toxic concentrations.
Anti-inflammatory potential of newly synthesized 4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated BV2 microglia.
N-(2-morpholinoethyl)nicotinamide derivatives are potent and selective inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) over MAO-B.
Design of novel nicotinamides as potent and selective monoamine oxidase a inhibitors.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).

Structure-activity relationships of 2-amino-3-aroyl-4-[(4-arylpiperazin-1-yl)methyl]thiophenes. Part 2: Probing the influence of diverse substituents at the phenyl of the arylpiperazine moiety on allosteric enhancer activity at the A₁ adenosine receptor.

2-(4-bromophenylcarbamoyl)-5-chlorophenyl-4-(trifluoromethyl)benzoate, a benzoate from 4-trifluoromethylbenzoic acid, has a higher lipophilicity (ClogP = 6.74) and greater antifungal activity against Trichophyton mentagrophytes (MIC = 0.49 µM) compared to its salicylanilide precursor.
Antifungal Activity of Salicylanilides and Their Esters with 4-(Trifluoromethyl)benzoic Acid.

2-hydroxy-4-methoxycarbonylisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-dione is a substituted analogue of 2-hydroxyisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-dione with an electron-withdrawing group at the 4-position, and its tautomeric forms, magnesium complex stoichiometry, and antiintegrase, anti-RNase H, and antiviral activities are studied.

Magnesium chelating 2-hydroxyisoquinoline-1,3(2H,4H)-diones, as inhibitors of HIV-1 integrase and/or the HIV-1 reverse transcriptase ribonuclease H domain: discovery of a novel selective inhibitor of the ribonuclease H function.

Ethyl 5-acetyl-4-methyl-2-(phenylamino)thiophene-3-carboxylate exhibits in vitro antimicrobial activity, with derivatives showing MIC values against Aspergillus fumigatus and Syncephalastrum racemosum.
Synthesis, in-vitro antibacterial, antifungal, and molecular modeling of potent anti-microbial agents with a combined pyrazole and thiophene pharmacophore.

Bis-(3-oxoundecyl) tetrasulfide is identified as the major compound in the A7 fraction of Bryopsis pennata chloroform extract, which exhibits strong larvicidal activity against Aedes aegypti (LC50 = 4.7 µg/mL) and Aedes albopictus (LC50 = 5.3 µg/mL).
Mosquitocidal and Oviposition Repellent Activities of the Extracts of Seaweed Bryopsis pennata on Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus.

Quercetin-5',8-disulfonate is a synthetic sulfated derivative of quercetin that, when administered orally at 100, 200, and 500 mg/kg bw prior to acute CCl4 oxidative damage in mice, attenuates serum ALT, AST, and LDH activities and hepatic malondialdehyde levels, and suppresses CCl4-induced depletion of glutathione peroxidase and total superoxide dismutase.
Protective effects of quercetin and quercetin-5',8-disulfonate against carbon tetrachloride-caused oxidative liver injury in mice.
The 2-thienyl-4-furylpyridine skeleton is important for topoisomerase I inhibitory activity in pyridine-based derivatives.
Synthesis of 2-(thienyl-2-yl or -3-yl)-4-furyl-6-aryl pyridine derivatives and evaluation of their topoisomerase I and II inhibitory activity, cytotoxicity, and structure-activity relationship.

2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazole derivatives have been developed as selective AdoA2A receptor antagonists, with one substituted derivative showing a Ki of 12 nM at human AdoA2A and over 500-fold selectivity versus AdoA1R.
2-Amino-5-benzoyl-4-phenylthiazoles: Development of potent and selective adenosine A1 receptor antagonists.

(Z)-5-(4-methoxybenzylidene)-2-(naphthalen-2-yl)-5H-thiazol-4-one (ST1237) is a potent direct 5-lipoxygenase (5-LO) inhibitor with IC50 values of 0.08–0.03 µM in the cell-free S100 assay and 0.12–0.02 µM in intact polymorphonuclear leukocytes (PMNL).
Synthesis and biological evaluation of a class of 5-benzylidene-2-phenyl-thiazolinones as potent 5-lipoxygenase inhibitors.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives exhibit anti-MERS-CoV activity in Vero cells.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.
Benzophenone bis-sulfamate derivatives are potent steroid sulfatase inhibitors with submicromolar IC50 values (e.g., 2.0 nM for 29f) against recombinant, purified steroid sulfatase.
4,4'-Benzophenone-O,O'-disulfamate: a potent inhibitor of steroid sulfatase.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives have been shown to possess high anti-MERS-CoV activity.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.

Ferric hydrogensulfate acts as a catalyst in the multi-component, one-pot condensation reaction for synthesizing amidoalkyl naphthols from beta-naphthol, aromatic aldehydes, and acetamide under solvent, solvent-free, and microwave conditions.
An efficient, simple and expedition synthesis of 1-amidoalkyl-2-naphthols as 'drug like' molecules for biological screening.

4-aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione is a core structural component of isoniazid-based triads that, when combined with isoniazid and isoniazid, confers promising anti-mycobacterial activity against the mc26230 strain of M. tuberculosis with MIC values in the range of 5.1–11.9 µM and non-cytotoxicity to Vero cells.
Design and synthesis of 4-Aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione-isoniazid triads as potential anti-mycobacterials.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.
Tetrahydropyrano[3,4-b]indole derivatives have been designed as inhibitors of HCV NS5B polymerase, with structure-based design focusing on the C1-position substituent.
The discovery and structure-activity relationships of pyrano[3,4-b]indole based inhibitors of hepatitis C virus NS5B polymerase.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
Congeners bearing an (E)-cyclooct-2-enylidenemethyl group exhibit more potent type I fatty acid synthase (FAS) inhibitory activity than (S)-3-demethylthiolactomycin with an unnatural configuration.
Synthesis and biological activity of enantiomeric pairs of 5-[(E)-cycloalk-2-enylidenemethyl]thiolactomycin congeners.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
Reversible photoligation of DNA via 5-vinyldeoxyuridine.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.
The conversion of 3H-squalene to sterols by rat liver microsomes and cytosol is inhibited by individual rat and human plasma lipoproteins at various concentrations.
Inhibition of rat hepatic sterol formation from squalene by plasma lipoproteins.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.
(R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine functions as a pharmacophore moiety that confers moderate inhibitory activity for DPP-4 and is associated with good pharmacokinetic properties.
Discovery of new chemotype dipeptidyl peptidase IV inhibitors having (R)-3-amino-3-methyl piperidine as a pharmacophore.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.

N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid is an active-site-directed fluorescent inhibitor of lipoprotein lipase, binding with an association constant (K_a) of 1.1 × 10^6 M^-1.
N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid as an active-site-directed fluorescent probe of lipoprotein lipase.
