6-deoxy-6-fluoro-alpha-D-glucopyranosyl phosphate acts as a competitive inhibitor of phosphoryl transfer by phosphoglucomutase, with its ground-state inhibition constant (Ki) providing insight into specific interactions with the sugar hydroxyls.
Binding energy and catalysis: deoxyfluoro sugars as probes of hydrogen bonding in phosphoglucomutase.
The binding of N-(5-Dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid to the active site of lipoprotein lipase is competitive and can be displaced by benzene boronic acid.
N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid as an active-site-directed fluorescent probe of lipoprotein lipase.

cis-4-decenoate levels are elevated above the control range in dried blood spots from individuals with medium chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase (MCAD) deficiency, making it a reliable and sensitive biomarker for MCAD deficiency screening.
Diagnosis of medium chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiency by measurement of cis-4-decenoic acid in dried blood spots.
3-nonaprenyl-4-hydroxybenzoate can exist as an intermediate pool in intact rat heart mitochondria and its synthetic rate in these mitochondria is similar to that of ubiquinone.
Possible existence of an intermediate pool of ubiquinone in rat heart mitochondria.

alpha-(2-pyridyl)glycine (2Pyg) can be incorporated into tripeptides via solid-phase Ugi reaction, and its presence enables the formation of unique peptide conformations with intramolecular hydrogen bonding between 2Pyg-NH and a pyridine nitrogen.

Studies on intramolecular hydrogen bonding between the pyridine nitrogen and the amide hydrogen of the peptide: synthesis and conformational analysis of tripeptides containing novel amino acids with a pyridine ring.

N-(5-Dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid is an active-site-directed fluorescent inhibitor that binds to lipoprotein lipase (EC 3.1.1.34) with an association constant (Ka) of 1.1 × 10^6 M^-1, indicating strong interaction.
N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid as an active-site-directed fluorescent probe of lipoprotein lipase.

(1S)-1-(trans-4-{[(4-trifluoromethoxyphenyl)sulfonyl]amino}cyclohexyl)-2-[(3S)-3-fluoropyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-oxoethanaminium chloride (MRL-A) is a DPP-IV inhibitor analog containing a fluoropyrrolidine moiety that undergoes metabolic activation in rat liver microsomes, primarily catalyzed by CYP3A1 and CYP3A2.
Metabolic activation of fluoropyrrolidine dipeptidyl peptidase-IV inhibitors by rat liver microsomes.

[14C]pyridoxine can be enzymatically converted to [14C]pyridoxal 5'-phosphate using pyridoxal kinase and pyridoxamine 5'-phosphate oxidase in the presence of ATP, achieving a yield greater than 60% from 550 nmol starting material and radiochemical purity greater than 98%.
An enzymatic method for the synthesis of [14C]pyridoxal 5'-phosphate from [14C]pyridoxine.
Congeners bearing an (E)-cyclooct-2-enylidenemethyl group exhibit more potent type I fatty acid synthase (FAS) inhibitory activity than (S)-3-demethylthiolactomycin with an unnatural configuration.
Synthesis and biological activity of enantiomeric pairs of 5-[(E)-cycloalk-2-enylidenemethyl]thiolactomycin congeners.

4-[4-(N,N-dimethylsulfamoyl)piperazino]-2-methylpyrimidine (SDI-1) is a sorbitol dehydrogenase inhibitor that, when administered orally, slightly delays the later stages of cataract formation in both diabetic and galactose-fed rats.
Anticataract activity of analogs of a sorbitol dehydrogenase inhibitor.

Epoxide ring opening, functional group interconversion, and deprotection of (2RS,3SR)-N(1)-benzyl-2-chloromethyl-3-benzyloxy-4,5-epoxypiperidine enable the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
An oxidation and ring contraction approach to the synthesis of (+/-)-1-deoxynojirimycin and (+/-)-1-deoxyaltronojirimycin.
GW427353 (solabegron) is a novel, selective ␤ 3-adrenergic receptor (AR) agonist investigated for its effects on bladder function in dogs.
GW427353 (solabegron), a novel, selective beta3-adrenergic receptor agonist, evokes bladder relaxation and increases micturition reflex threshold in the dog.

N-ADP binds to the high-affinity binding sites of F1-ATPase, and its binding can induce a slow formation of a 'ground-state quenching complex' at each site independently, even in the presence of 2.5 mM Mg2+.
Novel approaches towards characterization of the high-affinity nucleotide binding sites on mitochondrial F1-ATPase by the fluorescence probes 3'-O-(1-naphthoyl)adenosine di- and triphosphate.
5'-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N6-substitution function as dual-acting agonists at human A1 AR and antagonists at human A3 AR.
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.
RG7800 is a pyridopyrimidinone compound identified as the first small molecule SMN2 splicing modifier to enter human clinical trials for spinal muscular atrophy.
Specific Correction of Alternative Survival Motor Neuron 2 Splicing by Small Molecules: Discovery of a Potential Novel Medicine To Treat Spinal Muscular Atrophy.

4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs act as inhibitors of Walker carcinosarcoma 256 cells, with inhibitory activity dependent on the length of the aliphatic side chain and substitution at the terminal positions.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.
4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs exhibit antitumor activity against Walker carcinosarcoma 256, with the nitroalkene side chain being essential for this activity.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.

Amide derivatives of 8-[4-[[carboxymethyl]oxy]phenyl]-1,3-di-(n-propyl)xanthine act as selective A(2B) adenosine receptor antagonists, with para-substituted anilides showing high affinity (1–3 nM) and selectivity for human A(2B) receptors.
Anilide derivatives of an 8-phenylxanthine carboxylic congener are highly potent and selective antagonists at human A(2B) adenosine receptors.

Pentagalloylglucopyranosyl, when sulfated at positions 2 through 6, forms an allosteric inhibitor (SPGG) that binds to multiple anion-binding sites on factor XIa, contributing nearly 87% of binding energy through nonionic interactions.
Designing allosteric inhibitors of factor XIa. Lessons from the interactions of sulfated pentagalloylglucopyranosides.
5′-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N 6 -cycloalkyl or N 6 -3-halobenzyl substituents show subnanomolar affinity at hA 3 AR (K i = 0.34–0.58 nM).
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.
Alkyl and aryl groups at the C5 position of 1,2,4-tris(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-imidazole increase transactivation activity in estrogen receptor alpha positive MCF-7 breast cancer cells.
Structure-activity relationship study to understand the estrogen receptor-dependent gene activation of aryl- and alkyl-substituted 1H-imidazoles.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.
1-methyl-1 H-pyrazole-5-carboxamide derivatives inhibit the development of Haemonchus contortus larvae, with the first identified compound showing an IC50 of 0.29 µM.
Optimization of Novel 1-Methyl-1 H-Pyrazole-5-carboxamides Leads to High Potency Larval Development Inhibitors of the Barber's Pole Worm.

Thiadiazole carbamate compound 12 is the most potent inhibitor of lysosomal acid lipase (LAL) activity in eliciting decreases in cholesterol accumulation in NPC1 mutant cells and increases in neutral lipid accumulation in normal cells among the tested compounds.
Thiadiazole carbamates: potent inhibitors of lysosomal acid lipase and potential Niemann-Pick type C disease therapeutics.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
Hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic acid act as highly specific and potent reversible inactivators of copper-dependent amine oxidases, with greater affinity for plasma amine oxidase.
Inhibition of copper-dependent amine oxidases by some hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic and pyrrol-1-ylphenylacetic acids.

N-[1-(4-chloro-benzyl)-4-methoxy-1H-indol-2-ylmethyl]-propionamide is a selective antagonist of the human MT(2) melatonin receptor, with its binding mode involving accommodation of out-of-plane substituents in a hydrophobic pocket, which is fundamental for receptor selectivity.
Analysis of structure-activity relationships for MT2 selective antagonists by melatonin MT1 and MT2 receptor models.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.

cis-4-decenoate levels are elevated above the control range in dried blood spots from individuals with medium chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase (MCAD) deficiency, making it a reliable and sensitive biomarker for MCAD deficiency screening.
Diagnosis of medium chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiency by measurement of cis-4-decenoic acid in dried blood spots.

Under the described protocol, [3-13C]serine contributes approximately 100% of the methyl groups used for total body homocysteine remethylation, while accounting for only about 2.8% of total serine flux.
Tracer-derived total and folate-dependent homocysteine remethylation and synthesis rates in humans indicate that serine is the main one-carbon donor.

Microinjection of (1S,3R)-aminocyclopentanedicarboxylic acid (>5 nl, 1 mM) into the retrotrapezoid nucleus induces respiratory recovery in the ipsilateral phrenic nerve in a dose-dependent manner in rats with left C2 spinal cord hemisection.
Activation of a latent respiratory motor pathway by stimulation of neurons in the medullary chemoreceptor area of the rat.

In human breast invasive ductal carcinoma tissues, the initial velocity (Vc,6) of 14CO2 production from [6-14C]glucose increases with cancer stage, showing Vc,6 values of 1.34–1.90 (SBR I), 1.33–1.41 (SBR II), and 0.72–0.79 (SBR III).
Rate of 14CO2 production from variously labeled forms of [14C]glucose in human breast invasive ductal carcinoma tissues.
Pyridazinopyridinone compounds have been designed as novel p38 kinase inhibitors with demonstrated potency in enzyme and human whole blood assays.
Discovery of pyridazinopyridinones as potent and selective p38 mitogen-activated protein kinase inhibitors.

NONate (diethylamine NONOate) is a nitric oxide donor that induces a rapid increase in mammary blood flow up to 250% of preinfusion levels in the infused mammary gland when infused at 0.5 mg/h for 6 hours in goats.
Mammary blood flow does not limit milk yield in lactating goats.

Topical aminolevulinic acid (ALA) combined with blue light photodynamic therapy achieves a 75% or 100% clearance rate in 78% of patients with actinic keratoses on the face and 56% on the scalp at 12 weeks after 1 or 2 treatments.
Photodynamic therapy of actinic keratoses with topical aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride and fluorescent blue light.

(1S,3R)-aminocyclopentanedicarboxylic acid is a glutamate metabotropic receptor agonist that, when microinjected (1–100 nl, 1 mM) into the retrotrapezoid nucleus, increases respiratory-related activity in the contralateral phrenic nerve in rats with left C2 spinal cord hemisection.
Activation of a latent respiratory motor pathway by stimulation of neurons in the medullary chemoreceptor area of the rat.

3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionate is a catabolic product of phenylalanine metabolism by Clostridium species and is significantly higher in urine of ASDs children compared with age-matched controls.
Urinary 3-(3-Hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionic Acid, 3-Hydroxyphenylacetic Acid, and 3-Hydroxyhippuric Acid Are Elevated in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorders.
Zinc tartrate, when used as a counterion in zinc-based fixative recipes, improves nucleic acid and protein preservation compared to zinc acetate in tissue samples.
Novel zinc-based fixative for high quality DNA, RNA and protein analysis.
Topical Aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride (ALA) accumulates in actinic keratoses and, when activated by blue light, contributes to the eradication of actinic keratoses.
Photodynamic therapy of actinic keratoses with topical aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride and fluorescent blue light.

In mice with acute dextran sulfate sodium colitis, (1S,5S,6R,7R)-7chloro-3-amino-5methyl-2-azabicyclo[4.1.0]heptane hydrochloride (ONO-1714) reduces colonic nitrate and nitrite levels, numbers of nitrotyrosine-positive cells, colonic injury, and inflammation.
The inducible nitric oxide synthase inhibitor ONO-1714 blunts dextran sulfate sodium colitis in mice.
18F-labeled RGD peptide generated by tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation demonstrates prominent tumor uptake in vivo and confirmed receptor specificity.
Tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation for the rapid construction of integrin αvβ₃ targeted PET tracer based on a cyclic RGD peptide.
S1PC, a characteristic sulfur-containing amino acid produced during the aging process of garlic extract, increases IgA production in mouse splenic lymphocytes in culture.
S-1-Propenylcysteine promotes the differentiation of B cells into IgA-producing cells by the induction of Erk1/2-dependent Xbp1 expression in Peyer's patches.

Imidazole-4-acetic acid (I4AA) is a ligand that binds to GABAC receptors, and the response of the rho2A receptor to I4AA is altered when the serine residue at position 320 in the second transmembrane domain is replaced by proline, resulting in pharmacology similar to the rho1B receptor.
A single amino acid in the second transmembrane domain of GABA rho subunits is a determinant of the response kinetics of GABAC receptors.

p-[ 18 F]fluorohippurate ([ 18 F]PFH) is a potential renal tubular PET agent that provides quantitative imaging useful for estimating effective renal plasma flow and produces high-quality tomographic images in dynamic studies.
Single-step radiosynthesis and in vivo evaluation of a novel fluorine-18 labeled hippurate for use as a PET renal agent.
BLXA4 is a pro-resolving lipid mediator that, when administered as an oral rinse, is effective in reducing oral inflammation and is well-tolerated in humans.
Safety and Preliminary Efficacy of a Novel Host-Modulatory Therapy for Reducing Gingival Inflammation.

The 2-(2-chlorophenyl)thiazol-4-yl analog of 4-aryl-4H-chromene (compound 4b) exhibits potent in vitro cytotoxicity against nasopharyngeal epidermoid carcinoma KB, medulloblastoma DAOY, and astrocytoma 1321N1 cells.
Synthesis and in-vitro cytotoxicity of poly-functionalized 4-(2-arylthiazol-4-yl)-4H-chromenes.
3,4-difluorobenzylidene curcumin (CDF) shows high in vitro antiparasitic activity against Leishmania major and Toxoplasma gondii, with EC50 values in the range of 0.35 to 0.8 µM.
Activity of Fluorinated Curcuminoids against Leishmania major and Toxoplasma gondii Parasites.

At high concentrations, methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) induces aberrant HBV capsid protein assembly, favoring hexagonal arrays and promoting dissociation of metastable capsids by scavenging capsid protein and redirecting assembly into hexamer-rich structures.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.
Cholest-8(14)-en-3-ol is formed in significant amounts from cholesta-7,14-dien-3-ol under anaerobic incubation with a 10,000 x g supernatant fraction of rat liver homogenate.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.

3 beta-(p-chlorophenyl)tropan-2 beta-carboxylic acid 2-[p-(bromoacetamido)phenyl]ethyl ester is a potentially irreversible ligand for the cocaine receptor site on the dopamine transporter and exhibits the highest apparent potency among tested compounds in wash-resistant inhibition of [3H]WIN 35,428 binding.
Probes for the cocaine receptor. Potentially irreversible ligands for the dopamine transporter.

3-quinuclidinyl 4-bromobenzilate is a muscarinic cholinergic receptor ligand whose affinity is not significantly altered by replacing a phenyl ring with 2-furyl, 3-furyl, 2-thienyl, or 3-thienyl moieties.
Synthesis and receptor affinities of new 3-quinuclidinyl alpha-heteroaryl-alpha-aryl-alpha-hydroxyacetates.
Substituted 3,4-diphenyl-1,3-thiazols represent a new class of potent monoamine transporter inhibitors.
Discovery of substituted 3,4-diphenyl-thiazoles as a novel class of monoamine transporter inhibitors through 3-D pharmacophore search using a new pharmacophore model derived from mazindol.

N,N-Diethylaminoethanol 2,2-diphenyl-2-ethylthioacetate methyl iodide acts as a muscarinic receptor antagonist that displays competitive inhibition at low concentrations and allosteric inhibition at higher concentrations in CHO cells expressing human m2 and m3 muscarinic receptors.
Cardioselective derivatives of 2,2-diphenyl-2-ethylthioacetate do not discriminate between m2 and m3 muscarinic receptors expressed in CHO cells.

[5,6-3H]arachidonic acid can be enzymically converted to PGI2 by swine aortic microsomes, with tritium loss at the C-6 position, and the rate of tritium release can be used to monitor the enzymatic rearrangement.
Synthesis of [5,6-3H]arachidonic acid and its use in development of a sensitive assay for prostacyclin synthetase.
Cholest-8(14)-en-3-ol can be formed from cholest-8,14-dien-3-ol under anaerobic conditions during incubation with rat liver homogenate preparations.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.
2-iodophenyl-[1-(1-methylpiperidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indol-3-yl]methanone (AM2233) is a potent agonist at the rat CB1 cannabinoid receptor, with most of its affinity residing with the (R)-enantiomer.
Potent cannabinergic indole analogues as radioiodinatable brain imaging agents for the CB1 cannabinoid receptor.

Dibenzo[c,h]cinnoline analogues are aza analogues of benzo[i]phenanthridines and exhibit more potent topoisomerase I-targeting activity and cytotoxicity compared to their benzo[i]phenanthridine counterparts.
Substituted dibenzo[c,h]cinnolines: topoisomerase I-targeting anticancer agents.

In porcine gonadal P450arom, efficient estrogen synthesis is more dependent on P450 reductase levels than in the placental isozyme, resulting in higher release of 19-oxoandrostenedione under sub-optimal P450 reductase support.
Porcine gonadal and placental isozymes of aromatase cytochrome P450: sub-cellular distribution and support by NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase.

(S)-N-(1-benzylpyrrolidin-3-yl)-4-bromo-1-methoxy-2-naphthamide analogues bind with higher affinity at both D2 and D3 dopamine receptors and exhibit higher selectivity for D3 receptors compared to (R)-series analogues.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of naphthamides as dopamine D3 receptor ligands.
1-(2-chloroethyl)-3-trans-4-methylcyclohexyl-1-nitrosourea (MeCCNU) is a cytotoxic agent whose cytotoxicity is significantly enhanced in 9L rat brain tumor cells when polyamines are depleted by DFMO.
Possible factors in the potentiation of 1-(2-chloroethyl)-3-trans-4-methylcyclohexyl-1-nitrosourea cytotoxicity by alpha-difluoromethylornithine in 9L rat brain tumor cells.

(+)-bornyl p-coumarate is an antibacterial agent isolated from the crude chloroform bark extract of Piper caninum and exhibits activity against Gram-positive bacteria such as Bacillus cereus, Staphylococcus aureus, and Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Antibacterial hydroxycinnamic esters from Piper caninum from Paluma, north Queensland, Australia. The crystal and molecular structure of (+)-bornyl coumarate.
Substituted 3,4-diphenyl-1,3-thiazols are a class of potent inhibitors of the dopamine transporter (DAT), with K i values as low as 24 nM for certain derivatives.
Discovery of substituted 3,4-diphenyl-thiazoles as a novel class of monoamine transporter inhibitors through 3-D pharmacophore search using a new pharmacophore model derived from mazindol.

methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) is a heteroaryldihydropyrimidine antiviral that inhibits hepatitis B virus (HBV) production by enhancing the rate and extent of capsid protein assembly in vitro.
A heteroaryldihydropyrimidine activates and can misdirect hepatitis B virus capsid assembly.
Ethyl 6-cyclohexylchroman-2-carboxylate does not lower cholesterol levels but returns triglyceride levels to normal in hyperlipidemic rats.
Synthesis of ethyl 6-substituted-chroman- and -chromone-2-carboxylates. A comparative structure-activity study employing the 6-phenyl and phenoxy analogs in the triton hyperlipidemic rat model.
Ethyl 6-cyclohexylchroman-2carboxylate reduces serum cholesterol levels and inhibits hepatic 3-hydroxy-3-methyl-glutaryl Coenzyme A activity in normolipemic rats when administered chronically.
Comparison of hypocholesterolemic activity for cyclic analogs of clofibrate in normolipemic rats.

5alpha, 14beta-cholest-7-en-3beta-ol can be enzymically converted in rat liver homogenate under aerobic conditions to produce 5alpha-cholest-7-en-3beta-ol, 5alpha-cholest-8(14)-en-3beta-ol, and cholesterol, indicating inversion of the C/D cis ring junction.
Enzymatic conversion of 5alpha-cholesta-7, 14-dien-3beta-ol to 5alpha-cholesta-8, 14-dien-3beta-ol.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.
The formation clearance ratio of harmol glucuronide in rat liver tissue decreases as the rate of oxygen delivery decreases, with the effect being more pronounced in the fasted state.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.
3,5-diethyl hydantoin increases cardiac phosphorylase a activity in rats, an effect associated with decreased contractility, blood pressure, and ventricular rate.
The effect of 3,5-diethyl hydantoin on cardiac phosphorylase activity.
Harmol glucuronidation in rat liver is relatively insensitive to hypoxia, but sensitivity increases markedly in fasted animals.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.

In native and N-ethylmaleimide-treated rat liver microsomes, purified microsomal glutathione S-transferase, rat liver 9000g supernatant, and isolated rat hepatocytes, (2S)-S-(2-chloro-1,1,2-trifluoroethyl)glutathione is formed with 75–81% yield.
Stereochemistry of the microsomal glutathione S-transferase catalyzed addition of glutathione to chlorotrifluoroethene.

A 1:1 mixture of L-prolyl-L-leucyl-(-)-thiazolidine-2-carboxamide and L-prolyl-L-leucyl-(+)-thiazolidine-2-carboxamide shows marked activity in the Dopa potentiation test but does not antagonize oxotremorine-induced tremors.
New dopaminergic and potential anti-parkinson compounds, N,N-disubstituted beta-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)ethylamines.

3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide (MPCA) induces dose-dependent antinociception in the neurogenic and inflammatory phases of the formalin test in mice, with no effect on locomotion or motor performance in the rotarod test.
3-Methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception.

2-amino-3-(4-chlorobenzoyl)thiophene derivatives with various alkyl or aryl piperazine moieties at the 4-position of the thiophene ring have been evaluated for allosteric enhancer activity at A1 adenosine receptors (A1 ARs).
Synthesis of 2-amino-3-heteroaroylthiophenes and evaluation of their activity as potential allosteric enhancers at the human A1 receptor.
Imidazopyridine-dipropylacetamide functions as a TSPO ligand in the first reported rhenium complex containing this ligand, which shows high affinity for TSPO in C6 rat glioma cell membrane extracts.
A model radiopharmaceutical agent targeted to translocator protein 18 kDa (TSPO).

Prollyl-arginyl-glycinamide (AVP-(7-9]) reduces the magnitude of the pressor response induced by electrical stimulation of the mesencephalic reticular formation in urethane-anesthetized rats when administered intracerebroventricularly, indicating a mechanism similar to oxytocin fragments in modulating neurogenic hypertensive responses.
Reduction of a centrally induced pressor response by neurohypophyseal peptides: the involvement of lower brainstem mechanisms.
The antinociceptive effect of 3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide (MPCA) in the formalin test is not mediated by opioid receptors, as naloxone does not prevent its effect.
3-Methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception.
cis-3-hexenoyl-CoA is the best substrate for the isomerase activity of rat peroxisomal ACO I, with a KM of 63 ± 13 and a kcat of 21 ± 3 min⁻¹.
Intrinsic enoyl-CoA isomerase activity of rat acyl-CoA oxidase I.
H-7 induces rapid changes in astrocyte morphology that are similar to those elicited by PMA.
Astrocyte morphology altered by 1-(5-isoquinolinylsulfonyl) 2-methyl piperazine (H-7) and other protein kinase inhibitors.
Uridine monophosphorus, when administered as a conjugate targeted to hepatocytes, protects hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
Targeted protection of hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
2-(2(3H)-benzoxazolone-3-yl)acetic acid is a hydrolytic metabolite formed from methyl 2-(2(3H)-benzoxazolone-3-yl)acetate by rat hepatic microsomes in the presence of NADPH.
The in vitro hepatic microsomal metabolism of methyl 2-(2(3H)-benzoxazolone-3-yl)acetate in rats.

The (S)- and (R)-enantiomers of 5-methoxy-2-[N-(2-benzamidoethyl)-N-n-propylamino]tetralin exhibit high affinity for dopamine D2A and D3 receptors and serotonin 5-HT1A receptors, with Ki values for these receptors being in the nanomolar to low micromolar range.
Synthesis and Pharmacology of the enantiomers of the potential atypical antipsychotic agents 5-OMe-BPAT and 5-OMe-(2,6-di-OMe)-BPAT.

3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception in the neurogenic and inflammatory phases of the formalin test in mice, and this effect does not involve opioid receptors.
3-Methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception.
3,3-dimethyl-N-(tetrahydronaphthalen-1-yl)piperidine derivatives are potent and selective σ 1 ligands with poor affinity for D2, 5-HT 1A, and PCP receptors.
Novel potent sigma 1 ligands: N-[omega-(tetralin-1-yl)alkyl]piperidine derivatives.
Bromotamoxifen isomers do not induce detectable DNA adduct formation in rat liver following administration of equimolar doses, indicating they are inactive in this context.
Determination of DNA damage in F344 rats induced by geometric isomers of tamoxifen and analogues.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic acid act as highly specific and potent reversible inactivators of copper-dependent amine oxidases, with greater affinity for plasma amine oxidase.
Inhibition of copper-dependent amine oxidases by some hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic and pyrrol-1-ylphenylacetic acids.

Ferric hydrogensulfate acts as a catalyst in the multi-component, one-pot condensation reaction for synthesizing amidoalkyl naphthols from beta-naphthol, aromatic aldehydes, and acetamide under solvent, solvent-free, and microwave conditions.
An efficient, simple and expedition synthesis of 1-amidoalkyl-2-naphthols as 'drug like' molecules for biological screening.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.
Landomycin A inhibits the uptake of [3H]thymidine into DNA in murine smooth muscle cells, indicating decreased DNA synthesis.
Landomycin A inhibits DNA synthesis and G1/S cell cycle progression.
4-amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) derivatives represent a novel class of anti-cancer agents with activity influenced by the nature of substituents at the R1, R2, and R3 positions.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.
4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one analogs with certain substitutions exhibit cytotoxic and antitumor activity against various tumor and cancer cell lines, with ED50 values ranging from 0.01 to 2.1 µM.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.

7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) is a potent tubulin-polymerization inhibitor with high in vitro cytotoxic activity (GI50 1.9–3.2 nM), significant tubulin assembly inhibition (IC50 0.77 µM), and substantial inhibition of colchicine binding (99% at 5 µM).
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.

Ferric hydrogensulfate can be used as a catalyst in a one-pot multi-component condensation reaction to synthesize amidoalkyl naphthol derivatives from aryl aldehydes, 2-naphthol, and acetamide or acetonitrile.
An efficient, simple and expedition synthesis of 1-amidoalkyl-2-naphthols as 'drug like' molecules for biological screening.
4'-ethenylthymidine exhibits potent anti-HSV-1 and anti-HIV-1 activities with no significant cytotoxicity.
Nucleosides and nucleotides. 183. Synthesis of 4'alpha-branched thymidines as a new type of antiviral agent.
2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a new chiral ligand used to design platinum(II) complexes with improved aqueous solubility and cytotoxic activity.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Naphthylvinylpyridinium is a choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) inhibitor that reduces soman toxicity in mice.
Approaches to protection against nerve agent poisoning. (Naphthylvinyl)pyridine derivatives as potential antidotes.
6H-pyridocarbazole analogues are capable of DNA intercalation and exhibit higher cytotoxicity in L1210 cells in vitro compared to 11H-pyridocarbazoles.
Synthesis of 11H-pyridocarbazoles and derivatives. Comparison of their DNA binding and antitumor activity with those of 6H- and 7H-pyridocarbazoles.
7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) causes cell arrest in the G2/M phase, disrupts microtubule formation, and competes at the colchicine site on tubulin.
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.
22-(3-azidobenzoyloxy)methyl epothilone C is a C22-functionalized epothilone analog designed as a photoaffinity probe to study the beta-tubulin binding site.
Total synthesis and evaluation of 22-(3-azidobenzoyloxy)methyl epothilone C for photoaffinity labeling of beta-tubulin.

Nothospondin is a quassinoid isolated from Nothospondias staudtii that potently and dose-dependently inhibits the oncogenic transcription factor activator protein-1 (AP-1) at noncytotoxic concentrations.
Nothospondin, a new AP-1 inhibitory quassinoid from the Cameroonian plant Nothospondias staudtii.
2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

(E)-4,4'-dicyanostilbene is a stilbene derivative with an electron-withdrawing functional group that is 2-3 times more toxic than H2O2 in MTS cell viability assays in differentiated PC-12 cells under oxidative stress conditions.
The functional group on (E)-4,4'-disubstituted stilbenes influences toxicity and antioxidative activity in differentiated PC-12 cells.
2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol (HL) is a tridentate chiral ligand derived from 1R,2R-diaminocyclohexane and used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Dihydrotriazolopyridazine derivatives, such as AZD3514, are androgen receptor (AR) down-regulators that do not impair ligand-driven nuclear translocation and lack hERG channel potentiation.
Discovery of AZD3514, a small-molecule androgen receptor downregulator for treatment of advanced prostate cancer.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.
14S,22-diHDHA (Maresin-L1) acts as an autacrine or paracrine factor produced by leukocytes and platelets that diminishes diabetic impairment of macrophage reparative functions.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) attenuates the oxidative stress and inflammatory response in 661W cells exposed to lipoteichoic acid, including decreased pro-inflammatory factors IL-1beta, IL-12, and TNFalpha, and inhibition of nuclear factor kappa B and Egr1, Fosl1, and Lox12 gene expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.
14S,22-diHDHA (Maresin-L1) acts as an autocrine or paracrine factor that diminishes diabetic impairment of macrophage reparative functions.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.

4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol (SMBD) inhibits LPS-induced production of nitric oxide and prostaglandin E2 in BV2 microglia by suppressing gene expression of inducible NO synthase (iNOS) and cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), respectively, at non-toxic concentrations.
Anti-inflammatory potential of newly synthesized 4-[(butylsulfinyl)methyl]-1,2-benzenediol in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated BV2 microglia.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.

Quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt exerts an antagonistic effect on cadmium intoxication by counteracting cadmium-induced decreases in superoxide dismutase (SOD) and glutathione (GSH) levels in the mouse liver.
Influence of water-soluble flavonoids, quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt and morin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt, on antioxidant parameters in the subacute cadmium intoxication mouse model.
Both (2S,3R)- and (2R,3S)-hydroxycitric acid enantiomers produced by Bacillus megaterium G45C and Streptomyces sp. U121 are optically pure.
Absolute configuration of hydroxycitric acid produced by microorganisms.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.

Calcium silicate borate (Ca11Si4B2O22) ceramics can induce the formation of a hydroxyapatite layer on their surface when immersed in simulated body fluid (SBF) at 37°C, indicating bioactive mineralization ability relevant for hard tissue repair.
In situ hydroxyapatite nanofiber growth on calcium borate silicate ceramics in SBF and its structural characteristics.

Ferric hydrogensulfate acts as a catalyst in the multi-component, one-pot condensation reaction for synthesizing amidoalkyl naphthols from beta-naphthol, aromatic aldehydes, and acetamide under solvent, solvent-free, and microwave conditions.
An efficient, simple and expedition synthesis of 1-amidoalkyl-2-naphthols as 'drug like' molecules for biological screening.

The hydration of tricalcium oxy silicate (C3S) can be observed and its retardation influenced by the addition of polycarboxylate ether superplasticizers, as studied using fluorescence microscopy and calorimetry.
Fluorescence Microscopy of Superplasticizers in Cementitious Systems: Applications and Challenges.
19-hydroxy-Resolvin E1 is a major metabolic product of Resolvin E1 in isolated human PMN and murine spleen and lung, but less so in human blood, kidney, and liver.
Resolvin E1 metabolome in local inactivation during inflammation-resolution.

4-aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione is a core structural component of isoniazid-based triads that, when combined with isoniazid and isoniazid, confers promising anti-mycobacterial activity against the mc26230 strain of M. tuberculosis with MIC values in the range of 5.1–11.9 µM and non-cytotoxicity to Vero cells.
Design and synthesis of 4-Aminoquinoline-isoindoline-dione-isoniazid triads as potential anti-mycobacterials.

3,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid N-oxide (H2-pydcao) serves as an organic linker in the synthesis of the three-dimensional metal-organic framework [Zn(pydcao)(DMF)], where it acts as the ligand coordinating to Zn(II) centers.
A Zn(ii) metal-organic framework with dinuclear [Zn2(N-oxide)2] secondary building units.
14S,22-diHDHA is a maresin-like docosahexaenoic mediator biosynthesized by myeloid cells, monocytes, platelets, and neutrophils.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.

The thermolytic deprotection of 4-methylthio-1-butyl phosphate groups occurs via cyclodeesterification, resulting in the formation of a sulfonium salt side product that is inert to DNA nucleobases and does not cause significant desulfurization of phosphorothioate diesters.
Thermolytic 4-methylthio-1-butyl group for phosphate/thiophosphate protection in solid-phase synthesis of DNA oligonucleotides.
Quinolinium dichromate in water oxidizes vicinal and nonvicinal diols to their corresponding alpha-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
Quinolinium Dichromate Oxidation of Diols: A Kinetic Study.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
Concise means for accessing an advanced precursor to 1-deoxypaclitaxel.

Iron(II) tris-bipyridine complexes can exhibit diastereoselectivity in their solid-state structure, influenced by noncovalent interactions and solvent polarity, due to the chiral nature of the L-amino acid ligands.
Induction of diastereoselectivity in Fe(II) tris(amino acid-bipyridine) complexes.
1,4-dihydroquinoxaline-2,3-dione derivatives function as supramolecular hosts for water clusters, accommodating zero-, one-, and two-dimensional arrays of water in their hydrated structures.
Water superstructures within organic arrays; hydrogen-bonded water sheets, chains and clusters.
Quinolinium dichromate-mediated oxidation of diols proceeds almost twice as fast in D2O than in H2O.
Quinolinium Dichromate Oxidation of Diols: A Kinetic Study.
2-iodoselenophene can undergo copper-catalyzed coupling reactions with amides to form 2-nitrogenselenophene derivatives under mild conditions without the need for additional additives.
Copper-promoted carbon-nitrogen bond formation with 2-iodo-selenophene and amides.
3-thienylalanine is identified as an especially good substrate for phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase (PheRS) based on computational predictions using the HierDock protocol.
Virtual screening for binding of phenylalanine analogues to phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase.

The radical cation of 2,5-(MeO)2C6H3CH2OH decays via OHÀ-induced mechanisms involving oxygen acidity and intramolecular electron transfer, with a deuterium kinetic isotope effect of 3.7 compared to its adideuterated counterpart.
Structural effects on the OH-promoted fragmentation of methoxy-substituted 1-arylalkanol radical cations in aqueous solution: the role of oxygen acidity.

The 2′,5′-di-O-methyluridine analogue of guanosyl-(3′,3′)-uridine has a 2′-hydroxyl group that acts as an intramolecular hydrogen-bond donor, accelerating the cleavage of dinucleoside 3′,3′-monophosphates via a dianionic phosphorane intermediate by a factor of 23 compared to the parent compound.
Hydrolysis of 2',3'-O-methyleneadenosin-5'-yl bis-5'-O-methyluridin-3'-yl phosphate: the 2'-hydroxy group stabilizes the phosphorane intermediate, not the departing 3'-oxyanion, by hydrogen bonding.

Tetrameric zinc(II) bis(8-hydroxyquinoline) may enable low operating voltage organic light-emitting devices (OLEDs) due to its optimal pi-overlap pathways, altered charge distributions, and extended electronic states.
Electroluminescent zinc(II) bis(8-hydroxyquinoline): structural effects on electronic states and device performance.
N-tert-butyl-diphenylketenimine reacts with transient electrophilic phosphinidene complexes to give 2-aminophosphindoles as sole products, but also decomposes to isobutene.
Methylene-azaphosphirane as a reactive intermediate.
Lactosyl bromide can be used as a glycosyl donor for the S-glycosylation of homocysteine and cysteine containing peptides under two-phase conditions with sodium carbonate as base.
Synthesis of S-linked glycopeptides in aqueous solution.
1-deoxypaclitaxel analogues can be synthesized from the naturally occurring compound 1-deoxybaccatin VI via selective deacylation at specific acetyl positions.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
O-(diphenylphosphinyl)hydroxylamine (Dp-pONH2) can be used as a neutral source of electrophilic nitrogen to promote in situ aziridination of E-chalcone at room temperature.
Michael addition of amine derivatives to conjugate phosphinyl and phosphonyl nitrosoalkenes. Preparation of alpha-amino phosphine oxide and phosphonate derivatives.
Gold(I) diphosphine complexes can act as hosts that efficiently capture and release carbon dioxide in a controlled manner.
Guest escape and uptake in nonporous crystals of a gold(I) macrocycle.

The 2′,5′-di-O-methyluridine analogue of guanosyl-(3′,3′)-uridine has a 2′-hydroxyl group that acts as an intramolecular hydrogen-bond donor, accelerating the cleavage of dinucleoside 3′,3′-monophosphates via a dianionic phosphorane intermediate by a factor of 23 compared to the parent compound.
Hydrolytic reactions of guanosyl-(3',3')-uridine and guanosyl-(3',3')-(2',5'-di-O-methyluridine).

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl and 2-Indanyl Triazolopyrimidines Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase Display Potent and Selective Antimalarial Activity.
Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds are inhibitors of mitochondrial threonine dehydrogenase (TDH) that impede cell growth and induce autophagy in mouse embryonic stem (ES) cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.

Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds selectively inhibit TDH-mediated threonine catabolism in mouse ES cells, failing to affect the growth of HeLa cells at concentrations 300 times higher than those required to kill ES cells.
Targeted killing of a mammalian cell based upon its specialized metabolic state.
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives exhibit low toxicity and are effective against T. cruzi amastigote forms.
In vitro and in vivo trypanocidal activities of 8-methoxy-3-(4-nitrobenzoyl)-6-propyl-2H-cromen-2-one, a new synthetic coumarin of low cytotoxicity against mammalian cells.

Oral administration of 3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) at doses ≥10 mg/kg in MCMV-infected mice produces antiviral effects comparable to ganciclovir, as measured by survival and viral DNA levels in organs.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.

Repetitive intratesticular injection of 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline complex of oxovanadium(IV) at 7.5 mg/kg testis induces germ cell apoptosis, decreased sperm counts, and reduced testicular weights in vivo.
Bis(4,7-dimethyl and 5-dinitro-1,10-phenanthroline) sulfato-oxovanadium(IV)-mediated in vivo male germ cell apoptosis.

Vanillin semicarbazone exhibits potent anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma cells in mice, with efficacy comparable to bleomycin at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg (i.p.), when administered at 10 mg/kg (i.p.).
In vivo anticancer activity of vanillin semicarbazone.

Vanillin semicarbazone exhibits potent anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma cells in mice, with efficacy comparable to bleomycin at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg (i.p.), when administered at 10 mg/kg (i.p.).
In vivo anticancer activity of vanillin semicarbazone.
Diphenyl ditelluride inhibits d-ALA-D enzyme activity in mouse liver and brain, as measured by lowspeed supernatant assays.
Diphenyl diselenide and diphenyl ditelluride differentially affect delta-aminolevulinate dehydratase from liver, kidney, and brain of mice.

Conjugation of 1-(3-methoxyphenyl)-5-phenoxy-1H-tetrazole (T2) with silver nanoparticles increases its efficacy as an anti-Acanthamoeba agent and significantly reduces cytopathogenicity of Acanthamoeba castellanii compared to T2 alone.
Antiamoebic activity of synthetic tetrazoles against Acanthamoeba castellanii belonging to T4 genotype and effects of conjugation with silver nanoparticles.
Parenteral copper supplementation of the dam by copper edetate in late gestation has no effect on the liver copper concentration of the foal at birth.
Changes in liver copper concentration of thoroughbred foals from birth to 160 days of age and the effect of prenatal copper supplementation of their dams.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Isoxazolopyrimidine-Based Inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase with Antimalarial Activity.

Rubiyunnanin C is a newly isolated cyclopeptide from Rubia yunnanensis roots, and its isolation, structure, cytotoxic activities, and inhibitory activities against nitric oxide production in LPS- and IFNγ-induced RAW 264.7 murine macrophages have been described.
Rubiyunnanins C-H, cytotoxic cyclic hexapeptides from Rubia yunnanensis inhibiting nitric oxide production and NF-κB activation.
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives can lower parasitaemia in mice infected with the Y strain of T. cruzi.
In vitro and in vivo trypanocidal activities of 8-methoxy-3-(4-nitrobenzoyl)-6-propyl-2H-cromen-2-one, a new synthetic coumarin of low cytotoxicity against mammalian cells.
22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A is a newly identified 3-alkylpiperidine alkaloid isolated from the marine sponge Amphimedon sp. and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P388 murine leukemia cells.
Tetradehydrohalicyclamine A and 22-hydroxyhalicyclamine A, new cytotoxic bis-piperidine alkaloids from a marine sponge Amphimedon sp.
Ala-Ser-Leu is a novel angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide identified from silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein hydrolysate, with an IC50 value of 102.15 µM.

A novel angiotensin-І converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide from gastrointestinal protease hydrolysate of silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein: Biochemical characterization and molecular docking study.
Quarternary carbolinium cations, which are electronically charged derivatives of beta-carbolines, show much higher antimalarial potency in vitro than electronically neutral beta-carbolines.
Synthesis and evaluation of beta-carbolinium cations as new antimalarial agents based on pi-delocalized lipophilic cation (DLC) hypothesis.

Tetrahydro-2-naphthyl triazolopyrimidine analogues display a range of potencies against Plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) and lack activity against human DHODH or other key mammalian enzymes relevant for toxicological profiling.
Lead optimization of aryl and aralkyl amine-based triazolopyrimidine inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase with antimalarial activity in mice.

3-hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-N-[4(([5-(dimethylamino)-1-naphthyl]sulfonyl)amino)- phenyl]propanamide (BAY 38-4766) demonstrates antiviral activity against HCMV in a cell-encapsulation-in-hollow-fibers mouse model, with efficacy similar to ganciclovir.
Inhibition of murine cytomegalovirus and human cytomegalovirus by a novel non-nucleosidic compound in vivo.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
3-nonaprenyl-4-hydroxybenzoate can exist as an intermediate pool in intact rat heart mitochondria and its synthetic rate in these mitochondria is similar to that of ubiquinone.
Possible existence of an intermediate pool of ubiquinone in rat heart mitochondria.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
Reversible photoligation of DNA via 5-vinyldeoxyuridine.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Miniaturization of a liquid membrane sensor for the determination of bile acids.

7-[(2-aminoethyl)amino]-1-cyclopropyl-6-fluoro-1, 4-dihydro-4-oxo-3-quinoline carboxylic acid is a photochemically formed intermediate in ciprofloxacin degradation, identified by its structure elucidated from UV, mass, and NMR spectra.
Isolation and structure elucidation of an intermediate in the photodegradation of ciprofloxacin.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
Synthesis of trimethylhydroquinone derivatives as anti-allergic agents with anti-oxidative actions.

N-carbobenzoxyglycine vinyl ester inhibits pregnancy in mice when administered intravaginally and also inhibits acrosin enzymatic activity as measured by N-alpha-benzoyl-L-arginine ethyl ester and azocasein hydrolysis assays.
Antifertility activity of N-protected glycine activated esters.

4-(3,7,11,15-tetramethyl-6,10,14-hexadecatrienoyl)morpholine (E-0712) protects against liver injury induced by D-galactosamine and carbon tetrachloride in rats, as evidenced by reduced SGPT values and prothrombin time prolongation.
Protective effect of 4-(3,7,11,15-tetramethyl-6,10,14-hexadecatrienoyl)morpholine on liver injury induced by hepatotoxins in rats.
2'(3')-O-Bromoacetyluridine reacts rapidly and selectively with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A at pH 5.5 and 25°, forming N-3-carboxymethylhistidine-12 ribonuclease A as the product.
The kinetics and specificity of the reaction of 2'(3')-O-bromoacetyluridine with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A.
The presence of tyrosyl iodide in thyroid hormone analogs enhances their inhibition of 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase (5'-ITD) activity in rat liver homogenates.
Thyroid hormone analog inhibition of hepatic 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase activity.

[14C]pyridoxine can be enzymatically converted to [14C]pyridoxal 5'-phosphate using pyridoxal kinase and pyridoxamine 5'-phosphate oxidase in the presence of ATP, achieving a yield greater than 60% from 550 nmol starting material and radiochemical purity greater than 98%.
An enzymatic method for the synthesis of [14C]pyridoxal 5'-phosphate from [14C]pyridoxine.

N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid is an active-site-directed fluorescent inhibitor of lipoprotein lipase, binding with an association constant (K_a) of 1.1 × 10^6 M^-1.
N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid as an active-site-directed fluorescent probe of lipoprotein lipase.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
The 6-O-pivaloyl group of methyl 2,6-di-O-pivaloyl-alpha-D-glucopyranoside is selectively removed by esterase II from rabbit serum.
Partial purification of esterases from rabbit serum and their use in regioselective deacylations of sugars.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
1-Dehydro-10-gingerdione inhibits LPS binding to MD-2 with higher affinity than gingerol and shogaol from dietary ginger.
Inhibition of LPS binding to MD-2 co-receptor for suppressing TLR4-mediated expression of inflammatory cytokine by 1-dehydro-10-gingerdione from dietary ginger.
1-Dehydro-10-gingerdione suppresses LPS-induced expression of inflammatory cytokines and interferon-related genes in LPS-activated macrophages by down-regulating TLR4 signaling.
Inhibition of LPS binding to MD-2 co-receptor for suppressing TLR4-mediated expression of inflammatory cytokine by 1-dehydro-10-gingerdione from dietary ginger.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.
1,6-di(pyrid-2-yl)pyrene is a substrate that can be functionalized at the 2- and 2,7-positions via Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H bond activation.
The synthesis of 2- and 2,7-functionalized pyrene derivatives through Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H activation.

1-hexadecyl-3-methylimidazolium dimethyl-5-sulfoisophthalate (Hdmim)[DMSIP] is an imidazolium-based ionic liquid that, when incorporated into Pebax Rnew films at up to 5 wt.%, provides promising antimicrobial activity, good hydrophilicity, permeability, and thermal stability.
Heterogenized Imidazolium-Based Ionic Liquids in Pebax®Rnew. Thermal, Gas Transport and Antimicrobial Properties.
3-amino-N-(4-bromophenyl)-4-methoxybenzamide exhibits anti-EV 71 activity with IC50 values ranging from 5.7 ± 0.8 to 12 ± 1.2 µM in vitro.
Synthesis and antiviral activity of N-phenylbenzamide derivatives, a novel class of enterovirus 71 inhibitors.

Cyrtominetin inhibits the hemolytic activity of α-hemolysin (α-HL) by binding to the 'Loop' region of the protein, blocking the conformational transition from monomer to oligomer and thereby inhibiting protein activity, as shown by molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations.
Molecular insight into the inhibition mechanism of cyrtominetin to α-hemolysin by molecular dynamics simulation.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives exhibit anti-MERS-CoV activity in Vero cells.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.

Spiromastilactone D is a phenolic lactone isolated from the deep-sea fungus Spiromastix sp. MCCC 3A00308 that demonstrates broad anti-influenza activity by targeting viral attachment and entry through inhibition of hemagglutinin protein-sialic acid receptor interaction, and by inhibiting viral genome replication via targeting the viral RNP complex.
Spiromastilactones: A new class of influenza virus inhibitors from deep-sea fungus.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) reduces erythema intensity, thickness, and desquamation, as measured by PASI, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model when administered topically (1% w/w) or orally (125 mg/kg) for 14 days.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.

1,3-bis(N'-benzoylthioureido)benzene functions as an ionophore in PVC membrane ion-selective electrodes for Pb(II) ion detection, providing a Nernstian response in the concentration range 4.0×10⁻⁶ to 1.0×10⁻⁲ M with a slope of 31.5 ± 1.6 mV/dec, a response time of t90% = 14 s, and operational stability for at least 10 weeks.
Lead(II) ion selective electrodes with PVC membranes based on two bis-thioureas as ionophores: 1,3-bis(N'-benzoylthioureido)benzene and 1,3-bis(N'-furoylthioureido)benzene.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) is a prodrug of 2-(4-Chlorophenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetic acid (CBA) that inhibits psoriasis-associated inflammatory symptoms, including erythema, thickness, desquamation, hyperkeratosis, and hyperplasia, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives have been shown to possess high anti-MERS-CoV activity.
Identification of 4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives as potential MERS-CoV inhibitors.

4-((4-(2-chlorophenyl)thiazole-2-yl)amino)-4oxo-3-phenylbutanoic acid (2CTAP) is a functionally selective allosteric modulator of human free fatty acid receptor 2 (FFA2) that inhibits constitutive Ca2+ levels, antagonizes acetate-induced pERK1/2 phosphorylation, and prevents damage in intestinal ischemia reperfusion injury (IRI).
Characterisation of small molecule ligands 4CMTB and 2CTAP as modulators of human FFA2 receptor signalling.

Radical cation salts of 1,2,4,5-tetrakis(isopropylthio)benzene have been synthesized and characterized by UV-Vis absorption, EPR spectroscopy, conductivity measurement, single crystal X-ray diffraction, and DFT calculation.
Single-Crystal X-ray Structures of conductive π-Stacking Dimers of Tetrakis(alkylthio)benzene Radical Cations.

Ferrocenium boronic acid salt activates allylic alcohols to generate transient carbocations, enabling the formation of acyclic products with methyl-aryl quaternary carbon centers and high enantioselectivity (up to 90% yield, 97:3 enantiomeric ratio) under noble-metal-free conditions.
Dual Catalysis Using Boronic Acid and Chiral Amine: Acyclic Quaternary Carbons via Enantioselective Alkylation of Branched Aldehydes with Allylic Alcohols.

Bisdeoxycurcumin binds to human serum albumin (HSA) through a static quenching mechanism with approximately one binding site, interacting on both site I and site II, and increases α-helix structures at low concentrations.
Multi-spectroscopic analysis and molecular modeling on the interaction of curcumin and its derivatives with human serum albumin: a comparative study.

16-(3-methoxyphenyl)-9,10,11,12,13,16-hexahydro-15H-benzo [5',6']chromeno [2',3':4,5]pyrimido [1,2-a]azepin-15-imine is a benzochromene derivative identified as a potential multitarget-directed ligand for Alzheimer's disease therapy, acting as a human acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.36 ± 0.02 mM, exhibiting strong antioxidant activity (3.61 mmol Troloxe equivalents), and moderate beta-amyloid aggregation inhibition (40.3%).
Synthesis and Biological Assessment of Racemic Benzochromenopyrimidinimines as Antioxidant, Cholinesterase, and Aβ1-42 Aggregation Inhibitors for Alzheimer's Disease Therapy.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.

His-Gly-Ile, a peptide from soybean 11S glycinin hydrolysate, produces a less reduction in intracellular Ca(2+) concentration compared to other vasoactive peptides in angiotensin II-stimulated vascular smooth muscle cells.
Identification of peptides from soybean protein, glycinin, possessing suppression of intracellular Ca2+ concentration in vascular smooth muscle cells.
2-amino-5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole compounds act as benzodiazepine analogs and exhibit superior binding affinity toward GABA A receptors compared with diazepam.

Synthesis, receptor affinity and effect on pentylenetetrazole-induced seizure threshold of novel benzodiazepine analogues: 3-Substituted 5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-4H-1,2,4-triazoles and 2-amino-5-(phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazoles.
9-ethylpurine derivatives with substituents at the 2, 6, and 8-positions have been shown to exhibit nanomolar affinity for human adenosine receptors, particularly the A2A adenosine receptor subtype.
New A2A adenosine receptor antagonists: a structure-based upside-down interaction in the receptor cavity.

N-Cbz-prolinal is a sterically demanding, bulky, and conformationally restricted N-Cbz-amino aldehyde acceptor substrate for which the E. coli FucA F131A aldolase shows improved aldol activity compared to the wild type.

Structure-guided minimalist redesign of the L-fuculose-1-phosphate aldolase active site: expedient synthesis of novel polyhydroxylated pyrrolizidines and their inhibitory properties against glycosidases and intestinal disaccharidases.
Pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyrazole derivatives act as first-in-class inhibitors of p53-MDM2 interaction and the NF-kappaB pathway.
Double-edged swords as cancer therapeutics: novel, orally active, small molecules simultaneously inhibit p53-MDM2 interaction and the NF-κB pathway.

N-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3,4,5-trimethoxybenzamide derivatives with an N,N-dialkylaminoethoxy/propoxy moiety act as potential memory enhancers and exhibit acetylcholinesterase-inhibiting activity with IC50 values in the low micromolar range (4.0–16.5 µM).
N-(4-Hydroxyphenyl)-3,4,5-trimethoxybenzamide derivatives as potential memory enhancers: synthesis, biological evaluation and molecular simulation studies.
Pyridazinopyridinone compounds have been designed as novel p38 kinase inhibitors with demonstrated potency in enzyme and human whole blood assays.
Discovery of pyridazinopyridinones as potent and selective p38 mitogen-activated protein kinase inhibitors.

Arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate (AGHMB) promotes anastomotic wound healing in immunosuppressed rats with tacrolimus, as evidenced by similar neovascularization and inflammatory/fibroblast responses to honey.
Effect of a honey and arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate mixture on the healing of colon anastomosis in rats immunosuppressed with tacrolimus.

3,4,5-trihydroxy-N-(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)benzamide (SG-HQ2) is a synthetic analogue of gallic acid that attenuates histamine release by reducing intracellular calcium in human and primary peritoneal mast cells.
SG-HQ2 inhibits mast cell-mediated allergic inflammation through suppression of histamine release and pro-inflammatory cytokines.
2-amino-5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole is a potent benzodiazepine analogue examined for its binding orientation in the GABA A receptor binding site by molecular docking.

Synthesis, receptor affinity and effect on pentylenetetrazole-induced seizure threshold of novel benzodiazepine analogues: 3-Substituted 5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-4H-1,2,4-triazoles and 2-amino-5-(phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazoles.
Prolylthiazolidine derivatives with fused bicyclic heteroarylpiperazine substitution act as DPP-4 inhibitors, with some compounds exhibiting high selectivity and in vivo efficacy.
Fused bicyclic heteroarylpiperazine-substituted L-prolylthiazolidines as highly potent DPP-4 inhibitors lacking the electrophilic nitrile group.

Benzo[d]oxazol-5-amine derivatives have been synthesized and investigated for their ability to inhibit cholinesterases (eeAChE and eqBuChE) and blood-brain barrier permeability as potential multifunctional agents against Alzheimer's disease.
Discovery of novel series of 2-substituted benzo[d]oxazol-5-amine derivatives as multi-target directed ligands for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.

5-(3-[ 18 F]-fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan ([ 18 F]-L-FPTP) is a [ 18 F]-labeled 5-hydroxy-L-tryptophan tracer developed for clinical amino acid PET imaging, primarily transported by LAT2 in tumor cells, and shows high tumor uptake in model mice.
Radiosynthesis and biological evaluation of 5-(3-[18F]fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan for tumor PET imaging.
EED226 is a potent inhibitor of PRC2 activity that binds to the H3K27me3 pocket of EED and induces regression of tumor xenograft in vivo.
Discovery of First-in-Class, Potent, and Orally Bioavailable Embryonic Ectoderm Development (EED) Inhibitor with Robust Anticancer Efficacy.
Dihydrotriazolopyridazine derivatives, such as AZD3514, are androgen receptor (AR) down-regulators that do not impair ligand-driven nuclear translocation and lack hERG channel potentiation.
Discovery of AZD3514, a small-molecule androgen receptor downregulator for treatment of advanced prostate cancer.

Quercetin-5',8-disulfonate is a synthetic sulfated derivative of quercetin that, when administered orally at 100, 200, and 500 mg/kg bw prior to acute CCl4 oxidative damage in mice, attenuates serum ALT, AST, and LDH activities and hepatic malondialdehyde levels, and suppresses CCl4-induced depletion of glutathione peroxidase and total superoxide dismutase.
Protective effects of quercetin and quercetin-5',8-disulfonate against carbon tetrachloride-caused oxidative liver injury in mice.
Tetrafluorobenzene sulfonamide derivatives synthesized using click chemistry have been studied for their inhibitory activity against human carbonic anhydrase isoforms hCA I, II, IX, and XII.

Benzenesulfonamides Incorporating Flexible Triazole Moieties Are Highly Effective Carbonic Anhydrase Inhibitors: Synthesis and Kinetic, Crystallographic, Computational, and Intraocular Pressure Lowering Investigations.

Quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt exerts an antagonistic effect on cadmium intoxication by counteracting cadmium-induced decreases in superoxide dismutase (SOD) and glutathione (GSH) levels in the mouse liver.
Influence of water-soluble flavonoids, quercetin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt and morin-5'-sulfonic acid sodium salt, on antioxidant parameters in the subacute cadmium intoxication mouse model.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
2-[4-(5-chlorobenzo[d]thiazol-2-yl)phenoxy]-2-methylpropanoic acid (MHY908) potently inhibits mushroom tyrosinase activity with an IC50 of 8.19 µM in a dose-dependent manner.
Inhibition of melanogenesis by 2-[4-(5-chlorobenzo[d]thiazol-2-yl)phenoxy]-2-methylpropanoic acid (MHY908).

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.
5'-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N6-substitution function as dual-acting agonists at human A1 AR and antagonists at human A3 AR.
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

3-(benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole ([18F]-IND1) is a positron emission tomography radiotracer structurally related to the acetylcholinesterase inhibitor CP126,998, designed for noninvasive detection of acetylcholinesterase changes in Alzheimer's disease patients.
3-(Benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole: a PET radiotracer to measure acetylcholinesterase in brain.

2-(butyloxy)-4-[4-(methylsulfonyl)phenyl]-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyrimidine is a potent and selective cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitor with a favorable pharmacokinetic profile, high brain penetration, and good efficacy in rat models of hypersensitivity.
Identification of [4-[4-(methylsulfonyl)phenyl]-6-(trifluoromethyl)-2-pyrimidinyl] amines and ethers as potent and selective cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitors.
