EED226 is a potent inhibitor of PRC2 activity that binds to the H3K27me3 pocket of EED and induces regression of tumor xenograft in vivo.
An allosteric PRC2 inhibitor targeting the H3K27me3 binding pocket of EED.
4-indolylamino-5-phenyl-3-pyridinecarbonitrile derivatives act as inhibitors of PKCtheta and have been explored as potential anti-inflammatory agents.
Synthesis and PKCtheta inhibitory activity of a series of 4-indolylamino-5-phenyl-3-pyridinecarbonitriles.

His-Gly-Ile, a peptide from soybean 11S glycinin hydrolysate, produces a less reduction in intracellular Ca(2+) concentration compared to other vasoactive peptides in angiotensin II-stimulated vascular smooth muscle cells.
Identification of peptides from soybean protein, glycinin, possessing suppression of intracellular Ca2+ concentration in vascular smooth muscle cells.
The anti-microtubule activity of phenylahistin is dependent on a uniplanar pseudo-three-ring structure formed by hydrogen bonding between diketopiperazine and imidazole rings.
Synthesis and biological activities of phenylahistin derivatives.

3,4,5-trihydroxy-N-(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)benzamide (SG-HQ2) is a synthetic analogue of gallic acid that attenuates histamine release by reducing intracellular calcium in human and primary peritoneal mast cells.
SG-HQ2 inhibits mast cell-mediated allergic inflammation through suppression of histamine release and pro-inflammatory cytokines.

(E)-2-(2-(4-(dimethylamino)styryl)-6-methyl-4H-pyran-4-ylidene) malononitrile (PAD-1) is a smart dicynomethylene-4H-pyran fluorophore that rapidly enters the brain after intravenous injection and shows a turn-on fluorescence effect upon binding to aggregated beta-amyloid fibrils.
In vivo detection of cerebral amyloid fibrils with smart dicynomethylene-4H-pyran-based fluorescence probe.
5′-C-(ethyltetrazol-2-yl)adenosine derivatives with N 6 -cycloalkyl or N 6 -3-halobenzyl substituents show subnanomolar affinity at hA 3 AR (K i = 0.34–0.58 nM).
Exploring the Role of N6-Substituents in Potent Dual Acting 5'-C-Ethyltetrazolyladenosine Derivatives: Synthesis, Binding, Functional Assays, and Antinociceptive Effects in Mice ∇.

trans-1,4-bis[[1-cyclohexyl-3-(4-dimethylamino phenyl)ureido]methyl]cyclohexane (NTE-122) is a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase (ACAT) inhibitor that inhibits cell-free ACAT activity in human PMA-treated THP-1 cells and mouse J774.1 cells with IC50 values of 0.88 nM and 360 nM, respectively.
Effects of NTE-122, a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase inhibitor, on cholesterol esterification and high-density lipoprotein-induced cholesterol efflux in macrophages.
Calmidazolium stimulates steroidogenesis in rat Leydig cells, with a mechanism of action that may share a common site with LH but bypasses the need for cyclic AMP, Ca2+, and protein synthesis.
Calmidazolium is a potent stimulator of steroidogenesis via mechanisms not involving cyclic AMP, calcium or protein synthesis.

7-Methoxy-4-(2-methylquinazolin-4-yl)-3,4-dihydroquinoxalin- 2(1H)-one (5f) is a potent tubulin-polymerization inhibitor with high in vitro cytotoxic activity (GI50 1.9–3.2 nM), significant tubulin assembly inhibition (IC50 0.77 µM), and substantial inhibition of colchicine binding (99% at 5 µM).
Optimization of 4-(N-cycloamino)phenylquinazolines as a novel class of tubulin-polymerization inhibitors targeting the colchicine site.
Landomycin A inhibits the uptake of [3H]thymidine into DNA in murine smooth muscle cells, indicating decreased DNA synthesis.
Landomycin A inhibits DNA synthesis and G1/S cell cycle progression.

2-naphthylethyl-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (THI 53) inhibits LPS-mediated iNOS protein induction by reducing phosphorylation of tyrosine kinases, especially Tyk2 and STAT-1, and by affecting the JAK2/STAT-1 pathway.

Regulation of lipopolysaccharide-induced inducible nitric-oxide synthase expression through the nuclear factor-kappaB pathway and interferon-beta/tyrosine kinase 2/Janus tyrosine kinase 2-signal transducer and activator of transcription-1 signaling cascades by 2-naphthylethyl-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (THI 53), a new synthetic isoquinoline alkaloid.

3-(1-(4-bromophenyl)-5-phenyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl) pyridine exhibits cytotoxic activity against breast cancer and leukemic cells by inducing apoptosis, as shown by live-dead cell assay, cell cycle analysis, mitochondrial membrane potential assays, Annexin V-FITC staining, DNA fragmentation, Hoechst staining, and western blot.
Regioselective synthesis and biological studies of novel 1-aryl-3, 5-bis (het) aryl pyrazole derivatives as potential antiproliferative agents.
The hexapeptide Arg-Glu-Leu-Leu-Lys-Met, when displayed on a filamentous phage, is recognized by autoantibodies from patient ITP-2 that target the GPIIb/IIIa complex.
Characterization of autoantigenic epitopes on platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa using random peptide libraries.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
6-(substituted methylene)penems, potent broad spectrum inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamase. IV. Kidney stability, serum binding and additional biological evaluation of racemic derivatives.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Isonucleosides incorporating universal bases.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.
Congeners bearing an (E)-cyclooct-2-enylidenemethyl group exhibit more potent type I fatty acid synthase (FAS) inhibitory activity than (S)-3-demethylthiolactomycin with an unnatural configuration.
Synthesis and biological activity of enantiomeric pairs of 5-[(E)-cycloalk-2-enylidenemethyl]thiolactomycin congeners.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Identification of 3-sulfonylindazole derivatives as potent and selective 5-HT(6) antagonists.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Discovery of 5-arylsulfonamido-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole derivatives as potent, selective 5-HT6 receptor agonists and antagonists.
GW427353 (solabegron) is a novel, selective ␤ 3-adrenergic receptor (AR) agonist investigated for its effects on bladder function in dogs.
GW427353 (solabegron), a novel, selective beta3-adrenergic receptor agonist, evokes bladder relaxation and increases micturition reflex threshold in the dog.
4-indolylamino-5-phenyl-3-pyridinecarbonitrile derivatives act as inhibitors of PKCtheta and have been explored as potential anti-inflammatory agents.
Synthesis and PKCtheta inhibitory activity of a series of 4-indolylamino-5-phenyl-3-pyridinecarbonitriles.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.
Pyridazinopyridinone compounds have been designed as novel p38 kinase inhibitors with demonstrated potency in enzyme and human whole blood assays.
Discovery of pyridazinopyridinones as potent and selective p38 mitogen-activated protein kinase inhibitors.
14S,22-diHDHA (Maresin-L1) acts as an autacrine or paracrine factor produced by leukocytes and platelets that diminishes diabetic impairment of macrophage reparative functions.
Maresin-like lipid mediators are produced by leukocytes and platelets and rescue reparative function of diabetes-impaired macrophages.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.
Deltorphin II analogues with 6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid in position 1.
D-arabinonamide-5-phosphate is a strong high energy intermediate analog competitive inhibitor of fructose-6-phosphate isomerization catalyzed by Trypanosoma brucei phosphoglucose isomerase.
Competitive inhibition of Trypanosoma brucei phosphoglucose isomerase by D-arabinose-5-phosphate derivatives.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
6-(substituted methylene)penems, potent broad spectrum inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamase. IV. Kidney stability, serum binding and additional biological evaluation of racemic derivatives.

2-nitrofuran-5-carboxamide derivatives containing basic and/or electrophilic (alkylating) side chains have been synthesized and their biological activity, including as radiosensitizers and bioreductively activated cytotoxins, has been evaluated.
Synthesis of a series of nitrothiophenes with basic or electrophilic substituents and evaluation as radiosensitizers and as bioreductively activated cytotoxins.

Samarium(III) triflate (Sm(OTf)3) is used to activate benzylated glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones, enabling them to react with simple and carbohydrate alcohols to yield β-mixtures of glycosides in moderate to excellent yield.
A glycosylation protocol based on activation of glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones with samarium triflate.
H-Gly-Pro-Arg-Pro-Pro-NH2 is a synthetic peptide analog of the N-terminal portion of fibrin alpha-chain that exhibits potent inhibitory effects on fibrinogen/thrombin-mediated clotting.
Amino acids and peptides. XVIII. Synthetic peptides related to N-terminal portion of fibrin alpha-chain and their inhibitory effect on fibrinogen/thrombin clotting.

Octathymidylate, when covalently linked to an intercalating agent and complementary to a crucial A-T-rich region in the SV40 origin of replication, inhibits replication of SV40 viral DNA in CV1 cells by binding to open regions or forming local triple helices.
Inhibition of simian virus 40 DNA replication in CV-1 cells by an oligodeoxynucleotide covalently linked to an intercalating agent.
Phenylglycine analogues can exhibit agonist-dependent antagonism at group I metabotropic glutamate receptors (mGluR1, mGluR5), necessitating careful pharmacological consideration.
Antagonist activity of alpha-substituted 4-carboxyphenylglycine analogues at group I metabotropic glutamate receptors expressed in CHO cells.
gamma-L-glutamyl-L-tyrosine infusion in rats increases fractional excretion of phosphate from 2.8% ± 0.8% to 17.5% ± 5.2% (a 14.6% ± 4.8% increase) without changing dopamine excretion.
Effect of gamma-L-glutamyl-L-dopa on phosphate excretion.
Alpha-fluoromethylhistidine depletes neural histamine and does not prevent caloric stimulation-induced inhibition of locus coeruleus neuronal activity in rats.
Histaminergic influence on vestibular stimulation-induced locus coeruleus inhibition in rats.

Peptide 6A is a fibrinogen-degradation product that induces vasorelaxation in femoral artery by stimulating release of endothelium-derived relaxing factor, primarily 6-keto-PGF1 alpha, and increases femoral blood flow in vivo.
Fibrin(ogen) degradation product peptide 6A increases femoral artery blood flow in dogs.
Octathymidylate can be covalently linked to an acridine derivative and, upon UV irradiation, forms a local triple helix with viral DNA at A-T rich regions within the viral origin of replication.
Inhibition of simian virus 40 DNA replication in CV-1 cells by an oligodeoxynucleotide covalently linked to an intercalating agent.
Glutamyl-L-tyrosine, as a control, increases fractional excretion of phosphate from 2.8% ± 0.8% to 17.5% ± 5.2% (∆14.6% ± 4.8%, P < .03) in rats without affecting dopamine excretion.
Effect of gamma-L-glutamyl-L-dopa on phosphate excretion.

Sultamicillin, an orally active form of ampicillin/sulbactam, is bactericidal to Mycobacterium leprae in mice, with efficacy increasing from 54% inhibition at 0.01% to 93% inhibition at 0.20% of the drug in feed.
Bactericidal action of oral ampicillin/sulbactam against Mycobacterium leprae.

Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate) is essentially inactive compared to ryanodine for insecticidal activity against Musca domestica adults and Tribolium castaneum larvae, for toxicity to mice, and for competition with [3H]ryanodine at the Ca(2+)-ryanodine receptor complex of skeletal muscle.
Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate): a novel ryanoid from Ryania insecticide.
D-2-Aminophosphonoheptanoate (DAP-7) blocks the excitatory effects of NMDA but not kainic acid, quisqualic acid, or AMPA on heart rate and contractility in the spinal cord.
NMDA receptors in the intermediolateral column of the spinal cord mediate sympathoexcitatory cardiac responses elicited from the ventrolateral medullary pressor area.

RC-3095 acetate, a bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide (GRP) antagonist, administered subcutaneously twice daily at 10 micrograms for 4 weeks suppresses tumor growth in xenograft models of MCF-7 MIII human breast cancer in nude mice.
Inhibitory effect of bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide (GRP) antagonists RC-3950-II and RC-3095 on MCF-7 MIII human breast cancer xenografts in nude mice.
D-Phe-Cys-Tyr-D-Trp-Orn-Thr-Pen-Thr-NH(2) is a mu-selective opioid receptor antagonist that reduces alcohol consumption in a limited access model in alcohol-preferring C57BL/6 mice.
The effect of antagonists selective for mu- and delta-opioid receptor subtypes on alcohol consumption in C57BL/6 mice.
D-arabinonamide-5-phosphate is a strong high energy intermediate analog competitive inhibitor of fructose-6-phosphate isomerization catalyzed by Trypanosoma brucei phosphoglucose isomerase.
Competitive inhibitors of yeast phosphoglucose isomerase: synthesis and evaluation of new types of phosphorylated sugars from the synthon D-arabinolactone-5-phosphate.

1-aminoindane-1,5-dicarboxylate is a type I mGlu receptor antagonist that selectively attenuates the cardiovascular depressor and bradycardic responses to (R,S)-3,5-dihydroxyphenylglycine in the nucleus tractus solitarius.
Type I and II metabotropic glutamate receptors mediate depressor and bradycardic actions in the nucleus of the solitary tract of anaesthetized rats.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

5-Iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) is converted to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine (IUdR) by aldehyde oxidase in the liver, a reaction that does not require exogenous cofactors and is not catalyzed by mixed-function oxidases, xanthine oxidase, or many other oxido-reductases.
Conversion of 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine by aldehyde oxidase. Implication in hepatotropic drug design.

8-aza-2'-deoxyadenosine is a weakly cytotoxic nucleoside that, when formulated as a 5'-bis(pivaloyloxymethyl) phosphate prodrug, exhibits significantly enhanced cytotoxicity in CEM cells compared to the parent nucleoside.
Enhancement of nucleoside cytotoxicity through nucleotide prodrugs.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

Bis-chelated gold(I) diphosphine complexes, such as [Au(d2pypp)2]Cl, selectively induce apoptosis in breast cancer cells at submicromolar concentrations by targeting mitochondria and inhibiting the thioredoxin/thioredoxin reductase system.
A gold(I) phosphine complex selectively induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells: implications for anticancer therapeutics targeted to mitochondria.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been designed and synthesized as inhibitors of Aurora A kinase.
Design, synthesis, biological evaluation of 6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives as novel anticancer agents with Aurora kinase inhibition.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) enables radiosensitization in both MMR-proficient and MMR-deficient human colon cancer xenografts, with a sensitizer enhancement ratio (SER) of 1.48 at 2 Gy per fraction and 1.41 at 4 Gy per fraction in MMR-deficient tumors, and 1.21 at 2 Gy and 1.20 at 4 Gy in MMR-proficient tumors.
Differential radiosensitization in DNA mismatch repair-proficient and -deficient human colon cancer xenografts with 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose.

The pyrrolo[1,2,4]triazine nucleus effectively mimics the quinazoline kinase inhibitor template and can be substituted at positions 5 and 6 to confer inhibitory activity against EGFR and VEGFR-2 kinase activity.
Discovery of the pyrrolo[2,1-f][1,2,4]triazine nucleus as a new kinase inhibitor template.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives with alkylamino side chains act as highly selective ligands for binding to telomeric G-quadruplex DNA, effectively stabilizing the G-quadruplex structure and showing significant selectivity over duplex DNA.
Disubstituted 2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives as a new type of highly selective ligands for telomeric G-quadruplex DNA.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.
2'-(18)F-fluorofolic acid demonstrates high and specific uptake in folate receptor-positive KB tumors, enabling clear-cut visualization by PET.
A new 18F-labeled folic acid derivative with improved properties for the PET imaging of folate receptor-positive tumors.

N-(2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl)benzamide derivatives can be structurally optimized as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with compound 8r showing strong inhibition of FLT3 and FLT3 mutants relevant to AML.
Rational design and synthesis of 2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole derivatives as inhibitors targeting FMS-like tyrosine kinase 3 (FLT3) and its mutants.
Ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives can inhibit the growth of A549 lung cancer cells in a dose-dependent manner by inducing cell cycle arrest.
Synthesis, single-crystal characterization and preliminary biological evaluation of novel ferrocenyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrazin-4(5H)-one derivatives.

Methyl substitution of 1,10-phenanthroline to 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline in oxovanadium complexes enhances anti-leukemic activity, as seen in bis(4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline)sulfatooxovanadium(IV) which induces reactive oxygen species production, G1-arrest, and inhibits clonogenic growth in human leukemia cells at nanomolar concentrations.
Synthesis, X-ray structure, and anti-leukemic activity of oxovanadium(IV) complexes.

Oral administration of 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) at 1 g/kg/day for 14 days results in significantly higher 5-iododeoxyuridine (IUdR)-DNA incorporation in MMR-deficient HCT116 human colon cancer xenografts compared to MMR-proficient HCT116/3-6 xenografts.
Differential radiosensitization in DNA mismatch repair-proficient and -deficient human colon cancer xenografts with 5-iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

3-amino-N-(4-(4-(1,2-benzisothiazol-3-yl)-1-piperazinyl)butyl)-2 -pyridinecarboxamide is a heterocyclic carboxamide analogue that exhibits potent in vivo antipsychotic activity comparable to 1192U90 in the mouse climbing assay.
Synthesis and evaluation of heterocyclic carboxamides as potential antipsychotic agents.
Nalbuphine pivalate permeates skin predominantly through the lipid matrix, as shown by relative rates of passive and iontophoretic permeation in Wistar rat skins.
Delivery of nalbuphine and its prodrugs across skin by passive diffusion and iontophoresis.

trans-1,4-bis[[1-cyclohexyl-3-(4-dimethylamino phenyl)ureido]methyl]cyclohexane (NTE-122) is a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase (ACAT) inhibitor that inhibits cell-free ACAT activity in human PMA-treated THP-1 cells and mouse J774.1 cells with IC50 values of 0.88 nM and 360 nM, respectively.
Effects of NTE-122, a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase inhibitor, on cholesterol esterification and high-density lipoprotein-induced cholesterol efflux in macrophages.
Adamantyl triazole is a potent and selective inhibitor of 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase 1 that is unaffected by NADP(H) binding to the enzyme.
Adamantyl triazoles as selective inhibitors of 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1.

Cortisol 21-sulfate restores normal circadian rhythmicity of food intake in CRH1RÀ/À mutant mice, with both wildtype and mutant mice consuming 27 ± 2.5% and 26 ± 5.3% of their total food intake per 24 hours during the light period after CORT 21-sulfate administration.
Disruption of feeding behavior in CRH receptor 1-deficient mice is dependent on glucocorticoids.

(E)-4-(4-[(5-methyl-2-phenyl-1, 3-oxazol-4-yl)methoxy]benzyloxyimino)-4-phenylbutyric acid (TAK-559) exhibits marked glucose and lipid lowering activity in KKA(y) mice without significant body weight gain and demonstrates favorable pharmacokinetic properties with good absorption and duration.
Studies on non-thiazolidinedione antidiabetic agents. 2. Novel oxyiminoalkanoic acid derivatives as potent glucose and lipid lowering agents.
2-iodophenyl-[1-(1-methylpiperidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indol-3-yl]methanone (AM2233) is a potent agonist at the rat CB1 cannabinoid receptor, with most of its affinity residing with the (R)-enantiomer.
Potent cannabinergic indole analogues as radioiodinatable brain imaging agents for the CB1 cannabinoid receptor.

TAK-559 is a novel oxyiminoalkanoic acid derivative that acts as a partial agonist for hPPARg1, a dual agonist for hPPARg1 and hPPARa, and a slight agonist for hPPARy in transient transactivation, binding, and cofactor assays.
A novel oxyiminoalkanoic acid derivative, TAK-559, activates human peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor subtypes.
Compound 544, an adamantyl triazole, is a selective 11b-HSD1 inhibitor.
Adamantyl triazoles as selective inhibitors of 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1.
Nalbuphine pivalate shows enhanced in vitro passive permeation across various skin types when subjected to iontophoresis, with the aqueous pathway being the primary contributor to this enhancement.
Delivery of nalbuphine and its prodrugs across skin by passive diffusion and iontophoresis.
Calmidazolium stimulates steroidogenesis in rat Leydig cells, with a mechanism of action that may share a common site with LH but bypasses the need for cyclic AMP, Ca2+, and protein synthesis.
Calmidazolium is a potent stimulator of steroidogenesis via mechanisms not involving cyclic AMP, calcium or protein synthesis.

(2S)-N-(5-cyclopropyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-2-[4-(2-oxo-1-pyrrolidinyl)phenyl]propanamide (PHA-533533) is a CDK2/cyclin A-E inhibitor with a K(i) of 31 nM for CDK2/cyclin A, inhibiting tumor cell proliferation in various cell lines with an IC50 in the submicromolar range.
3-Aminopyrazole inhibitors of CDK2/cyclin A as antitumor agents. 2. Lead optimization.
Methyl 1,5-diphenyl-2,3-diazabicyclo[3.1.0]hex-2-ene-6-carboxylate exhibits good binding affinity for GABA A R5 receptors.
A new pyridazine series of GABAA alpha5 ligands.
Naltrexonazine is a selective mu(1)-opioid receptor antagonist that does not reduce the antitussive effect of endomorphin-2 in mice.
The antitussive effects of endomorphin-1 and endomorphin-2 in mice.

Radioiodinated 2-iodophenyl-[1-(1-methylpiperidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indol-3-yl]methanone binds selectively to brain CB1 receptors in mouse and rat brain sections, with little to no specific binding to CB1 receptor knockout mice.
Potent cannabinergic indole analogues as radioiodinatable brain imaging agents for the CB1 cannabinoid receptor.
D-Phe-Cys-Tyr-D-Trp-Orn-Thr-Pen-Thr-NH(2) is a mu-selective opioid receptor antagonist that reduces alcohol consumption in a limited access model in alcohol-preferring C57BL/6 mice.
The effect of antagonists selective for mu- and delta-opioid receptor subtypes on alcohol consumption in C57BL/6 mice.
(2S)-N-(5-cyclopropyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-2-[4-(2-oxo-1-pyrrolidinyl)phenyl]propanamide (also known as PHA-533533) is a potent CDK2 inhibitor with a favorable druglike preclinical profile.
3-Aminopyrazole inhibitors of CDK2/cyclin A as antitumor agents. 2. Lead optimization.

(1R,1 ′ R,2R,2 ′ R)-N 1 ,N 1 ′ -(anthracene-1,8-diylbis(methylene))bis(N 2 ,N 2 -bis(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)cyclohexane-1,2-diamine) (R-ABDC, 4a) is an optically active dinuclear ligand designed to position a Fe(II) center at the 1- and 8-positions of an anthracene bridge, facilitating DNA intercalation and DNA-cleaving activity in the presence of FeSO 4 ⋅7H 2 O and excess sodium ascorbate.
Design and synthesis of an anthranyl bridged optically active dinuclear iron(II)-ligand and evaluation of DNA-cleaving activity.
2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a new chiral ligand used to design platinum(II) complexes with improved aqueous solubility and cytotoxic activity.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol (HL) is a tridentate chiral ligand derived from 1R,2R-diaminocyclohexane and used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

2-benzoyl-6-methylpyridine thiosemicarbazone (6-MeBpT) is a novel thiosemicarbazone iron chelator whose analogues with a single methyl substituent on the pyridine ring exhibit the highest anti-proliferative activity among tested compounds.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of substituted 2-benzoylpyridine thiosemicarbazones: novel structure-activity relationships underpinning their anti-proliferative and chelation efficacy.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

1-(4-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)benzoyl)-3-cyanopyrrolo[1,2-a]quinoline is a highly active apoptosis inducer in caspase activation and cell growth inhibition assays with low nanomolar EC50 and GI50 values in human breast cancer (T47D), colon cancer (HCT116), and hepatocellular carcinoma (SNU398) cell lines.

Discovery of 1-benzoyl-3-cyanopyrrolo[1,2-a]quinolines as a new series of apoptosis inducers using a cell- and caspase-based high-throughput screening assay. Part 1: Structure-activity relationships of the 1- and 3-positions.
Benzophenone bis-sulfamate derivatives are potent steroid sulfatase inhibitors with submicromolar IC50 values (e.g., 2.0 nM for 29f) against recombinant, purified steroid sulfatase.
4,4'-Benzophenone-O,O'-disulfamate: a potent inhibitor of steroid sulfatase.

2-ethylamino-curcumin derivatives with substituted triazole moieties act as TRAIL sensitizers in TRAIL-resistant human CRT-MG astroglioma cells, exhibiting sensitization effects without or with slight cytotoxicity.
Synthesis of diethylamino-curcumin mimics with substituted triazolyl groups and their sensitization effect of TRAIL against brain cancer cells.

5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate (IP-dUMP) is a mechanism-based, irreversible inactivator of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase, covalently modifying Tyr94 at the enzyme's active site.
Modification of Escherichia coli thymidylate synthase at tyrosine-94 by 5-imidazolylpropynyl-2'-deoxyuridine 5'-monophosphate.

6-methylchroman-2-carboxylic acid N-(4-chlorophenyl)amide derivatives have been designed and synthesized as potential NF-κB inhibitors, with the most active compound in the series being 2-times more potent than the reference compound KL-1156.
Structure-activity relationship of indoline-2-carboxylic acid N-(substituted)phenylamide derivatives.
4-(1H-indazol-5-yl)-6-phenylpyrimidin-2(1H)one analogs exhibit strong affinity for CDC7 in vitro.
4-(1H-indazol-5-yl)-6-phenylpyrimidin-2(1H)-one analogs as potent CDC7 inhibitors.
Benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives can be synthesized via a microwave-assisted one-pot four-component approach and exhibit cytotoxic and antimicrobial activities.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of novel benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives.
2-fluoro-5-((4-oxo-3,4-dihydrophthalazin-1-yl)methyl)benzoic acid derivatives can function as dual inhibitors of poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase-1 (PARP-1) and histone deacetylase-1 (HDAC-1).
Design, synthesis and anticancer activities of novel dual poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase-1/histone deacetylase-1 inhibitors.
4-amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) derivatives represent a novel class of anti-cancer agents with activity influenced by the nature of substituents at the R1, R2, and R3 positions.
Antitumor agents 278. 4-Amino-2H-benzo[h]chromen-2-one (ABO) analogs as potent in vitro anti-cancer agents.
Tasiamide is an acyclic peptide containing a hydroxy acid, two N-methylamides, and an ester, structural features common among cyanobacterial peptides.
Tasiamide, a cytotoxic peptide from the marine cyanobacterium Symploca sp.
Benzophenone disulfamate compounds exhibit high inhibitory potency against steroid sulfatase (estrone sulfatase, E.C. 3.1.6.2.).
4,4'-Benzophenone-O,O'-disulfamate: a potent inhibitor of steroid sulfatase.

Benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives can be synthesized via a microwave-assisted, one-pot, four-component approach using 2-hydroxy-1,4-naphthoquinone, aromatic aldehydes, methyl hydrazine, and o-phenylenediamine.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of novel benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives.
A pyridazin-4-one fragment acts as a cathepsin S inhibitor with an IC50 of 170 µM, serving as a template for designing more potent CatS inhibitors.
Diazinones as P2 replacements for pyrazole-based cathepsin S inhibitors.
The intrinsic deuterium isotope effect for N,N-dibenzyl-1,4-diaminobutane oxidation by mouse N-acetylpolyamine oxidase is 6.7.
Mechanistic studies of para-substituted N,N'-dibenzyl-1,4-diaminobutanes as substrates for a mammalian polyamine oxidase.
Carboxyethylhydroxychroman is a main route of excretion for ␥-tocopherol (␥-T) in vivo.
Alpha- and gamma-tocotrienols are metabolized to carboxyethyl-hydroxychroman derivatives and excreted in human urine.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.

Nickel Gluconate, when administered as part of a Heel-Potentised Swine Organ Preparation (D10/D30/D200), is an effective primary treatment for pediatric ranula and intraoral mucocele, with 89% of ranulas and 67% of labial mucoceles responding completely to this homotoxicological therapy.
Nickel Gluconate-Mercurius Heel-Potentised Swine Organ Preparations: a new therapeutical approach for the primary treatment of pediatric ranula and intraoral mucocele.
Dihydrotriazolopyridazine derivatives, such as AZD3514, are androgen receptor (AR) down-regulators that do not impair ligand-driven nuclear translocation and lack hERG channel potentiation.
Discovery of AZD3514, a small-molecule androgen receptor downregulator for treatment of advanced prostate cancer.
Butanone-etheno-2'-deoxyadenosine is a DNA adduct related to lipid peroxidation detected in gastric mucosae from Japanese and Chinese individuals.
Lipid peroxidation-induced DNA adducts in human gastric mucosa.
Macimorelin acetate is an accurate, well-tolerated, and safe diagnostic test for patients with adult growth hormone deficiency (AGHD).
Macimorelin as a Diagnostic Test for Adult GH Deficiency.
Nicotinamide riboside is the only known vitamin precursor that supports neuronal NAD+ synthesis.
Nicotinamide riboside is uniquely and orally bioavailable in mice and humans.
Carboxyethylhydroxychroman is a main route of excretion for ␥-tocopherol (␥-T) in vivo.

Tocopherol metabolites 2, 5, 7, 8-tetramethyl-2-(2'-carboxyethyl)-6-hydroxychroman (alpha-CEHC) and 2, 7, 8-trimethyl-2-(2'-carboxyethyl)-6-hydroxychroman (gamma-CEHC) in human serum after a single dose of natural vitamin E.
Butanone-etheno-2'-deoxyadenosine is a lipid peroxidation-induced DNA adduct identified in the gastric mucosa of gastric cancer patients.
Lipid peroxidation-induced DNA adducts in human gastric mucosa.

Topical aminolevulinic acid (ALA) combined with blue light photodynamic therapy achieves a 75% or 100% clearance rate in 78% of patients with actinic keratoses on the face and 56% on the scalp at 12 weeks after 1 or 2 treatments.
Photodynamic therapy of actinic keratoses with topical aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride and fluorescent blue light.

ChloraPrep, a solution containing 2% chlorhexidine gluconate and 70% isopropyl alcohol, is the most effective surgical skin-preparation solution for eliminating bacteria from the hallux and toes in the foot and ankle prior to surgery.
Efficacy of surgical preparation solutions in foot and ankle surgery.

cis-4-decenoate levels are elevated above the control range in dried blood spots from individuals with medium chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase (MCAD) deficiency, making it a reliable and sensitive biomarker for MCAD deficiency screening.
Diagnosis of medium chain acyl CoA dehydrogenase deficiency by measurement of cis-4-decenoic acid in dried blood spots.
Butanone-etheno-2'-deoxyadenosine is a lipid peroxidation-related DNA adduct identified in human gastric mucosa, with levels ranging from 0 to 30,000 per 10^9 bases.
Lipid peroxidation-induced DNA adducts in human gastric mucosa.
N-carbobenzoxyglycine vinyl ester provides 100% inhibition of fertility when administered intravaginally.
Antifertility activity of N-protected glycine activated esters.
Carboxyethylhydroxychroman is a main route of excretion for ␥-tocopherol (␥-T) in vivo.
Urinary excretion of 2,7, 8-trimethyl-2-(beta-carboxyethyl)-6-hydroxychroman is a major route of elimination of gamma-tocopherol in humans.
19-hydroxy-Resolvin E1 is a major metabolic product of Resolvin E1 in isolated human PMN and murine spleen and lung, but less so in human blood, kidney, and liver.
Resolvin E1 metabolome in local inactivation during inflammation-resolution.
Topical Aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride (ALA) accumulates in actinic keratoses and, when activated by blue light, contributes to the eradication of actinic keratoses.
Photodynamic therapy of actinic keratoses with topical aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride and fluorescent blue light.
BLXA4 is a pro-resolving lipid mediator that, when administered as an oral rinse, is effective in reducing oral inflammation and is well-tolerated in humans.
Safety and Preliminary Efficacy of a Novel Host-Modulatory Therapy for Reducing Gingival Inflammation.

Ferulic acid 4-o-glucuronide is a urinary biomarker for wholegrain and rye bread that is not discriminatory in post-prandial urine when comparing standardized to non-standardized meals, but is a biotransformation product of other well-functioning biomarkers.
Biomarkers of Whole-Grain and Cereal-Fiber Intake in Human Studies: A Systematic Review of the Available Evidence and Perspectives.

(S)-6-(3-methoxypyrrolidin-1-yl)-2-phenylpyrimidine-4-carboxamide is a potent and selective P2Y 12 antagonist with low nanomolar IC50 values in binding and light transmission aggregometry assays, and shows no inhibition of major P450 enzymes or the hERG channel.

4-((R)-2-{[6-((S)-3-Methoxypyrrolidin-1-yl)-2-phenylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino}-3-phosphonopropionyl)piperazine-1-carboxylic Acid Butyl Ester (ACT-246475) and Its Prodrug (ACT-281959), a Novel P2Y12 Receptor Antagonist with a Wider Therapeutic Window in the Rat Than Clopidogrel.
Topically applied liposome-encapsulated copper palmitate provides significant protection against both Photofrin- and aminolevulinic acid-induced protoporphyrin IX photosensitivity in animal skin.
Topical use of liposomal copper palmitate formulation blocks porphyrin-induced photosensitivity in rats.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.
A 0.1% copper-zinc malonate-containing cream applied topically increases elastin synthesis and promotes regeneration of elastic fibres in human skin in vivo.
Extracellular matrix in cutaneous ageing: the effects of 0.1% copper-zinc malonate-containing cream on elastin biosynthesis.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.
The formation clearance ratio of harmol glucuronide in rat liver tissue decreases as the rate of oxygen delivery decreases, with the effect being more pronounced in the fasted state.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.
bis(7-hydroxyheptyl) octanedioate is a new fatty acid ester isolated from Feces Trogopterus, and its structure was elucidated by chemical and spectroscopic analysis.
Four new fatty acid esters from the Feces of Trogopterus xanthipes.

5-hydroxymethyl-2-hydroxy-4-methoxy-phenylethanone is a newly isolated compound from Craterellus odoratus that has been evaluated for inhibitory activity against human 11b-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase (11b-HSD1) and cytotoxic activity on five human cancer cell lines.
Four new compounds from the basidiomycete Craterellus odoratus.
NGA0187 is a neuritogenic compound isolated from the fermentation broth of Acremonium sp. TF-0356.
A novel neuritogenic compound, NGA0187.

Pentosan polysulfate functions as a chiral selector in capillary electrochromatography (CE) for the separation of basic analytes, particularly enantioresolved tryptophan derivatives and other compounds with two amine groups.
Separation of enantiomers by capillary electrophoresis using pentosan polysulfate.
Pentosan polysulfate is a linear polysaccharide that is being examined as a chiral selector for use in capillary electrophoresis enantioseparations.
Separation of enantiomers by capillary electrophoresis using pentosan polysulfate.
Topical copper palmitate treatment avoids the prolonged inflammatory skin reaction associated with Photofrin II-mediated photodynamic therapy.
Topical use of liposomal copper palmitate formulation blocks porphyrin-induced photosensitivity in rats.

In human breast invasive ductal carcinoma tissues, the initial velocity (Vc,6) of 14CO2 production from [6-14C]glucose increases with cancer stage, showing Vc,6 values of 1.34–1.90 (SBR I), 1.33–1.41 (SBR II), and 0.72–0.79 (SBR III).
Rate of 14CO2 production from variously labeled forms of [14C]glucose in human breast invasive ductal carcinoma tissues.
Harmol glucuronidation in rat liver is relatively insensitive to hypoxia, but sensitivity increases markedly in fasted animals.
Fasting increases the sensitivity of hepatic harmol glucuronidation to hypoxia.

hypoxylonol E is a benzo[j]fluoranthene derivative isolated from Hypoxylon truncatum that exhibits antiproliferative activity against HUVECs (human umbilical vein endothelial cells) and HUAECs (human umbilical artery endothelial cells).
Hypoxylonols C-F, benzo[j]fluoranthenes from Hypoxylon truncatum.

Heronapyrrole A is a structurally unique 2-nitropyrroloterpene produced by Streptomyces sp. (CMB-M0423) and shows significant activity against Gram-positive bacteria without adverse effects on mammalian cell lines.
Heronapyrroles A-C: farnesylated 2-nitropyrroles from an Australian marine-derived Streptomyces sp.

N'-methyl 5-hydroxytryptamine (N'-methyl 5-HT) is an indole alkaloid whose concentration in the skin secretions of the Australian Golden Bell frog, Litoria aurea, is 0.26 µg per mg of skin secretion as determined by LC/ESI-ion trap mass spectrometry.
Characterisation and determination of indole alkaloids in frog-skin secretions by electrospray ionisation ion trap mass spectrometry.

(+)-bornyl p-coumarate is an antibacterial agent isolated from the crude chloroform bark extract of Piper caninum and exhibits activity against Gram-positive bacteria such as Bacillus cereus, Staphylococcus aureus, and Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Antibacterial hydroxycinnamic esters from Piper caninum from Paluma, north Queensland, Australia. The crystal and molecular structure of (+)-bornyl coumarate.

Copper-zinc malonate cream at 0.1% concentration applied to the periorbital area can promote regeneration of elastic fibres in the upper papillary dermis, including those projecting perpendicularly to the dermal-epidermal junction, as shown by Hart's stain in skin biopsies at 6 weeks.
Extracellular matrix in cutaneous ageing: the effects of 0.1% copper-zinc malonate-containing cream on elastin biosynthesis.
Copper-zinc malonate, when applied topically as a 0.1% cream, is proposed to restore functional elastic fibres in the skin by promoting the synthesis of uncrosslinked tropoelastin.
Extracellular matrix in cutaneous ageing: the effects of 0.1% copper-zinc malonate-containing cream on elastin biosynthesis.

[1-13C]-butyric acid can be administered via jejunal vein in piglets, and its enrichment in arterial blood reflects splanchnic tissue synthesis, with a mean arterial to portal (ART/PV) ratio of 0.74 ± 0.1 and arterial enrichment of 96.0% over 120 minutes.
Butyric acid is synthesized by piglets.

4-hydroxycinnamic ethyl ester derivatives, both monomeric and dimeric, exhibit antioxidant activity against copper-catalyzed human LDL oxidation, with lower oxidation potential and IC50 values for the monomeric series: sinapate > ferulate > p-coumarate.
4-hydroxycinnamic ethyl ester derivatives and related dehydrodimers: Relationship between oxidation potential and protective effects against oxidation of low-density lipoproteins.

Replacement of the Pro residue in the cyclic hexapeptide L-363,301 with N-(4-aminobutyl) glycine (Nlys) maintains binding to recombinant human somatostatin receptors, and the length of the basic peptoid residue does not influence binding to the hsst2 receptor.
Synthesis and binding potencies of cyclic hexapeptide analogs of somatostatin incorporating acidic and basic peptoid residues.
p-ethoxybenzhydroxamic acid acts as a competitive inhibitor of the trypanosome alternative oxidase (TAO) with respect to decyl-CoQ-H2, competing for the enzyme's active site.
Competition between inhibitors of the trypanosome alternative oxidase (TAO) and reduced coenzyme Q9.

N,N'-bis-(3-aminopropyl) cyclohexane-1,4-diamine (DCD) is a synthetic polyamine that, when administered intravenously at 2.5, 5, and 10 mg/kg in rats, dose-dependently reduces integrated C-fiber reflex responses and wind-up activity.
Effect of the synthetic polyamine N,N'-bis-(3-aminopropyl) cyclohexane-1,4-diamine (DCD) on rat spinal cord nociceptive transmission.
The thioacetate methyl ester prodrug derivative of compound 6m acts as an orally active and potent inhibitor of endothelin-converting enzyme-1 (ECE-1) activity in rats.
Synthesis and biological activity of novel potent endothelin-converting enzyme-1 inhibitors.

2,3-dihydro-1-butyl-2-thioxoindole-3-one exhibits in vitro antimycobacterial activity against Mycobacterium tuberculosis, M. kansasii, M. fortuitum, M. intracellulare, and M. avium, showing higher activity than isoniazid.
New antimycobacterial 2,3-dihydro-1-alkylindole-2-thiones.

The R-(-) isomer of methyl 1-(2,2-dimethylindan-1-yl)imidazole-5-carboxylate (CGA 214372) is a strong uncompetitive inhibitor of P450-dependent obtusifoliol 14 alpha-demethylase (P450OBT.14DM) with an I50 of 8 x 10^-9 M and I50/Km of 5 x 10^-5 in maize microsomal assays.
Plant sterol biosynthesis: novel potent and selective inhibitors of cytochrome P450-dependent obtusifoliol 14 alpha-methyl demethylase.

The penetration rate of diethylmalonate through decontaminated skin is increased by dehydration of the skin, due to the higher hydration ability of certain decontamination solutions lowering the skin's barrier property.
Wet decontamination-induced stratum corneum hydration--effects on the skin barrier function to diethylmalonate.

Unlike RANTES, aminooxypentane (AOP)-RANTES does not induce late signaling responses in CCR5-transfected HEK-293 cells, including FAK association, impaired cell polarization for migration, or chemotaxis.
Similarities and differences in RANTES- and (AOP)-RANTES-triggered signals: implications for chemotaxis.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) inhibits apoptosis and reduces infarct size in myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury by activating the PI3K/Akt pathway and reducing inflammation.

(S)-1-(alpha-naphthylmethyl)-6,7-dihydroxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline (CKD712) reduces rat myocardial apoptosis against ischemia and reperfusion injury by activation of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/Akt signaling and anti-inflammatory action in vivo.

6-Chloro-(4S)-cyclopropyl-3,4-dihydro-4-((2-pyridyl)-ethynyl)quinazol in- 2(1H)-one (L-738,372) is a reversible, nonnucleoside inhibitor of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase that acts as a mixed noncompetitive inhibitor against poly(rC).oligo(dG) as primer-template.

Inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by a quinazolinone and comparison with inhibition by pyridinones. Differences in the rates of inhibitor binding and in synergistic inhibition with nucleoside analogs.

(1S)-1-(trans-4-{[(4-trifluoromethoxyphenyl)sulfonyl]amino}cyclohexyl)-2-[(3S)-3-fluoropyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-oxoethanaminium chloride (MRL-A) is a DPP-IV inhibitor analog containing a fluoropyrrolidine moiety that undergoes metabolic activation in rat liver microsomes, primarily catalyzed by CYP3A1 and CYP3A2.
Metabolic activation of fluoropyrrolidine dipeptidyl peptidase-IV inhibitors by rat liver microsomes.

Decylchloroacetate is hydrolyzed by Pseudomonas fluorescens lipase in emulsion systems, with the reaction rate not being influenced by stirring speed or organic volume fraction at low surface coverage.
Pseudomonas fluorescens lipase adsorption and the kinetics of hydrolysis in a dynamic emulsion system.

Bisdeoxycurcumin binds to human serum albumin (HSA) through a static quenching mechanism with approximately one binding site, interacting on both site I and site II, and increases α-helix structures at low concentrations.
Multi-spectroscopic analysis and molecular modeling on the interaction of curcumin and its derivatives with human serum albumin: a comparative study.
A 2-strand Kirchmayr-Kessler barbed suture made of glycolic carbonate is significantly weaker than a 2-strand polydioxane suture in terms of tensile strength when used in a tendon repair technique.
Biomechanical evaluation of flexor tendon repair using barbed suture material: a comparative ex vivo study.

Total body retention of [14C]pyridoxine administered orally is modestly but significantly lower in old rats compared to young rats, with similar tissue distribution and biokinetic parameters in both age groups.
Retention of 14C label is lower in old than in young Wistar rats after oral dosing with [14C]pyridoxine.
N-(3,5-difluorophenyl)-2- pyridinecarbothioamide is a potent selective mucosal protectant that is equipotent with ranitidine against gastric injury caused by stress in rats.
N-phenyl-2-pyridinecarbothioamides as gastric mucosal protectants.

Aminooxypentane (AOP)-RANTES is a derivative of RANTES that can trigger immediate responses such as Ca2+ influx, receptor dimerization, tyrosine phosphorylation, and recruitment of Galphai and JAK/STAT to the CCR5 receptor in CCR5-transfected HEK-293 cells.
Similarities and differences in RANTES- and (AOP)-RANTES-triggered signals: implications for chemotaxis.

6-Chloro-(4S)-cyclopropyl-3,4-dihydro-4-((2-pyridyl)-ethynyl)quinazol in- 2(1H)-one (L-738,372) inhibits the azidothymidine-resistant form of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase (D67N, K70R, T215Y, K219Q) with 2-3-fold greater potency than the wild-type enzyme.

Inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by a quinazolinone and comparison with inhibition by pyridinones. Differences in the rates of inhibitor binding and in synergistic inhibition with nucleoside analogs.

Benzylamino propanamide derivatives, such as NW-1029, exhibit affinity for the Na(+) channel complex and act as voltage- and use-dependent inhibitors of Na(+) currents in rat dorsal root ganglion sensory neurons.
Anti-allodynic effect of NW-1029, a novel Na(+) channel blocker, in experimental animal models of inflammatory and neuropathic pain.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

2′-deoxyguanosine-5′-tetraphosphate can be efficiently incorporated into DNA during polymerase reactions when modified with four different length coumarin-PEG tags at the 5′-terminal phosphate, allowing for single-molecule discrimination of the tags by nanopore ionic current blockade.
PEG-labeled nucleotides and nanopore detection for single molecule DNA sequencing by synthesis.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Iodide-2-thiouracil binary clusters show that low energy electron attachment leads to the formation of anion states with both dipole bound and valence bound character, with the dipole bound state decaying rapidly (~50 fs) after excitation near the vertical detachment energy.
Electron attachment dynamics following UV excitation of iodide-2-thiouracil complexes.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
pppGpG forms a ternary complex with cGAS and Mn2+, which induces a unique α-helix formation at the cGAS active site, facilitating cGAMP synthesis.
Mn2+ Directly Activates cGAS and Structural Analysis Suggests Mn2+ Induces a Noncanonical Catalytic Synthesis of 2'3'-cGAMP.
Adenosine 3'-phosphonate acts as an inhibitor of the MurMN/FemABX family of tRNA-dependent ligases, which are implicated in high-level penicillin resistance in gram-positive bacteria.
Adenosine phosphonate inhibitors of lipid II: alanyl tRNA ligase MurM from Streptococcus pneumoniae.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Replacing the phosphoramidate linkage in deoxyadenosine phosphoramidate with a phosphodiester linkage increases the substrate's affinity for and incorporation velocity by HIV-1 reverse transcriptase.
Influence of the linkage between leaving group and nucleoside on substrate efficiency for incorporation in DNA catalyzed by reverse transcriptase.
The 7-propynyl derivative of 8-aza-7-deaza-2'-deoxyadenosine increases DNA duplex stability due to increased polarizability and hydrophobic character of the propynyl group.
Propynyl groups in duplex DNA: stability of base pairs incorporating 7-substituted 8-aza-7-deazapurines or 5-substituted pyrimidines.

The fraction of 2'-methylguanosine 5'-phosphoguanosine formed from 2'-methylguanosine in a reaction at constant total monomer concentration (0.7 M) varies from 50% to about 10%, with the order self-condensation > binary mixture (U,G > C,G) > U,C,G mixture.
Sequence- and regio-selectivity in the montmorillonite-catalyzed synthesis of RNA.
1,6-di(pyrid-2-yl)pyrene is a substrate that can be functionalized at the 2- and 2,7-positions via Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H bond activation.
The synthesis of 2- and 2,7-functionalized pyrene derivatives through Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H activation.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

5-Fluorouracil dipalmitate shows higher in vitro antitumor activity when encapsulated in liquid-crystalline liposomes compared to solid-type liposomes, although its incorporation efficiency is lower in liquid-crystalline liposomes.
[Synthesis and properties of 5-fluorouracil derivatives].

2,5-dichloro-3,6-bis((3-carboxy-1-pentyl-4-quinolone-6-yl)amino)-1,4-benzoquinone inhibits prion protein conversion by affecting both the kinetics and thermodynamics of fibrillation, shortening the lag phase and reducing aggregate formation without significant cytotoxicity at 10 µM.
Multitarget Therapeutic Leads for Alzheimer's Disease: Quinolizidinyl Derivatives of Bi- and Tricyclic Systems as Dual Inhibitors of Cholinesterases and β-Amyloid (Aβ) Aggregation.

The 4-bromophenyl benzyloxy l-alanyl phosphate derivative of DOT is a potent prodrug that inhibits HIV replication with a 50% effective concentration of 0.089 µM, representing a 75-fold increase in anti-HIV activity compared to the parent nucleoside DOT without increased cytotoxicity.
Phosphoramidate and phosphate prodrugs of (-)-beta-D-(2R,4R)-dioxolane-thymine: synthesis, anti-HIV activity and stability studies.

Introducing a lipophilic moiety onto the pyridine unit of GdPCTA- [12] can create a micellar MRI contrast agent with higher T1-relaxivity above the critical micellar concentration (CMC) compared to previously reported q=1 complexes.
Unexpected aggregation of neutral, xylene-cored dinuclear GdIII chelates in aqueous solution.

Glycerol sebacate is a synthetic elastomer that can be used as a material for hybrid heart patches, supporting hESC-derived cardiomyocytes for more than 3 months when pre-conditioned in culture medium.
An elastomeric patch derived from poly(glycerol sebacate) for delivery of embryonic stem cells to the heart.

N-acetylcysteine acts as a thiol catalyst that facilitates the formation of lactate and glycerate from glyceraldehyde by forming a hemithioacetal with pyruvaldehyde and subsequently catalyzing its hydrolysis.
Thiol-catalyzed formation of lactate and glycerate from glyceraldehyde.
3',5'-O-dipalmitoyl-5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine exhibits antitumour activity in murine models at 100–600 times lower doses than free 5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine.
[Synthesis and properties of 5-fluorouracil derivatives].
6(S)-methyl 4a-hydroxytetrahydropterin is a good substrate for 4a-hydroxytetrahydropterin dehydratase, as it is completely consumed by the enzyme in the absence of biphasic progress curves.
Catalytic characterization of 4a-hydroxytetrahydropterin dehydratase.
3'-isocyano-3'-deoxythymidine has a much greater affinity (Ki) for MT-4 cell dThd kinase than 2',3'-dideoxyuridine derivatives.
Anti-retrovirus activity of 3'-fluoro- and 3'-azido-substituted pyrimidine 2',3'-dideoxynucleoside analogues.

2,5-dichloro-3,6-bis((3-carboxy-1-pentyl-4-quinolone-6-yl)amino)-1,4-benzoquinone inhibits prion protein conversion by affecting both the kinetics and thermodynamics of fibrillation, shortening the lag phase and reducing aggregate formation without significant cytotoxicity at 10 µM.
Potent and selective structure-based dibenzofuran inhibitors of transthyretin amyloidogenesis: kinetic stabilization of the native state.

TPA-NAC is a nonfluorogenic prodrug designed for chemo-selective labeling of human serum albumin in live cells, featuring a conjugated acceptor skeleton with N-acetyl-l-cysteine (NAC) to enable fluorescence turn-on and drug release upon covalent bonding.
Synthesis and evaluation of bioorthogonal pantetheine analogues for in vivo protein modification.
Ethylenebis(hydroxyphenylglycine) exhibits superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity when complexed to Cu(II) and Mn(II) ions.
The design of synthetic superoxide dismutase mimetics: seven-coordinate water soluble manganese(ii) and iron(ii) complexes and their superoxide dismutase-like activity studies.
3',5'-O-dipalmitoyl-5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine exhibits antitumour activity in murine models at 100–600 times lower doses than free 5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine.
Synthesis and interaction with uridine phosphorylase of 5'-deoxy-4',5-difluorouridine, a new prodrug of 5-fluorouracil.

The yield of feruloylated monoglycerol feruloyl monooleoylglycerol increases sharply from 0% to 91% at 1.33 h and then increases smoothly to 96% at 10 h during the esterification of ethyl ferulate and glycerol with oleic acid catalyzed by Candida antarctica lipase.
A novel method for the synthesis of glyceryl monocaffeate by the enzymatic transesterification and kinetic analysis.
3',5'-O-dipalmitoyl-5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine exhibits antitumour activity in murine models at 100–600 times lower doses than free 5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine.
Synthesis and biological activity of 5-fluoro-2',3'-dideoxy-3'-fluorouridine and its 5'-phosphate.
Daphnetoxin is an activator of both classical and novel PKC isoforms, exhibiting selectivity over mezerein.
Identification of potent, selective protein kinase C inhibitors based on a phorbol skeleton.

1-(2-deoxy-2-fluoro-β-D-arabinofuranosyl)-5-methylcytosine is a nucleoside analog with antiviral activity and low human cell toxicity, possibly through selective binding to HSV-induced thymidine kinase.
Differential activity of potential antiviral nucleoside analogs on herpes simplex virus-induced and human cellular thymidine kinases.
8-propynylated 2'-deoxyadenosine derivatives have been studied for their ability to stabilize oligonucleotide duplexes.
Biophysical and biochemical properties of oligodeoxy-nucleotides containing 4'-C- and 5'-C-substituted thymidines.
5-Dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives with various substituents on the sugar moiety exhibit antimycobacterial activity depending on the length of the substituent at the C5 position.
Recent advances in the synthesis of conformationally locked nucleosides and their success in probing the critical question of conformational preferences by their biological targets.
8-propynylated 2'-deoxyadenosine derivatives have been studied for their ability to stabilize oligonucleotide duplexes.
Stereocontrolled synthesis of diene and enyne sugar-modified nucleosides and their interaction with S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine hydrolase.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.

Arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate (AGHMB) promotes anastomotic wound healing in immunosuppressed rats with tacrolimus, as evidenced by similar neovascularization and inflammatory/fibroblast responses to honey.
Effect of a honey and arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate mixture on the healing of colon anastomosis in rats immunosuppressed with tacrolimus.

Quercetin-5',8-disulfonate is a synthetic sulfated derivative of quercetin that, when administered orally at 100, 200, and 500 mg/kg bw prior to acute CCl4 oxidative damage in mice, attenuates serum ALT, AST, and LDH activities and hepatic malondialdehyde levels, and suppresses CCl4-induced depletion of glutathione peroxidase and total superoxide dismutase.
Protective effects of quercetin and quercetin-5',8-disulfonate against carbon tetrachloride-caused oxidative liver injury in mice.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) reduces erythema intensity, thickness, and desquamation, as measured by PASI, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model when administered topically (1% w/w) or orally (125 mg/kg) for 14 days.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.

Vanillin semicarbazone exhibits potent anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma cells in mice, with efficacy comparable to bleomycin at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg (i.p.), when administered at 10 mg/kg (i.p.).
In vivo anticancer activity of vanillin semicarbazone.

Vanillin semicarbazone exhibits potent anticancer activity against Ehrlich ascites carcinoma cells in mice, with efficacy comparable to bleomycin at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg (i.p.), when administered at 10 mg/kg (i.p.).
In vivo anticancer activity of vanillin semicarbazone.

Methyl-2-(4-chloro-phenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetate (MCBA) is a prodrug of 2-(4-Chlorophenyl)-5-benzoxazoleacetic acid (CBA) that inhibits psoriasis-associated inflammatory symptoms, including erythema, thickness, desquamation, hyperkeratosis, and hyperplasia, in an imiquimod-induced psoriatic mouse model.
Synthesis and In-Vivo Evaluation of Benzoxazole Derivatives as Promising Anti-Psoriatic Drugs for Clinical Use.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.
A randomized, double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled comparative clinical trial of arginine aspartate plus adenosine monophosphate for the intermittent treatment of male erectile dysfunction.

O-methyl-L-tyrosinyl-gamma-L-glutamyl-L-cysteinylglycine (UPF1) accelerates recovery of human bronchial epithelial cells from cigarette smoke condensate exposure and restores redox pathway and energy production metabolites more rapidly than N-acetylcysteine.
Alterations of bronchial epithelial metabolome by cigarette smoke are reversible by an antioxidant, O-methyl-L-tyrosinyl-γ-L-glutamyl-L-cysteinylglycine.
Intravitreal pre-treatment with magnesium acetyltaurate reduces caspase-3 activation and prevents retinal cell apoptosis and optic nerve axon loss induced by endothelin-1.
Protective effect of magnesium acetyltaurate against endothelin-induced retinal and optic nerve injury.

3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionate is a catabolic product of phenylalanine metabolism by Clostridium species and is significantly higher in urine of ASDs children compared with age-matched controls.
Urinary 3-(3-Hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropionic Acid, 3-Hydroxyphenylacetic Acid, and 3-Hydroxyhippuric Acid Are Elevated in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorders.

Cytarabine ocfosfate hydrate is an oral chemotherapeutic agent that can induce persistent molecular complete remission in elderly patients with relapsed acute myeloid leukemia (AML) harboring an inv(16)(p13.1q22) genetic abnormality.
Persistent molecular remission of refractory acute myeloid leukemia with inv(16)(p13.1q22) in an elderly patient induced by cytarabine ocfosfate hydrate.

(6R)-6-[N-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)sulfamoyl]cyclohex-1-ene-1-carboxylate (TAK242) is a TLR4 pathway inhibitor that, in a rat model of sepsis induced with lipopolysaccharide, improves heart function, reduces cardiomyocyte degeneration and necrosis, decreases inflammatory cell infiltration, apoptosis, and caspase-3 expression, and lowers mRNA and protein levels of tumor necrosis factor-alpha, Interleukin-6, Notch intracellular domain, Hes1, and P-P65 NF-kappaB in the heart.
Effects of the interaction of Notch and TLR4 pathways on inflammation and heart function in septic heart.
6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) prevents and reverses fulminant malignant hyperthermia (MH) crises triggered by isoflurane anesthesia in mouse models.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.

The anti-endosialin-MC-VC-PABC-MMAE antibody-drug conjugate is selectively cytotoxic to endosialin-positive cells in vitro and produces profound and durable antitumor efficacy in preclinical human tumor xenograft models of endosialin-positive sarcomas and neuroblastomas.
Anti-Endosialin Antibody-Drug Conjugate: Potential in Sarcoma and Other Malignancies.
18F-labeled RGD peptide generated by tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation demonstrates prominent tumor uptake in vivo and confirmed receptor specificity.
Tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation for the rapid construction of integrin αvβ₃ targeted PET tracer based on a cyclic RGD peptide.

(4S)-4-(3-fluoro-4-fluorophenyl)-L-glutamate (18F-FSPG, or BAY 94-9392) is a PET tracer designed to assess system xC transporter activity and can visualize tumors in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma.
(4S)-4-(3-18F-fluoropropyl)-L-glutamate for imaging of xC transporter activity in hepatocellular carcinoma using PET: preclinical and exploratory clinical studies.
Hydroxyprolyl-Hydroxyproline is a fecal amino acid analog whose levels in AML patients show a remarkable positive correlation with the gut bacteria biomarkers Collinsella and Coriobacteriaceae.
Alteration of gut microbiome and correlated amino acid metabolism are associated with acute myelocytic leukemia carcinogenesis.

Daily administration of 3-O-[(E)-4-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-2-oxobut-3-en-1-yl]kaempferol (Fla-OEt) in diet-induced obese mice significantly decreases oral glucose tolerance test, intraperitoneal insulin tolerance test, and serum lipids.
Flavonoid derivative exerts an antidiabetic effect via AMPK activation in diet-induced obesity mice.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.

3Beta-(4-Methylphenyl)-2beta-[3'-(4-fluorobenzyl)isoxazol-5-yl]tropane exhibits high selectivity for the dopamine transporter (IC50 = 5.9 nM, Ki at NET = 454 nM, Ki at 5-HTT = 113 nM) among tested analogs.
Synthesis and monoamine transporter binding properties of 2beta-[3'-(substituted benzyl)isoxazol-5-yl]- and 2beta-[3'-methyl-4'-(substituted phenyl)isoxazol-5-yl]-3beta-(substituted phenyl)tropanes.
6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) prevents and reverses fulminant malignant hyperthermia (MH) crises triggered by isoflurane anesthesia in mouse models.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.

(2S,4R,5R)-2-(bis(4-fluorophenyl)methyl)-5-((4-methoxybenzyl)amino)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-4-ol (D-473) is a compound whose stereochemistry at the hydroxyl group on the pyran ring significantly influences its affinities for monoamine transporters, indicating a stereospecific preference for interaction.
Novel Potent Dopamine-Norepinephrine and Triple Reuptake Uptake Inhibitors Based on Asymmetric Pyran Template and Their Molecular Interactions with Monoamine Transporters.

4-(2-(benzhydryloxy)ethyl)-1-((R)-2-hydroxy-2-phenylethyl)-piperidin-3-ol derivatives exhibit high affinity for the dopamine transporter (DAT) and moderate to high affinity for the norepinephrine transporter (NET) in vitro.
Synthesis and biological characterization of (3R,4R)-4-(2-(benzhydryloxy)ethyl)-1-((R)-2-hydroxy-2-phenylethyl)-piperidin-3-ol and its stereoisomers for activity toward monoamine transporters.

1-(4-chlorophenyl)-4,4,4-trifluoro-3-hydroxy-2-buten-1-one is a protonophore with potent in vitro and in vivo antibacterial activity against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), showing a MIC of 4 µg/ml and reduced intracellular MRSA in macrophages.
Activity of a novel protonophore against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus.
CH3Hg-Hcy is a transportable substrate of hOAT1, as shown by its uptake in MDCK II cells expressing hOAT1.
Handling of the homocysteine S-conjugate of methylmercury by renal epithelial cells: role of organic anion transporter 1 and amino acid transporters.
Correctors containing the 4-methoxyphenyl cyclopropane carboxamide moiety are inactive for F508del-CFTR correction.
Structure-guided combination therapy to potently improve the function of mutant CFTRs.
