Correctors containing the 4-methoxyphenyl cyclopropane carboxamide moiety are inactive for F508del-CFTR correction.
Small-molecule correctors of defective DeltaF508-CFTR cellular processing identified by high-throughput screening.
C35 phosphodolichol acts as a mycoketide anchor required for T cell activation when co-cultured with MDDC.
CD1c tetramers detect ex vivo T cell responses to processed phosphomycoketide antigens.

Dodecyl-alpha-D-maltoside is a non-ionic detergent that stabilizes and enables crystallization of the human nuclear receptor hRARgamma ligand-binding domain by mediating a crystal contact in the coactivator binding region.
NFkappaB selectivity of estrogen receptor ligands revealed by comparative crystallographic analyses.

Phosphatidylcholine(16:0/26:2(5Z,9Z)) is an endogenous phospholipid found in the aqueous humor of both control and POAG donor tissues, with significantly higher amounts in control aqueous humor compared to POAG donor aqueous humor.
Phospholipid profiles of control and glaucomatous human aqueous humor.
Vesicles formed from single-chain β-D-galactopyranosylamide-based amphiphiles can encapsulate molecules of various sizes and host model biochemical reactions.
Kinetic resolution of constitutional isomers controlled by selective protection inside a supramolecular nanocapsule.
Selected 4-(2-(benzhydryloxy)ethyl)-1-((R)-2-hydroxy-2-phenylethyl)-piperidin-3-ol compounds show moderate affinity for the serotonin transporter (SERT) in vitro.
Synthesis and biological characterization of (3R,4R)-4-(2-(benzhydryloxy)ethyl)-1-((R)-2-hydroxy-2-phenylethyl)-piperidin-3-ol and its stereoisomers for activity toward monoamine transporters.
(-)-(2R,3R)-5-(2-[18F]fluoroethoxy)-3-(4-phenylpiperidin-1-yl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-naphthalen-2-ol specifically maps cholinergic areas in vivo.

Synthesis and biological evaluation of novel carbon-11 labeled pyridyl ethers: candidate ligands for in vivo imaging of alpha4beta2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (alpha4beta2-nAChRs) in the brain with positron emission tomography.
(-)-(2R,3R)-5-(2-[18F]fluoroethoxy)-3-(4-phenylpiperidin-1-yl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-naphthalen-2-ol specifically maps cholinergic areas in vivo.
Development of radioligands with optimized imaging properties for quantification of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors by positron emission tomography.

3β-(4-bromophenyl)tropane-2β-carboxylic acid methyl ester (7c) increases NET potency 4- to 7-fold (160–270 nM) compared to 7a, while decreasing DAT affinity (16–170 nM) and showing little effect on 5-HTT affinity (Ki values from 3.1 to 4.8 nM).
Synthesis and structure-activity relationship of 3beta-(4-alkylthio, -methylsulfinyl, and -methylsulfonylphenyl)tropane and 3beta-(4-alkylthiophenyl)nortropane derivatives for monoamine transporters.

(-)-(2R,3R)-5-(2-[18F]fluoroethoxy)-3-(4-phenylpiperidin-1-yl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-naphthalen-2-ol exhibits stereospecific cholinergic tracer behavior and pharmacological effects, with activity limited to the (-)-enantiomer.

Synthesis and biological evaluation of novel carbon-11 labeled pyridyl ethers: candidate ligands for in vivo imaging of alpha4beta2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (alpha4beta2-nAChRs) in the brain with positron emission tomography.
AGA (100 mM) is a connexin inhibitor that reduces the magnitude of hyposmotic shock-induced ATP release by 75% in intact porcine lenses, but does not abolish ATP release completely.
Hyposmotic stress causes ATP release and stimulates Na,K-ATPase activity in porcine lens.
LRE is a cell adhesion binding motif that, when incorporated into alginate microcapsules, is hypothesized to restore ECM interactions and enhance islet survival.
Functionalization of Alginate with Extracellular Matrix Peptides Enhances Viability and Function of Encapsulated Porcine Islets.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene (BM 225) is a glibenclamide isostere that increases 45Ca outflow and insulin release in rat pancreatic islets at 10 and 25 µM in the presence of 8.3 mM D-glucose.
In vitro and in vivo effects of new insulin releasing agents.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.
(24S)-2α,3α,5α,22,23-pentahydroxystigmastan-6-one is a synthetic brassinosteroid derivative that inhibits HSV-1 and arenavirus replication in cell culture.
Antiviral effect of brassinosteroids against herpes virus and arenaviruses.

trans-1,4-bis[[1-cyclohexyl-3-(4-dimethylamino phenyl)ureido]methyl]cyclohexane (NTE-122) is a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase (ACAT) inhibitor that inhibits cell-free ACAT activity in human PMA-treated THP-1 cells and mouse J774.1 cells with IC50 values of 0.88 nM and 360 nM, respectively.
Effects of NTE-122, a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase inhibitor, on cholesterol esterification and high-density lipoprotein-induced cholesterol efflux in macrophages.

3-methyl-aminothiophene dicarboxylic acid (3-MATIDA) is a selective and potent mGlu 1 receptor antagonist that exhibits neuroprotective effects in cultured neocortical cells and hippocampal slices subjected to oxygen-glucose deprivation (OGD) in vitro.
The novel and systemically active metabotropic glutamate 1 (mGlu1) receptor antagonist 3-MATIDA reduces post-ischemic neuronal death.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
Synthesis of trimethylhydroquinone derivatives as anti-allergic agents with anti-oxidative actions.
3,4-diamino-3-cyclobutene-1,2-dione functions as an amino acid isostere when incorporated into VLA-4 integrin antagonists to improve the rate of clearance.
Squaric acid derivatives as VLA-4 integrin antagonists.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.
(1R,6S)-6-methoxycarbonyl-3-cyclohexene-1-carboxylic acid is a versatile chiral synthon that can be obtained by enantioselective hydrolysis of the corresponding meso diester with pig liver esterase.
Chiral synthon obtained with pig liver esterase: introduction of chiral centers into cyclohexene skeleton.

N-[1-(4-chloro-benzyl)-4-methoxy-1H-indol-2-ylmethyl]-propionamide is a selective antagonist of the human MT(2) melatonin receptor, with its binding mode involving accommodation of out-of-plane substituents in a hydrophobic pocket, which is fundamental for receptor selectivity.
Analysis of structure-activity relationships for MT2 selective antagonists by melatonin MT1 and MT2 receptor models.

Octathymidylate, when covalently linked to an intercalating agent and complementary to a crucial A-T-rich region in the SV40 origin of replication, inhibits replication of SV40 viral DNA in CV1 cells by binding to open regions or forming local triple helices.
Inhibition of simian virus 40 DNA replication in CV-1 cells by an oligodeoxynucleotide covalently linked to an intercalating agent.

1-(2-nitro-4,5-dimethoxyphenyl)-N,N,N',N'-tetrakis[(oxycarbonyl)methyl]-1,2-ethanediamine (DM-nitrophen) is a caged Ca2+ compound that, upon two-photon excitation photolysis, induces local and sustained increases in intracellular Ca2+] in rat ventricular myocytes.
Calcium waves in skinned cardiac myocytes evoked by two-photon excitation photolysis of caged calcium.
MDL 73404 is an a-tocopherol analogue that reduces infarct size in a rat model of myocardial ischaemia and reperfusion, with maximum reduction at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg per h.
Protective effects of an alpha-tocopherol analogue against myocardial reperfusion injury in rats.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).
1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated as nonsteroidal progesterone receptor modulators with antiprogestant activity.
Development of progesterone receptor antagonists from 1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline agonist pharmacophore.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
6-(substituted methylene)penems, potent broad spectrum inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamase. IV. Kidney stability, serum binding and additional biological evaluation of racemic derivatives.

Kibdelone A is a novel bioactive heterocyclic polyketide produced by the actinomycete Kibdelosporangium sp. (MST-108465) and exhibits potent and selective cytotoxicity against human tumor cell lines, as well as significant antibacterial and nematocidal activity.
Kibdelones: novel anticancer polyketides from a rare Australian actinomycete.

4‐({1‐[5‐({[1‐tert‐butyl‐5‐(4‐fluorophenyl)‐1H‐pyrazol‐4‐yl]carbonyl}amino)‐2‐chlorobenzyl]piperidin‐4‐yl}oxy)benzoic acid (compound‐T1) is a novel FXR antagonist synthesized to study its pharmacological profile in a high-fat diet-induced dyslipidemic hamster model.
Antidyslipidemic potential of a novel farnesoid X receptor antagonist in a hamster model of dyslipidemia: Comparative studies of other nonstatin agents.

N-(2-bromo-5-(trifluoromethyl) phenyl)-6-methoxy-7-(3-(piperidin-1-yl)propoxy)quinazolin-4-amine (V1801) is a gefitinib analogue that induces apoptosis in L858R/T790M-EGFR harboring non-small cell lung cancer cells and overcomes gefitinib resistance in mice inoculated with NCI-H1975 cells.
Gefitinib analogue V1801 induces apoptosis of T790M EGFR-harboring lung cancer cells by up-regulation of the BH-3 only protein Noxa.

steffimycin E is a tetracyclic quinone compound with a sugar moiety that exhibits anti-mycobacterial activity against Mycobacterium avium complex, including M. intracellulare, M. bovis BCG, and M. smegmatis.
Steffimycin E, a new anti-mycobacterial agent against Mycobacterium avium complex, produced by Streptomyces sp. OPMA02852.
PDTC can form a complex with copper that significantly inhibits proteasome activity and leads to accumulation of ubiquitinated proteins in MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells.
Clioquinol and pyrrolidine dithiocarbamate complex with copper to form proteasome inhibitors and apoptosis inducers in human breast cancer cells.

Tetracyclic and linear extended pyrazolopyridine derivatives act as acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, with the tetracyclic cyclopentane derivative (12a) being four times less potent (IC50 = 23.2 µM) than the corresponding cyclohexane derivative (12b) (IC50 = 6.39 µM), possibly due to entropic effects from the more flexible cyclopentane ring.
Synthesis, pharmacological assessment, and molecular modeling of acetylcholinesterase/butyrylcholinesterase inhibitors: effect against amyloid-β-induced neurotoxicity.

Bis(Ph 2 SnCl)Xylene elicits apoptosis in NFS-70 and EL4 cells at 10 µM, and necrosis in the Kit 225 cell line at the same concentration, while causing low levels of both necrosis and apoptosis in HeLa cells at 10 µM.
Cytotoxic effects of two organotin compounds and their mode of inflicting cell death on four mammalian cancer cells.
Puerarin 6''-O-xyloside exhibits cytotoxic and antitumour activity against human lung carcinoma A549 cells by inducing apoptosis.
In vitro and in vivo antitumour activities of puerarin 6″-O-xyloside on human lung carcinoma A549 cell line via the induction of the mitochondria-mediated apoptosis pathway.

N-(2-bromo-5-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)-6-methoxy-7-(3-(piperidin-1-yl)propoxy)quinazolin-4-amine (V1801) is a gefitinib mimetic that moderately inhibits EGFR kinase activity and significantly suppresses proliferation and induces apoptosis in T790M EGFR-harboring non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) cells in vitro and in vivo.
Gefitinib analogue V1801 induces apoptosis of T790M EGFR-harboring lung cancer cells by up-regulation of the BH-3 only protein Noxa.

Tetracyclic and linear extended pyrazolopyridine derivatives act as acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, with the tetracyclic cyclopentane derivative (12a) being four times less potent (IC50 = 23.2 µM) than the corresponding cyclohexane derivative (12b) (IC50 = 6.39 µM), possibly due to entropic effects from the more flexible cyclopentane ring.
Structure-activity relationship studies of benzyl-, phenethyl-, and pyridyl-substituted tetrahydroacridin-9-amines as multitargeting agents to treat Alzheimer's disease.

Tetracyclic and linear extended pyrazolopyridine derivatives act as acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, with the tetracyclic cyclopentane derivative (12a) being four times less potent (IC50 = 23.2 µM) than the corresponding cyclohexane derivative (12b) (IC50 = 6.39 µM), possibly due to entropic effects from the more flexible cyclopentane ring.
New azepino[4,3-b]indole derivatives as nanomolar selective inhibitors of human butyrylcholinesterase showing protective effects against NMDA-induced neurotoxicity.
Puerarin 6''-O-xyloside increases the expression of caspase-3, caspase-7, caspase-9, and Bax, and decreases the expression of Bcl-2 in A549 lung carcinoma cells.
In vitro and in vivo antitumour activities of puerarin 6″-O-xyloside on human lung carcinoma A549 cell line via the induction of the mitochondria-mediated apoptosis pathway.
Bis(Ph 2 SnCl)Xylene induces apoptosis at high concentrations (2 and 10 µM) and necrosis at low concentrations (0.08 and 0.4 µM) in the EL4 cell line.
Cytotoxic effects of two organotin compounds and their mode of inflicting cell death on four mammalian cancer cells.
Inhibition of 6-phosphoguanosine diphosphate (6PGD) sensitizes RCC cells to standard chemotherapy drugs by inhibiting AMPK signaling and promoting NADPH-mediated metabolic reprogramming.
6PGD Upregulation is Associated with Chemo- and Immuno-Resistance of Renal Cell Carcinoma via AMPK Signaling-Dependent NADPH-Mediated Metabolic Reprograming.

3-({[bis (2chloroethyl)amino][(1-methyl-2-nitro-1H-imidazol-5-yl)methoxy]phosphoryl}amino)-N,N,N-trimethyl-propane-1-aminium iodide (ICF05016) is a nitroheteroaryl-based phosphoramidic mustard compound functionalized with a quaternary ammonium group and a 2-nitroimidazole group, designed for targeted delivery to chondrosarcoma via aggrecan and hypoxia-triggered release.
Structure-activity relationship study of hypoxia-activated prodrugs for proteoglycan-targeted chemotherapy in chondrosarcoma.
Penicipyrroether A is a novel pyrrospirone alkaloid with a unique 6/5/6/5 polycyclic fusion, representing the first example of its structural type.
Novel Bioactive Penicipyrroether A and Pyrrospirone J from the Marine-Derived Penicillium sp. ZZ380.

5,5-dibromo-1,1-bis(3-indolyl)-1-(p-t-butylphenyl)methane (5,5-dibromoDIM) inhibits Panc-1 pancreatic cancer cell growth through activation of endoplasmic reticulum stress pathways, including upregulation of GRP78 and CHOP/GADD153, and induction of DR5 and apoptosis.
Structure-dependent activation of endoplasmic reticulum stress-mediated apoptosis in pancreatic cancer by 1,1-bis(3'-indoly)-1-(p-substituted phenyl)methanes.

Tetracyclic and linear extended pyrazolopyridine derivatives act as acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, with the tetracyclic cyclopentane derivative (12a) being four times less potent (IC50 = 23.2 µM) than the corresponding cyclohexane derivative (12b) (IC50 = 6.39 µM), possibly due to entropic effects from the more flexible cyclopentane ring.

Multipotent drugs with cholinergic and neuroprotective properties for the treatment of Alzheimer and neuronal vascular diseases. I. Synthesis, biological assessment, and molecular modeling of simple and readily available 2-aminopyridine-, and 2-chloropyridine-3,5-dicarbonitriles.

3-(4-{[benzyl(ethyl)amino]methyl}phenyl)-6-{ [5-(diethylamino)pentyl]oxy}-2H-chromen-2-one is a nanomolar human acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with significant inhibitory activity toward Ab42 self-aggregation.
Structure-activity relationship studies of benzyl-, phenethyl-, and pyridyl-substituted tetrahydroacridin-9-amines as multitargeting agents to treat Alzheimer's disease.
Chaetoglobosin E is a cytotoxic indole-3-yl-cytochalasane isolated from soil-derived Chaetomium globosum strains.
HPLC-DAD-Guided Isolation of Diversified Chaetoglobosins from the Coral-Associated Fungus Chaetomium globosum C2F17.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
8-(1-decynyl)benzolactam-V8 is a PKC modulator that exhibits improved potency and selectivity for classical PKC isozymes.
Synthesis and modeling study of (2S,5R,6R)- and (2S,5R,6S)-6-hydroxy- 8-(1-decynyl)-benzolactam-V8 as protein kinase C modulators.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

Acid-promoted de-tert-butylation of N-nitroso-N-tert-butylamine generates a diazonium cation in situ, which can be used for cyclo-etherification to produce substituted benzofuran-3(2H)-ones and P-stereogenic chiral dihydrobenzo[d][1,3]oxaphosphole 3-oxides.
Metal-Free Cycloetherification by in Situ Generated P-Stereogenic α-Diazanium Intermediates: A Convergent Synthesis of Enantiomerically Pure Dihydrobenzooxaphospholes.
(E)-1-decenyl(phenyl)-λ3-iodane undergoes vinylic S–N2 reactions with thioureas to yield (Z)-S-vinylisothiouronium salts with inversion of configuration.
Stereoselective synthesis of (Z)-enethiols and their derivatives: vinylic S(N)2 reaction of (E)-alkenyl(phenyl)-lambda3-iodanes with thioamides.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
A facile copper-catalyzed one-pot domino synthesis of 5,12-dihydroindolo[2,1-b]quinazolines.
3-thienylalanine is identified as an especially good substrate for phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase (PheRS) based on computational predictions using the HierDock protocol.
Virtual screening for binding of phenylalanine analogues to phenylalanyl-tRNA synthetase.

1-chloromethylbenzotriazole reacts with 2-aminopyridines and 1-aminoisoquinoline to produce 2-amino-1-[alpha-benzotriazol-1-ylmethyl]pyridinium chlorides and 1-amino-2-(alpha-benzotriazol-1-ylmethyl)isoquinolinium++ + chloride, respectively.
Efficient syntheses of imidazolo[1,2-a]pyridines and -[2, 1-a]isoquinolines.

3-(hydroxymethyl)benzophenone undergoes a highly efficient (Φ ∼ 0.6) intramolecular redox reaction in acidic (pH < 3) aqueous solution to form 3-formylbenzhydrol (95%) with trace amounts of the fully oxidized 3-formylbenzophenone (5%).
Formal intramolecular photoredox chemistry of meta-substituted benzophenones.
Benzoxazole carboxylic acid can be converted to its methyl ester via a catalytic reaction with CO2 using tzNHC-copper(I) complexes as catalysts.
Direct C-H carboxylation with carbon dioxide using 1,2,3-triazol-5-ylidene copper(I) complexes.

The thermolytic deprotection of 4-methylthio-1-butyl phosphate groups occurs via cyclodeesterification, resulting in the formation of a sulfonium salt side product that is inert to DNA nucleobases and does not cause significant desulfurization of phosphorothioate diesters.
Thermolytic 4-methylthio-1-butyl group for phosphate/thiophosphate protection in solid-phase synthesis of DNA oligonucleotides.
Cyclopropeniminium scaffolds are sufficiently stable in biological media to be used in bioorthogonal ligation reactions.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
6,7-diethynylquinoxaline derivatives exhibit strong exotherms indicative of enediyne cyclization and undergo C(1)-C(6) Bergman cyclization in the presence of 1,4-cyclohexadiene.
Syntheses, thermal reactivities, and computational studies of aryl-fused quinoxalenediynes: effect of extended benzannelation on Bergman cyclization energetics.
Bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene derivatives can be synthesized by reacting cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes in the presence of a rhenium catalyst, [ReBr(CO)(3)(thf)](2).
Rhenium-catalyzed formation of bicyclo[3.3.1]nonene frameworks by a reaction of cyclic beta-keto esters with terminal alkynes.

3-phosphinoylindole frameworks can be synthesized via a cascade annulation involving phosphinoylation, cyclization, and desulfonylation of 2-alkynyl-aniline derivatives with secondary phosphine oxides, enabling C–P and C–N bond formation.
A Cascade Phosphinoylation/Cyclization/Desulfonylation Process for the Synthesis of 3-Phosphinoylindoles.
Imidazole-1-sulfonyl azide tetrafluoroborate salt is used to catalyze a DNA-compatible diazo-transfer reaction that converts primary amines into azides in good to excellent yields.
DNA-Compatible Diazo-Transfer Reaction in Aqueous Media Suitable for DNA-Encoded Chemical Library Synthesis.
(E)-1-decenyl(phenyl)-λ 3 -iodane undergoes nucleophilic vinylic substitution with N,N-dimethylthioformamide in dichloromethane to yield (Z)-enethiol 2a.
Stereoselective synthesis of (Z)-enethiols and their derivatives: vinylic S(N)2 reaction of (E)-alkenyl(phenyl)-lambda3-iodanes with thioamides.
